### W17 drip-88 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 2 repos — at sst/opencode, the small-but-contaminated #24522 race fix where the actual `currentSessionID` snapshot + post-`await` re-comparison guard against stale `sdk.client.session.get()` mutations is correct and minimal but the diff also smuggles in (a) a brand-new `sync_prompt_context_on_session_switch` kv signal + command-palette toggle that belongs to PR #24508 and (b) a Monokai scrollbar palette swap that belongs to PR #24507, plus a 0-byte `packages/sdk/js/openapi.json` placeholder; the disciplined #24523 closes #23490 by replacing lexicographic `lastUser.id < lastAssistant.id` and `m.info.id <= lastFinished.id` predicates in the `SessionPrompt.run` back-scan with array-index comparisons (`lastUserIndex < lastAssistantIndex`, `for (let i = lastFinishedIndex + 1; i < msgs.length; i++)`), unconditionally correct under non-monotonic message IDs and a small perf win on the skip-loop, missing only a regression test exercising lexically-inverted IDs; the small #24524 swaps `void Promise.all([...])` for `return Promise.all([...]).then(...).catch(e => { Log.Default.error(...); setStore('status','complete') })` in the TUI background-sync chain so the ~12 parallel SDK rejections are observed and the status no longer sticks at `"partial"` indefinitely; the substantial #24525 attacks three independent OOM-amplification paths in one PR — `Bus` swaps `PubSub.unbounded` → `PubSub.sliding<Payload>(1024)` for the wildcard channel, both global and instance SSE routes get bounded `AsyncQueue<string|null>(256)` plus an overflow-then-`stop()` policy that closes the connection rather than silently dropping individual messages, the tool `metadata()` callback dedups via 100ms throttle + `JSON.stringify(input)` fingerprint (key-order fragile) and now preserves prior `title`/`metadata`/`time.start` when the new value is `undefined`, and the shell-streaming path spills to disk via `createWriteStream(outputPath, {flags:"a"})` once `bufferedOutput` exceeds `limits.maxBytes` while keeping a 30 KB sliding preview, with `Effect.ensuring(toolMetadata.delete(...))` cleanup that's correct but the `outputSink.end()` Promise wrapper doesn't `stream.destroy()` on error; the misleadingly-titled #24526 ships under "Simplify Infinity mode judge prompt" but is actually a +798/−1 initial Infinity-mode UX implementation including a new permission-deny-by-default `infinity` agent in `agent/agent.ts`, a polling `useQuery({queryKey:["infinity",params.id], refetchInterval: 10000})` client that `.catch(() => ({active:false}))` silently masks server errors, identical `"∞"` glyph for both toggle states, V2 server endpoints `infinitySet`/`infinityClear`/`infinityStatus`, and regenerated SDK gen files — needs splitting into the cleanup PR the title claims and a separate feature PR for the toggle UX. At charmbracelet/crush, #2690 is the better-shaped sibling of the already-reviewed #2691 NOTADB fix, combining `db.SetMaxOpenConns(1)` with `_txlock=immediate` across both modernc and ncruces drivers (the latter requires DSN-string param injection because the driver doesn't expose a Go-level config struct), addressing both the WAL-frame-interleaving corruption and the deferred→writer upgrade contention as separate failure modes; #2686 lands a clean Moonshot/Moonshot-China env-var resolver with `cmp.Or(MOONSHOT_API_KEY, KIMI_API_KEY)` precedence and 95 lines of test, but ships a `replace charm.land/catwalk => github.com/ne275/catwalk v0.0.0-20260423090449-...` directive pointing at a personal-account GitHub fork — supply-chain risk that needs to be split into "international half (no replace)" + "China half (after upstream Catwalk tag)"; and #2643 fixes two coupled reasoning-display bugs in the TUI by bypassing `getCachedRender` while `isSpinning()` (the cache was keyed only by width and captured the empty pre-stream content forever) and threading a new `showThinking bool` parameter through `ExtractMessageItems → NewAssistantMessageItem` plus the action handler for `ActionToggleThinkingVisibility`, with the concern that `refreshMessages()` re-runs `ListMessages` from the database on every toggle which forces a full chat-history re-layout instead of a lightweight in-memory rebuild.

### W17 drip-89 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 3 repos — at sst/opencode, the new background-task sidebar plugin at #24532 (`feature-plugins/sidebar/tasks.tsx:14-43`) correctly models both "tool still running" and "tool completed but child session still busy" lifecycle states with a `runningTasks` memo, paired with a misleading `Locale.duration(0)` "0ms" → "In Progress" label fix at `routes/session/index.tsx:2014-2016`, but needs a one-line guard for empty-string `subagent_type` and reactivity confirmation on `state.session.status(childSessionId)`; #24528 restructures the TUI startup Promise from a swallow-everything async-executor into a proper `(resolve, reject)` shape with a `fail()` cleanup that destroys partial renderers, except the inner `void (async () => {...})()` IIFE is missing the `.catch(fail)` hookup that would actually wire rejections through, leaving the fix incomplete despite the correct skeleton; #24531 bumps `@opentui/core`/`solid` 0.1.103→0.1.104 (theme-mode feedback loop fixes) and silently *deletes* the local `util/terminal.ts` OSC 4/10/11 color-query helper without explaining whether 0.1.104 supersedes it or whether the call sites are gone — needs `rg "Terminal\.colors\("` audit at the head SHA. At charmbracelet/crush, #2675 bundles three unrelated fixes (empty-tool-call-input → `"{}"` normalization at `agent.go:618-624`, named-arg regex Unicode widening from `\$([A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*)` to `\$([\p{L}_][\p{L}\p{N}_]*)` at `commands.go:17`, plus `message/content.go` edits) where the regex change silently makes `$lowercase` start matching when it previously didn't — a soft breaking change for existing user command files that the PR body doesn't acknowledge; #2681 fixes the GPT-5-mini class hidden-reasoning empty-title bug by bumping `maxOutputTokens` from 40→256 and adding a second retry path at `agent.go:994-1009` that calls the large model when the small one returned cleanly with empty visible text (`!isUsableTitleResponse(resp)`), gated by a model-ID inequality check to avoid double-calling the same model, but ships no test for the new branch; #2652 is a textbook ctx-cancellation propagation fix threading `context.Context` through `searchFilesWithRegex`/`fileContainsPattern`/the inner `filepath.Walk` callback at eight boundaries in `internal/agent/tools/grep.go`, with the load-bearing check at `:181-183` between ripgrep failure and regex fallback (previously, ctx-cancellation during ripgrep manifested as a ripgrep error, so the fallback ran anyway burning CPU after the user already cancelled); #2647 is a 4-line off-by-`offsetLine` fix at `list/list.go:88` changing the `AtBottom()` early-exit guard from `totalHeight > l.height` to `totalHeight - l.offsetLine > l.height`, accounting for the lines of the topmost item that are scrolled out of view, restoring auto-scroll on long-message sessions. At BerriAI/litellm, #26425 splits `_handle_authentication_error()` into a "expected auth deny" branch (BudgetExceededError, ProxyException 401/403, HTTPException 401/403) that logs at WARNING without traceback and an "everything else" branch that keeps the original `exception()` ERROR + traceback — reduces routine access-control noise hitting Datadog/OTEL error budgets, but loses the alerting signal for *server-side* JWT-decode 401s that should still page on-call, and bundles 4 unrelated black/ruff formatting-only diffs in `factory.py`/`predibase/transformation.py`/`xecguard.py` that should be split out.

### W17 drip-90 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 3 repos. At sst/opencode, #24535 introduces multi-root workspaces via a new `WorkspaceProvider` + `dialog-workspace-create.tsx` + Playwright e2e — the `once()` callback wrapper at `dialog-workspace-create.tsx:23-31` correctly prevents double-fire of the directory-picker reopen path, but it captures `currentName`/`currentFolders` at `addFolder()` invocation time so any keystrokes typed into the name field while the picker is open are silently dropped on reopen, plus the dedupe at `:56-60` only does `result.trim()` with no `path.resolve`/trailing-slash strip so `~/foo` and `~/foo/` create two entries pointing at the same dir, and `workspace.new` and `workspace.create` are both registered as common commands without explaining the relationship; #24520 parameterizes the Dockerfile with `BASE_IMAGE`/`DIST_SUFFIX` build-args to produce alpine and ubuntu variants from one recipe, with the alpine→`apk` vs ubuntu→`apt-get` dispatch keyed on `[ -f /etc/alpine-release ]` (more robust would be `command -v apk`) and silently bumping the alpine pin from floating `alpine` to `alpine:3.21` and adding `:latest` to the alpine publish set — both behavior changes that need callouts; #24477 centralizes `parts → PromptInfo` reconstruction in a new `routes/session/prompt-info.ts` factory and rewrites the producer side at `session/prompt.ts:1622-1655` to attach `metadata.command = { invocation, name, arguments }` to the first text part of a command invocation, with the consumer at `prompt-info.ts:24-32` doing a two-pass reduce that finds the command then drops free-form text (the `agg.input = command ?? ""` overrides redundantly with the inner `!command` guard — pick one), and the three dialog refactors gain a latent `?? []` crash-fix on missing parts. At charmbracelet/crush, #2721 is a 3-line fix to the rename-dialog input width arithmetic at `sessions_item.go:91-97` swapping `InfoTextFocused.GetHorizontalFrameSize()` (wrong sibling style — copy-paste bug) for `ItemFocused.GetHorizontalFrameSize()` plus a `cursorPadding=1` reservation and a `max(0, ...)` clamp; #2711 reworks the todo-spinner+pill-render trigger at `internal/ui/model/ui.go:582-596` so any session update unconditionally re-renders pills (fixing stale pills on todo add/complete/reorder/edit) and the spinner starts/stops on steady-state rather than rising-edge of `hasInProgressTodo`, but silently removes the `m.updateLayoutAndSize()` call that previously fired when the first in_progress todo arrived — if pills can change height (multi-row wrap on long todo text), neighboring panes will miscalculate until an unrelated layout event; #2699 threads `workspaceRoot` (the LSP client's `cwd`) through `HandleApplyEdit` → `ApplyWorkspaceEdit` → `applyTextEdits`/`applyDocumentChange` and validates every URI before write/create/rename/delete, with a Windows-specific `normalizeURIPath()` that strips the `\C:\...` / `/C:/...` leading-separator-before-drive-letter trap that otherwise makes `filepath.Abs` prefix the current drive yielding `D:\C:\...`, and uses `fsext.HasPrefix` for separator-aware boundary checking — symlink resolution is intentionally out of scope. At BerriAI/litellm, #26551 fixes a bare `return` inside an `async generator` in `tool_permission.py:683-690` that was silently swallowing all upstream chunks (clients received only `data: [DONE]`) by re-wrapping the assembled response in `MockResponseIterator` and yielding its chunks before returning, with a regression test that uses `assert len(chunks) >= 1` — too weak, would pass for "yielded one empty chunk" — and bundles unrelated black/ruff formatter diffs in `factory.py`/`predibase/transformation.py`/`xecguard.py` that should be split; #26550 fixes non-ASCII response-header truncation by switching `aiohttp_transport.py:343` from `response.headers` (CIMultiDict[str,str], already lossy-decoded by aiohttp) to `response.raw_headers` (the bytes-tuple form httpx accepts and decodes losslessly), and parallel-fixes the credential-leak masker at `http_handler.py:381-388` to iterate `headers.raw` and compare bytes literals (`b"content-encoding"`) — the two paths use different APIs (`raw_headers` for aiohttp client response, `.headers.raw` for httpx response) which deserves a one-line clarifying comment in each file, and bundles the same unrelated formatter diffs as #26551.

### W17 drip-91 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At sst/opencode, #24317 adds a Ctrl+E keybind in the agent picker that opens the model selector pre-targeted at the highlighted agent and persists `agent.<name>.model` back into `opencode.json`/`opencode.jsonc` via `jsonc-parser`'s `modify()`+`applyEdits()` at `context/local.tsx:148-260`, with a fragile `isJsonc` heuristic (`file.includes("//") || file.includes("/*")`) that mistriggers on URL strings in descriptions and a hardcoded `["explore", "general", "translator"]` exclusion list at `dialog-agent.tsx:23-31` that silently desyncs if any of those agents get renamed — plus the PR bundles a second feature (`vision_model` config + `Provider.getVisionModel()` fallback at `provider/provider.ts:1647-1672`) that should be split; #24321 fixes Windows bash detection so PATH wins over the bundled Git-for-Windows `bash.exe`, adds MSYS2 UCRT64 candidates (`{git}/../../../usr/bin/bash.exe`, `{git}/../bash.exe`), and explicitly excludes the WSL `%SystemRoot%\System32\bash.exe` trap via a case-insensitive `Filesystem.windowsPath()` compare at `shell.ts:101-105` with three solid Windows-specific tests using a `withWindowsEnv()` scoped env-mutation helper. At charmbracelet/crush, #2527 adds left-click hitbox dispatch for the three permission-dialog buttons by computing `[3]image.Rectangle` during `Draw()` at `internal/ui/dialog/permissions.go:462-541` — the layout math is duplicated between the rendered string and the hitbox computation (any future change to inner padding silently desyncs them), the 1-cell `hitboxPad` causes adjacent-button overlap that's resolved by switch-case order, and the `ui.go:677-680` change from `m.dialog.Update(msg)` to `m.handleDialogMsg(msg)` is a separate latent bug-fix that should be called out in the commit message; #2520 replaces the immediate Ctrl+D-on-empty-editor exit with a quit-prompt dialog gated on a five-clause `shouldPromptQuitOnEOF` predicate at `ui.go:2957-2972` (focus is editor, state is chat or landing, no buffered input, no completion popup, no attachments), with three table-style tests covering the empty/non-empty/has-attachment cases. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3598 adds a `--json-schema '<json>'` / `@path` CLI flag that registers a synthetic `structured_output` tool whose parameter schema **is** the user-supplied schema, with a strict `SchemaValidator.compileStrict()` Ajv path that fails fast at CLI parse time (in contrast to the lenient runtime `validate()` that silently skips unsupported schemas for MCP compat) — the `schemaRootAcceptsObject()` walker at `config.ts:172-203` correctly handles `type: "object"`, array-typed `type`, root `anyOf`/`oneOf` recursion, and lenient-default for bare `{}`, but the "rejected when combined with `-i`" yargs check is claimed in the body without visible diff confirmation; #3593 threads an optional `argumentHint` field through ACP `availableCommands` (preserves `input: null` when unset for backward compat at `Session.ts:984`), bundled-skill loader, file-based markdown frontmatter (`argument-hint: "[issue-number]"` at `FileCommandLoader.ts:358`), and skill-derived commands, with per-loader unit tests at every layer. At BerriAI/litellm, #26371 registers Telnyx (`https://api.telnyx.com/v2/ai`, `TELNYX_API_KEY`) as an OpenAI-compatible provider via the JSON registry — three-way agreement between `providers.json` key, `LlmProviders.TELNYX` enum, and the `model="telnyx/..."` URL prefix, four solid tests covering registration/config/dynamic-class/resolution at `tests/test_litellm/llms/openai_like/test_telnyx_provider.py:18-78`, conservative endpoints-support matrix (`chat_completions: true, embeddings: true`, all else false), and a docs page whose model table lists three placeholder names (`Kimi-K2.6`, `GLM-5.1-FP8`, `MiniMax-M2.7`) that need verification against Telnyx's live model list. At block/goose, #8795 makes the previously-unused `dynamic_models: Option<bool>` field on `DeclarativeProviderConfig` actually short-circuit the `/v1/models` API call when set to `false` (with `is_endpoint_not_found` 404-fallback preserved when unset), with parallel changes in `crates/goose/src/providers/anthropic.rs:97-114,298-302` and `crates/goose/src/providers/openai.rs:160-177,516-520` — the construction-time validation rejects `dynamic_models: false` + empty `models` with a precise error message, and the Anthropic test suite covers all four scenarios (static-only, dynamic-true, none-falls-back-on-404, validation-error) using `wiremock::MockServer` returning 500 to prove no API call happens; the duplicated validation logic across both provider files is a code smell that wants a `validate_dynamic_models()` helper in `declarative_providers.rs`.

### W17 drip-92 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At sst/opencode, #24538 is a single-row addition to the `ecosystem.mdx` table at `:73` registering Sortie (a ticket→agent orchestrator that recently shipped an opencode adapter), preserving column padding and matching surrounding row tone — pure additive docs; #24533 drops a 7-doc 849-line `docs/architecture/` suite covering prompt composition, agents/modes, tooling, execution loops, state/memory, security/config, and the Effect-TS+Pub/Sub framework, but reads as a polished marketing-shaped architecture brief rather than an "open-this-repo-where-do-I-start" doc, several "the system does X" claims (e.g. `00_architecture-overview.md:34` on a "Fast Loop intercepts execution failures and autonomously re-prompts the agent" daemon) outrun what `session/prompt.ts` actually implements, and at least one cross-reference path (`src/session/instruction.ts`) doesn't appear to exist in the current monorepo — maintainer should decide whether to own a `docs/architecture/` tree at all before merging. At charmbracelet/crush, #2723 adds Kagi as a second search engine alongside DuckDuckGo (1066/-18 across 18 files): clean `searchKagi` against `kagi.com/api/v0/search` with typed 401/403/`402 PaymentRequired` mapping at `internal/agent/tools/search.go:140-149`, correct `parseKagiSearchResults` filtering of `t != 0` related-query rows, a thread-safety refactor of `maybeDelaySearch()` that snapshots delay under lock then sleeps unlocked at `:295-308` (only DuckDuckGo path uses it; Kagi opt-out is unstated), new `SearchEngine` typed enum + `Engine()` silent-coerce-to-DuckDuckGo at `config/config.go:381-385`, `select_search_engine` command + `SearchEngines` dialog + `KagiAPIKeyInput` dialog wired through `ui/model/ui.go:1525-1561` with auto-prompt-then-auto-apply UX, but **`schema.json:813` adds `web_search` to the `Tools` required list which breaks back-compat for strict-validators on existing user configs** and the `Engine()` silent-coerce should `slog.Warn` on unknown values. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3611 is a 2-line skill-prompt fix to `packages/core/src/skills/bundled/review/SKILL.md:20,531` so `/review`'s "Match the language of the PR" rule falls back to the user's `/language output` preference for local reviews (no PR target) — PR-review path explicitly preserved, the two phrasings are minorly out-of-sync; #3605 adds Space-to-preview to the `/resume` session picker via a new `SessionPreview.tsx` (164+) that inline-renders the previewed session through the real `HistoryItemDisplay` pipeline, gated by a new opt-in `enablePreview` prop with the **best comment in the PR** at `SessionPicker.tsx:31-36` explaining why it's opt-in (preview's Enter forwards to `onSelect`, fine for resume but destructive for delete flows), clean `viewMode: 'list' | 'preview'` state machine in `useSessionPicker.ts:98-104` with belt-and-suspenders preview-owns-keyboard guarding at `:234-238` and `:319`, narrow-terminal hardening via `Math.max(10, columns-4)` to dodge `'─'.repeat(n)` `RangeError`, and the documented lossy `null`-config preview ("tool_group entries degrade to name-only") that should make it into release notes. At BerriAI/litellm, #26516 fixes a real authz gap on three MCP OAuth proxy endpoints (`/server/oauth/{server_id}/{authorize,token,register}`) that previously authenticated callers but didn't check per-server access — anyone with a valid proxy key could initiate OAuth handshakes against the proxy's stored credentials for any registered server, fix centralizes the policy in `_get_cached_temporary_mcp_server_or_404` at `mcp_management_endpoints.py:1473-1493` with a `resolved_from_temp_cache` flag that 403s temp-cache servers for non-admins (admin-only setup flow) and falls through to `get_allowed_mcp_servers` for the registry path, signature change forces compile-time discovery of the three call-sites at `:1515,:1561,:1599`, two new tests cover the non-admin branches but the temp-cache + non-admin branch is missing — should ship with a security advisory acknowledging the prior gap. At block/goose, #8837 fixes a real CI-flake mechanism in `ui/desktop/tests/integration/test_providers_lib.ts` where vitest's external `testTimeout` aborts uncatchably so the existing flaky-test try/catch wrapper can't suppress timeouts from hung providers like `nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-30b-a3b`, fix adds an internal 55s `setTimeout` inside `runGoose()` that `child.kill('SIGKILL')` and rejects with a catchable Error (preserving partial output in the message for CI logs), bumps vitest's per-test budget to 90s on flaky tests so the internal timer always trips first with 35s of headroom, double-resolve guarded via a `settled` flag on close/error/timer paths; #8798 adds NVIDIA NIM as a declarative provider via `nvidia.json` plus extends `DeclarativeProviderConfig` with `model_doc_link`/`setup_steps` so providers can override the OpenAI-default settings UI (registry test at `init.rs:241-275` confirms NVIDIA does **not** expose `OPENAI_HOST`/`OPENAI_BASE_PATH`), and bundles a canonical-limit fix at `model.rs:158-165` with a strict `output < context` predicate that stops requesting the entire context window as `max_tokens` when canonical metadata reports `output == context` (the NVIDIA case) — but the strict `<` doesn't catch the asymptotic "output is almost as large as context" pathology, and the three concerns (provider add + UX field plumbing + canonical-limit fix) bundled in one PR muddies bisect history.

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24538 Sortie ecosystem-table row, QwenLM/qwen-code #3611 surgical 2-line skill-prompt language-fallback fix, BerriAI/litellm #26516 MCP-OAuth authz centralization that closes a real privilege-escalation gap, block/goose #8837 vitest external-timeout bypass via internal `setTimeout`+SIGKILL with `settled`-flag double-resolve guard), 3 merge-after-nits (charmbracelet/crush #2723 Kagi adds a back-compat-breaking required `web_search` field in `schema.json:813`, QwenLM/qwen-code #3605 Space-to-preview ships a documented-but-uncalled-out lossy-`null`-config preview behaviour, block/goose #8798 strict `output < context` predicate misses the asymptotic "output ≈ context" pathology and bundles three concerns in one PR), 1 needs-discussion (sst/opencode #24533 849-line architecture-docs suite ships marketing-shaped prose with at least one stale path reference and unverified "the system does X" claims — maintenance-cost decision more than code-quality issue).

### W17 drip-93 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At sst/opencode, #24544 fixes a real correctness bug in `SessionPrompt.run` at `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:1322-1474` where lexicographic message-ID comparisons (`lastUser.id < lastAssistant.id`, `m.info.id <= lastFinished.id`) broke loop-exit and message-replay logic for any client supplying custom (non-monotonic) message IDs — fix introduces three index trackers (`lastUserIndex`/`lastAssistantIndex`/`lastFinishedIndex`) captured during the existing reverse-scan and replaces both predicates with array-position comparisons, with the second loop also collapsing from `for...of msgs + skip` into `for (let i = lastFinishedIndex + 1; ...)` for a free perf bonus; #24543 closes a session-route race where an in-flight `await sdk.client.session.get()` could mutate workspace state for the wrong session if the user navigated away mid-flight, fix at `routes/session/index.tsx:185-218` captures `currentSessionID` before the await and short-circuits with `if (route.sessionID !== currentSessionID) return` after each suspension point, also wires a new `sync_prompt_context_on_session_switch` kv toggle, but bundles two unrelated changes (a 0-byte `packages/sdk/js/openapi.json` build artifact and a scrollbar `theme.background`/`theme.borderActive` recolor) that should be split out. At charmbracelet/crush, #2519 fixes `ProjectNeedsInitialization()` at `internal/config/init.go` so that projects with custom `options.initialize_as` (e.g. `bazel-bin/AGENTS.md`, `docs/LLMs.md`) are no longer flagged as needing re-initialization — old code was scanning the workdir for hardcoded `defaultContextPaths` filenames; new code routes through `Options.ContextPaths` (which now includes `InitializeAs` after `setDefaults` is reordered at `load.go:405-412`) and adds `resolveContextPath()` with a proper `~`/`$VAR`/relative/absolute resolver, with two pinning tests (`init_test.go:11-35` for the regression and `load_test.go:63-77` for the defaulting). At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3653 is a pure-refactor follow-up to #3629 that extracts the duplicated `QWEN_CODE_API_TIMEOUT_MS` env-override block out of `resolveModelConfig()` and `resolveQwenOAuthConfig()` into `applyTimeoutEnvOverride()` at `modelConfigResolver.ts:108-130`, with 8 new tests (4 `[Regression]` covering the original OAuth-path bug + cross-path precedence + env-vs-settings ordering, 4 `[Additional]` covering scientific-notation/hex parsing, empty-string fallthrough, and a per-auth-type sweep) — exactly the kind of behaviour-preserving refactor that locks down implicit precedence with explicit assertions; #3583 is a 864-line PRD docs-only PR proposing an in-terminal Custom-API-Key wizard at `docs/design/custom-api-key-auth-wizard-prd.md` to replace the current static "edit settings.json yourself" screen, with reasonable scoping (protocol select → base URL → key → models → JSON review → save+auth, no per-model `generationConfig`/`capabilities`) but two governance questions a maintainer needs to settle before code follows: (1) does this repo want PRDs committed to a new `docs/design/` tree at all, and (2) the auto-generated `envKey` naming scheme + shadowing semantics vs already-exported shell env vars (`OPENAI_API_KEY` etc.) are not locked down. At block/goose, #8811 is a one-line `Cargo.toml` hygiene fix moving `insta = "1.43.2"` from `[dependencies]` to `[dev-dependencies]` (snapshot-test crate accidentally in the runtime build graph for every dependent of `goose`) — minimum viable change, version pin preserved exactly; #8805 (RFC) groups the desktop UI session list by `working_dir` via a new `src/utils/projectSessions.ts` with `groupSessionsByProject`/`getProjectLabel`/`resolveNewChatWorkingDir` helpers, well-covered by a 150-line unit test (trailing-separator canonicalization, blank-dir → `'Unknown'` fallback, basename-collision disambiguation `'work/goose'` vs `'forks/goose'`, Windows path handling), threaded through `ChatSessionsDropdown`/`SessionsList`/`CondensedRenderer`/`ExpandedRenderer`/`NavigationPanel` — but plumbs `recentSessions` and `recentSessionsByProject` as **two parallel data shapes** for the same content rather than deriving the flat list from the grouped one, which is a maintenance hazard for follow-up work. At cline/cline, #10425 is a bot-generated security upgrade resolving 26 CVEs across 9 packages including critical CVE-2026-33896 in node-forge (cert-chain validation bypass when intermediates lack `basicConstraints`), 4 HIGH XXE/injection CVEs in `@xmldom/xmldom`, HIGH CRLF-injection in `basic-ftp`, HIGH ReDoS in `path-to-regexp`, HIGH path-traversal in `hono`'s `toSSG()`, and an axios prototype-pollution patch — direct surface is one tiny `axios 1.15.0 → 1.15.1` bump, the rest is lockfile churn, but the bot's own breaking-change analysis is **incomplete** for `hono` (4.12.9 → 4.12.15, six minor versions), `follow-redirects`, `@aws-sdk/xml-builder`, and `@hono/node-server`, plus a net-new transitive `@nodable/entities@2.1.0` package shows up uncommented — needs a five-minute manual changelog scan before merging.

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24544 SessionPrompt.run lex-ID-vs-array-position fix with predicate semantics preserved + free perf bonus, charmbracelet/crush #2519 `initialize_as` honored via reordered `setDefaults` + new `~`/`$VAR`/relative/absolute path resolver with two pinning tests, QwenLM/qwen-code #3653 behaviour-preserving timeout-env extraction with 8 lock-down tests including the original OAuth-path regression, block/goose #8811 one-line `insta` `[dependencies]`→`[dev-dependencies]` hygiene), 3 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24543 stale-session-effect race-fix bundles a 0-byte `openapi.json` artifact and an unrelated scrollbar recolor, block/goose #8805 project-grouping ships solid unit-tested helpers but plumbs two parallel `recentSessions`/`recentSessionsByProject` data shapes that wants consolidation, cline/cline #10425 bot-driven 26-CVE bump including critical node-forge cert-chain bypass but breaking-change analysis is incomplete for `hono`/`follow-redirects`/`@aws-sdk/xml-builder`/`@hono/node-server` and a net-new `@nodable/entities` transitive needs provenance check), 1 needs-discussion (QwenLM/qwen-code #3583 864-line custom-API-key-wizard PRD has reasonable UX scoping but needs maintainer alignment on whether `docs/design/` PRDs belong in-tree at all and on the auto-generated-`envKey` naming + shell-env-shadowing semantics before implementation can follow).

---

### W17 drip-94 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At sst/opencode, #24548 bridges all 13 TUI routes onto the experimental Effect HttpApi via a new 286-line `httpapi/tui.ts` (handlers built once at layer time with `Bus.Service`/`Session.Service` resolved up-front, `commandAliases` table at lines 23-37 preserves snake_case wire-contract while internal events use dotted form, `selectSession` declares `HttpApiError.NotFound` but the actual mapping needs eyeballing, `Bus.layer` is explicitly re-provided in `httpapi/server.ts:86-91` which may double-register if it's already global) plus a 79-line integration test and an accurate checklist flip in `specs/effect/http-api.md:329-345`; #24541 fixes a swallowed-rejection bug in `cli/cmd/tui/context/sync.tsx:421-451` where the inner `Promise.all` was previously fired off as `void Promise.all(...).then(...)` with no `.catch`, leaving the spinner stuck in `partial` forever and risking unhandled-rejection crashes if any of `sessionListPromise`/`consoleStatePromise`/`sdk.client.command.list`/`provider.auth`/`vcs.get`/`project.workspace.sync()` rejected — fix returns the inner promise from the outer chain and adds a `.catch` that logs `error`/`name`/`stack` (matching the sibling bootstrap-failure handler's style) and force-completes status on both branches; #24537 is a one-liner adding `args: params,` to the edit-tool result at `tool/edit.ts:206` to prevent a TUI render crash (issue #24529) where the renderer destructures `result.args.filePath` and throws on `undefined`, but ships without a regression test and echoes the entire param payload (including potentially-large `oldString`/`newString`) back to the wire when a `{ filePath }` projection would be cheaper. At charmbracelet/crush, #2725 fixes a real width-math bug in `internal/ui/model/status.go:101-110` where the old code used the *current* `msgWidth` as the truncation budget (going negative whenever the message exceeded `area.Dx()-indWidth`) and ignored `msgStyle`'s `GetPaddingLeft+GetPaddingRight` entirely — new code computes `avail := max(0, area.Dx()-indWidth-msgPad)` first, truncates to `avail`, then re-measures and pads to `avail-w` (also fixes a subtle off-by-one on east-asian-wide-char status messages); #2724 restores a missing `case app.UpdateAvailableMsg:` in `internal/ui/model/ui.go:856-867` with two-branch render (production "Crush update available: vX → vY", dev "This is a development version of Crush. The latest version is vY"), `util.InfoTypeUpdate` for distinct styling, and a 10s TTL — TTL is hardcoded inline rather than reusing `DefaultStatusTTL` from the immediately-preceding case. At block/goose, #8822 is a dependabot `candle-nn 0.9.2 → 0.10.2` bump that lands the lockfile in a hazardous state with **two `candle-core` major-versions resolved side-by-side** (`Cargo.lock:1588-1640` shows both 0.9.2 and 0.10.2 entries, same for `candle-kernels` and `candle-metal-kernels`), a **net-new `cudaforge` transitive** replacing `bindgen_cuda` in the 0.10.2 kernel path that needs provenance check, plus `float8 0.6.1→0.7.0`, `safetensors 0.6→0.7`, `tokenizers→0.22.2`, and a migration of `candle-metal-kernels` off the deprecated `metal` crate to the `objc2-*` family — needs `cargo tree -i candle-core@0.9.2` to identify the 0.9.2-pinning dependent before merge. At cline/cline, #10315 fixes a real pricing-field-name swap in `src/shared/api.ts` for gemini-flash-3-preview where `cacheWritesPrice: 0.05` was overstating spend (Google publishes the $0.05/MTok rate on cache **reads**, with cache writes free) — applied symmetrically to `vertexModels:1017-1021` and `geminiModels:1530-1542`, but the PR also silently deletes a 13-line `tiers` block from `geminiModels` that defined a discounted long-context tier (`inputPrice: 0.3/outputPrice: 2.5/cacheReadsPrice: 0.03` for ≥200k context), with no PR-description mention of whether that tier was actually retired upstream or is an oversight, and `vertexModels` keeps no analogous tier deletion creating cross-provider divergence. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3654 is a placeholder PR shipping only a one-line `TOOL_EXECUTION_UNIFICATION.md` at the repo root pointing at issue #3247, with the file body containing literal `\n\n` escape sequences instead of real newlines (suggests `echo -e` or similar tool that didn't strip backslash escapes) and no design content beyond a one-sentence abstract — should be a draft PR with a fleshed-out design doc under `docs/design/` (mirroring the in-flight #3583 PRD precedent) or held until actual refactor commits exist.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24541 swallowed-rejection sync.tsx fix with both branches force-completing status, charmbracelet/crush #2725 padding-aware truncation budget that also fixes wide-char off-by-one, charmbracelet/crush #2724 restored `UpdateAvailableMsg` case with dev-build branching), 3 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24548 TUI HttpApi bridge needs alias-table comment + `Bus.layer` double-provide check + `selectSession` 404-mapping eyeball, sst/opencode #24537 edit-tool args one-liner needs regression test + payload-size note, block/goose #8822 candle-nn bump dual-stack lockfile + cudaforge provenance check), 1 needs-discussion (cline/cline #10315 cache-field rename is correct but the silent `tiers` deletion is undocumented and asymmetric vs `vertexModels`), 1 request-changes (QwenLM/qwen-code #3654 placeholder-only PR with literal `\n\n` escapes instead of real newlines and zero reviewable design content).

### W17 drip-95 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At sst/opencode, #24547 bridges all six PTY routes (list/create/get/update/remove + websocket connect) onto the experimental Effect HttpApi via a new 205-line `httpapi/pty.ts` — JSON CRUD declared as a typed `HttpApi.make("pty")` group with `HttpApiError.NotFound` mapping at `pty.ts:152,164`, websocket upgrade carved off into a separate raw-router `ptyConnectRoute` at `pty.ts:184-234` (necessary because HttpApi doesn't model upgrades), defensive payload spread of `args`/`env`/`size` to prevent shared-mutable observation by `Pty.Service`, `closed`-flag closure state with `Effect.ensuring` flipping it exactly once and `Effect.catchReason("SocketError", "SocketCloseError", ...)` swallowing the two expected websocket terminations cleanly — but `Pty.defaultLayer` is provided twice (once at `server.ts:73` for the JSON routes, once at `pty.ts:233` for the connect route) which would split state across the two surfaces if `defaultLayer` isn't a memoized singleton, and `remove` is silently idempotent (always `Schema.Boolean: true`) which contradicts the OpenAPI summary's "remove and terminate" framing; #24536 is a 2-line home-page fix at `home.tsx:28` filtering the synthetic `id === "global" && worktree === "/"` placeholder out of the `recent` projects memo, paired with a `<Match when={recent().length > 0}>` swap at `:90` so the recent-projects section header doesn't render an empty list when only the synthetic project exists — sentinel is hard-coded in the view layer rather than centralized as a `synthetic === true` flag. At charmbracelet/crush, #2532 is a six-touch typo + log-normalization PR fixing `its`→`it's` in the README, four lowercase `mcp`→`MCP` initialism + sentence-case `slog` messages in `internal/agent/tools/mcp/init.go:198,226,280,292`, and an `intentation`→`indentation` comment fix in `internal/app/app.go:343` — all keys/levels preserved, but leaves a pre-existing `slog.Warn` saying `Error closing MCP session` (level/text mismatch) untouched; #2493 is a real refactor adding `golang.org/x/sync/singleflight` to coalesce concurrent LSP server startup at `manager.go:181-220` so N editors opening N `.go` files simultaneously trigger exactly one gopls bring-up — critical move is `baseCtx := context.WithoutCancel(ctx)` at `:191` so caller-A canceling doesn't abort the shared startup that caller-B is still waiting on, plus `res.Shared` log-level demotion at `:218-222` (first caller logs `Error`, subsequent shared callers log `Debug`), with the three former open-coded "is this client active?" checks consolidated into a new `getActiveClient()` helper at `:238-245`; no concurrent-startup test added, and the `WaitForServerReady`-fail path at `:217-219` flips state to `StateError` but still returns `(client, nil)` to callers (preserved behavior, but the implicit "next `getActiveClient` will reject and retry" state machine wants a comment). At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3577 is a 401-line new skill `tmux-real-user-testing` with a 142-line helper script that prescribes `tmux capture-pane -p` rendered-grid snapshots as the primary report-grade artifact (correctly demoting `tmux pipe-pane` raw-byte logs to "optional forensic" because React Ink TUI output is mostly ANSI control sequences) — script uses `set -euo pipefail`, `printf '%q'` eval-safe quoting on the start subcommand at `script:60-62`, `tmux has-session` collision pre-check at `:51-54`, character-by-character `send` with 0.15s inter-key sleep at `:73-80` to dodge Ink's input debounce, and `type-submit` at `:84-89` separating the typed text from `Enter` with 0.5s sleep to dodge tmux interpreting `"text Enter"` as a single key sequence; the `tmux new-session ... "$*"` interpolation at `:33` won't survive scenario commands containing shell-significant whitespace (worth a `printf -v cmd '%q '` swap), and the auth-test example at `SKILL.md:225` uses a relative path that doesn't match the canonical `.qwen/skills/tmux-real-user-testing/scripts/...` path used in the same file at `:69-72`; #3537 routes web-fetch content-processing through the configured `fastModel` when set with fallback at `web-fetch.ts:156-158` via `this.config.getFastModel() ?? (this.config.getModel() || DEFAULT_QWEN_MODEL)` — the `??` correctness depends on `Config.getFastModel()` normalizing the empty-string sentinel `""` to `undefined` (the test mock at `web-fetch.test.ts:41` returns `undefined` for the unconfigured case but the on-disk default at `settings.md:210` is `""`), defensive `||` chain would handle both; two new tests at `web-fetch.test.ts:246-294` cover the configured-and-fallback cases via `mockGenerateContent.toHaveBeenCalledWith(..., 'qwen-fast')` 4th-positional-arg assertion, and the `settings.md:210` doc update expands the `fastModel` description from two surfaces (prompt suggestions + speculative execution) to five (adds session recap, forked side-queries `auto-memory`/`auto-dream`/`/btw`, and web-fetch processing) which was previously underselling its scope. At block/goose, #8820 is a dependabot 5/-5 patch bump of `postcss 8.5.6 → 8.5.10` scoped to `/documentation` (Docusaurus build-time CSS pipeline only, not in any runtime bundle), four-patch span with no documented breaking changes in 8.5.x, lockfile internally consistent (only the postcss block touched, no transitive cascades), and the `package.json` constraint floor bump from `^8.4.35` to `^8.5.10` is conservative-over-tight but standard dependabot behavior. At cline/cline, #10308 is a 39-line new `docs/provider-config/quicksilverpro.mdx` documenting QuickSilver Pro as an OpenAI-compatible inference API for DeepSeek V3/R1 and `qwen3.5-35b` — follows the standard frontmatter + getting-started + supported-models + configuration-steps shape used by neighboring provider pages, with two genuinely useful Notes: non-standard `usage.cost`/`usage.currency` fields in responses (informational; cline doesn't currently surface these in its cost UI) and the December-2025 DeepSeek-pulled-R1-from-direct-API context giving QuickSilver Pro a unique R1 access angle — but the model identifier `qwen3.5-35b` doesn't match Qwen's official naming convention (no `Qwen3.5` line in the upstream lineup as of this window) so either it's a QuickSilver re-label that wants a footnote citing the upstream model card, or the docs need correction.

### W17 drip-96 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At sst/opencode, #24553 hardens shell cancellation by distinguishing real cancellation from signal-delivery failure in the Effect runner at `runner.ts:22-35` (cancellation now becomes `Effect.interrupt` instead of `Effect.die`, and the `onInterrupt`-absent case escalates to `Effect.die(Cancelled)` rather than swallowing it), then hoists the cancellation-message ordering in `prompt.ts:129-128` so the user sees the cancel notice before the partial-output flush — the +129/−128 in `prompt.ts` looks like pure re-wrap but a `--ignore-all-space` diff confirmation would speed review, and the `Deferred.make<void>()` allocation needs a `finishShell` reap-confirmation; #24528 fixes a real async-executor anti-pattern in TUI startup at `app.tsx:72-71` where a `Promise<void>` was being thrown into an IIFE without `.catch()`, leaving the renderer in a half-mounted state on `waitForThemeMode`/`render` rejection — the new test at `app.test.ts:29` uses a 100ms race assertion which is the right way to assert "doesn't hang", though the partial-renderer-cleanup claim from the PR body wants a code-citation back-check, and the test coverage should extend to `waitForThemeMode` and `render` rejection branches to lock down all three async hops. At charmbracelet/crush, #2723 wires Kagi as a fully-supported search backend (not just a flag) — `internal/agent/tools/search.go:97-6` adds the `searchKagi` parser, `internal/config/config.go:49-2` adds the schema, `internal/ui/dialog/kagi_api_key_input.go:148` adds the API-key dialog, `internal/ui/dialog/search_engines.go:267` adds the engine-picker, and `internal/agent/tools/search_test.go:183` covers the parser thoroughly; the per-call `search_engine` override is a nice touch, but the `maybeDelaySearch` rewrite needs maintainer confirmation on whether the contract is "min gap between any two searches" (in which case the rewrite needs a CAS or token-bucket) vs the looser "min gap between calls from same caller"; #2714 adds macOS `terminal-notifier` integration via build-tag split (`internal/ui/notification/native_darwin.go` +146 new, `native_other.go` renamed from `native.go` with `//go:build !darwin`) using a static TERM_PROGRAM-keyed map to resolve the parent terminal app's bundle identifier with osascript fallback for the long tail — the progressive-enhancement design (no behavior change without `terminal-notifier` installed) means low merge risk, but `NewNativeBackend` shouldn't be called on the hot path because the osascript-fallback latency would bite, and the LookPath-fail-→-beeep fallback branch wants even one test. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3649 exposes LSP status and startup diagnostics via a new 81-line `lsp/types.ts` snapshot schema + `NativeLspService.getStatusSnapshot` at `:63` + a system-info CLI surface at `systemInfo.ts:55-1`, with a 78-line docs expansion at `docs/users/features/lsp.md` and a 90-line test file — the design is solid (clean optional-chain backward compat, debug-only-double-snapshot discipline is the right cost model), but the PR is explicitly Draft pending tmux E2E smoke and the body says #3615 "should be reviewed/resolved first," plus the `initializationError` field is typed `string` but stores `Error` objects at runtime which needs tightening before merge; #3645 corrects `OPENAI_MODEL` precedence in `modelConfigUtils.ts:28-11` (+473/-1 of test matrix in the sibling test file) so the env fallback re-lands without re-breaking `/model` selection — this is the correct re-attempt after the #3633 revert, with comprehensive case-A-through-case-E coverage including the originally-broken #3567 path; non-blocking nits are a user-facing warning when Edge Case 5 fires (settings-model-doesn't-match → silent env fallback) and tightening `Settings['model']` to accept `undefined` so the `as unknown as` cast in the test can be removed. At block/goose, #8844 is a +61/−3 single-file fix in `crates/goose/src/config/base.rs` that detects when a yaml-quoted string holds a numeric value (e.g., `"42"` instead of `42`) and re-parses it via a new `parse_env_value` helper before propagating the original `yaml_err` on fallback failure — fast path is unchanged, four tests cover the full int/float/quoted/unquoted matrix, addresses confirmed real-user issue #8437 with a documented user-side workaround that this PR removes; clean defensive change. At cline/cline, #10418 adds `deepseek-v4` and `deepseek-v4-reasoner` to `src/shared/api.ts:22` and the `isDeepseekReasoner` switch at `src/core/api/providers/deepseek.ts:3-2` — diff is mechanically correct and small, but the substantive content (model existence, context window, max output, four pricing values, reasoning semantics) is entirely unsourced, there's a parallel PR #10401 from a different author for the same models that needs deduplication, the `isDeepseekReasoner` semantic widening is unsigned, and the related-issue link is a literal `#XXXX` placeholder.

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (block/goose #8844 single-file quoted-numeric yaml-config fallback with `parse_env_value` helper preserving fast-path and original `yaml_err` semantics, four tests covering the full int/float/quoted/unquoted matrix), 5 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24553 shell-cancellation hardening distinguishing cancellation from signal-failure with `Effect.die(Cancelled)` escalation when `onInterrupt` is absent — wants `Deferred.make<void>()` reap confirmation + `--ignore-all-space` diff to verify the +129/−128 prompt.ts is pure re-wrap, sst/opencode #24528 TUI startup rejection fix for the async-executor anti-pattern with a 100ms-race "doesn't hang" assertion — wants extended coverage to `waitForThemeMode`/`render` rejection branches + partial-renderer-cleanup code citation, charmbracelet/crush #2723 Kagi backend wiring with parser + dialog + schema + per-call override — blocked on `maybeDelaySearch` semantics confirmation (CAS/token-bucket needed if "min gap between any two searches"), charmbracelet/crush #2714 darwin `terminal-notifier` build-tag split with TERM_PROGRAM-keyed bundle-id map + osascript fallback — wants `NewNativeBackend` hot-path check + LookPath-fail-→-beeep test, QwenLM/qwen-code #3645 OPENAI_MODEL precedence re-land after #3633 revert with comprehensive +473-line test matrix — wants user-facing warning on Edge-Case-5 silent env fallback + `Settings['model']` undefined-typing tightening), 2 needs-discussion (QwenLM/qwen-code #3649 LSP status snapshot is well-designed but PR is Draft pending tmux E2E smoke + dependency on #3615 + `initializationError: string` field actually stores `Error` objects at runtime, cline/cline #10418 deepseek-v4 model definitions are entirely unsourced with parallel PR #10401 from a different author for the same models needing deduplication + `isDeepseekReasoner` semantic widening unsigned + `#XXXX` placeholder for related issue).

---

### W17 drip-97 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos. At sst/opencode, #24554 is a maintainer-authored 727/-677 sweep across 300+ files removing module barrel `index.ts` files (deleted barrels: `effect/index.ts -5`, `config/index.ts -16`, `lsp/index.ts -3`, `tui/util/index.ts -4`, `tui/plugin/index.ts -3`) and rewriting imports to target specific modules directly to avoid cyclic-init failures (motivation: a `MessageV2.Assistant` initialization cycle); diff exceeds GitHub's 300-file cap making PR-UI review degraded, verification is just `bun typecheck` + `bun run src/temporary.ts --help` smoke, the four self-reexports added on `effect/runner.ts`/`tui/util/clipboard.ts`/`tui/util/selection.ts`/`tui/util/sound.ts` are the right pattern for namespace-style imports surviving the cleanup, and follow-up CI lint (`madge --circular` or equivalent) wants filing to prevent regression; #24458 is a 1-line ecosystem docs add for `opencode-adaptive-thinking` ("Let agents adjust model reasoning effort during a session") at `packages/web/src/content/docs/ecosystem.mdx:20` correctly placed in alphabetical order between `awesome-opencode` and `opencode-daytona` with matching column padding. At charmbracelet/crush, #2580 is a 41,781/-1,310 mega-PR self-attributed to AI generation that ships a brand-new "agent kernel" (context manager, compression, memory, hybrid-brain coordinator, task scheduler/classifier, permission-grade system, WebSocket+HTTP server, circuit breaker, streaming monitor, dependency manager, state machine, guardian system) plus committed SQLite artifacts at repo root (`crush.db`, `crush.db-shm`, `crush.db-wal`), two new vendored Go modules (`cmd/hybrid-brain/go.mod`+540-line `go.sum`, `cmd/claudecode-bridge/go.mod`), 17+ traditional-Chinese architecture markdown files under `docs/architecture/`, a 935-line CHANGELOG.md, and a near-complete `AGENTS.md` rewrite (+169/-154) — diff exceeds GitHub's 20k-line cap and the root `go.sum` *loses* 285 lines (suspicious regenerate-from-scratch artifact). At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3576 lands browser-based OpenRouter OAuth (+723-line `openrouterOAuth.ts` with PKCE flow, localhost callback, and CLI subcommand `qwen auth openrouter --key`) plus a `/manage-models` interactive dialog (+648-line `ManageModelsDialog.tsx`, +211-line `manageModels.ts`) that fetches OpenRouter's `/api/v1/models` catalog, filters non-text-output models, applies a curated recommended-default subset, and persists selections to `settings.modelProviders.openai` while preserving non-OpenRouter entries — the catalog-vs-enabled-set design split is documented up front in a new +89-line `docs/design/openrouter-auth-and-models.md`, reusing `AuthType.USE_OPENAI` is the right call, but the targeted `vitest` run failed in the review worktree (dep resolution), all 8 i18n locales need parity confirmation, the localhost-port-bind failure recovery wants explicit handling, and the recommended-default model set isn't pinned in the design doc. At block/goose, #8819 is a one-line dependabot bump of `actions-rust-lang/setup-rust-toolchain` from v1.15.4 to v1.16.0 at `.github/workflows/ci.yml:122` with full SHA pinning preserved (`150fca88...` → `2b1f5e9b...`), 1.16.0 release adds only the unrelated `cache-save-if` input which the workflow doesn't use. At cline/cline, #10304 is a 5212/-4984 Vertex AI overhaul that legitimately decouples `vertexRegion` per plan/act mode (proto fields 242/243, fallback chain at `core/api/index.ts:130-138`/`:172-180`) and adds three new model entries, but `src/shared/api.ts` shows +4862/-4727 of formatter drift (semicolon and quote-style toggle) drowning the actual logic changes, the PR template's Related-Issue field is left as literal `#XXXX`, no tests are added for a 5k-line behavioral change, the `mapReasoningEffortToGeminiThinkingLevel` change at `gemini.ts:36-45` casts `"MEDIUM" as ThinkingLevel` instead of using the SDK enum, and `state-keys.ts +4/-0` lands without a corresponding settings-store touch. At BerriAI/litellm, #26567 is a +32/-8 single-file fix to `mcp_semantic_filter/hook.py` adding `"anthropic_messages"` to the call-type allowlist at `:154` (so Anthropic-SDK clients hitting `/v1/messages` actually run the filter) and partitioning the tools list into `mcp_tools` (name-prefixed `mcp__`) vs `passthrough_tools` at `:223-237` to stop the indiscriminate filter from dropping user-defined function tools — partition loop has a minor parens-clarity nit and the project's own contributing rule requires a test in `tests/test_litellm/` which isn't present; #26566 is a +20/-2 fix to `prompt_templates/factory.py:107-130` for `map_system_message_pt` that handles list-typed content blocks (Anthropic typed-block format) instead of crashing with `TypeError` on `str + list` concatenation, fixes upstream issue #23757, prepends a fresh `{"type": "text", ...}` block when the target is a list and string-concats when the target is a string — preserves the all-string fast path exactly.

---

### W17 drip-98 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos. At sst/opencode, #24532 is a +85/-1 TUI sidebar plugin (`feature-plugins/sidebar/tasks.tsx` registered into `INTERNAL_TUI_PLUGINS` at `plugin/internal.ts:25`) that surfaces in-flight `task`-tool work via dual-state recognition — checks both the brief `tool.state.status === "running"` window and the longer-lived child-session `props.api.state.session.status(childSessionId).type === "busy"` window at `tasks.tsx:21-43`, paired with a one-line label fix at `routes/session/index.tsx:2015-2016` swapping the misleading `0ms` duration for `"In Progress"` when `endedAt` isn't yet set; rows have a small duplicated `type.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + type.slice(1)` block in both branches that wants a `formatTaskRow(input)` helper, and the dual-state predicate is non-obvious enough to deserve at least one Vitest mock-`session.status` regression test even though every existing sidebar plugin has zero tests; #24520 parameterizes the `packages/opencode/Dockerfile` via `ARG BASE_IMAGE=alpine:3.21` + `ARG DIST_SUFFIX=-musl` so the `script/publish.ts:64-90` `buildImage(tags, buildArgs)` helper can produce **two parallel images** under the same `ghcr.io/anomalyco/opencode` registry (alpine `:version`/`:channel`/**`:latest`** + Ubuntu 24.04 `:ubuntu`) — motivation is real (musl breaks ROCm-PyTorch in containers), the `/etc/alpine-release` probe is the right idiom for libc-conditional package install, and `--no-cache` on apk + `rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*` on apt both keep image bloat sane, but the `:latest` tag is a contract change unmentioned in the PR body, the `*) exit 1 ;;` arch arm at `:21` swallows debugging info, the author admits "CI doesn't run on forks" and ticks "tested locally" anyway with a "not applicable" caveat, and there's no `docker run --rm $image:latest --version` vs `:ubuntu --version` cross-image readback CI guard to prevent tag drift over future releases. At charmbracelet/crush, #2711 is a small +11/-3 fix in `internal/ui/model/ui.go:580-600` to the `pubsub.Event[session.Session]` branch that was only re-rendering todo pills on the rising-edge transition `prevHasInProgress=false → hasInProgressTodo=true`, silently skipping the most common `in_progress → completed` transition (and reorders, additions where none start in_progress, content edits, the final all-completed state); fix mirrors the already-correct sibling `pubsub.Event[message.Message]` branch — spinner-start gate widens to `hasInProgressTodo && isAgentBusy() && !todoIsSpinning`, a new spinner-stop branch closes the "session-event path that didn't route through message-event" leak that left the spinner ticking forever, and `m.renderPills()` is called unconditionally on every session update; PR body explicitly defers two adjacent latent bugs (`pubsub/broker.go:103-109` silent drops on full 64-slot subscriber buffer, `ui.go:1057-1063` tool-result discarded if parent tool-call item not yet in chat) as out-of-scope, which is the right scope discipline; #2691 caps the shared `*sql.DB` to one writer via `db.SetMaxOpenConns(1)` at `internal/db/connect.go:48-57` to prevent a real `SQLITE_NOTADB (26)` corruption mode where parallel sub-agents (session/message/history/filetracker share one handle) interleave WAL frames and auto-checkpoints, and mid-checkpoint cancellation (context cancel/SIGINT/OOM) desyncs the main DB file from the WAL — bricking the project session until `.crush/crush.db` is manually deleted; the existing 30s `busy_timeout` queues concurrent callers behind the single connection so call sites need no changes, and the inline 10-line comment block is exemplary at preempting "why is this hardcoded to 1?" archaeology; trade-off (giving up WAL's concurrent-readers benefit) is justified because the corruption-under-cancellation surface is the actual bug, with a follow-up worth filing if filetracker reads start hitting the 30s wall. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3647 is a +463/-37 8-file fix bundling three concerns (compact-row rendering with `… and N more` overflow, recent-history visibility guard to dedupe vs an inline/recent `TodoWrite` result, and the actual flicker root-cause: `useLayoutEffect` deps that included a freshly-allocated `stickyTodos` array, causing `measureElement` to re-fire on every status flip and PowerShell to render the resulting same-value `setControlsHeight` as visible flicker) — fix replaces those deps with a single `stickyTodosLayoutKey` derived via `getStickyTodosLayoutKey(todos, width, maxVisible)` plus a `useStableStickyTodos` ref-based memo at `AppContainer.tsx:166-186` that promotes derived state to a stable identity if its render-key didn't change, and a `setControlsHeight((prev) => prev === measured ? prev : measured)` no-op guard at `:1605-1611`/`:1614-1618`; new test `does not remeasure footer height for sticky todo status-only updates` at `AppContainer.test.tsx:1426-1462` is the right shape (rerender with a `pending → in_progress` flip, assert `mockedMeasureElement.toHaveBeenCalledTimes(callsAfterInitialRender)`); the recent-history visibility guard remains a heuristic (Ink `<Static>` exposes no per-item viewport API, PR body acknowledges this), the 64-column ceiling at `:1597` is hardcoded, and the testing matrix shows only Windows manual repro (⚠️ on macOS/Linux). At block/goose, #8843 is a +413/-13 single-file fix to `crates/goose/src/providers/formats/bedrock.rs` that handles Bedrock `ContentBlock::ReasoningContent` (emitted by `openai.gpt-oss-safeguard-120b` and other gpt-oss-on-Bedrock models) instead of falling into the previous `_ => bail!("Unsupported content block type from Bedrock")` catch-all — symmetric inbound (`ReasoningContent::ReasoningText → MessageContent::Thinking` preserving optional signature, `RedactedContent(Blob) → RedactedThinking` base64-encoded) and outbound (`Thinking → ReasoningContentBlock::ReasoningText` re-emit with signature, `RedactedThinking → RedactedContent` base64-decoded with tracing-warn fallback when decode fails) at `:58-94` and `:362-364`, signature change `Result<MessageContent>` → `Result<Option<MessageContent>>` plus `.filter_map(|r| r.transpose())` at `:316-319` for the SDK's `non_exhaustive`-Unknown arm, and the catch-all replaced by **explicit listing** of all other variants (`Audio`/`CitationsContent`/`Document`/`GuardContent`/`Image`/`SearchResult`/`Video`) at `:382-388` so an outdated SDK match arm fails loud rather than silently truncating output; previously `Thinking`/`RedactedThinking` were both mapped to `Text("")` outbound, which broke multi-turn correctness for Bedrock reasoning models that require signed reasoning replay, and a `bedrock_content_block_kind` helper at `:421-437` returns variant names only (no payload) so logs can't leak model output. At cline/cline, #10384 is a +68/-7 fix in `src/core/api/retry.ts` that adds a `maxRetryAfter` option (default 60s) to the `withRetry` decorator so a server-supplied `retry-after: 10800` (3 hours, e.g. Gemini quota exhaustion) doesn't silently `setTimeout` for the full duration with no UI feedback — cap is gated inside `if (retryAfter) { … }` at `:56-71` so it can't change exponential-backoff behavior (which is already capped by `maxDelay: 10s`), original error is re-thrown preserving `status: 429` and the upstream `headers["retry-after"]` for caller-side surfacing, two new tests pin the cap-fires and cap-doesn't-fire branches at `retry.test.ts:181-235`, and the existing Unix-timestamp test was correctly updated to use `sinon.stub(Date, "now")` and pass `maxRetryAfter: Infinity` since its synthetic timestamp now exceeds the default 60s cap; a `parseInt → Number.parseInt` swap at `:61` is unrelated biome-lint cleanup and the PR template's "existing behavior preserved" + "guard only activates when delay > maxRetryAfter" boxes are unchecked despite being true by reading the diff. At BerriAI/litellm, #26550 fixes a real `UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode characters` crash in `LiteLLMAiohttpTransport.handle_async_request` at `aiohttp_transport.py:344` by swapping `headers=response.headers` (already-decoded `str`, ASCII-normalized by `httpx.Headers`) for `headers=response.raw_headers` (`Tuple[Tuple[bytes, bytes], ...]`, bytes round-trip cleanly through httpx's latin-1 path) — mirrored at `http_handler.py:381-388` for the masked-error path that rebuilds `httpx.Response` for `MaskedHTTPStatusError`, with the filter comparison correctly switching `("content-encoding", "content-length")` → `(b"content-encoding", b"content-length")` to match byte-typed keys and the dict-comprehension correctly switching to list-of-tuples to preserve duplicate-name headers (`Set-Cookie`); new test at `test_aiohttp_transport.py:266-309` exercises a `本地化消息` UTF-8 header round-trip, three existing test doubles updated with `raw_headers = ()` to keep them satisfying the new access pattern; PR includes drive-by `make format` churn across `factory.py`/`predibase/transformation.py`/`xecguard.py` that wants splitting out, and the PR template's CI-link boxes are all empty.

### W17 drip-100 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At sst/opencode, #24570 is a +5/-4 mobile-viewport fix in `packages/app/src/pages/session/composer/session-question-dock.tsx:122-135` where the `measure()` callback used `scroller.firstElementChild + .sticky` class probe to find the session title — a prior refactor wrapped the title in a `setContentRef` div, breaking the probe, leaving `--question-prompt-max-height` unset, falling back to `100dvh`, and clipping the Dismiss/Submit buttons below the iPhone 14 viewport on long-description questions; fix swaps the brittle ordinal-position+class probe for `scroller.querySelector("[data-session-title]")` with `scroller.getBoundingClientRect().top` as the untitled-session fallback, behavior-matrix complete (titled-wrapped, untitled, scroller-missing all handled correctly), but the `[data-session-title]` attribute itself isn't visible in the diff (needs a one-grep confirmation that the sticky title element actually emits it) and the `.scroll-view__viewport` selector is the same brittleness class as the original bug (worth a follow-up `data-scroll-viewport` hook). At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3656 fixes #3606 session-load corruption where two top-level JSON records glued onto one physical line as `{"a":1}{"b":2}` previously caused `JSON.parse` to throw and drop **every** subsequent record — adds `_recoverObjectsFromLine<T>(line)` at `packages/core/src/utils/jsonl-utils.ts:47-127` with a brace-depth state machine that respects `inString`/`escape` (correct ordering: escape eats one char then resets, inString swallows `{`/`}`, depth-zero `}` triggers `JSON.parse(line.slice(start, i+1))`, unparseable fragments silently skipped) and a 132-line test file covering all five interesting branches plus integration through `read`/`readLines`; fast path unchanged, top-level-objects-only limitation explicitly documented in JSDoc — wants debug-level telemetry on recovery to keep the root-cause writer bug visible and a one-line `if (!line.trimStart().startsWith("{"))` early-out to make the limitation explicit; #3657 adds a three-layer VS Code notification system (orange tab dot for task-complete, blue for needs-input, `window.showInformationMessage` with "Show" button, platform sound) gated by two new boolean settings `qwen-code.dotIndicator`/`qwen-code.notifications` (both default `true`) plus an `onDidReceiveMessage` handler-consolidation refactor — test scaffolding at `WebViewProvider.test.ts:1264+` is well-engineered with explicit `vi.hoisted()` callback refs, `vi.mock('child_process')` neutralizing audio exec, `vi.useFakeTimers()` for the 20s threshold, and disciplined `beforeEach` reset of all four callback refs; concerns are Linux ENOENT for `canberra-gtk-play`/`paplay` not exercised in mocks, focus-vs-visibility (`webviewView.visible`) not tested alongside `window.state.focused`, notification-guard reset semantics (`idleNotificationSent`/`attentionNotified`) need a state-machine comment, and "Show" button strings are hardcoded English without i18n extraction. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26014 fixes #12083 sandbox name race by replacing the `docker ps -a --format "{{.Names}}"` probe-and-increment loop in `packages/cli/src/utils/sandbox.ts:490-516` with a uniform `${prefix}-${randomBytes(6).toString('hex')}` (suffix entropy raised from 16-bit → 24-bit, birthday bound ~4096 concurrent containers vs realistic ~10), `--name`/`--hostname`/`SANDBOX` env var stay aligned, integration-test prefix preserved for CI cleanup, test pin uses deterministic `randomBytes` mock + `mockedExecCommands` array assertion that the `ps -a` probe was removed, plus symmetric integration-test variant — no collision-fallback (acceptable at 2^-24) and `--rm` in the docker invocation prevents the name-leak that random suffixes would otherwise cause; #26011 fixes #25987 where `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` and other TLS-init env vars from `.gemini/.env` stopped working after the #24667 parent/child split (root cause: Node reads TLS env vars in C++ at process start, before any user JS runs, so the child's `loadEnvironment()` happens too late) — fix hand-parses `$HOME/.gemini/.env` (no `dotenv` import, preserves lightweight-parent contract) for a 7-key allowlist (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`, `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED`, `SSL_CERT_FILE`, `SSL_CERT_DIR`, `HTTPS_PROXY`, `HTTP_PROXY`, `NO_PROXY`) and injects matched values into the child spawn env with shell-wins precedence; the 197-line test at `packages/cli/index.test.ts` covers the parser thoroughly (CRLF, `=` in values, escaped `#` in quoted strings, `export` prefix, malformed lines) and pins the critical security-model decisions (HOME-only not project-local with explicit "trust-gated, child handles it" comment, non-allowlisted keys silently dropped) — wants the production code in `index.ts` eyeballed for spawn-env merge order, a `console.warn` when `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0` is injected from `.env`, project-local `.env` migration footgun documented in release notes, and a `.env` size cap. At openai/codex, #19706 fixes #19702 TUI markdown renderer where the next list marker visually attached to the previous item's fenced code block even when the source markdown had a blank line — adds two state stacks to `Renderer` at `codex-rs/tui/src/markdown_render.rs:154-158` (`list_needs_blank_before_next_item: Vec<bool>` per-list-depth, `list_item_contains_code_block: Vec<bool>` per-item), with `start_codeblock` flipping the current item's flag, `TagEnd::Item` consuming it and bubbling to the parent list's flag, and `start_item` consuming the parent list's flag via `std::mem::take` and emitting a blank line; push/pop balance verified across both stacks (`list_needs_blank_before_next_item` push at start_list/pop at end_list, `list_item_contains_code_block` push at start_item/pop at TagEnd::Item), tests pin both directions (code-block-items get blanks, simple items stay compact) plus an insta snapshot — uses Rust 2024 `let-chains` syntax that needs MSRV check, and nested-list / mixed-list / sublist-with-code-block cases are untested; #19717 fixes a real Windows CI flake in `responses_stream_includes_turn_metadata_header_for_git_workspace_e2e` where `git rev-parse HEAD` occasionally failed even with readable `.git/` metadata, dropping `latest_git_commit_hash` from `x-codex-turn-metadata` — fix at `codex-rs/git-utils/src/info.rs:147-167` keeps the fast `git rev-parse HEAD` path then falls through to a new `get_head_commit_hash_from_gix` helper using `gix::discover(base).head_id()` that handles loose refs, packed-refs, and worktree `commondir` layouts; test setup is exemplary (`GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=<empty>` + `GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1` for hermetic config isolation), with `pretty_assertions::assert_eq` for diff-friendly failures and `cwd.is_dir() ? cwd : cwd.parent()?` shim handling the file-vs-dir caller — wants explicit detached-HEAD + unborn-HEAD + submodule + loose-ref-only tests for matrix completeness, plus the same `let-chains` MSRV check as #19706. At BerriAI/litellm, #26569 is a 4112/-126 staging-rollup re-attempt of #26216 ("had too many conflicts") with a meaningless title ("Litellm oss staging 04 21 2026 2"), all required-checklist boxes empty (including the bold "at least 1 test is a hard requirement"), and at minimum two completely unrelated features visible in the first 250 diff lines (Reducto OCR provider with `parse-v3`/`parse-legacy` models and three-shape document upload behavior + Rubrik tool-blocking guardrail with explicit fail-open semantic and `RUBRIK_WEBHOOK_URL` env fallback) plus likely several more deltas — both feature docs are well-structured individually but the PR shape destroys bisect/backport granularity and explicitly violates the project's own "isolated scope" hard-requirement checkbox, additionally Rubrik's hardcoded fail-open posture is a security choice that deserves CODEOWNERS-level review with a fail-CLOSED config option.

with `[data-session-title]` selector but selector itself not grep-confirmed in diff and parent `.scroll-view__viewport` is same brittleness class, QwenLM/qwen-code #3657 three-layer VS Code notification system with disciplined hoisted-callback test scaffolding but Linux ENOENT path unmocked + focus-vs-visibility coverage gap + notification-guard reset state-machine undocumented + hardcoded English strings, google-gemini/gemini-cli #26011 hand-rolled `.env` parser preserving lightweight-parent contract with thorough edge-case test coverage and explicit "trust-gated, child ### W17 drip-101 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos. At sst/opencode, #24576 is a +11/-3 two-file LSP-tool surface fix that adds a `query?: string` parameter to the `workspaceSymbol` operation in `packages/opencode/src/tool/lsp.ts:32-34` and threads it through at `:95` (`lsp.workspaceSymbol(args.query ?? "")`) instead of the prior hardcoded empty string that forced clangd/pyright/gopls to return their full unfiltered symbol table — empty string remains LSP-3.17-spec-valid and is documented as "requests all symbols", the `execute` signature is tightened from a hand-rolled object type to `Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Parameters>` so the body stops drifting from the schema, and `lsp.txt` adds an explicit "filePath is not sent in the workspace/symbol request — used by opencode to select and start the matching server" note that pre-empts the obvious misreading of the tool schema; #24572 is a +29/-1 one-line clamp in `SessionNs.getUsage`'s `safe()` helper at `session/session.ts:300-303` (`if (!Number.isFinite(value) || value < 0) return 0`) that fixes the visible bug where the sidebar "$ spent" indicator decreased mid-session — root cause is providers disagreeing about whether `inputTokens` includes cache reads, with new-only-reporters making `adjustedInputTokens = inputTokens - cacheReadTokens - cacheWriteTokens` go negative and the negative cost delta added back to the running total (closes upstream #22618), test at `compaction.test.ts:2092-2117` synthesizes the bug shape (`inputTokens: 100`, `cacheReadTokens: 200`) and pins both `result.tokens.input === 0` and `result.cost === 0` but doesn't pin the output-side-still-billed case and the helper has no warn-on-clamp telemetry for surfacing the underlying provider drift. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3661 ports the CLI's `useAttentionNotifications` to the VS Code companion as a three-layer system (orange tab dot for task-completed, blue for needs-input, VS Code notification bubble with "Show" button gated by `!(window focused AND panel visible)`, and platform sound via afplay/SystemSounds.Asterisk/canberra-gtk-play+paplay) plus an `onDidReceiveMessage` handler-consolidation refactor (+766/-46 across 5 files), gated by two new boolean settings `qwen-code.dotIndicator`/`qwen-code.notifications` at `package.json:235-247` (both default `true`), with a 20-second floor on the task-completed flavor and immediate fire on action-needed, blue priority over orange never downgraded — test scaffolding at `WebViewProvider.test.ts:1-90` adds four hoisted callback refs and `mockWindowState: { focused: true }` for deterministic focus state, but Linux ENOENT-on-both-binaries is unmocked, the focus-vs-visibility off-diagonal cells aren't pinned independently, the `idleNotificationSent`/`attentionNotified` reset trigger is undocumented as a state machine, and the "Show" button strings are hardcoded English with no i18n extraction. At openai/codex, #19763 is the second of a three-PR auth-loading stack (#19762 async first, this PR eager runtime, #19764 JWKS verify) that flips `AgentIdentityAuth` from a lazy `Arc<OnceCell<String>>` for `process_task_id` to eager-load-at-construction with a plain `String` field — `new(record) → load(record).await` at `agent_identity.rs`, `process_task_id() → &str` not `Option<&str>`, custom `Clone` impl deleted in favor of `derive(Clone)`, `from_agent_identity_jwt` flipped sync→async at `manager.rs:249-252`, and the `is_chatgpt_auth → uses_codex_backend` widening of the connector-list gate at `connectors.rs:148-155`/`:220-227` is an unmentioned behavior change inside a "refactor"-titled PR; the deleted `load_auth_reads_agent_identity_from_env` test at `auth_tests.rs:625-651` leaves a real coverage gap that the new `agent_identity_authapi_base_url()` free-function seam was extracted to fill via wiremock, and the auth-loading hot path now does a network round-trip on every load with no changelog note about cold-start-latency or fail-loud-on-transient-registration consequences; #19705 is a +2565/-236 64-file feature add making hooks discoverable from plugins (parallel to the existing skills surface) — extends `HookSource` enum with `Plugin` variant visible at `analytics/src/events.rs:687`, adds `hooks/list` returning discovered entries including disabled-by-user-config ones (paralleling `skills/list` shape), adds `hooks/config/write` keyed by "stable hook keys" with persistence via new `codex-rs/config/src/hook_config.rs` and edit path at `core/src/config/edit.rs`, runtime preview/execution filters disabled hooks while listing preserves them, and the `codex-plugin` crate gains `codex-config` as a reverse dep for the user-level `[[hooks.config]]` schema — but the "stable hook key" format and forward/backward-compat policy is undefined in the PR body, the disabled-but-listed semantics aren't documented in the `HooksListResponse` schema, there's no GC story for `[[hooks.config]]` entries when a plugin is uninstalled, and the integration test at `app-server/tests/suite/v2/hooks_list.rs` doesn't cover the round-trip (list → disable → list-shows-disabled → enable → list-shows-enabled with reload between steps). At BerriAI/litellm, #26573 is a 6995/-89 29-file LLM-generated ("🤖 Generated with Claude Code") third-party Mavvrik vendor integration with 10 production files under `litellm/integrations/mavvrik/` (`Service` facade, `client.py` for REST, `exporter.py` for DB→DataFrame→CSV with OFFSET-paginated streaming, `uploader.py` for GCS bulk + chunked resumable, `orchestrator.py` for pod-lock + catch-up date loop, `logger.py` `CustomLogger` subclass, `settings.py` AES-encrypted credential persistence, `_http.py` shared httpx with retry+exponential backoff) plus 11 mirror-tree test files — design is sound (streaming + idempotent-by-calendar-date GCS objects + Mavvrik-owned restart marker + Redis distributed lock for multi-replica + AES creds), benchmark claim is 500k rows → 13MB / 84s with ~2MB peak RAM, and the standard "register a new logger + add a router" wiring pattern at `litellm/__init__.py`/`custom_logger_registry.py`/`proxy_server.py` matches existing vendor integrations — but OFFSET pagination on multi-million-row spend tables is O(n) per page, the catch-up loop has no max-window bound (30 days offline = 30 sequential full-table dumps blocking the orchestrator), the GCS uploader is hardcoded not pluggable, and a 7000-line LLM-authored vendor integration touching encrypted-credential persistence and `proxy_server.py` deserves an explicit maintainer conversation about vendor-acceptance + line-by-line ownership for the security-adjacent paths regardless of test coverage. At block/goose, #8855 is a routine grouped dependabot rollup (`cargo-minor-and-patch` group, 23 updates, +192/-147 across `Cargo.lock` + 5 `Cargo.toml` files) — visible bumps include `windows-sys 0.60.2 → 0.61.2` propagated consistently across all `anstream`/`anstyle-query`/`aws-lc-rs`/`axum` consumers, `aws-lc-rs 1.16.2 → 1.16.3`, `aws-lc-sys 0.39.0 → 0.40.0` (a minor on a crypto-adjacent `*-sys` crate hidden inside the "patch" group worth eyeballing for the Bedrock code path), `aws-smithy-types 1.3.5 → 1.4.7` (four patches accumulated), `axum 0.8.8 → 0.8.9`, plus a `tokio-tungstenite 0.29.0` qualified-version reference suggesting multi-version coexistence in the dep tree (binary-size + compile-time hit). At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #25989 is a +88/-1 two-file MCP-tool dispatch fix closing #25952 — adds a `resolveMcpToolNameLoose` private method at `tool-registry.ts:840-866` plus a `collapseMcpSeparators` free helper at `:870-877` that normalizes `mcp_*` names by replacing hyphens with underscores past the prefix, fires only after literal and legacy-alias lookups both miss and only for `MCP_TOOL_PREFIX`-prefixed names, refuses to silently dispatch on ambiguity (returns `undefined` if two registered MCP tools collapse to the same key), and emits a debug log every time it resolves so the underlying model-hallucination bug stays visible — three test pins at `tool-registry.test.ts:289-322` cover the snake_case-shorthand-resolves case, the two-tools-collapse-to-same-name ambiguity case, and the non-MCP-tools-unaffected blast-radius case; collapse is one-way (hyphens → underscores only) and lookup is O(n) over `allKnownTools` per fallback, both acceptable as v1 since the loop only fires on miss.
