Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24576 surgical 8-line LSP-3.17-conformance fix threading `query?: string` through `workspaceSymbol` with tightened `Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Parameters>` execute signature and explicit "filePath used for server selection only" tool-doc note, block/goose #8855 grouped 23-update dependabot minor+patch rollup with consistent `windows-sys 0.60.2→0.61.2` propagation across all consumers and CI as the right gate for batched bumps, google-gemini/gemini-cli #25989 surgical hyphen/underscore-tolerant MCP tool-name fallback with prefix-gated blast radius + ambiguity-returns-undefined safety + debug log on every resolution and three test pins covering resolves/ambiguous/non-MCP), 4 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24572 one-line `safe()` clamp at `session.ts:300-303` fixing visible negative-spend regression with provider-disagreement root cause and test pin but missing warn-on-clamp telemetry and helper-comment, QwenLM/qwen-code #3661 three-layer VS Code notification system with disciplined hoisted-callback test scaffolding and backward-compat default-true settings but Linux ENOENT path unmocked + focus-vs-visibility off-diagonal cells unpinned + guard-reset state-machine undocumented + hardcoded English strings, openai/codex #19763 lazy-OnceCell→eager-construction `AgentIdentityAuth` refactor part 2 of 3 with derived Clone + Option-removal + sync→async `from_agent_identity_jwt` but unmentioned `is_chatgpt_auth→uses_codex_backend` connector-gate widening + deleted env-var test with no wiremock replacement + no cold-start-latency note in changelog, openai/codex #19705 64-file plugin-discovered hooks feature paralleling skills surface with `HooksListParams`/`HooksConfigWriteParams` protocol additions + `HookSource::Plugin` variant + user-level `[[hooks.config]]` persistence but undefined "stable hook keys" format + missing disabled-but-listed schema doc + no plugin-uninstall GC story + missing list↔write round-trip integration test), 1 needs-discussion (BerriAI/litellm #26573 7000-line LLM-generated Mavvrik vendor integration with sound streaming+idempotent+pod-safe+AES-creds design and 11-mirror-tree-test coverage but vendor-acceptance + security-path ownership for `settings.py`/`proxy_server.py` + OFFSET-vs-cursor pagination + catch-up-loop max-window all warrant explicit maintainer conversation before review proceeds).

### W17 drip-102 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #25980 is a +70/-3 input-validation fix that wraps `resolveToRealPath()` inside `checkPermissions()` at `atCommandProcessor.ts:188-208` in a `try/catch` so a greedy-`@`-regex capture of pasted JSON/code (which previously fed an 8KB+ string into `fs.realpathSync` and threw `ENAMETOOLONG`/`EINVAL` as an unhandled rejection that killed the interactive session) silently drops just that entry — comment names the greedy-regex root cause, paths.ts:robustRealpath stays strict for internal callers, and two new tests at `:1542-1597` pin both the no-crash invariant (`@` + 8KB junk → `resolves.toEqual([])`) and the "sibling real mention still resolves" non-regression direction; #25981 is a +27/-0 three-file fix threading `setUpdateInfo` through `useSlashCommandProcessor` (parallel to the already-threaded `setBannerVisible`) and invoking `setUpdateInfo(null)` from inside `ui.clear()` at `slashCommandProcessor.ts:222-227` — restores `/clear` dismissal of the "update available" banner that broke when commit `885af07d` moved the banner from a history item into the `<Notifications>` live area driven by `updateInfo` state on `AppContainer`, with `setUpdateInfo` correctly added to the `useCallback` deps array at `:273-279` and the `import type { UpdateObject }` chosen for zero runtime cost; #26009 is a +599/-14 opt-in `experimental.windowsBash` setting that on Windows routes `run_shell_command` through `bash -c "<cmd>"` discovered via PATH → `HKLM\SOFTWARE\GitForWindows` registry probe → `ProgramFiles`/`ProgramFiles(x86)`/`LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\Git` filesystem probes (covering all-users / 32-bit-installer / per-user installs), correctly gated `enableWindowsBash && isWindows && !isStrictSandbox` at `shellExecutionService.ts:411-436` so sandbox path takes precedence, with `MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1` + `LANG=LC_ALL=C.UTF-8` set when bash routing fires + `windowsBashNotFoundWarned` static flag making the no-bash warning fire once per session + a 276-line integration test using `it.skipIf(!bashPath)` that runs against real bash on Linux/macOS — design rationale is structural (foundation models emit Unix shell syntax regardless of system-prompt instructions because the training prior dominates context), but the `cachedBashPath` is process-lifetime with no settings-change invalidation hook, the registry probe assumes the 64-bit hive (no `/reg:64` fallback for 32-bit Node on 64-bit Windows), `MSYS2_ARG_CONV_EXCL=*` is the canonical wider knob for MSYS2 users that's missing alongside `MSYS_NO_PATHCONV`, and the warn-on-not-found goes to `process.stderr.write` rather than the project's logging surface. At openai/codex, #19753 is a +356/-32 MCP lifecycle fix closing #12491/#12976/#18881/#19469 by adding explicit `McpConnectionManager::shutdown()` / `begin_shutdown()` (the latter returns a `'static + Send` future so callers can release the manager's write lock before awaiting per-client shutdown — verified at `handlers.rs:943-948`), `AsyncManagedClient::shutdown()` correctly handling the in-flight startup case (cancel token then match on `StartupOutcomeError::Cancelled` → no-op), and replacing the Drop-only `_process_group_guard: Option<ProcessGroupGuard>` with an explicit `process: ProcessHandle` + `terminate()` so cleanup is a deliberate call rather than Drop-ordering accident — the four cited issue numbers each map to a distinct call site this PR touches (`Op::Shutdown` at `handlers.rs:943`, channel-close fallback at `handlers.rs:1236`, MCP refresh in `session/mcp.rs`, connector probing at `connectors.rs:349`), and the explicit `Drop` impl at `connection_manager.rs:633-639` keeps unexpected-drop cancellation working; #19707 is a +126/-16 surgical fix for the remote-compact `'tools.defer_loading' requires tools.tool_search` 400 — extracts the existing `build_prompt` deferred-tool filter into a shared `model_visible_specs_for_prompt(router, turn_context)` helper at `session/turn.rs:963-985` and uses it from `compact_remote.rs:165-168` where the raw `tool_router.model_visible_specs()` was being sent without the dynamic-tool filtering the normal turn already did, with the `if deferred_dynamic_tools.is_empty()` fast-path preserved verbatim and smoke-tested against the live compact endpoint with `codex_app::automation_update` from `openai/openai#849632` (failure reproduced first, fix verified 200 with `compaction_summary`); #19712 is the missing config-write surface for the plugin-discovered-hooks feature shipped in #19705 — adds `hooks/list` and `hooks/config/write` JSON-RPC methods with a `HookConfigSelector` discriminated by `source` (`plugin` requires `pluginId`+`key`, `user`/`project` requires `key` only) declared as closed `oneOf` with `additionalProperties: false` at `HooksConfigWriteParams.json:21-104`, persistence into `[[hooks.config]]` TOML blocks via new `core/src/config/hook_config.rs` with `set_hook_config(&mut self, selector, enabled) -> bool` and three TOML round-trip helpers including the critical `normalize_hook_config_source_path` for the project-source variant, and a `hooks_config_write_params_round_trips_selector_variants` test pinning the JSON↔Rust reversibility — but the "stable hook key" format that was undefined in #19705 is still undefined here, no GC story for orphaned `[[hooks.config]]` entries when a plugin uninstalls, the `normalize_hook_config_source_path` symlink-vs-lexical-canonicalize choice isn't documented, and the `HooksListResponse` schema doesn't appear in the diff to confirm it carries an explicit per-entry `enabled: bool`. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3663 is a +808/-21 supplemental TUI flicker fix after #3591 targeting one specific reproducible class — long assistant streaming text taller than `stdout.rows` triggers Ink 6.2.3 to emit `ESC[2J ESC[3J ESC[H` and replay all `<Static>` output before each new dynamic frame — by introducing `slicePendingTextForHeight(text, maxHeight, maxWidth)` at `ConversationMessages.tsx:84-150` that uses `toCodePoints(text)` for correct grapheme iteration + `getCachedStringWidth(char)` floored at 1 for combining marks + a tail-preserving sliding window keeping `visibleContentHeight = targetMaxHeight - 1` lines via `visibleLines.shift()`, gated behind `isPending` in both `PrefixedMarkdownMessage:208-242` and `ContinuationMarkdownMessage:253-280` so the final transcript still goes through full `MarkdownDisplay` after streaming completes, with `MIN_PENDING_PREVIEW_HEIGHT = 2` floor preventing one-row terminals from collapsing to zero visible content + a `PendingTextPreview` component rendering correctly-pluralized `... first N streaming line(s) hidden ...`, plus a 170-line design doc whose explicit "non-claims" section bounds the scope to streaming flicker (not resize/compact-toggle/static-replacement classes) and a 276-line ANSI-capture integration test driving 220 chunks at 70ms against an 88×26 PTY with synchronized-output disabled and asserting `clearTerminalPairCount == 0` + `finalDoneCount == 1` + `framesCaptured >= 40` as objective deterministic pass/fail signals; companion shell-side change requires both a semantic viewport change AND new renderable output before sending another live shell chunk so resize-soft-wrap reflows no longer masquerade as fresh shell output. At block/goose, #8854 is a +28/-140 deletion of the `defaults.yaml` config-overlay mechanism — removes `defaults_path: Option<PathBuf>` field from `Config` (`base.rs:122-167`), the public `Config::new_with_defaults<P1, P2, P3>` constructor (`:284-310`), `find_workspace_or_exe_root()` probe, `load_defaults`/`merge_missing_defaults` helpers, and four tests including `test_full_precedence_env_over_config_over_defaults` that pinned the documented config-precedence layer — replaced by a single `set_security_defaults()` that reads `DEFAULT_SECURITY_PROMPT_ENABLED`/`DEFAULT_SECURITY_COMMAND_CLASSIFIER_ENABLED` env vars and seeds the corresponding `SECURITY_*` keys via `set_param` (writing-through to user config, not overlaying) wired in two places: `goose-server/src/commands/agent.rs:40` and `SecurityManager::new()` at `security/mod.rs:55`.

Verdict mix: 5 merge-as-is (gemini-cli #25980 textbook input-validation fix at the user-input→filesystem boundary with paired no-crash + sibling-still-resolves regression tests + comment naming the greedy-regex root cause + intentional preservation of strict `paths.ts` semantics for internal callers, gemini-cli #25981 27-line restoration of prior `/clear` behavior after commit `885af07d` refactor with `setUpdateInfo` threaded the same shape as `setBannerVisible` and pinned by a focused `mockSetUpdateInfo` test, codex #19753 explicit MCP shutdown surface closing four cited issues with correct `'static + Send` future shape that releases the write lock before per-client awaits + Drop-to-explicit-call refactor of process-handle cleanup + matching call sites at `Op::Shutdown`/channel-close/refresh/connector-probe, codex #19707 surgical extract-method dedup of deferred-dynamic-tool filtering between normal-turn and remote-compact with empty-deferred-set fast-path preserved verbatim and live-endpoint smoke verification, qwen-code #3663 bounded-scope streaming-flicker fix with grapheme-aware visual-width slicer + tail-preserving sliding window + isPending gating preserving final-markdown formatting + ANSI-level deterministic acceptance metrics + an unusually disciplined design doc with explicit non-claims), 2 merge-after-nits (gemini-cli #26009 well-isolated experimental.windowsBash routing with sound `enableWindowsBash && isWindows && !isStrictSandbox` gating + sandbox-takes-precedence + once-per-session warn-flag + Git-for-Windows-aware registry+filesystem probe chain but cachedBashPath is process-lifetime with no settings-change invalidation, registry probe assumes 64-bit hive without `/reg:64` fallback, MSYS2_ARG_CONV_EXCL=* missing alongside MSYS_NO_PATHCONV, and the not-found warn bypasses the project's logging surface; codex #19712 sound discriminated-union protocol design with `HookConfigSelector` correctly tagged by `source` + closed `oneOf` + `additionalProperties: false` + JSON↔Rust round-trip pin + path-normalization helper for project-source variant but undefined "stable hook key" format inherited from #19705, no orphan-GC story for plugin-uninstall, undocumented symlink-vs-lexical canonicalize choice in `normalize_hook_config_source_path`, and missing per-entry `enabled: bool` in `HooksListResponse` schema), 1 request-changes (block/goose #8854 deletes a public constructor + documented config-precedence layer with empty PR description, no `#[deprecated]` migration path, no test pinning the new "env-var seeded into user config on first launch is idempotent + preserves user overrides + doesn't reseed wrong-type keys" contract, `is_ok()` check that conflates "key absent" with "key present but wrong type" silently overwriting misconfigured-but-intentional user values, and a deployment-model change from "pkg-shipped overlay never persists to user config" to "env-var-driven seeding writes through to user config on first run" that's not acknowledged in docs).

### W17 drip-103 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos. At sst/opencode, #24553 hardens shell cancellation with a `cancelled: Deferred.Deferred<void>` on `ShellHandle` that `stopShell` flips before `Fiber.interrupt` so the await arm at `runner.ts:155-163` can distinguish cooperative cancellation from incidental interrupt-only causes via `(yield* Deferred.isDone(cancelled)) || Cause.hasInterruptsOnly(exit.cause)`, plus a full rewrite of `SessionPrompt.shellImpl` inside `Effect.uninterruptibleMask` so message/tool-part bookkeeping is materialized atomically before any cancellable work runs and an `awaitDone` helper deduplicates the `RunnerCancelled → onInterrupt ?? Effect.die` pattern across the three `case` arms in `ensureRunning` — wants a regression test pinning "abort-mid-stream → assistant `time.completed` set + tool part `completed` + aborted metadata appended", a comment on the `RunnerCancelled` vs `Cancelled` tag relationship in `awaitDone`, and acknowledgment that `Deferred.succeed` is silently no-op on double-stopShell; #24544 fixes a real session-resume / cross-device-merge correctness bug by switching `SessionPrompt.run`'s post-step loop guards from lexicographic `lastUser.id < lastAssistant.id` to position-anchored `lastUserIndex < lastAssistantIndex` at `session/prompt.ts:1366` and pivoting the `step > 1` follow-up-user-text scan from filter-by-id to a position-anchored `for (let i = lastFinishedIndex + 1; i < msgs.length; i++)` at `:1471-1474` — `MessageID.ascending()` is only sortable when generated locally in monotonic order, and the upstream walk that establishes `lastUser`/`lastAssistant`/`lastFinished` already takes array position as truth so the downstream id-lex was actively inconsistent, but ships with no test and the "no user message at all" edge (`lastUserIndex === -1 < someIndex` short-circuits) is a behavior change worth a one-line guard. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3648 is a maintenance compile-fix adding explicit `export type { NotificationRecordPayload }` and `export { SESSION_TITLE_MAX_LENGTH }` named re-exports to `packages/core/src/index.ts` (alongside the existing `export *` lines, which under `verbatimModuleSyntax` don't re-export named-only `type` exports the way ACP needs them) plus a local `subtype: string | undefined` discriminant relaxation in `HistoryReplayer.replayRecord`'s user-case block scope so the `'notification' | 'cron'` literal-union check survives upstream `ChatRecord` discriminant tightening, with four-each regression tests pinning both the dynamic-import contract and the literal value `200` for the const; #3617 fixes #3616 (LM Studio rejecting multimodal tool-result content with HTTP 400 "Invalid 'messages' in payload") by adding an opt-in `splitToolMedia: boolean` flag to `ContentGeneratorConfig` that scans the converted `role: "tool"` message in `core/openaiContentGenerator/converter.ts:444-491` for `image_url`/`input_audio`/`video_url`/`file` parts, strips them out (replacing tool content with joined text or a `[media attached in following user message]` placeholder so LM Studio's empty-string rejection is also avoided), and emits a separate `role: "user"` follow-up message carrying `[ {type:'text',text:'(attached media from previous tool call)'}, …mediaParts ]` — gating defaults `?? false` at `pipeline.ts:524` so absence-in-config preserves prior behavior for permissive providers, the test at `converter.test.ts:385-457` mirrors the existing embedded-image test exactly with just `splitToolMedia: true` set and pins both the `typeof toolMessage.content === 'string'` invariant and the `data:image/png;base64,…` URL in the follow-up user message, only-media branch coverage and a comment explaining the `as unknown as ContentPartText[]` runtime-vs-type cast are minor follow-ups. At openai/codex, #19771 generalizes the per-call-site filter pattern that #19707 had to apply specifically to remote-compact by moving the `defer_loading` filtering of dynamic tools from `build_prompt` (per-turn) into `ToolRouter::from_config` (once at construction) at `core/src/tools/router.rs:71-93`, deleting the `filter_deferred_dynamic_tool_spec` body + `HashSet<ToolName>` collection + `ResponsesApiNamespaceTool`/`ToolSpec` imports from `turn.rs` and reproducing them verbatim in `router.rs` with a tasteful `if deferred_dynamic_tools.is_empty() { return Some(spec); }` early-return at `:301-303` for the no-deferred fast path — composition order with the existing `code_mode_only_enabled` filter is correct (code-mode first, defer-loading second), the `model_visible_specs_filter_deferred_dynamic_tools` integration test at `router_tests.rs:142-225` pins the fundamental invariant (deferred ≠ unregistered: `find_spec(namespaced("codex_app", hidden_tool)).is_some()` while `model_visible_specs()` returns only the visible one), and `compact_remote.rs:60+` adds a wire-payload `assert_tools_payload_does_not_defer` recursive walk asserting at the HTTP boundary not just the Rust API boundary. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #25975 extends `createTransport` env-var expansion in `mcp-client.ts:2270-2295` from just `mcpServerConfig.env[*]` to also cover `command`, `args[*]`, and `cwd` before handing to `StdioClientTransport` — also opportunistically switches the env-value expansion source from `expansionEnv` to `sanitizedEnv` so the allowlist now applies uniformly across all four expansion sites (a meaningful security tightening that the test rewrite at `:2131-2169` honestly surfaces by adding `allowedEnvironmentVariables: ['GEMINI_TEST_VAR']` to the local context), with three new tests pinning args expansion + command/cwd expansion + sequential `${VAR1}_${VAR2}` syntax — wants the security-tightening side-effect called out in the PR description and CHANGELOG (users relying on prior un-allowlisted behavior will see literal `$FOO` strings), a negative-case test for "var not on allowlist → arg left as literal", and a null-arg defensive guard in the `.map`. At block/goose, #8814 converts `InferenceRuntime::get_or_init` from `-> Arc<Self>` to `-> Result<Arc<Self>>` at `local_inference.rs:65-91`, replacing the `panic!("Failed to init llama backend: {}", e)` arm with `return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(...))` and threading `?` through the two call sites (`AppState::new` at `state.rs:51` and `LocalInferenceProvider::from_env` at `:359`) — the `BackendAlreadyInitialized` `unreachable!` arm is correctly preserved as a true mutex-protected invariant rather than blanket-converted, distinguishing real-can't-happen from user-facing init failure (missing GPU, ENOENT on model deps), and the `#[cfg(feature = "local-inference")]` gate is preserved so non-local-inference builds are untouched; #8843 is a three-coupled correctness fix to the Bedrock content-block round-trip in `formats/bedrock.rs` — outbound `MessageContent::Thinking` and `RedactedThinking` no longer silently map to `Text("")` (which would corrupt Bedrock reasoning models like Claude-with-reasoning and `openai.gpt-oss-*` that require reasoning blocks replayed unmodified with signature across turns) but construct proper `ReasoningContent::ReasoningText` (with conditional `if !signature.is_empty() { builder.signature(...) }` respecting Bedrock's `Option<String>` omitted-vs-empty distinction) and `ReasoningContent::RedactedContent` blocks (base64-decoded blob, with a documented same-as-prior `Text("")` fallback on decode error scoped specifically to the cross-provider Anthropic-API case where `data` is opaque not guaranteed-base64); inbound `from_bedrock_content_block` signature widens to `Result<Option<MessageContent>>` with `from_bedrock_message` adding `.filter_map(|r| r.transpose())` so the SDK's `non_exhaustive` `Unknown` arm filters silently while named known-unsupported variants (`Audio | CitationsContent | Document | GuardContent | Image | SearchResult | Video`) explicitly `bail!` with a stable `bedrock_content_block_kind` discriminant string — defensive enumeration makes "we forgot to handle this when the SDK added it" a *visible* failure, six dense round-trip tests pin signature-present, signature-absent, redacted base64 round-trip, `Image` typed-error, `CitationsContent` typed-error, and the outbound thinking → `ReasoningText` direction.

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (QwenLM/qwen-code #3648 maintenance compile-fix with two explicit `export type`/named re-exports + local `subtype: string | undefined` discriminant relaxation in a block scope + four-each regression tests pinning dynamic-import contract and literal const value `200`, QwenLM/qwen-code #3617 opt-in `splitToolMedia` flag fixing LM Studio HTTP 400 with text/media partition in single-pass loop + textOnly-collapses-to-string tool message + placeholder for empty-text case + follow-up user message mirroring exact embedded-image test shape, openai/codex #19771 push-the-filter-into-the-router architectural fix making `model_visible_specs()` actually mean what its name says with verbatim move of `filter_deferred_dynamic_tool_spec` + early-return fast path + router-level test pinning deferred ≠ unregistered + wire-payload `assert_tools_payload_does_not_defer` recursive walk, block/goose #8814 panic→Result conversion at user-visible boundary preserving `BackendAlreadyInitialized` `unreachable!` as true mutex invariant + `#[cfg(feature = "local-inference")]` gate intact + propagated through both known callers with `?`, block/goose #8843 three-coupled Bedrock reasoning-content round-trip fix with outbound proper `ReasoningContent::ReasoningText`/`RedactedContent` construction respecting Bedrock `Option<String>` signature omitted-vs-empty distinction + inbound `Result<Option<MessageContent>>` widening with named-arm enumeration of known-unsupported variants making non_exhaustive SDK additions a visible bail rather than silent truncation + six dense round-trip tests + privacy-aware `bedrock_content_block_kind` discriminant for logging), 4 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24553 cancelled-deferred + uninterruptibleMask architectural rework correctly distinguishing cooperative cancellation from incidental interrupt-only causes but with `RunnerCancelled` vs `Cancelled` tag mismatch undocumented + double-stopShell idempotence not called out + missing abort-mid-stream regression test for the rewritten `shellImpl`, sst/opencode #24544 position-anchored loop-guard correctness fix for cross-device-merge / session-resume scenarios where lexicographic id ordering breaks but ships with no test and "no user message at all" edge `lastUserIndex === -1 < someIndex` is a behavior change worth a one-line guard, google-gemini/gemini-cli #25975 args/command/cwd env-var expansion with opportunistic security-tightening switch from `expansionEnv` to `sanitizedEnv` enforcing allowlist uniformly + three new tests covering args/command-cwd/sequential `${VAR1}_${VAR2}` but security-tightening side-effect deserves explicit PR description + CHANGELOG note + missing negative-case "not on allowlist → literal `$VAR`" test + null-arg defensive guard in `.map`).


---

### W17 drip-104 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos. At sst/opencode, #24574 is a +22/-1 mechanical decoupling refactor splitting the previously-shared empty-content normalization branch in `normalizeMessages()` at `packages/opencode/src/provider/transform.ts:55` so the Anthropic and Bedrock SDKs each get their own copy of the filter — the duplicated bodies are byte-for-byte identical down to the `(msg): msg is ModelMessage => msg !== undefined && msg.content !== ""` type predicate, no functional change, the `ignore:` PR title prefix correctly signals no changelog entry, and a one-line snapshot pinning "Anthropic and Bedrock both filter empty array parts" would lock the behavioral parity that this refactor is explicitly preserving; #24573 is a +4-line provider-options fix at `transform.ts:829-832` forcing `toolStreaming = false` when `model.api.npm === "@ai-sdk/google-vertex/anthropic"` (closes #24562) — branch fires before the existing OpenAI-family `store: false` branch with no flag interaction, keyed on the exact npm package string rather than `providerID` so it correctly targets only the `/anthropic` sub-package not other Vertex models, but the one-directional force-off has no escape hatch for future Vertex server-side fixes and lacks a one-assertion regression pin keyed on the literal npm string. At openai/codex, #19778 is a +1811/-75 third-installment plugin-discovered-hooks PR completing the trilogy started by #19705 and #19712 — adds the actual `hooks/list` and `hooks/config/write` JSON-RPC handlers in `app-server/src/codex_message_processor.rs:1256-1391` with `cwds` defaulting to session cwd when empty and `effective_enabled` returned for caller UI re-render, the user-config write path through `core/src/config/edit.rs` and `core/src/config/hook_config.rs:1715-1820` with `set_hook_config(&mut self, key, enabled) -> bool` plus three unit tests covering disabled-entry-write/enabled-removes-entry/inline-table-preservation, and crucially the round-trip integration test `hooks_list_shows_discovered_hook_and_config_write_disables_it` at `app-server/tests/suite/v2/hooks_list.rs:1517-1577` that pins list → write {enabled:false} → list-shows-disabled and directly closes the round-trip-missing critique drip-101 raised against #19705 and drip-102 raised against #19712 + adds explicit per-entry `enabled: bool` to `HooksListEntry` closing drip-102's schema-doc gap — but the "stable hook key" format is still undefined three PRs into the stack and the plugin-uninstall GC story for orphaned `[[hooks.config]]` entries is still missing despite this PR adding 55 lines to `app-server/README.md`; #19772 is a +377/-175 permissions-migration PR making `ConfigToml`'s default-resolution path produce a `PermissionProfile` directly rather than building a legacy `SandboxPolicy` and converting back — `derive_permission_profile` at `config/src/config_toml.rs:644-660` is the new canonical entry point with `derive_sandbox_policy` kept as a thin wrapper for migration discipline (no `#[deprecated]` attribute means no compile-time signal to stop new call sites), `PermissionProfile::workspace_write_with(...)` constructor takes a `NetworkSandboxPolicy::{Enabled,Restricted}` enum widening the prior boolean `network_access` field at `:692-696`, the Windows-downgrade-if-unsupported check is hoisted out of the closure at `:678-681` preserving the `WindowsSandboxLevel::Disabled` carveout for the experimental Windows sandbox opt-in, and the third test family in the verification list (`with_additional_legacy_workspace_writable_roots_protects_metadata`) pins the real footgun fix that missing `.git` and `.agents` remain writable for legacy compatibility so `git init` / `agents init` flows don't break. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26016 is a +5/-3 docs-hygiene fix targeting two distinct broken-link classes — `CONTRIBUTING.md:416` switches from root-anchored `![](/docs/assets/connected_devtools.png)` (only resolves on the docs site under a specific base path) to relative `![Connected React DevTools](docs/assets/...)` (resolves both on github.com and the docs site, with free alt-text accessibility win), `docs/CONTRIBUTING.md` flips from a `120000` symlink (which GitHub web view renders as a broken link) to a three-line forwarding stub that preserves the historical URL for any external doc site or third-party tool with hardcoded paths, and `docs/index.md:127` swaps `/docs/contributing` (route doesn't exist in docs-site routing) for `../CONTRIBUTING.md` matching the relative-path style of sibling links; #25974 is a +21/-0 13-line behavior fix in `ThemeManager.findThemeByName` at `packages/cli/src/ui/themes/theme-manager.ts:658-662` adding a linear scan over `this.fileThemes.values()` matching by `theme.name` so a file-loaded theme can be looked up by its **internal display name** (the JSON file's `name` field) not just by its absolute filesystem path — placed correctly between the existing path-based `fileThemes.get(themeName)` O(1) fast path at `:655` and the final `return undefined` at `:665`, paired with an 18-line regression test at `theme-manager.test.ts:155-168` that stubs `fs.existsSync`/`readFileSync` and pins the exact lookup-by-internal-name scenario, but the scan returns the first match by insertion order so two file-loaded themes declaring the same internal `name` silently shadow each other (reachable in practice because `fileThemes` is keyed by absolute path so duplicates load successfully under different keys) and there's no negative-case test pinning that `findThemeByName('does-not-exist')` still returns `undefined`. At BerriAI/litellm, #26575 bundles two coupled but independently-significant changes in the MCP OAuth discovery path — (1) extends `_descovery_metadata()` and `_fetch_single_authorization_server_metadata()` so a tenant-scoped issuer URL of the form `https://<azure-identity-host>/<tenant-id>/v2.0` resolves correctly via a new issuer-rooted `.well-known/openid-configuration` candidate URL at `mcp_server_manager.py:1684` plus a fallback `_build_authorization_server_metadata` at `:1726-1751` with three guards (hostname in a small allowlist of public identity-authority hostnames, exactly two path segments, second segment literally `v2.0`) returning tenant-derived `oauth2/v2.0/authorize` and `oauth2/v2.0/token` URLs, AND (2) removes `Depends(user_api_key_auth)` from the `/v1/mcp/server/oauth/{server_id}/authorize` and `/.../token` routes plus weakens the admin-only check at `mcp_management_endpoints.py:1478-1480` from `if not _user_has_admin_view(user_api_key_dict)` to `if user_api_key_dict is not None and not _user_has_admin_view(user_api_key_dict)` so passing `None` bypasses entirely — discovery half (1) is mergeable but the route-deauthing (2) creates an open-redirect surface where any unauthenticated caller can probe arbitrary `server_id`s with a caller-controlled `redirect_uri` parameter, and the diff doesn't show an allowlist check on the cached server record; also note `_descovery_metadata` previously *raised* `RuntimeError` on unexpected 200-without-challenge as a real misconfiguration signal that's now silently dropped at `:1488-1502`. At block/goose, #8856 adds a +40/-0 GitHub Actions workflow `.github/workflows/dependabot-auto-merge.yml` that on every dependabot PR open/synchronize fetches dependabot metadata via `dependabot/fetch-metadata@d7267f607e9d3fb96fc2fbe83e0af444713e90b7` (full-SHA pin, good supply-chain hygiene) and if the update type is `version-update:semver-patch` or `version-update:semver-minor` auto-approves (`gh pr review --approve`) and enables auto-merge with `--merge` strategy — gated on `github.event.pull_request.user.login == 'dependabot[bot]' && github.repository == '<owner>/goose'` with the repo-name guard correctly defending against fork-pushes-to-canonical-remote (but pinning the canonical owner-name as a string literal silently disables on future repo-rename), `permissions:` correctly scoped to `contents: write` + `pull-requests: write` with no `id-token`, the approve and enable-auto-merge gate predicates duplicated verbatim (cheap to factor), and major-version bumps intentionally left for human review; relies on branch protection on `main` to require CI green before auto-merge actually fires (correct architectural choice — workflow shouldn't duplicate branch-protection logic — but a misconfigured branch protection silently auto-merges unverified bumps, worth one-time maintainer verification).

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24574 mechanical Anthropic/Bedrock decoupling refactor with byte-identical duplicated bodies preparing future divergence with no behavior change, sst/opencode #24573 four-line `toolStreaming = false` fix for `@ai-sdk/google-vertex/anthropic` with correct npm-string match closing #24562, google-gemini/gemini-cli #26016 docs-hygiene fix targeting two distinct broken-link classes with relative-path conversion + symlink-to-stub forwarder + accessibility alt-text win), 3 merge-after-nits (openai/codex #19778 third-installment hooks-API PR closing the round-trip-missing critique drip-101/102 raised against #19705/#19712 with explicit list→write→list integration test + per-entry `enabled: bool` schema field but stable-hook-key format and plugin-uninstall GC story still undefined three PRs in, openai/codex #19772 sound permissions-migration step pushing profile-native resolution upstream with cwd-aware metadata defaults preserved and `NetworkSandboxPolicy` enum widening over boolean but `derive_sandbox_policy` wrapper missing `#[deprecated]` attribute and the boolean→enum widening lacks an explicit network-policy-default-case test pin, google-gemini/gemini-cli #25974 13-line `findThemeByName` linear-scan extension at correct fast-path-priority position with paired regression test but duplicate-internal-name policy undefined and missing negative-case `findThemeByName('does-not-exist')` pin, block/goose #8856 well-scoped 40-line dependabot auto-merge workflow with full-SHA action pin + correct permission scoping + repo-name guard against fork-pushes but relies on unverified branch-protection-on-main as load-bearing safety net and `--merge` vs `--squash` strategy hardcoded), 1 needs-discussion (BerriAI/litellm #26575 bundles a mergeable Azure-Entra OIDC discovery extension with a security-relevant route-deauthing of `/mcp/server/oauth/{server_id}/authorize` and `/.../token` that creates an open-redirect surface absent a `redirect_uri` allowlist on the cached server record + silently drops the prior `RuntimeError("OAuth discovery must not succeed without a challenge")` misconfiguration signal — should split into two PRs).

### W17 drip-105 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos. At sst/opencode, #24543 ships a real fix for stale-closure session-route writes in `routes/session/index.tsx:185-211` (captures `route.sessionID` once, re-checks after every `await sdk.client.session.get` and `await sync.session.sync`, three exit paths now consistent) but bundles three unrelated changes (a `sync_prompt_context_on_session_switch` kv toggle whose label "Keep model/agent when switching sessions" disagrees with the underlying flag semantics, a scrollbar color change to `theme.background`/`theme.borderActive` with no rationale or contrast screenshot, and an empty 0-byte `packages/sdk/js/openapi.json` that will throw on any downstream `JSON.parse`); #24537 is a +11/-1 mechanical fix for crash #24529 adding `args: params` to the result object of all eight built-in tools (`apply_patch`, `bash`, `edit`, `glob`, `grep`, `lsp`, `read`, `write`) with `read.ts` correctly covering all three of its return paths and `bash.ts:601-615` switching to `const result = yield* run(...); return { ...result, args: params }` to preserve run's existing keys, but the contract begs to be enforced in `Tool.define`'s type signature so the next tool author can't forget. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3645 reorders OpenAI auth model precedence from `argv > OPENAI_MODEL > QWEN_MODEL > settings.model.name` to `argv > settings.model.name > OPENAI_MODEL > QWEN_MODEL` at `modelConfigUtils.ts:95-122` with the load-bearing trick being a `filteredEnv` that strips `OPENAI_MODEL`/`QWEN_MODEL` whenever the resolved model didn't actually come from those env vars (`delete filteredEnv['OPENAI_MODEL']` if `resolvedModel !== env['OPENAI_MODEL']`) so the downstream `resolveModelConfig` can't re-apply env precedence and undo the fix, with 494 lines of pin tests across 8 cases + 4 edge cases using `expect.objectContaining({modelProvider})` for low-brittleness specificity, but a stray `// trigger rebuild` dev-loop comment at line 493 needs stripping and the side-effect that env-resolved models now get a real `modelProvider` lookup (where they previously got `undefined`) should be called out in the PR description; #3618 reworks vscode-companion slash-command Enter-vs-fill behavior by pushing the decision from "client decides by name" to "server attaches `input?: {hint}|null` per command" with `Session.ts:984-991` applying `input: cmd.kind !== CommandKind.BUILT_IN || cmd.completion != null ? {hint: cmd.argumentHint ?? ''} : null` and `App.tsx:822-836` consuming via `!serverCmd.input && !fillOnly` auto-submit, plus a bonus `clientActions` lookup-table cleanup collapsing three previously-separate `auth`/`account`/`model` blocks and treating `/login` as a legacy alias for `/auth` in `useMessageSubmit.ts:101-115`, but the `null` vs `undefined` semantic distinction the type carries is collapsed by the consumer's truthy check and the new fall-through fill-but-don't-submit Enter path lacks a webview test pin. At openai/codex, #19774 continues the multi-PR permissions migration (siblings #19772 already merged, #19709/#19776 in flight) by making `SessionConfiguredEvent` profile-only on the wire — drops the legacy `sandbox_policy: SandboxPolicy` field, changes `permission_profile` from `Option<PermissionProfile>` to non-optional `PermissionProfile`, and adds a custom `Deserialize` impl at `protocol.rs:3638-3697` with a private `Wire` struct that resolves `(Some(profile), _) => profile`, `(None, Some(sandbox_policy)) => from_legacy_sandbox_policy_for_cwd`, `(None, None) => missing_field` so back-compat lifts old emitters and on-disk session logs into a profile losslessly while new producers get type-level enforcement; the `(None, None)` error message names only `permission_profile` (slightly misleading for genuinely-old payloads missing both), the `(Some, Some)` "profile wins, sandbox_policy silently discarded" arm lacks a test pin, and the duplicated `Wire` struct will silently drop new fields on legacy-payload deserialization without a maintenance comment or `#[serde(flatten)]` refactor; #19709 fixes the inactive-thread (delegated worker) apply-patch approval flow which previously rendered an empty diff in the approval prompt, by buffering `FileUpdateChange` lists in `ThreadEventStore` (new `file_change_changes(turn_id, item_id)` at `thread_events.rs:160-191` walks buffer in reverse then falls back to committed `self.turns`), recovering them at approval time via `thread_routing.rs:275-281`'s `unwrap_or_default()`-guarded lookup, converting wire→core via new `file_update_changes_to_core` at `app_server_approval_conversions.rs:46-69` (correctly threads `move_path` for renames), and architecturally splitting "overlay = the question, history cell = the diff" so the diff stays visible after approval — with a strong end-to-end integration test at `tests.rs:2542-2611` pinning the buffered-payload-to-rendered-text flow but the `move_path: Some(...)` rename case and `Delete` arm lack converter test coverage. At BerriAI/litellm, #26579 is a minimum-viable +56/-0 fix adding `parallel_tool_calls` and `text` to the chatgpt-passthrough Responses-API allowlist at `transformation.py:96-105` so structured-output schemas (`text.format.json_schema`) and explicit parallel-tool-call disables survive the proxy translation, with parametrized tests covering `json_schema`/`json_object`/`text` formats plus `parallel_tool_calls=False` and the existing drops-unsupported test correctly extended to lock the contract centrally — but a `parallel_tool_calls=True` symmetric-preservation case is missing and sibling Responses-API transformations (openai/azure passthroughs) likely have the same allowlist gap. At block/goose, #8836 drops `-i` from the login-shell PATH-resolution invocations at `goose-mcp/src/subprocess.rs:60-65` and `goose/src/agents/platform_extensions/developer/shell.rs:171-189` (both flatpak and non-flatpak paths) so the spawned shell can't `tcsetpgrp()` to claim the controlling-terminal foreground process group from goose, fixing the SIGTTOU-on-rustyline-raw-mode-entry suspension bug, with high-quality 5-line comments at both sites explaining the mechanism so a future contributor won't re-add the flag — but users who export `PATH` from `.bashrc` or interactive `.zshrc` blocks (rather than login files like `.bash_profile`/`.zprofile`) will see a smaller PATH after this change and the trade-off isn't called out in the PR description.

| [#8836](https://github.com/block/goose/pull/8836) | fix: drop -i flag from login shell PATH resolution to prevent SIGTTOU | [2026-W17/drip-105/block-goose-pr-8836.md](2026-W17/drip-105/block-goose-pr-8836.md) |

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (BerriAI/litellm #26579 minimum-viable additive fix adding `parallel_tool_calls` and `text` to the chatgpt-passthrough Responses-API allowlist with parametrized tests covering all three `text.format` shapes plus the boolean preservation, with the existing drops-unsupported test correctly extended to lock the contract centrally — only nice-to-have follow-ups are a `parallel_tool_calls=True` symmetric case and a sibling-provider audit), 6 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24537 mechanical eight-tool `args: params` threading correctly fixing crash #24529 with all three `read.ts` return paths covered and bash's `{...result, args: params}` spread preserving run's keys but the contract begs to be lifted into `Tool.define`'s type signature and `apply_patch.ts`'s early-return path needs verification, QwenLM/qwen-code #3645 sound precedence-reorder using the right "strip env vars at the boundary" pattern with 8+4 cases of pin-test coverage but a stray `// trigger rebuild` comment at line 493 and an undocumented modelProvider-lookup behavior change for env-resolved models, QwenLM/qwen-code #3618 well-architected client/server contract migration moving the Enter-vs-fill decision to the server's `input` field with bonus `clientActions` table cleanup and `/login`→`/auth` legacy aliasing but the `null` vs `undefined` semantic distinction is collapsed by the truthy consumer check and the fill-don't-submit Enter path lacks a webview test pin, openai/codex #19774 disciplined wire-format migration with type-level enforcement for new producers and lossless `(Some, _)`/`(None, Some)`/`(None, None)` resolution for legacy payloads but the missing-field error message names only one of two relevant fields and the `Wire` struct needs maintenance discipline against silent field drift, openai/codex #19709 right "buffer-then-recover" architecture with a strong end-to-end integration test pinning wire-payload-to-rendered-text and the correct overlay/history split but missing converter coverage for `move_path: Some(...)` renames and `Delete` plus an undocumented turn-id-empty-string wildcard, block/goose #8836 correct fix for a real foreground-group-stealing SIGTTOU bug applied symmetrically to flatpak and non-flatpak paths with comments good enough to deter regression but the PATH-source-file behavior trade-off for `.bashrc`/interactive-`.zshrc` users isn't called out), 1 request-changes (sst/opencode #24543 the stale-effect guard at `routes/session/index.tsx:185-211` is the right fix and merge-ready in isolation but the PR is three unrelated changes in one trench coat — guard, kv-toggle with mismatched label, and unmotivated scrollbar colors — plus an empty 0-byte `openapi.json` that will throw on downstream `JSON.parse`; needs splitting), 0 needs-discussion.

### W17 drip-106 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos. At openai/codex, #19773 is the third installment in the in-flight permissions migration (siblings #19772 already merged, #19774/#19776 in flight) making `ThreadSessionState.permission_profile` non-optional with two new helpers `active_legacy_permission_profile_for_cwd`/`active_legacy_sandbox_policy_for_cwd` that derive a fallback profile from local legacy `SandboxPolicy` against the **target thread's cwd** rather than the global session cwd — the pin test rewrite at `tests.rs:2858-2871` (`thread_read_session_state_does_not_reuse_primary_permission_profile`) names the trap explicitly and the `chatwidget.rs:1618-1622` re-wrap to `Some(session.permission_profile)` is the unavoidable boundary cost until #19774 ships profile-only `SessionConfigured`; the `app_server_session.rs` `.expect("response includes profile")` is the one risk site for rolling-upgrade scenarios; #19755 ships Responses stream lifecycle diagnostics with a `ResponseStreamLifecycleRecorder` private to codex-api, a `ResponseStreamTerminalState` enum (`StreamError`/`ClosedBeforeCompletion`/`IdleTimeout`/successful-completion), and a `finalize_lifecycle_error` helper that wraps three terminal-error sites in `responses_websocket.rs` ~597-621 — the integration test asserts both the upstream provider message AND the `Stream lifecycle: diagnostic:` / `last_event=response.incomplete` log-grep contract; the `ResponseEvent::Created` change from unit to `{ response_id: Option<String> }` correctly distinguishes "created arrived but no id" from "no created event at all" and the `otel/.../session_telemetry.rs:1045-1048` pattern-match update with `Created { .. }` ignores the field at the telemetry boundary; #19753 fixes a four-issue regression cluster (#12491/#12976/#18881/#19469) where stdio MCP server processes survive thread shutdown — adds `McpConnectionManager.begin_shutdown(&mut self) -> impl Future + Send + 'static` (the `'static` bound enables fire-and-forget vs. wait-for-it semantics), drains clients via `std::mem::take` so the manager appears empty before children exit, propagates `cancel_token` into `AsyncManagedClient`, and crucially relocates the `or_cancel(&cancel_token_for_fut)` wrapper in `rmcp_client.rs:144-185` from inside `start_server_task` to outside the entire `async {...}` so cancellation aborts the spawn stage too; the integration test in the diff tail dispatches a long-running call (`sleep_after_ms: 300_000`) then `client.shutdown().await` and asserts both `wait_for_process_exit(server_pid)` and that the call task joins within 5s — that's the actual leak path with a real OS process and PID polling. At BerriAI/litellm, #26581 is a 5-line allowlist fix at `litellm/proxy/hooks/mcp_semantic_filter/hook.py:150-167` adding `"anthropic_messages"` to the call-type tuple — pre-fix, every `/v1/messages` request from Claude SDK clients silently received the unfiltered MCP tool catalog because the hook's guard short-circuited; the load-bearing 4-line comment names this as a configured-but-silently-inactive failure mode, and a new `_AnthropicMessagesMockFilter` stub correctly walks Anthropic typed content blocks via `for m in reversed(messages)`; #26577 fixes a real GIL-hold blocking the asyncio event loop — `stream_chunk_builder` was unconditionally calling `count_reasoning_tokens` (which calls `tiktoken.encode()` holding the GIL for tens of seconds on 100k+ token reasoning responses) even though `calculate_usage` was already discarding the recount when the provider supplied `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens`; the new `chunks_have_reasoning_tokens` at `streaming_chunk_builder_utils.py:509-555` is the right gating predicate with provably-semantics-preserving (not estimation-tradeoff) skip and defensive triple-fallback chunk-shape sniffing (dict / `getattr` / `_hidden_params['usage']`) covering the three known shapes — but the PR bundles an orthogonal Bedrock cache-point-TTL feature across 4 unrelated files; #26576 is a 3-line gating-condition fix at `proxy_server.py:3242-3252` changing `len(cache_params.keys()) == 0` to `"host" not in cache_params and "url" not in cache_params` — pre-fix, `cache_params: {mode: default_off}` plus `REDIS_HOST` env vars silently dropped to InMemoryCache (each pod isolated, multi-pod spend tracking broken with no log signal), and the two new tests pin both the positive (env-var fallback fires + preserves `mode`) and negative (explicit `host` wins over `REDIS_HOST` env var) branches. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3667 replaces a `setInterval(checkModelChange, 1000)` polling loop in `AppContainer.tsx:415-431` with a `Config.onModelChange(cb): () => void` pub/sub subscription; the static-region remount-key approach (which was producing visible duplicate banner output at the top of the terminal on every `/model` switch because Ink's `<Static>` is append-only) is replaced by an explicit `refreshStatic()` call inside the `setCurrentModel` updater, gated by a `if (prev === model) return prev` no-op guard — the side-effect-inside-state-updater shape is unconventional but justified to keep "decide whether to refresh" and "actually refresh" co-located; the 246-line `MainContent.test.tsx` mocks `<Static>` with spy-instrumented passthrough and pins that header re-render is driven by `refreshStatic()`, not remount-key churn. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26005 fixes two coupled bugs in the `/skills link` consent flow: (1) the dialog stayed mounted forever after Yes/No because nobody ever called `setConfirmationRequest(null)` — the type signature `(value: ConfirmationRequest) => void` *forbade* the cleanup, so the fix widens to `ConfirmationRequest | null` and `skillsCommand.ts:115-126` wraps the user `onConfirm` with a closure that clears the slot before delegating; (2) the `DialogFooter` advertised ESC dismissal but `ConsentPrompt` had no ESC handler — the new `useKeypress` hook at `ConsentPrompt.tsx:23-37` matches `Command.ESCAPE` via `keyMatchers[Command.ESCAPE](key)` and calls `onConfirm(false)`, with the test switching from `render` to `renderWithProviders` (necessary because keypress hooks need provider context) and writing the `\x1b` escape byte via `act(async () => { stdin.write('\x1b'); })`.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (openai/codex #19753 textbook regression-coverage fix for a four-issue stdio-MCP-process-leak cluster with the right architecture — `'static`-bound `begin_shutdown` future for fire-and-forget vs wait-for-it semantics, `std::mem::take` drain that makes the manager appear empty before children exit, defensive `Drop` last-resort sweep, and the subtle but important `or_cancel` relocation in `rmcp_client.rs:144-185` to cover the spawn-stage cancellation race — pinned by an integration test that dispatches a long-running call mid-shutdown and asserts both PID-polled process exit and call-task join within 5s; BerriAI/litellm #26581 minimum-possible 5-line allowlist fix for a configured-but-silently-inactive semantic-filter regression on `anthropic_messages` call type with a load-bearing 4-line comment that prevents future "consolidation" PRs from yanking the entry back out, plus a purpose-built Anthropic-content-block-walking test stub; BerriAI/litellm #26576 3-line gating-condition fix correctly identifying that the predicate should be "did the user supply *connection details*?" not "did the user supply ANY cache_params?" — provably backward-compatible because configs that worked before either had empty cache_params or had explicit host/url, and the two-test coverage pins both the positive env-var-fires-and-preserves-non-connection-keys and negative explicit-host-wins-over-env-var branches), 5 merge-after-nits (openai/codex #19773 disciplined drop-Option-derive-default migration with the cwd-binding discipline correctly captured in the rewritten `thread_read_session_state_does_not_reuse_primary_permission_profile` pin test but the `app_server_session.rs` `.expect("response includes profile")` is a TUI-panic risk during rolling upgrades and the cwd-mismatch scenario lacks a direct grant-binding pin test; openai/codex #19755 right-shape lifecycle-diagnostic ship with the typed terminal-state enum and the operator-grep-friendly `Stream lifecycle: diagnostic:` / `last_event=...` log contract pinned by integration test, but the idle-timeout terminal-state arm assignment lacks a guarding comment and `finalize_lifecycle_error`'s in-place message mutation needs a sweep for downstream `==` vs `.contains()` test brittleness; BerriAI/litellm #26577 sound GIL-hold elimination with provably-semantics-preserving skip predicate (`calculate_usage` was already discarding the recount) and defensive triple-fallback chunk-shape handling, but bundles an orthogonal Bedrock cache-point-TTL feature across 4 unrelated files diluting the win and the triple-fallback shapes need a maintenance comment to survive future cleanup; QwenLM/qwen-code #3667 right architectural fix replacing 1000ms polling with event-driven `Config.onModelChange` pub/sub plus explicit `refreshStatic()` (decoupling change-detection from re-render mechanism) and pinned by a 246-line `MainContent.test.tsx` that locks the `<Static>` no-remount contract, but the side-effect-inside-state-updater shape has a Strict-Mode double-invocation tradeoff that's unaddressed and AppContainer-level unsubscribe pinning is missing; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26005 correct two-bug fix coupling the type-system-forbade-cleanup widening (`ConfirmationRequest | null`) with a closure-wrap dismissal at the call site and a `useKeypress`-based ESC handler matching `Command.ESCAPE`, but missing a test for the headline dismissal-after-confirm bug and other consent-flow callers likely have the same lifecycle bug pending an audit), 0 request-changes, 0 needs-discussion.

---

### W17 drip-107 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At openai/codex, #19792 migrates the MultiAgentV2 session-thread cap from the legacy `agents.max_threads` knob to a first-class `features.multi_agent_v2.max_concurrent_threads_per_session` field with default 4 (root + 3 subagents) — drops the `[agents].max_concurrent_threads_per_session → max_threads` alias from `CONFIG_KEY_ALIASES` (`config/src/key_aliases.rs:11-22`), adds the new field to `MultiAgentV2Config` (`core/src/config/mod.rs:705-720`) with `DEFAULT_MULTI_AGENT_V2_MAX_CONCURRENT_THREADS_PER_SESSION = 4` and profile→base→default resolution in `resolve_multi_agent_v2_config` (`mod.rs:1583-1610`), and at `mod.rs:2087-2117` rejects coexistence with `agents.max_threads` as `InvalidInput("agents.max_threads cannot be set when multi_agent_v2 is enabled")` while deriving `agent_max_threads = saturating_sub(1)` so cap=1 cleanly resolves to `Some(0)` (root-only) and trips the new early-return in `tools/handlers/agent_jobs.rs:534-541` with a model-visible `RespondToModel("agent thread limit reached…")` instead of falling through `normalize_concurrency`; three pin tests cover default-counts-root, rejects-legacy-coexistence, and cap-1-disallows-subagents — wants `CodexErr::AgentLimitReached.max_threads` renamed/documented now that the field carries `cap−1` semantics, a one-release deprecation warning for the dropped alias, and a quick audit of `with_max_concurrent_threads_per_session(None)` consumers since the feature-off branch flipped from `Some(_)` to `None`. #19761 adds a hidden, macOS-only `codex app-server device-check-probe` subcommand for prototyping Apple's DeviceCheck attestation across three layers — an Objective-C shim at `app-server/src/devicecheck_probe.m:1-100` compiled by `build.rs` via the `cc` crate (gated `cfg(target_os = "macos")`) that dynamically loads `DeviceCheck.framework` via `dlopen`/`NSClassFromString` rather than linking it (so non-macOS app-server builds and macOS hosts without DeviceCheck both compile), wraps `DCDevice.currentDevice` + `generateTokenWithCompletionHandler:` in a 30-second `dispatch_semaphore_wait` timeout, and returns a C struct with manual `[release]` lifetime ownership transferred via `codex_devicecheck_probe_free`; a safe Rust wrapper at `devicecheck_probe.rs:7-72` ferrying into `DeviceCheckProbeReport` with `token_base64` marked `#[serde(skip_serializing)]` so it never lands in printed JSON, and a non-macOS stub returning structured `supported=false`; CLI plumbing at `cli/src/main.rs:471-902` with a single `--token-out PATH` arg that opens `0o600` on Unix and `anyhow::bail!`s when no token was produced — wants `token_base64` made `pub(crate)` (or wrapped in a `Secret<String>`/zeroize newtype) since the field is currently `pub` and a third-party serializer bypasses the `skip_serializing` guard, plus a one-line ABI comment near the `objc_msgSend` typed-cast call sites flagging the `objc_msgSend_stret` requirement on x86_64 for any future struct-returning selector. At sst/opencode, #24591 closes #18567 by deleting the desktop-only "MessageNav sidebar + single-message focused" branch from `routes/share/[shareID].tsx` (−64/+3 in one file) and collapsing both viewports onto the existing continuous-scroll `turns()` rendering — eliminates the `firstUserMessage()!.id!` non-null assertion that crashed shared sessions whose first message wasn't user-led, removes the `wide()`-conditional `md:hidden`/`lg:hidden` switching from `Tabs`, and drops the now-unused `MessageNav` import; the diff is pure deletion with shrinking failure modes, but the silent loss of deep-link `store.messageId` focus and the side-by-side `SessionReview` pane on desktop deserves explicit acknowledgment in the PR body before merge along with either a confirmation that `turns()` already scroll-into-views on `store.messageId` or a small `createEffect` follow-up. #24588 is a one-character-class typo fix in the Italian CLI documentation (`packages/web/src/content/docs/it/cli.mdx:601`) removing a duplicated "Abilita Abilita TY LSP per i file python" so the row matches the surrounding "Abilita …" parallel-style convention — pure docs, +1/−1, suggests an `rg -n 'Abilita Abilita|Disabilita Disabilita|Mostra Mostra'` audit across the locale tree as a follow-up class-cleanup. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3668 adds an opt-in, settings-driven cost estimator for `/stats model` and the session summary panel — introduces `billing.{currency, modelPrices}` at the top of `settingsSchema.ts:108-150` keyed either on bare model IDs or `authType:model` for provider-specific pricing with per-bucket `discounts.input`/`cachedInput`/`output` multipliers (default 1.0), implements pricing math across `modelBilling.ts` + `sessionBilling.ts` with `authType:model > model` precedence and a `cachedInput → input` fallback, gates the rendering surface on `hasBilling` so users without configured prices see the existing /stats output unchanged, and pins the pricing math end-to-end with a `1M uncached + 250k cached + 500k output @ $5/$1/$15 → $11.50` assertion in `ModelStatsDisplay.test.tsx:357-405`; merge depends on switching the hardcoded `$` prefix to `Intl.NumberFormat({ style: "currency", currency })` so non-USD currencies render correctly, renaming `discounts` → `priceMultipliers` (or documenting that `0.25` means "pay 25% of list", which is a multiplier not a discount) before users encode configs against the current ambiguous semantics, and either dropping the `cachedInput → input` fallback or surfacing a UI warning when the fallback fires (since silently billing cached input at uncached rates is *more* than reality, not less). At BerriAI/litellm, #26580 fixes a silent-drop correctness bug in `SemanticToolFilterHook` where the embedding-based filter was previously fed *every* tool in the request and would discard user-defined function tools and model-specific schemas (`web_search_preview`, Anthropic `computer`) whenever the embedding decided they weren't a semantic match — partitions tools by `mcp__` name prefix at `hook.py:248-264`, short-circuits the hook entirely when no MCP tools are present (saving an embeddings round-trip), passes only `mcp_tools` to `filter.filter_tools`, and reconstructs ordering via the new `_recombine_preserving_order` helper at `:131-159` that anchors the filtered MCP block at the position of the first MCP tool in the input and absorbs subsequent MCP slots; the filter-stats header shifts from `original_tool_count→filtered_count` to `mcp_count→filtered_count` so dashboards measuring filter ratio stop being polluted by pass-through counts — wants an integration test combining the `_should_expand_mcp_tools` `litellm_proxy` expansion path with the new partitioning, a mixed-tool ordering pin test, and the `"mcp__"` prefix exposed as a module-level constant (or imported from the gateway that assigns it) so the partitioning predicate stays in sync. #26578 fixes #26191 by replacing `await self.db.disconnect()` in both `recreate_prisma_client` (`prisma_client.py:218-235`) and `_do_direct_reconnect` (`utils.py:4134-4145`) with a direct `SIGTERM`-then-`SIGKILL` of the prisma query-engine PID — `disconnect()` ultimately calls a synchronous `process.wait()` that blocks the asyncio event loop for 30-120s when the DB is unreachable, long enough for `/health/liveliness` to fail and the orchestrator to restart the pod (defeating self-heal entirely); the regression-prevention test idiom `mock_prisma.disconnect.side_effect = AssertionError("disconnect would block the event loop")` paired with `disconnect.assert_not_called()` is excellent — any future "helpful" reintroduction of `disconnect()` fails fast with an assertion message that explains exactly why the call is forbidden. At block/goose, #8857 adds a declarative `preserves_thinking` flag to `DeclarativeProviderConfig` (`config/declarative_providers.rs:82-95`) so JSON-defined Anthropic-engine providers can opt into preserving upstream thinking blocks across turns without a Rust subclass, plus a new `AnthropicFormatOptions { preserve_unsigned_thinking, preserve_thinking_context }` struct (`formats/anthropic.rs:42-68`) with a `for_model` resolver that lets per-model `request_params` and `ANTHROPIC_PRESERVE_THINKING_CONTEXT` env vars override the provider-level flag and *forces* `preserve_unsigned_thinking = true` whenever `preserve_thinking_context` is true (the `|| preserve_thinking_context` invariant at `:62` — preserved context without preserved unsigned-thinking would be incoherent); the wire-format work softens `response_to_message` from `ok_or_else("Missing thinking signature")` to `unwrap_or_default()` symmetrically with the streaming path, and ships a built-in Z.AI provider (`declarative/zai.json`) that uses the new flag against the GLM-4.x/4.5/4.6/4.7/5.x model family — wants a test pinning the `preserve_thinking_context + ThinkingType::Adaptive` interaction (the `clear_thinking: false` injection at `:584-585` and the `output_config: {effort}` adaptive arm don't obviously compose into a wire-format Anthropic accepts) and a round-trip regression test for the `#[serde(skip)]` `format_options` field since reconstructing an `AnthropicProvider` through the inventory layer would silently default to `preserves_thinking: false`.

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24588 pure +1/−1 docs typo fix removing duplicated "Abilita Abilita" from the Italian CLI env-var table with no semantics or schema change and matching the surrounding parallel-style convention; BerriAI/litellm #26578 targeted SIGTERM-instead-of-disconnect fix for the event-loop-blocking `process.wait()` in prisma-client-py's `disconnect()` path that was causing 30-120s liveness-probe failures during DB outages, with the load-bearing regression-prevention test idiom `disconnect.side_effect = AssertionError("disconnect would block the event loop")` + `disconnect.assert_not_called()` pinning the inverted contract so any future "helpful" reintroduction fails fast with an explanatory message), 6 merge-after-nits (openai/codex #19792 well-scoped MultiAgentV2 thread-cap migration with three pin tests covering default-counts-root, rejects-legacy-coexistence, and cap-1-disallows-subagents and the right `saturating_sub(1)` + `InvalidInput` fail-loud contract but `CodexErr::AgentLimitReached.max_threads` field semantics now mean `cap−1` and need a rename or doc comment, the dropped `agents.max_concurrent_threads_per_session` alias deserves a one-release deprecation warning rather than silent drop, and the `with_max_concurrent_threads_per_session(None)` feature-off branch needs a quick consumer audit since the previous `Some(_)` may have meant "cap is enforced" downstream; openai/codex #19761 well-scoped hidden macOS-only DeviceCheck probe with the right security posture — `0o600` file permission, `#[serde(skip_serializing)]` on `token_base64`, dynamic `dlopen`/`NSClassFromString` framework loading instead of linking, 30-second `dispatch_semaphore_wait` timeout, structured non-macOS fallback — but `token_base64` is `pub` so a third-party serializer bypasses the `skip_serializing` guard (should be `pub(crate)` or wrapped in a zeroize/`Secret` newtype) and the `objc_msgSend` typed-cast call sites need a one-line ABI comment flagging the `objc_msgSend_stret` requirement on x86_64 for future struct-returning selectors; sst/opencode #24591 correct simplification deleting the desktop-only MessageNav sidebar/single-message branch from the share page and removing the `firstUserMessage()!.id!` crash-on-non-user-led-sessions assertion as a free side-effect, but the silent loss of deep-link `store.messageId` focus and the side-by-side `SessionReview` pane on desktop deserves explicit acknowledgment in the PR body before merge along with either a confirmation that `turns()` scroll-into-views on `store.messageId` or a small `createEffect` follow-up; QwenLM/qwen-code #3668 well-tested opt-in cost estimator with the right `hasBilling` short-circuit gating, `authType:model > model` precedence for proxy-vs-direct pricing, and end-to-end pricing-math pin test, but the hardcoded `$` currency prefix doesn't generalize to `JPY`/`EUR` (should route through `Intl.NumberFormat`), `discounts` semantics is a multiplier not a discount and `0.25` means "pay 25% of list" rather than "discount by 25%" (rename to `priceMultipliers` or document explicitly), and the `cachedInput → input` fallback silently bills cached input at *more* than reality and should either be dropped or surface a UI warning; BerriAI/litellm #26580 correct partition-by-`mcp__`-prefix fix for the silent-drop correctness bug where `SemanticToolFilterHook` was discarding user-defined function tools and model-specific schemas through the embedding filter, with the right "anchor at first MCP slot, absorb subsequent slots" recombination and a free latency win from short-circuiting when no MCP tools are present, but wants the `_should_expand_mcp_tools` + partitioning interaction covered end-to-end, a mixed-tool interleaved-ordering pin test, and the `"mcp__"` prefix exposed as a module-level constant; block/goose #8857 well-architected declarative `preserves_thinking` flag with the principled `|| preserve_thinking_context` invariant forcing `preserve_unsigned_thinking = true` and the symmetric parse-side/emit-side relaxation of missing-signature handling for non-canonical-Anthropic providers, but the `preserve_thinking_context + ThinkingType::Adaptive` interaction at `anthropic.rs:584-585` will inject both `output_config: {effort}` and `thinking: {clear_thinking: false}` simultaneously which Anthropic's wire format may not accept, and the `#[serde(skip)]` `format_options` field on `AnthropicProvider` will silently default to `preserves_thinking: false` if any code path reconstructs the provider through the inventory layer rather than `Self::from_config`).

### W17 drip-108 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At sst/opencode, #24595 is a +0/−1 surgical deletion at `packages/opencode/src/session/llm.ts:381` removing the unconditional `"User-Agent": \`opencode/${InstallationVersion}\`` injection from the default-headers branch so that `...input.model.headers` and `...headers` spreads at `:382-383` can finally win as the spread order already implies — the pre-fix code put a default *inside* the conditional object literal that callers couldn't see in any config and couldn't fully override, and the fix lets per-provider header configs control UA cleanly with no behavior surface expansion, but a one-assertion regression-pin ("given empty `model.headers` and no caller `headers`, outgoing bag has no `User-Agent`") and a one-line PR-body note about the version-attribution side-effect for self-hosted operators scraping provider logs would lock the contract. At openai/codex, #19797 is a +1500/−352 substantive refactor threading the codex runtime HTTP client through the entire MCP OAuth login + discovery surface — adds `pub fn http_client_for_server(config, runtime_environment) -> Result<Arc<dyn HttpClient>>` at `codex-mcp/src/mcp/auth.rs:355-358` re-exported from `codex-mcp::lib`, gives `_with_client` siblings to `discover_supported_scopes`/`perform_oauth_login_silent`/`perform_oauth_login_return_url` so all four call sites flow through the same proxy/TLS/trace-context-aware client (closing a real corp-proxy/custom-CA gap where ad-hoc `reqwest::Client` instances were cutting fresh per-call), and pins the new contract with three unit tests at `auth.rs:399-460` covering local-environment-fallback/default-environment-success/remote-environment-error plus a +309-line integration test at `rmcp-client/tests/streamable_http_remote_oauth.rs` against the in-tree `test_streamable_http_server` binary — but the `default_environment().unwrap_or(local)` env-resolution snippet is duplicated five-fold across `codex_message_processor.rs:5961`/`plugin_mcp_oauth.rs:23,107`/`mcp_cmd.rs:323,417`/`mcp_skill_dependencies.rs:128` (should be one `EnvironmentManager::default_or_local(cwd)` method), error handling is asymmetric (CMP arm returns JSON-RPC error with code, plugin arm just `warn!`s and `continue`s with no `ServerNotification` so the TUI never learns), and the `compute_auth_statuses` public-iterator-API gets a new `runtime_environment` parameter that breaks external library consumers without a CHANGELOG line. At BerriAI/litellm, #26585 is a 5-line minimum-surface fix for a silent-drop bug in `tool_permission.py:683-691`'s `async_post_call_streaming_iterator_hook` where the `if not tool_calls:` branch did a bare `return` inside an `async def` generator (yielding nothing → clients received only `data: [DONE]` with no content frames) — the fix wraps `assembled_model_response` in `MockResponseIterator` and re-emits chunks before the return, with an excellent regression test at `:492-532` whose load-bearing docstring "Hook must yield at least one chunk for plain-text responses; got none — bare return bug" pins the inverted contract by name so any future "simplification" PR that yanks the re-emit fails fast with an explanatory message; #26583 is a +106/−0 route-enum gap fix at `litellm/proxy/_types.py:586-590,729-733` adding `/spend/logs/v2` and `/spend/logs/ui/{request_id}` to `spend_tracking_routes` plus all five `/spend/logs/*` routes to `proxy_admin_view_only_routes` so the `PROXY_ADMIN_VIEW_ONLY` role (whose description states "view all spend across the platform") can actually load the admin Logs UI page that was 403'ing on every grid cell pre-fix, with two parametrized positive tests at `test_route_checks.py:1198-1297` covering all five routes for the viewer role and `/spend/logs/v2`+`/spend/logs/ui/{id}` parity for `INTERNAL_USER`/`INTERNAL_USER_VIEW_ONLY` (handler-shared with already-allowed `/spend/logs/ui`) — the per-row data filter `_can_user_view_spend_log` enforces user-level scoping separately so widening the route enum doesn't expand data visibility, just route reachability. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #25963 is a 5-line behavior fix at `mcp-client.ts:2274-2293` adding `expandEnvVars(arg, expansionEnv)` to `mcpServerConfig.args` before `StdioClientTransport` spawn (was silently passing `${VAR}` literals through to Docker invocations of the form `args: ["run", "--env", "DISCORD_TOKEN=${DISCORD_TOKEN}"]`), with a free side-effect bug fix at `:2290` switching `xcrun mcpbridge` detection from `mcpServerConfig.args?.includes('mcpbridge')` to `expandedArgs.includes('mcpbridge')` so templated bridge names don't skip the `XcodeMcpBridgeFixTransport` adapter wrap — pinned by a 30-line test at `:2160-2189` with `try/finally` env restoration but missing coverage of the `xcrun mcpbridge` predicate fix and the unset-env-var fallback semantics (load-bearing for security: an empty-string substitution into `--env DISCORD_TOKEN=` would silently send blank credentials to Docker); #25957 is a +47/−25 architectural refactor moving "hook fired a system message → display in chat history" from a return-value side-channel (where every caller of `fireSessionStartEvent` had to remember to pull `result.systemMessage` and call `historyManager.addItem`) to a single `CoreEvent.HookSystemMessage` subscriber — three coordinated edits: (1) `events.ts:355` switches `emit` to `_emitOrQueue` so events fired during early bootstrap before `AppContainer` mounts are queued and replayed (load-bearing for the refactor's safety), (2) `AppContainer.tsx:498-507` removes the inline `if (result.systemMessage)` branch that was double-emitting, (3) `clearCommand.ts:55-75` removes the same byte-identical block, with the test at `AppContainer.test.tsx:3580-3622` locating the registered handler via `mockCoreEvents.on.mock.calls.find` and asserting a synthetic payload reaches `addItem` with the new `source: 'test-hook'` field that wasn't previously carried — but missing a bootstrap-ordering pin test for the `_emitOrQueue` queue-then-replay contract, an unmount-unsubscribe assertion (repeated mounts add stale subscribers), and confirmation that `MessageType.INFO` renderers actually read the new `source` field; #25950 is a 5-line behavior fix at `FileCommandLoader.ts:152-175` adding `if (!storage.isWorkspaceHomeDir())` gate around the workspace-commands dir push so `cwd === $HOME` doesn't scan `~/.gemini/commands/` twice (once tagged `USER_FILE`, once tagged `WORKSPACE_FILE`) and produce phantom "command conflict" warnings shadowing every user-file command with a duplicate workspace-file entry, with a regression test at `:317-344` mocking `getProjectRoot` to return `homedir()` and asserting both `commands.length === 2` (not 4) and `commands.every(c => c.kind === 'user-file')` — but `isWorkspaceHomeDir()` is consumed without being shown so symlink/realpath/case-sensitivity safety needs confirmation, and the `homedir` import added to `FileCommandLoader.test.ts:9` from `@google/gemini-cli-core` assumes a re-export that isn't visible in the diff. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3673 is a +2154/−12 substantial new feature surface adding opt-in `autoSkill` background project-skill extraction — six structural pieces: (1) +460-line design doc at `docs/design/skill-nudge/skill-nudge.md`, (2) settings schema entry at `settingsSchema.ts:1123-1133` with `Config.getAutoSkillEnabled()` accessor and feature gate at `client.ts:692-694`, (3) `MemoryManager` extension at `manager.ts:114-185` adding a `scheduleSkillReview` task type and `'reviewing-skills'` `MemoryTaskStatus`, (4) new +240-line `skillReviewAgentPlanner.ts` planner module producing structured `SkillReviewByAgentResult` proposals, (5) new +261-line `SkillManageTool` at `tools/skill-manage.ts` registered in `agent-core.ts:339` so the planner agent can create/update/delete skills, (6) +436-line integration test at `skillReviewNudge.integration.test.ts` end-to-end pinning manager → planner → tool → nudge — architecturally sound (planner-agent/persistence-tool split is the right separation, gated default-off has near-zero off-path overhead, settings schema parity with `vscode-ide-companion/schemas/settings.schema.json`) but introduces three cross-cutting concerns deserving maintainer alignment before merge: (1) no cost-attribution surface for the background agent's token consumption (pairs poorly with #3668's billing work), (2) `SkillManageTool` deletes skills without a recovery path or trash directory (planner could be jailbroken via conversation content to unlink user skills), (3) trigger cadence/dismiss-count loop not visible — if users dismiss every nudge the feature should stop pestering them.
