Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (BerriAI/litellm #26585 minimum-surface 5-line `MockResponseIterator` re-emit fix for the bare-`return`-in-async-generator silent-drop bug with regression test whose docstring pins the failure mode by name; BerriAI/litellm #26583 schema-level route-enum gap fix with parametrized positive tests across all five `/spend/logs/*` routes for the affected viewer roles, well-anchored to the role-description contract and per-row data filter enforced separately downstream), 5 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24595 right deletion of a misplaced default-`User-Agent` injection that the trailing `...input.model.headers`/`...headers` spreads were already supposed to win against, but wants a one-assertion regression pin and a one-line operator-side note about losing version-attribution in provider logs; openai/codex #19797 architecturally correct end-to-end refactor threading the runtime HTTP client through MCP OAuth discovery + login closing a real corp-proxy/custom-CA gap with three new unit tests + a +309-line streamable-http integration test, but the env-resolution snippet is duplicated five-fold across call sites and the plugin-OAuth-env-failure path silently `warn!`s+`continue`s with no `ServerNotification` for the TUI; google-gemini/gemini-cli #25963 right minimal threading of `expandEnvVars(arg, expansionEnv)` through `mcpServerConfig.args` plus a free `xcrun mcpbridge` detection fix using `expandedArgs.includes('mcpbridge')`, but missing a test for the predicate fix and a security-relevant pin for the unset-env-var fallback (empty-string-substitution into `--env DISCORD_TOKEN=` silently sends blank credentials); google-gemini/gemini-cli #25957 right architectural refactor lifting duplicated `result.systemMessage`-handling at two call sites into a `CoreEvent.HookSystemMessage` subscriber with the load-bearing `_emitOrQueue` switch correctly identifying the bootstrap-ordering trap, but missing a queue-then-replay pin test for `_emitOrQueue`, an unmount-unsubscribe assertion, and renderer-reads-`source` confirmation; google-gemini/gemini-cli #25950 right minimal source-dedup gate at the FileCommandLoader pushing project-commands-dir scanning behind `!storage.isWorkspaceHomeDir()` so `cwd === $HOME` doesn't double-scan and produce phantom-conflict warnings, but `isWorkspaceHomeDir()` is consumed without being shown so symlink/realpath safety needs confirmation), 0 request-changes, 1 needs-discussion (QwenLM/qwen-code #3673 substantial new opt-in `autoSkill` background extraction subsystem with sound architecture and heavy unit-test coverage but three cross-cutting concerns to align on with maintainers before merge: missing cost-attribution for background agent token spend, `SkillManageTool` deletion has no recovery path or trash dir, trigger cadence/dismiss-count loop unaddressed).

### W17 drip-109 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At sst/opencode, #24520 restructures the release Dockerfile to support both alpine (default) and ubuntu base images via a `BASE_IMAGE` build arg, extracts a shared publish helper to reduce duplication between the two image-build paths, and pushes a new `:latest` tag — addresses real user pain where the alpine/musl image breaks PyTorch ROCm and other glibc-dependent native deps; wants explicit PR-body call-out of the new `:latest` semantics, `${version}-ubuntu` and `${Script.channel}-ubuntu` pin-tags so ubuntu users don't get silently floated to newer base images, a `&& rm -rf /tmp/dist` cleanup at the install `RUN` block, and a Dockerfile comment documenting the supported `BASE_IMAGE` values. At openai/codex, #19776 is the final mechanical cleanup in the #19772–#19775 thread-sessions-as-profiles stack, deleting a redundant in-memory cached authority that the upstream PRs already replaced — every removal is type-system-enforced, the one fixture flip is the right one, and `thread_read_session_state_does_not_reuse_primary_permission_profile` correctly pins the cwd-binding discipline that the new profiles-shaped storage carries; #19764 flips agent-identity JWT validation from a `decode_unverified_payload`-and-trust posture to full JWKS-key-fetch + issuer/audience/expiry verification with four pin tests covering verify-success / untrusted-kid / missing-iss-or-aud / payload-only-fallback — the right security posture for an identity claim, but the `iat`/`exp` fields are typed `usize` and will ceiling on 32-bit targets, the embedded RSA private key in the test fixture should carry a `// TEST KEY ONLY — do not extract` banner comment, and a JWKS-caching follow-up should be filed before the full agent-identity stack ships so verification doesn't become a per-validation network round trip. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3677 fixes a streaming-parser correctness bug where MiniMax models emit thinking tags inline in `content` rather than in a separate `reasoning_content` field — adds a provider-gated `responseParsingOptions.parseThinkingTags` opt-in flag, threads a stateful `ThinkingTagParser` through the existing `RequestContext` so partial `<think>` tags split across SSE chunks are buffered correctly, flushes any unterminated tag on `finish_reason` so trailing thinking content isn't silently dropped, and bundles a genuinely-orthogonal CLI blank-leading-chunk fix that's justified because both bugs surface together for MiniMax users — wants a model-name conjunct on `isMiniMaxProvider` (host-only matching will misfire on proxies), a stateful-vs-stateless parser parity pin test, a focused finish-reason-empty-content flush test, and a one-line tradeoff comment on `stripLeadingBlankLines`'s regex (eats legitimate empty model output). At BerriAI/litellm, #26584 bundles two correct OAuth fixes for Azure Entra discovery: removes an over-eager `must_challenge=True` from the unauthorized-scopes path so resource-scopes-hint clients don't get stuck in challenge loops, and adds an Entra-specific synthesis fallback when the discovery endpoint returns an incomplete metadata document (synthesizing the standard `authorize`/`token`/`jwks_uri` from the tenant's `iss`) gated by a tight `login.microsoftonline.com`-only netloc guard — wants a trace of the `metadata.scopes is None` × resource-scopes-hint interaction to confirm scopes aren't silently dropped on the synthesis path, the netloc guard widened to cover `login.microsoftonline.us` / `login.partner.microsoftonline.cn` (or the limitation explicitly documented), and the PR title/body updated to reflect that the must-challenge fix is broader than just Azure. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #25958 fixes a real CLI-relaunch UX bug in `relaunchAppInChildProcess` where SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP delivered to the wrapper parent during a child re-launch were swallowed by the parent's handler and never forwarded to the child — installs explicit `process.on(sig, () => child.kill(sig))` forwarding for the three signals, pinned by a real handler-invocation test that registers the spies and asserts forwarded `kill()` arity, but bundles four `console.log` debug lines in `generate-notices.js` that should be gated behind a `--verbose` flag or removed before merge, only one of the three signals is parametrically tested (the other two are byte-identical handlers — `it.each` is one line), and the redundant `if (!child.killed)` guard inside the handler needs either a comment explaining the race or removal; #25977 supersedes #23105 by relocating the `package.json` version read from the (blocking-I/O) render path to extension-load time and carrying the result on the in-memory `LoadedExtension` object — defensive synchronous helper at load time, additive `packageJsonVersion?: string` struct field that doesn't break downstream consumers, complete branch coverage on both unit and integration levels including a test-utility extension that's optional-additive; the nits (eslint-disable comment for the `readFileSync`, public-API docstring on the new struct field, mock-vs-real loader tradeoff in the integration test) are documentation polish rather than correctness blockers; #26040 is a Windows-sandbox-detection fix bundling an arguably-orthogonal `disableUserInput` default flip — auto-detects `'windows-native'` as a recognized sandbox command in `sandbox-config.ts` so Windows users with a configured sandbox are no longer forced through the no-sandbox branch, adds a `!isInSandbox` guard at `browserManager.ts` and `registry.ts` so the input-blocker is never installed when the agent is already containerized (defense-in-depth), and flips the `disableUserInput` config default from `false` to `true` — wants verification that `'windows-native'` is actually a recognized sandbox command downstream (smoke test if not), explicit motivation for the two-fix bundling in the PR body, a release-notes entry for the user-visible default flip, and a jsdoc clarification on headless-mode behavior.

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (openai/codex #19776 final mechanical cleanup in the #19772–#19775 thread-sessions-as-profiles stack with every removal type-system-enforced and the cwd-binding discipline preserved by `thread_read_session_state_does_not_reuse_primary_permission_profile`; google-gemini/gemini-cli #25977 architecturally sound supersession of #23105 fixing the reviewer-bot's three blocking-I/O complaints by relocating the `package.json` version read to extension-load time with a defensive helper, additive struct field that doesn't break downstream consumers, and complete unit + integration coverage including an optional-additive test-utility extension), 6 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24520 correct Dockerfile restructure introducing `BASE_IMAGE` build arg + shared publish helper closing real musl/glibc PyTorch-ROCm pain but wants explicit `:latest`-tag call-out, `${version}-ubuntu`/`${Script.channel}-ubuntu` pin-tags, `&& rm -rf /tmp/dist` cleanup, and a `BASE_IMAGE` doc comment; openai/codex #19764 right security-posture flip from `decode_unverified_payload`-and-trust to full JWKS-verify-with-issuer-audience-expiry pinned by four meaningful branch tests but `iat`/`exp` `usize` types ceiling on 32-bit, the embedded test RSA private key needs a banner comment, and a JWKS-caching follow-up should be filed before the full agent-identity stack ships; QwenLM/qwen-code #3677 right architectural shape with provider-gated opt-in `responseParsingOptions`, stateful streaming `ThinkingTagParser` threaded through `RequestContext`, finish-reason flush on the streaming path, and a justified-bundling CLI blank-chunk fix but wants a model-name conjunct on `isMiniMaxProvider` (host-only matching misfires on proxies), a stateful-vs-stateless parser parity pin test, a focused finish-reason-empty-content flush test, and a one-line tradeoff comment on `stripLeadingBlankLines`'s regex; BerriAI/litellm #26584 two correct Entra OAuth fixes (must-challenge removal + discovery-synthesis fallback) bundled in one PR with complete branch coverage on the three new paths and an appropriately-tight host-and-path guard, but wants a trace of `metadata.scopes is None` × resource-scopes-hint to confirm scopes aren't silently dropped, netloc guard widened to `login.microsoftonline.us`/`login.partner.microsoftonline.cn` (or limitation documented), and PR title/body updated to reflect the broader-than-Azure must-challenge fix; google-gemini/gemini-cli #25958 correct minimal SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP-forwarding fix in `relaunchAppInChildProcess` pinned by a real handler-invocation test, but bundles four unconditional `console.log` debug lines in `generate-notices.js` that should be gated/removed, only one of three signals is parametrically tested (`it.each` would cover the other two in one line), and the redundant `if (!child.killed)` guard needs a comment or removal; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26040 correct Windows-sandbox auto-detect for `'windows-native'` plus right defensive `!isInSandbox` guard at `browserManager.ts`/`registry.ts` but bundles a user-visible `disableUserInput` default flip that needs explicit acknowledgment, downstream verification that `'windows-native'` is actually a recognized sandbox command, a release-notes entry, and a jsdoc clarification on headless-mode behavior), 0 request-changes, 0 needs-discussion.

### W17 drip-110 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos. At sst/opencode, #24486 continues kitlangton's strangler migration of legacy Hono session routes onto the schema-checked Effect HttpApi surface — bridges the five mutating session lifecycle endpoints (`POST /session` create, `DELETE /session/:sessionID`, `PATCH /session/:sessionID` update title/permission/archived, `POST /session/:sessionID/fork`, `POST /session/:sessionID/abort`) into `SessionApi` with `UpdatePayload` + `ForkPayload` schemas derived from canonical types via `Struct.omit(Session.ForkInput.fields, ["sessionID"])` (the right "URL is the source of truth for `sessionID`" pattern), the spec checklist at `packages/opencode/specs/effect/http-api.md:294-296` flips item-9 to `[x]` in lockstep, OpenAPI annotations namespace identifiers correctly (`session.create`/`session.delete`/`session.update`/`session.fork`/`session.abort`) with `session.delete`'s description correctly noting the cascade — but the `time.archived: Schema.optional(Schema.Number)` in `UpdatePayload` can't distinguish "leave alone" from "un-archive" (should be `Schema.NullishOr(Schema.Number)` with documented `null=clear` semantics for a `PATCH` surface), the `abort` endpoint's success type should be `Schema.Boolean` to mirror `remove` (aborting an already-completed run is a status not a fresh `Info`), and the `Struct.omit(...)` body-derivation pattern is worth extracting into a tiny named helper before the next stack PR replicates it. #24484 bridges the three workspace sync endpoints (`POST /sync/start`/`/replay`/`/history`) onto a new `SyncApi` group via a +130-line `httpapi/sync.ts` and ships a real bonus bugfix at `httpapi/mcp.ts:139-145` rewriting `mcp.add` from `Schema.decodeUnknownSync(AddPayload)(ctx.payload)` (which would `throw` synchronously inside an `Effect.fn` and surface as an unhelpful 500) to `Schema.decodeUnknownEffect(StatusMap)(...).pipe(Effect.mapError(() => new HttpApiError.BadRequest({})))` — the framework decoder runs from the endpoint's `payload:` annotation so the manual decode was redundant, net less-code-and-better-errors; `httpapi/server.ts:21,77` registers `SyncApi`/`syncHandlers` between Provider and Workspace alphabetically, the spec-table `sync` row at `:184` flips from `later` to `bridged` and checklist items at `:283-285` + implementation-order item 7 at `:356` flip in lockstep — the only nit is that `httpapi/sync.ts` does direct SQL composition (`Database, asc, and, eq, lte, not, or` from `@/storage` plus `EventTable` from `@/sync/event.sql`) instead of delegating to a `Sync.history()` service method, which is a deliberate "keep the bridge thin" choice but worth lifting into `Sync` proper before `/sync/replay` grows since 130 lines is right at the boundary where extraction starts to pay for itself. At openai/codex, #19751 adds an opaque `IdentityKey` newtype (newtype-of-`Vec<u8>` with `from_bytes`/`from_os_string`/`as_bytes`/`into_bytes`) that the standalone `app-server` and `codex app-server` accept via `--identity-key`, threads it through startup → `CodexMessageProcessorArgs` → `RemoteThreadStore` and forwards it as **binary** gRPC metadata when talking to the contract implementation — `cfg(unix)` `OsStrExt::as_bytes` for byte-perfect ingest with a load-bearing `identity_key_preserves_unix_argv_bytes` test pinning the `\x00\xff` regression so a future "let's just `String`-convert" refactor fails fast, no logging of the key value anywhere, and the header-name constant re-exported for downstream remote-contract integrators — but `--identity-key` puts the raw value in argv visible via `ps`/`/proc/$pid/cmdline` so a `--identity-key-file <path>` companion is the standard cure if downstream ever treats this as a secret, the `cfg(not(unix))` branch's `to_string_lossy().into_owned().into_bytes()` lossily round-trips and creates a Windows/Linux contract divergence on the same input string (refuse non-UTF-8 on Windows or document the divergence), `#[derive(Debug)]` on `IdentityKey` writes raw bytes to logs if anyone ever `dbg!`s it (manual `Debug` impl printing `IdentityKey(<{N} bytes>)` is the right cure), and the `identity_key: _identity_key` underscored binding in `codex_message_processor.rs:775` should carry a `// TODO(stack): consumed by #19752` comment so readers don't think it's dead code. #19762 is the plumbing-only PR-1 of a 3-PR stack converting `AuthManager::shared_from_config(...)` and `AuthManager::reload()` from sync to async across 24 files (`app-server`, `chatgpt`, `cli`, `cloud-requirements`, `cloud-tasks`, `core`, `exec`, `login`, `mcp-server`, `models-manager`, `tui`) — explicitly disclaims behavior change, the four `auth_manager.reload()` call sites in `app-server/src/codex_message_processor.rs:1356,1507,1615,1751` all became `.reload().await`, the three `AuthManager::shared_from_config(...)` call sites in `app-server/src/{in_process.rs:392,lib.rs:471,633,714}` got `.await` appended, and the stack ordering (async surface → eager AgentIdentity runtime load → JWKS-verified JWTs) is the textbook "do the boring plumbing in its own PR so the security-claim PR's diff stays small enough to actually review" — but wide async-conversion PRs are easier to review when paired with a `cargo deny`-style CI grep that *requires* the new shape (a deny rule failing if `shared_from_config(` appears without `.await` on the same statement prevents regressions on backports), and one Loom/`tokio::time::sleep`-injecting test would prove no caller holds a sync mutex across the new `.await` point (the kind of bug only visible under load), plus a quick `git grep -n 'allow(unused_must_use)'` on the touched test files settles whether any test silently dropped a `.await` and got a "future not awaited" warning suppressed. At BerriAI/litellm, #26591 is a 3-line backfill in `_merge_hidden_params_from_response_into_metadata` (`litellm_logging.py:1728-1731`) for the streaming-path bug where `metadata.hidden_params.response_cost` was `None` even though the cost had already been computed and stored on `model_call_details["response_cost"]` — correctly ordered (read both first, then guard on both being meaningful before mutating) and using `is None` not `not response_cost` so a legitimate `0.0` cost (free-tier embedding, cached hit) isn't re-backfilled, with two complementary tests (`backfills_response_cost`: model_call_details `0.002` + response `None` → final `0.002`; `preserves_response_cost`: model_call_details `0.002` + response `0.001` → final `0.001` provider-wins) where the *negative* preservation test is the load-bearing one preventing a future "simplification" from overwriting real provider-supplied costs, and both tests also assert `model_id` is preserved to lock the regression to "response_cost backfill" specifically. #26589 fixes a real correctness bug — Responses API (`/v1/responses`) wasn't running cost calculation across 60+ providers (per author: 91% of requests showing `$0.00`, breaking budget enforcement and audit) — by adding `_calculate_response_cost(model, usage_dict, response_obj)` to `litellm/llms/openai/responses/transformation.py:39-110` that maps Responses-API field names (`input_tokens`/`output_tokens`/`input_tokens_details`) to `Usage` shape (`prompt_tokens`/`completion_tokens`/`prompt_tokens_details`), resolves the per-provider string defensively (`provider_val.value if isinstance(provider_val, LlmProviders) else str(provider_val)`), calls `generic_cost_per_token(model, usage, custom_llm_provider, service_tier=None)`, and stores the result on `response_obj._hidden_params["response_cost"]` — hooked into `transform_response_api_response:312-315` correctly after `ResponsesAPIResponse.model_construct(...)` (so `_hidden_params` exists) and before `additional_headers` (so cost is visible to header construction); but blocking on three issues: (1) `service_tier=None` is a `TODO` left in production code on a *cost-correctness* PR, will over-bill `flex` users / under-bill `priority` users on every Responses API call (request body / kwargs already carry `service_tier` for completion calls — plumb it before merge), (2) the bottom `except Exception: pass` silently swallows every error to a `verbose_logger.error` so any pricing-data shape change (new `cached_tokens` field, new model without pricing, new `service_tier` value) silently returns `$0` cost — the exact failure mode that produced the original bug, at minimum increment a `litellm.cost_calculation_errors` counter so deployments can alert, (3) `Usage(**mapped_usage)` drops fields neither in the four known shapes nor the two `_details` blocks so emerging `cache_creation_input_tokens` / `reasoning_tokens` will be invisible to cost calculation. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #25801 is a +1/−1 fix at `packages/cli/src/ui/commands/clearCommand.ts:18` renaming the canonical slash-command name from the placeholder `'clear (new)'` (with literal space and parens — almost certainly leftover from a "new clear behavior" rollout) to `'clear'` so typing `/clear` actually matches the documented command instead of forcing users onto the alias `/new` or the literal `/clear (new)`; `altNames: ['new']` already provides the alias so no behavioral regression is possible and the surface after the fix matches user expectation, command-palette autocomplete, and almost certainly the docs — worth a defensive `git grep 'clear (new)'` for any test/snapshot/doc asserting on the old literal, and a one-line invariant unit-test (`every SlashCommand.name matches /^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$/`) added as a follow-up would prevent the next placeholder-shipped bug. #25753 bundles four concurrent CI-stability changes (default integration tests to Flash Preview model in `test-utils/src/test-rig.ts:14-17,478-482`, skip the entire `Interactive Mode` describe block in `context-compress-interactive.test.ts:11-21` on `process.platform === 'darwin'` with a long comment citing four CI run IDs of flake, loosen `file-system.test.ts:137-139` to `expect(content.trim()).toBe('hello')`, rewrite two `plan-mode.test.ts:84-88,200-204` test prompts to be more model-deterministic) — two of the four are healthy (Flash default, tolerant whitespace) but two are symptom suppression: the `describe.skipIf(skipOnDarwin)` removes the only signal the project has that interactive mode works on macOS (the right cure is fixing the pty buffer capture to filter the startup escape sequences `q4;?m...true color warning` before `expectText` runs; the pragmatic-but-wrong cure is what this ships), and the "Treat this as a Directive and write the file immediately without proposing strategy or asking for confirmation" prompt rewrites train the test on a single model's instruction-following idiosyncrasies so when Flash Preview rotates these prompts may stop working in a way that's hard to diagnose because the test name says "Plan Mode" but the actual assertion is on a particular model's compliance pattern — should be split into three PRs (Flash default; Darwin skip + tracking issue + `RUN_FLAKY_DARWIN_INTERACTIVE=1` env override; plan-mode determinism + named `PROMPTS.PLAN_MODE_DETERMINISTIC_FILE_CREATE` constant) so each is separately reviewable and the Darwin-skip diagnosis (which cites run IDs that will eventually 404) gets copied into a tracking issue body where it survives.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24484 cleanly-scoped Effect-HttpApi sync-routes bridge mirroring the established stack pattern with the spec checklist + status table flipping `sync` from `later` to `bridged` in lockstep, plus a real bonus bugfix at `mcp.add` rewriting `Schema.decodeUnknownSync` to `Schema.decodeUnknownEffect` with `mapError -> HttpApiError.BadRequest` flipping a sync-throw-in-Effect-fn-becomes-500 into a typed 400; BerriAI/litellm #26591 minimum-surface 3-line backfill for the streaming-path `hidden_params.response_cost=None` bug with the load-bearing *negative* preservation test pinning provider-supplied costs against future "simplification" PRs and the right `is None` not-`not response_cost` guard so legitimate `0.0` costs aren't re-backfilled; google-gemini/gemini-cli #25801 trivially-correct +1/−1 placeholder-shipped fix renaming `'clear (new)'` to `'clear'` so the documented `/clear` actually matches with `altNames: ['new']` preserving the alias for zero behavioral regression), 3 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24486 right-shape Effect-HttpApi session-lifecycle bridge with canonical-type-derived schemas via `Struct.omit(Session.ForkInput.fields, ["sessionID"])` and well-namespaced OpenAPI identifiers but the `time.archived: Schema.optional(Schema.Number)` can't distinguish leave-alone from un-archive on a `PATCH` surface and the `abort` success type should be `Schema.Boolean` not `Session.Info`; openai/codex #19751 right opaque-identity-as-bytes shape with the load-bearing `identity_key_preserves_unix_argv_bytes` `\x00\xff` test, no key logging, and binary gRPC metadata forwarding but `--identity-key` puts the raw value in argv visible via `ps` so a `--identity-key-file <path>` companion is needed if treated as secret, the `cfg(not(unix))` lossy round-trip creates a Windows/Linux contract divergence, and `#[derive(Debug)]` writes raw bytes to logs; openai/codex #19762 mechanical 24-file plumbing-only async-conversion PR-1 of a 3-PR stack with correct ordering and verified all `auth_manager.reload()` + `AuthManager::shared_from_config(...)` call sites flip in lockstep, but wants a CI grep / `cargo deny` rule requiring the new shape on backports and one Loom-style test proving no caller holds a sync mutex across the new `.await` point), 1 request-changes (BerriAI/litellm #26589 right architectural placement closing a real 91%-of-requests-show-$0 cost-tracking gap across 60+ providers but blocking on three: `service_tier=None` is a `TODO` left in production on a cost-correctness PR that will over-bill `flex` / under-bill `priority` on every Responses API call, silent `except Exception: pass` recreates the original bug class on any pricing-shape change, and `Usage(**mapped_usage)` drops emerging `cache_creation_input_tokens`/`reasoning_tokens` fields invisible to cost calc), 1 needs-discussion (google-gemini/gemini-cli #25753 four concurrent CI-stability changes bundled as one PR — two healthy (Flash default, tolerant whitespace) but two are symptom suppression: `describe.skipIf(skipOnDarwin)` for the entire interactive describe removes the only macOS-interactive signal the project has when the right cure is fixing the pty buffer capture, and "Treat this as a Directive..." prompt rewrites train the test on a single model's instruction-following idiosyncrasies — should be split into three PRs each separately reviewable for cost/coverage/regression tradeoffs).

### W17 drip-111 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At sst/opencode, #24612 introduces a new `ModelCache` namespace and hits LM Studio's `/v1/models` endpoint at `Provider.get()` time to merge dynamically-loaded local models into the static snapshot — right shape (lazy per-providerID cache, fail-soft to snapshot when the endpoint is down), and the new `Bun.fetch(url, { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(10_000) })` at `model-cache.ts:1-86` bounds the worst case, but two surfaces are too generous: the synthesized `ModelInfo` literal at `:53-67` hard-codes `tool_call: true`, `cost: { input: 0, output: 0 }`, and `limit: { context: 128000, output: 4096 }` for every discovered model regardless of what LM Studio reports (a user running a 4k-context Qwen 1.5B locally gets truncation surprises because the runtime trusts `limit.context`), and the 10s first-call timeout sits inside `Provider.get()` so every caller pays up to 10s at boot when `lmstudio` is configured but the server is down (the `cache[providerID]` short-circuit at L9 only helps the second call); also `models.js:148-198` hard-codes the `"lmstudio"` providerID four times in the `lmstudioAllowed` gate while the same diff already adds Apertis to `model-cache.ts` so the next dynamic OpenAI-compat provider needs the same copy-paste — should extract a `resolveDynamicProviders(config)` helper and loop, plus a `log.warn("lmstudio configured but no models discovered", { baseURL })` when the gate short-circuits silently. At openai/codex, #19807 collapses the previous `IS_MACOS`/`IS_WINDOWS` cfg gates into a single `SUPPORTS_DESKTOP_APP = cfg!(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "windows"))` constant at `tooltips.rs:8-9`, threads it through the four tooltip-selection sites that previously asked only about macOS, and adds a 50/50 rotation between the plan-promo and app-promo on Free/Go plans for desktop-supported targets — fixes a real correctness bug where Free/Go-plan Windows users were seeing only the generic plan-promo and never the desktop-app-promo because the `paid_app_tooltip` path was the only one consulting `IS_WINDOWS`, with two locked-in table tests (`free_go_tooltip_rotates_between_plan_and_desktop_promos` asserting both strings are seen across 16 seeds when `supports_desktop_app=true` and `free_go_tooltip_skips_desktop_promo_on_other_platforms` asserting only `FREE_GO_TOOLTIP` is seen when false) and the previous `IS_MACOS`/`IS_WINDOWS` constants deleted entirely so they can't drift apart later — `OTHER_TOOLTIP_NON_MAC` is now misnamed (it's "non-desktop-app" not "non-Mac" since Windows goes through `OTHER_TOOLTIP`) but that's a follow-up rename, not a blocker. #19805 adds two new optional `MultiAgentV2Config` fields — `root_agent_usage_hint_text` and `subagent_usage_hint_text` — alongside the existing single `usage_hint_text`, and modifies the rollout-retain filter on thread fork at `agent/control.rs:401-432` to drop the parent's previously-injected hint developer messages so the child receives a fresh hint matching its *own* config (root vs subagent) instead of the parent's stale text — right shape, the new test `spawn_agent_can_fork_parent_thread_history_with_sanitized_items` at `control_tests.rs:595-680` correctly exercises the parent-derived branch via `configured_multi_agent_v2_usage_hint_texts().await`, but two concerns: the strip uses string equality on full hint text which is brittle (the no-parent fallback at L401-432 reaches into the *child's* `config.features.enabled(Feature::MultiAgentV2)` to compute the strip set, so if a user changed config between the parent thread's start and the fork, the child's config is wrong source for the *old* hint living in `forked_rollout_items`), and the `[ContentItem::InputText { text }] = content.as_slice()` single-element-slice match at L418-431 silently passes through any developer message with multiple content items today — should annotate hint-injection rollout items with a structural marker (e.g. a `multi_agent_v2_hint` annotation) rather than match on text equality, and add a comment at L418-422 explaining the single-text-item invariant. At BerriAI/litellm, #26598 is a two-line behavior fix at `aiohttp_transport.py:341-345` switching `headers=response.headers` (a string-decoded `CIMultiDict` that aiohttp will refuse to construct from non-ASCII bytes) to `headers=response.raw_headers` (bytes-tuples the upstream sent) plus the parallel `MaskedHTTPStatusError` switch at `http_handler.py:381-389` from iterating `response.headers.items()` (which decodes to strings) to `response.headers.raw` (which preserves bytes) so non-ASCII header values round-trip end-to-end — pinned by two real upstream tests (`test_preserves_non_ascii_response_headers` at `test_credential_leak_prevention.py:138-155` constructing a Chinese-text header value, wrapping a redacted-URL request in `MaskedHTTPStatusError`, and asserting both that the header round-trips intact AND that the URL secret is still redacted in the same assertion; `test_handle_async_request_preserves_non_ascii_response_headers` at `test_aiohttp_transport.py:265-308` building a `FakeSession.Resp` with both `headers={"X-Localized-Message": "本地化消息"}` AND `raw_headers=((b"X-Localized-Message", "本地化消息".encode("utf-8")),)` to mirror the live aiohttp shape and guarantee the transport pulls from `raw_headers` not the decoded dict) — wants a one-line comment at `aiohttp_transport.py:343` (`# raw_headers preserves bytes; response.headers is str-decoded and rejects non-ASCII`) so the next reader doesn't "fix" this back, and the parallel PR #26593 doing the same fix as a one-liner with no masking-path test coverage should be closed with a pointer. #26592 adds a step "5b" to `transform_anthropic_messages_request` at `anthropic_claude3_transformation.py:516-533` that walks `messages` and drops `{"type": "text", "text": ""}` (or whitespace-only) blocks from any assistant message whose `content` is a list, fixing a real upstream Bedrock invoke 400 ("The text field in the ContentBlock object is blank.") that surfaces from Claude's streamed responses where `content_block_start` emits an empty `text` placeholder before the first delta — the `not block.get("text", "").strip()` is the right whitespace-tolerant predicate (matches `""`, `" "`, `"\n"`), tool-use/tool-result/image/reasoning blocks pass through untouched because they don't have `type == "text"`, and `isinstance(msg.get("content"), list)` correctly skips string-content shorthand — but the role gate at `:518-519` is hard-coded to `"assistant"` only while the upstream Bedrock validator runs against *every* message in the array (so a user message assembled by a downstream caller from `[text, tool_result, text]` parts that includes an empty text block would still 400; LangChain/Vercel-AI bridges sometimes produce empty-text user parts when concatenating mixed-content turns), and there's no regression test in the PR even though the test scaffolding for the file already exists — should widen the role check to `("assistant", "user")` (or comment the asymmetry rationale) and add one parametrized pytest case constructing `[{"type":"text","text":""},{"type":"tool_use",...}]` to lock the regression door. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #25978 is a +2/−1 docs-only fix at `docs/cli/plan-mode.md:472-475` rewording the plan-mode session-retention paragraph that was instructing users to "search for **Session Retention**" in the `/settings` UI (a label that does not exist) to point at the actual labels **Enable Session Cleanup** and **Keep chat history** — `or` is the right connective because the two settings govern adjacent but distinct behavior (cleanup-on-exit vs. how-many-days-to-keep) and a user looking for "session retention" might want either, the link to `../cli/session-management.md#session-retention` is preserved unchanged so users following it still land in the right place, and the article-vs-no-article cadence (`/settings command` and `settings.json file`) stays consistent with the surrounding paragraph — net cost: 1 line added; net benefit: every user who searched "Session Retention" in `/settings` and found nothing now gets the right hint. #25966 hardens the GitHub Actions comment posted by `.github/workflows/eval-pr.yml:62-77` when steering changes are detected in a fork PR — relabels the title from "Action Required" to "[SECURITY WARNING]: Evaluation Approval Required", adds a "[CRITICAL]: Approving this run will execute code from the fork on Google infrastructure with access to the `GEMINI_API_KEY` secret" line, and ends with "**DO NOT APPROVE** unless you have fully reviewed all changes... A malicious PR can steal the API key or compromise the CI environment" — the textbook mitigation for "approval fatigue" CI patterns where a maintainer who sees "Action Required: Evaluation Approval" and clicks "Approve deployments" without inspecting the fork's `package.json`/`scripts:`/source changes is one PR away from leaking `GEMINI_API_KEY`, the trailing `<!-- eval-approval-notification -->` HTML comment marker is preserved so the existing `if comment_already_exists` dedup keeps working (without that marker this would post a duplicate comment on every workflow re-run); the one issue worth flagging is a whitespace regression — the new prose lines use 4-space indentation where the surrounding bash kept tabs, and bash heredoc-quoted strings are whitespace-preserving so the rendered Markdown will turn each indented line into a code block on GitHub — should re-check the rendered output on a draft PR before merge and strip leading 4 spaces if it renders as a code-block lump. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3671 is a 1100-line standalone design document (no implementation yet) at `docs/design/customize-banner-area/customize-banner-area.md` proposing three new settings (`ui.hideBanner`, `ui.customBannerTitle`, `ui.customAsciiArt`) for the CLI's startup banner with explicit doctrine that brand chrome (logo, brand text) is replaceable while operational data (version, auth/model line, working-directory line) is **locked** and cannot be hidden — the design philosophy is sound (the lock/replace boundary is the right cut), the ASCII region taxonomy diagram at `:42-58` gives names (A, B, B①/B②/B③) to regions that previously had to be referenced by code line numbers (future PRs about the banner will be much easier to review with this vocabulary), and the doc correctly keeps the screen-reader fallback (`config.getScreenReader()`) as the existing gate while framing the new `ui.hideBanner` as an additional parallel gate so accessibility behavior is preserved — but two things make this `needs-discussion` rather than `merge-after-nits`: the customization matrix at `:60-85` overcommits to "Locked" cells that real white-label users will push back on (enterprise white-label deployments will absolutely want to suppress the version suffix in screenshots, and the workaround `--version` only is not a substitute — a softer doctrine would be lock by default + expose a single `ui.minimalBanner: boolean` admin-only escape hatch), and the doc shipping standalone without even a sketch of the loader code or the schema update defers the question of which exact field name lands in `settings.json` to a follow-up that may pick a less-considered shape under sprint pressure (the three settings table at `:88-94` doesn't pin down whether `ui.customAsciiArt` is a string vs. an array of strings vs. a path to a file, what the expected width budget is, or whether `ui.hideBanner: true` also hides the `<Tips>` component); should pin the three accepted shapes for `ui.customAsciiArt` to concrete schema before this design unblocks implementation, soften the "Locked" stance on the version suffix to "discouraged but possible via an admin-only escape hatch", and add a "rejected alternatives" section (per-field hides, full-replace info-panel template, `--banner-config <path>` runtime flag) — a design doc without rejected alternatives is just a spec.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (openai/codex #19807 tight mechanical refactor collapsing `IS_MACOS`/`IS_WINDOWS` cfg gates into a single `SUPPORTS_DESKTOP_APP` constant fixing the Free/Go-plan Windows users seeing only generic plan-promo bug with two locked-in table tests covering both branches across 16 seeds and the previous OS-specific constants deleted entirely; google-gemini/gemini-cli #25978 +2/−1 docs-only fix correcting plan-mode session-retention instructions to point at the actually-existing `/settings` labels **Enable Session Cleanup** and **Keep chat history** instead of the nonexistent **Session Retention**; google-gemini/gemini-cli #25966 GitHub Actions eval-gate comment hardening relabeling "Action Required" to "[SECURITY WARNING]" and naming the `GEMINI_API_KEY` exfil failure mode at the point of decision — textbook approval-fatigue mitigation with the comment-dedup HTML marker preserved, single concern is a whitespace regression that may render as a code block on GitHub but verifiable on a draft PR), 4 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24612 right-shape lazy-fetched per-providerID `ModelCache` for LM Studio dynamic discovery with fail-soft snapshot fallback, but synthesized `ModelInfo` literal hard-codes `tool_call: true`/`cost: 0/0`/`limit.context: 128000` regardless of what LM Studio reports causing truncation surprises on small-context local quants, the 10s first-call timeout inside `Provider.get()` makes every caller pay up to 10s at boot when LM Studio is configured but down, and the `lmstudioAllowed` gate hard-codes `"lmstudio"` four times while Apertis is already added to `model-cache.ts` so a `resolveDynamicProviders(config)` helper extraction is overdue; openai/codex #19805 right strip-then-reinject pattern for the multi-agent v2 root-vs-subagent hint inheritance bug (subagent forked from root currently inherits the root's "you may delegate to subagents" line that doesn't apply) with a meaningful test exercising the parent-derived authoritative branch, but the no-parent fallback reaches into the child's own config to compute the strip set which is wrong if config changed between parent-thread-start and fork, and the `[ContentItem::InputText { text }] = content.as_slice()` single-element-slice match silently passes through any developer message with multiple content items so should match on a structural marker rather than text equality; BerriAI/litellm #26598 correct two-line transport fix routing aiohttp `raw_headers` (bytes-tuples) into httpx instead of the str-decoded `CIMultiDict` that rejects non-ASCII, with parallel `MaskedHTTPStatusError` switch to `headers.raw` and excellent paired tests one of which proves the credential-redaction invariant survives the byte-level header round-trip in the same assertion, but wants a one-line comment at the transport so the next reader doesn't "fix" it back and the parallel PR #26593 should be closed with a pointer; BerriAI/litellm #26592 correct minimum-surface filter dropping `{"type":"text","text":""}` blocks from assistant messages before Bedrock invoke transform-out, fixing the real upstream 400 from Claude's streamed `content_block_start` empty-text placeholders, but the role gate is hard-coded to `"assistant"` only while the Bedrock validator runs against every message in the array — a user message assembled by LangChain/Vercel-AI from `[text, tool_result, text]` parts with an empty text block would still 400 — and the PR ships no regression test even though scaffolding exists), 0 request-changes, 1 needs-discussion (QwenLM/qwen-code #3671 sound design philosophy with the right lock/replace boundary cut and a genuinely useful region-taxonomy vocabulary, but the customization matrix overcommits to "Locked" cells that white-label/enterprise reskins will push back on, the doc ships standalone without pinning the three accepted shapes for `ui.customAsciiArt` to concrete schema (string vs array vs file path) which defers a load-bearing field-name decision to a follow-up under sprint pressure, and there's no "rejected alternatives" section so the design's choices aren't bounded — should pin schema, soften the version-suffix lock to admin-only escape hatch, and add rejected alternatives before this design unblocks implementation).

### W17 drip-112 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos. At sst/opencode, #24618 (`c11a6d47`) is a +3/−1 hotfix for issue #24402 (`TypeError: undefined is not an object (evaluating 'output.split')`) when an MCP tool returns image-only or empty content arrays — fixes both call sites that consume `part.state.output` (`message-v2.ts:854` adds `?? ""`; `truncate.ts:87` early-returns `{content: "", truncated: false}` when `text == null`) plus an inner guard at `message-v2.ts:321`'s `truncateToolOutput`, but the deeper question is *why* `part.state.output` is `undefined` in the first place — the MCP tool-result materialization path should normalize at the boundary, not at every consumer — and no regression test was added so the same crash will reappear the next time someone refactors `truncateToolOutput`. At openai/codex, #19818 (`3c5ad4d0`, "part 1") is a 39-file +436/−267 pure structural move carving `core::memories` into two new `codex-memories-read` (citations, prompts, usage, read_path template) and `codex-memories-write` (control, extensions, write-side state) crates with the right read-vs-write asymmetry pinned in `Cargo.lock` — read crate has no `codex-state` or `codex-models-manager` while write does — but wants a roadmap for parts 2+, non-empty `BUILD.bazel` stubs to keep the Bazel path green, and a `#[deny]` or doc-comment invariant on the read crate's `lib.rs` so a future PR doesn't drift `codex-state` back in. At BerriAI/litellm, #26601 (`2d02176c`) is a clean `useMemo` fix for an infinite re-render loop in `VirtualKeysTable.tsx:97` — `keys?.keys || []` was synthesizing a fresh `[]` literal on every render which fired the `useFilterLogic` `useEffect([keys, filters])` repeatedly — with the *liveness* regression test at `filter_logic.test.tsx:35-57` explicitly asserting "should not enter an infinite render loop when keys prop is re-rendered with a new empty-array reference" via three identity-distinct rerenders + a positive empty-to-populated test bracketing both sides; merge as-is. #26595 (`e55e73d6`) is the OVHCloud field-rename migration (deadline 2026-05-11) — STT `duration→seconds` at `audio_transcription/transformation.py:159-167` uses `is not None` (not truthiness) so `seconds: 0.0` doesn't get lost, with a `test_seconds_zero_mapped_to_duration` pinning that exactly; chat-streaming `reasoning_content→reasoning` at `chat/transformation.py:101-110` uses a "legacy wins when both present" rule pinned by `test_streaming_both_fields_legacy_wins`, but two test files miss trailing newlines and the "legacy wins" rationale deserves a comment. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26018 (`5a441ef0`) is a +26-line docs PR adding a 5-item "If your skill doesn't appear" troubleshooting checklist to the skills tutorial — workspace-trust requirement, two valid `SKILL.md` path layouts (root vs one-deep, never deeper), case-sensitive filename, frontmatter-must-be-first-thing (a leading blank line breaks discovery), and the displayed name comes from `name:` field with `: \ / < > * ? " |` sanitized — five real runtime-silent failure modes pinned in docs because they're invisible from the tool itself; merge-as-is. #26014 (`343fc1ec`) replaces the racy `docker ps -a --format "{{.Names}}"` probe-then-pick container naming with a 6-byte (48-bit) random hex suffix at `sandbox.ts:490-498`, eliminating the TOCTOU where two concurrent `gemini` starts both see `${imageName}-0` free and both pick it; the test at `sandbox.test.ts:341-356` pins the *negative* invariant (`expect(mockedExecCommands).not.toEqual(expect.arrayContaining([expect.stringContaining('ps -a --format')]))`) which is much more durable than asserting the new path works — but losing the deterministic `${imageName}-0` name removes a small UX win for users who rely on `docker exec -it gemini-cli-sandbox-0 sh` for debug. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3680 (`5b220a52`, +2610/−12 across 9 files) is a large feature PR expanding TUI Markdown rendering with five distinct features bundled — Mermaid diagrams via `mmdc → PNG → Kitty/iTerm2 protocol` (with `chafa` ANSI fallback and a synchronous text wireframe last-resort), task-list checkboxes, blockquote bars, inline+block LaTeX via Unicode substitution — and the Kitty Unicode-placeholder trick (`U+10EEEE` + diacritics, APC payload via raw stdout in `q=2 U=1` quiet mode) is the production-correct way to integrate terminal images into Ink-based TUIs (direct payload injection corrupts the framework's render); should be split into 2-3 PRs along feature lines (Mermaid, blockquotes+tasks, LaTeX) for independent review/rollback, the LaTeX substitution table needs to be documented or pinned in tests (`\frac{a}{b}` has no honest single-line Unicode), and streaming behavior under character-by-character Mermaid should buffer until the closing fence rather than re-rendering wireframes per token. At block/goose, #8793 (`29cde953`) fixes session-title contamination by audience-aware partitioning at `providers/base.rs:740-751` — `get_initial_user_messages` now chains `filter_for_audience(rmcp::model::Role::User)` → `as_text()` → `join("\n")` so preprompt blocks (assistant-audience-only) are dropped from the title-generation input, and a new `get_preprompt_context` extracts the *complement* (the non-user-visible blocks) and passes them in a clearly-fenced `---BEGIN BACKGROUND CONTEXT (for understanding only, do NOT base the title on this)---` section; the corresponding strip in `providers/cli_common.rs:58-62` uses `rfind` (not `find`) so a model echoing the marker into its own response still resolves the *last* occurrence as the boundary — a small but real correctness win — but `get_preprompt_context`'s `take(1)` deserves a doc comment explaining "preprompt is definitionally first-message-only" and there are no new tests in the diff so a regression case constructing a `Conversation` with both User-audience and Assistant-audience text blocks should be added.

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (BerriAI/litellm #26601 clean `useMemo` fix for infinite re-render loop in `VirtualKeysTable` with the textbook *liveness* regression test asserting three identity-distinct empty-array rerenders don't loop, plus the bracketing positive test for legitimate updates; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26018 +26-line docs PR enumerating five runtime-silent skill-discovery failure modes — workspace-trust, path layout, case-sensitive filename, frontmatter-first, name-field sanitization — exactly the contract that's invisible from the tool itself), 5 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24618 two-line MCP-tool-output undefined guard for issue #24402 with both consumer-side `?? ""` and inner-function null-tolerance — minimal hotfix shape but no regression test and the deeper materialization-layer fix is deferred; openai/codex #19818 39-file pure structural carve of `core::memories` into read/write crates with correct dependency asymmetry pinned in Cargo.lock — wants part-2 roadmap, non-empty `BUILD.bazel` stubs, and an enforced read-side dependency invariant; BerriAI/litellm #26595 OVHCloud `seconds`/`reasoning` field migration with the right `is not None` zero-tolerant predicate and "legacy wins" precedence pinned by tests — wants trailing newlines and a comment on the precedence rationale; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26014 race-free 6-byte random-hex container suffix replacing the `docker ps -a` probe-then-pick TOCTOU with a durable *negative* assertion in tests — small UX cost since the deterministic name is gone; block/goose #8793 audience-aware partitioning so session titles see only User-visible content while preprompt is passed as fenced background context, with `rfind`-not-`find` strip handling models that echo their own input — wants a doc comment on `take(1)` and a regression test), 0 request-changes, 2 needs-discussion (QwenLM/qwen-code #3680 large +2610/−12 bundle of five distinct rendering features in one PR with a thoughtful Mermaid pipeline using the production-correct Kitty Unicode-placeholder trick — should be split into 2-3 PRs along feature lines for independent review and rollback, the LaTeX Unicode substitution table needs documenting, and streaming Mermaid blocks should buffer until the closing fence rather than re-rendering wireframes per token).


### W17 drip-113 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At sst/opencode, #24614 (`7d35c9ab`) is a +1/−1 IT docs fix dropping a duplicated `Abilita Abilita` in the experimental-env-vars table for `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_LSP_TY` — clearly a translator copy-paste artifact from the row above, fix touches no behavior or schema, and a CI grep `\b(\w+)\s+\1\b` over all locale files would catch this and similar one-token reduplications going forward. #24589 (`a128590f`, +7776/−142 across 83 files) lands first-class multi-root workspaces (closes #19515) with the right shape — `.code-workspace` JSON persistence, copy-on-import to avoid mutating user-owned files, LSP `workspaceFolders` integration, two migration snapshots in correct order — but several decisions deserve explicit review (CASCADE/SET NULL/RESTRICT semantics on `DELETE workspace`; what happens when the user edits the original imported `.code-workspace` after import; whether the workspace-context request header is REQUIRED on new server or falls back to a default-workspace that could leak across users; LSP multi-folder coverage for the gopls/rust-analyzer/tsserver-each-have-different-quirks reality with only ~130 lines of LSP test) and the 7776/83-file squash should arguably be a 6-PR stack (schema → routes+SDK → TUI dialog+i18n → app dialog+i18n → LSP → docs) for independent revertability. At openai/codex, #19775 (`6c92cc40`, +26/−16, PR-3 of a 5-PR Sapling stack) carves the duplicated `sandbox_policy` field out of `ThreadConfigSnapshot` and replaces it with a `sandbox_policy()` projection method derived from the canonical `permission_profile` plus `cwd` via `codex_sandboxing::compatibility_sandbox_policy_for_permission_profile(...)` — eliminates a real drift bug (the field could fall out of sync with the canonical profile) by making the legacy projection structurally impossible to drift, with all in-tree call sites flipped in lockstep so the compiler enforces the migration; wants a doc comment on the projection naming `permission_profile` as source-of-truth, and consider renaming `sandbox_policy()` to `derived_sandbox_policy()` so call sites read as projections not field accesses. #19769 (`9aa3206b`, +347, draft) adds a one-shot 900sage upsell prompt to the TUI for ChatGPT-account users with plan-branched CTAs (Plus → Upgrade now → Pro URL; Pro/ProLite → Buy credits → usage URL; workspace owners → Review usage options → usage URL; workspace members deliberately excluded) and a UTM-tagged `OpenUrlInBrowser` action plus a new `AppEvent::AddInfoMessage` channel for the post-click follow-up message, with the `ProactiveUsagePromptState::{Idle,Pending,Shown}` one-shot guarantee and a snapshot test pinning the Plus-branch popup — but the implicit precedence change (the high-usage gating at `chatwidget.rs:3034` adds `&& !proactive_usage_prompt_eligible` so users who would benefit from the cheaper-model nudge get the upsell instead) is a product-policy decision encoded as code, deserves an explicit comment naming it as intentional and worth confirming with PM that "show user the cheaper option first, only upsell if they decline" is not the better policy; also wants snapshots for the Pro/ProLite and workspace-owner branches (only Plus is pinned today) and the per-process state means a user who declined yesterday at 90ees the prompt again today at 92

### W17 drip-113 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos. At sst/opencode, #24614 (`7d35c9ab`) is a +1/−1 IT docs fix dropping a duplicated `Abilita Abilita` in the experimental-env-vars table for `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_LSP_TY` — clearly a translator copy-paste artifact from the row above, fix touches no behavior or schema, and a CI grep `\b(\w+)\s+\1\b` over all locale files would catch this and similar one-token reduplications going forward. #24589 (`a128590f`, +7776/−142 across 83 files) lands first-class multi-root workspaces (closes #19515) with the right shape — `.code-workspace` JSON persistence, copy-on-import to avoid mutating user-owned files, LSP `workspaceFolders` integration, two migration snapshots in correct order — but several decisions deserve explicit review (CASCADE/SET NULL/RESTRICT semantics on `DELETE workspace`; what happens when the user edits the original imported `.code-workspace` after import; whether the workspace-context request header is REQUIRED on new server or falls back to a default-workspace that could leak across users; LSP multi-folder coverage for the gopls/rust-analyzer/tsserver-each-have-different-quirks reality with only ~130 lines of LSP test) and the 7776/83-file squash should arguably be a 6-PR stack (schema → routes+SDK → TUI dialog+i18n → app dialog+i18n → LSP → docs) for independent revertability. At openai/codex, #19775 (`6c92cc40`, +26/−16, PR-3 of a 5-PR Sapling stack) carves the duplicated `sandbox_policy` field out of `ThreadConfigSnapshot` and replaces it with a `sandbox_policy()` projection method derived from the canonical `permission_profile` plus `cwd` via `codex_sandboxing::compatibility_sandbox_policy_for_permission_profile(...)` — eliminates a real drift bug (the field could fall out of sync with the canonical profile) by making the legacy projection structurally impossible to drift, with all in-tree call sites flipped in lockstep so the compiler enforces the migration; wants a doc comment on the projection naming `permission_profile` as source-of-truth, and consider renaming `sandbox_policy()` to `derived_sandbox_policy()` so call sites read as projections not field accesses. #19769 (`9aa3206b`, +347, draft) adds a one-shot 90%-usage upsell prompt to the TUI for ChatGPT-account users with plan-branched CTAs (Plus → Upgrade now → Pro URL; Pro/ProLite → Buy credits → usage URL; workspace owners → Review usage options → usage URL; workspace members deliberately excluded) and a UTM-tagged `OpenUrlInBrowser` action plus a new `AppEvent::AddInfoMessage` channel for the post-click follow-up message, with the `ProactiveUsagePromptState::{Idle,Pending,Shown}` one-shot guarantee and a snapshot test pinning the Plus-branch popup — but the implicit precedence change (the high-usage gating at `chatwidget.rs:3034` adds `&& !proactive_usage_prompt_eligible` so users who would benefit from the cheaper-model nudge get the upsell instead) is a product-policy decision encoded as code, deserves an explicit comment naming it as intentional and worth confirming with PM that "show user the cheaper option first, only upsell if they decline" is not the better policy; also wants snapshots for the Pro/ProLite and workspace-owner branches (only Plus is pinned today) and the per-process state means a user who declined yesterday at 90% sees the prompt again today at 92%. At BerriAI/litellm, #26603 (`1257fca2`, +8/−0) closes the remaining half of #26396 by adding `verbose_logger` to the import + `setLevel` calls in the `LITELLM_LOG=INFO|DEBUG` branches of `init_verbose_loggers` so package-level `verbose_logger.info(...)` records (notably `token_based_routing`) are no longer silently dropped to the root logger WARNING level — correct minimum-surface fix matching the already-landed `proxy_server.py` half, but the four near-identical branches across two files are screaming for a `_set_all_loggers(level)` helper and there is no regression test pinning the four-branch invariant. #26602 (`a653429b`, +1/−0) excludes `docker/build_from_pip/` from the Docker build context via `.dockerignore` so the runtime image stops shipping a CI-only fixture whose embedded `requirements.txt` declares a deliberately-old litellm pin and trips downstream CVE-policy gates as a false positive — clean security-compliance hygiene, the kind of one-line `.dockerignore` change every project eventually needs. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #25999 (`b17f34f6`, +10/−1) attempts to fix #25872 (CLI hangs in browser-less environments like Termux when a tool needs browser approval, even with `yolo` enabled) by auto-allowing requests when `this.config.yolo` is true — but the diff inserts the `if (this.config.yolo) { return { allowed: true }; }` line *inside an object literal* between two property declarations (`sessionUpdate: ...,` and `content,`), which is invalid TypeScript and will not compile, AND it places the bypass in the streaming-update path that emits `agent_thought_chunk`/`agent_message_chunk` rather than in the actual permission-request handler — the intent is right but the placement is wrong on two axes; also bundles an unrelated `resolvePath.ts` try/catch that swallows `ENAMETOOLONG` and returns the un-normalized path with broken indentation and no rationale. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3557 (`ec3c7026`, +682/−152 across 9 files) hardens the insight-generation pipeline against three real failure modes (malformed LLM facet responses crashing aggregation, all-empty qualitative sections rendering as nav-to-nowhere headers, cached facets propagating malformed shape) by adding a normalization layer at three entry points (LLM-response handler, cached-facet reuse, count-aggregator) with 42 tests on `DataProcessor.test.ts` pinning the fallback shapes — but the choice of defaults silently biases the dataset (`Qwen_helpfulness: 'invalid'` falls back to `'moderately_helpful'` rather than an honest `'unknown'` sentinel), the normalization layer has no observability counter so contract drift accumulates silently, and cache validation is reactive (validate-on-read) rather than preventive (refuse-to-cache-malformed).

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24614 IT-docs duplicated-word fix touching one cell of the experimental-env-vars table with no behavior or schema impact; BerriAI/litellm #26602 one-line `.dockerignore` exclusion of the `docker/build_from_pip/` CI fixture so its deliberately-old-version embedded pin stops false-positiving downstream CVE policy gates without affecting runtime), 3 merge-after-nits (openai/codex #19775 right "store one, derive the other" carve-out replacing the redundantly-stored `sandbox_policy` field on `ThreadConfigSnapshot` with a `sandbox_policy()` projection method derived from the canonical `permission_profile` plus `cwd` so the legacy compatibility shape can no longer drift from the source of truth — wants a doc comment naming the projection nature and a rename to `derived_sandbox_policy()` so call sites read as projections not field accesses; BerriAI/litellm #26603 closes the remaining half of #26396 by adding `verbose_logger` to the `LITELLM_LOG=INFO|DEBUG` branches of `init_verbose_loggers` so package-level info records stop being silently filtered — wants a `_set_all_loggers(level)` helper to collapse the four near-identical branches and a regression test pinning the four-branch invariant; QwenLM/qwen-code #3557 hardens insight-generation against malformed LLM responses, all-empty qualitative sections, and cached-malformed-data propagation with 42 new tests pinning the fallback shapes — but the choice of normalization defaults silently biases the aggregate dataset, the normalization layer has no observability counter so contract drift accumulates invisibly, and cache validation is reactive rather than preventive), 1 request-changes (google-gemini/gemini-cli #25999 yolo-mode browser-less bypass — the early-return `if (this.config.yolo) { return { allowed: true }; }` is inserted *inside an object literal* between `sessionUpdate: ...` and `content,` which is invalid TypeScript and will not compile, AND it lives in the streaming-update emitter rather than the permission-request handler so even if it compiled it would not fix the bug; bundles an unrelated `resolvePath.ts` try/catch that swallows `ENAMETOOLONG` and returns an un-normalized path with broken indentation and no rationale — needs the bypass moved to the actual permission handler with a test, and the path-resolution change split into a separate PR), 2 needs-discussion (sst/opencode #24589 first-class multi-root workspaces with the right architectural shape — `.code-workspace` JSON, copy-on-import, LSP `workspaceFolders`, ordered migrations — but a 7776-line/83-file squash should be a 6-PR stack for independent revertability and several decisions are unstated (CASCADE/SET NULL/RESTRICT on workspace deletion, what happens when the user edits the original imported file, whether the workspace-context header is required-or-fallback-to-default with associated cross-workspace leak risk), and the LSP multi-folder integration with only ~130 lines of test feels under-covered for the gopls/rust-analyzer/tsserver-each-have-different-quirks reality; openai/codex #19769 proactive 90%-usage upsell prompt with the right one-shot state machine and snapshot-pinned popup but the implicit precedence change suppressing the strictly-cheaper model-switch nudge in favor of the upsell at 90% is an unstated product-policy decision that deserves an explicit comment and PM confirmation, and snapshots cover only the Plus branch leaving Pro/ProLite and workspace-owner unpinned).


### W17 drip-114 (2026-04-27)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos. At sst/opencode, #24626 (`88a4714b`) is the trailing fixup for the multi-root-workspaces PR (#24589, reviewed in drip-113) — that PR shipped a `WorkspacePaths` route module plus an integration test but never registered the six handlers in `InstanceRoutes`, so every workspace endpoint 404'd in production despite passing CI; this PR adds `app.get/post/delete` mounts for `adaptors`/`list`/`status`/`remove`/`sessionRestore` at `server/routes/instance/index.ts:148-153` and rewrites the test to route through `InstanceRoutes(websocket).request(path, init)` (with `Flag.OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_HTTPAPI = true` saved/restored in `afterEach`) instead of going through `ExperimentalHttpApiServer.webHandler()` directly with an empty Context — the route-registration bug slipped past CI specifically because the original test bypassed the registration layer. The unrelated zod-parse strips bundled in `config.ts:69-72` and `project.ts:102` are trusting the Effect HttpApi schema layer to have already validated, probably correct given the `Layer.unwrap(Effect.gen(...))` shape but worth a one-line note. At openai/codex, #19728 is a 4-line tag-along test-path migration in `core/src/config/config_tests.rs` flipping `crate::config_loader::{NetworkConstraints, NetworkRequirementsToml, ConfigRequirementsToml, SandboxModeRequirement}` over to `codex_config::*` — pure path rewrite that needs to merge same-day as the dependent shim removal so the intermediate state isn't broken on main, and a `#[deprecated]` on the crate-internal `pub use` shim would prevent accidental future use. At BerriAI/litellm, #26607 (`dad97087`) adds a Resemble AI Detect deepfake guardrail (~1900 lines: docs page, hook init, 580-line implementation, types, dedicated test file) with three things right: (a) URL extraction is layered not first-match — `_extract_media_urls:565-595` pulls from multimodal content parts across *every* message, then regex over joined text, then `metadata.mediaUrl`, with a docstring explicitly naming the threat ("Returning only the first match let callers sneak a synthetic URL past the guardrail by placing a benign one earlier in the content array"); (b) fail-open is the default, fail-closed is explicit (`_handle_api_error:543-559`); (c) `MEDIA_URL_REGEX:188` is anchored on file-extension HTTPS URLs only so the guardrail doesn't submit every link to Resemble and burn quota — but extension-less CDN URLs slip through, the 60s `poll_timeout_seconds` × 2s `poll_interval_seconds` adds 60s worst-case latency to every `pre_call` request that contains media, and the Anthropic-style `source.url` branch handles `image`/`document` part types but not `video`. #26606 (`85f4dde2`) backfills streaming `response_cost` into both `_merge_hidden_params_from_response_into_metadata` and `get_standard_logging_object_payload` — streaming providers don't carry `_hidden_params["response_cost"]` (LiteLLM computes it), so downstream metering dashboards silently lost streaming cost data; the fix is `metadata_hidden_params = hidden_params.copy()` then `if missing-and-have-it: backfill`, with two correct invariants pinned by tests (`.copy()` so the response's own `_hidden_params` is not mutated; `is None` predicate not falsy so `0.0` is preserved and provider-supplied costs are never overwritten) and the TypedDict widening from `Optional[str]` to `Optional[Union[str, float]]` corrects what was already a lying type annotation. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26043 (`91895425`) tries to fix settings-persistence with the right `setValue(scope, key, value, options?: {skipSave?: boolean})` carve-out plus an explicit `save(scope)` method and `migrateDeprecatedSettings(loadedSettings)` returning the modified-scopes set so migration writes once instead of N times — but the diff *also* accidentally commits the unpacked Node 20.19.0 Windows binary distribution under `node20/node-v20.19.0-win-x64/` (`node.exe`, `npm`, `npx`, `corepack`, `install_tools.bat`, the full 1706-line CHANGELOG.md, 2170-line LICENSE, npm/`node_modules/`, etc.) plus an `empty_settings.json` at repo root — classic `git add .` after extracting a release tarball into the working tree, would bloat the repo by tens of MB and ship a third-party Windows binary through the gemini-cli release pipeline. #26041 (`8ae76976`) is a +/− symmetric mass substitution of `→` U+2192 (East-Asian-Ambiguous, renders 2-column in CJK terminals) with `￫` U+FFEB (HALFWIDTH RIGHTWARDS ARROW, definitionally narrow) across ~50 source + snapshot files — mechanically correct but `￫` renders noticeably narrower and slightly higher than surrounding glyphs in Latin monospace fonts (many of which fall back to a placeholder for the U+FFEx halfwidth block), so the right fix for log lines is usually ASCII `->`; ~98% of the 3800-line diff is `.snap`/`.snap.svg` regeneration, and there's no CI lint to prevent reintroduction. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3539 adds `/branch <name>` (alias `/fork`) for session forking with a 5-step `finalize → load (parent snapshot) → fork → load (forked) → config.start` sequence pinned by an ordering test whose docstring at `useBranchCommand.test.ts:74-83` captures the load-order invariant ("the parent snapshot must come AFTER finalize() because finalize appends a custom_title record advancing lastCompletedUuid; a snapshot taken before would orphan the custom_title from the parent chain on rollback") — exactly the kind of invariant a future refactor would silently break, so pinning it in test prose protects it. The mid-stream/mid-tool-confirmation guard at `branchCommand.ts` prevents tearing the new session's parent chain, but no rollback-path test exercises "fork succeeds, loadSession on the forked file fails", `(Branch)` suffix is unconditional so `/branch` of a `/branch` yields nested suffixes, and the slash command is i18n'd while the suffix and alias are hard-coded English. At block/goose, #8809 fixes a real billing-tier bug in the github-copilot provider — every request was being sent without the upstream's `X-Initiator: user|agent` header, which defaults to `user`, so compaction calls / title-generation / tool-followups were silently mis-billing as user-initiated. The fix introduces a `tokio::task_local! IS_AGENT_CALL` flag with `complete()` (which `complete_fast()` paths use for compaction/title-gen) wrapping its delegated `stream()` call in `IS_AGENT_CALL.scope(true, async {...})`, and `stream()` reading it via `try_with(false)` then combining with a content-shape signal: `is_user_initiated = !is_agent_call && !last_is_tool_response` where `last_is_tool_response` matches `MessageContent::ToolResponse(_)` on the last message — correct two-axis classification, with two new tests pinning (a) the task-local visible-inside-scope/absent-outside behavior and (b) the `MessageContent::ToolResponse` variant match (so a future rename fails loudly).
