Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24626 trailing route-registration fixup for the multi-root-workspaces PR plus a test rewrite that routes through `InstanceRoutes` instead of `ExperimentalHttpApiServer.webHandler()` so the same "declared but not mounted" shape would fail loudly next time; openai/codex #19728 pure 4-line `config_loader → codex_config` test path rewrite as tag-along to the ongoing crate carve-out), 4 merge-after-nits (BerriAI/litellm #26607 layered URL-extraction Resemble Detect guardrail with the right fail-open default and the right "every message every part" scan with the threat-model paragraph in the docstring — but extension-anchored regex misses extension-less CDN URLs, 60s polling timeout adds latency to every pre_call request with media, and Anthropic-style `source.url` branch covers image/document not video; BerriAI/litellm #26606 streaming response_cost backfill into both metadata-merge and standard-logging paths with .copy()-so-response-isn't-mutated and is-None-predicate-so-zero-and-provider-supplied-are-preserved invariants pinned by tests, plus the TypedDict widening from `Optional[str]` to `Optional[Union[str, float]]` correcting a lying annotation — wants `.get()` not `[]` for defensive depth and a negative test for both-None-stays-None; QwenLM/qwen-code #3539 `/branch` (alias `/fork`) session forking with the parent-snapshot-after-finalize invariant pinned in test prose — no rollback-path test, unconditional `(Branch)` suffix yields nested suffixes on re-branching, and i18n is split between the slash-command description and the hard-coded suffix; block/goose #8809 X-Initiator header fix using a `tokio::task_local! IS_AGENT_CALL` flag set by `complete()` and read by `stream()`, combined with a `last_is_tool_response` content-shape signal for the right two-axis classification, with two valuable tests pinning the task-local scoping mechanism and the `MessageContent::ToolResponse` variant match so a rename fails loudly — wants `HeaderValue::from_static` instead of `.parse().unwrap()`, a "spawn inside scope doesn't inherit" test documenting the boundary, and a comment naming the rare user-pasted-tool-response false-positive), 1 request-changes (google-gemini/gemini-cli #26043 settings-persistence fix has the right `setValue(skipSave)` + explicit `save()` carve-out and the right `migrateDeprecatedSettings` returning modified-scopes set so migration writes once not N times — but the diff also accidentally commits the unpacked Node 20.19.0 Windows binary distribution under `node20/node-v20.19.0-win-x64/` including `node.exe`, the full 1706-line CHANGELOG.md, 2170-line LICENSE, npm/npx wrappers, plus an empty_settings.json at repo root: classic git-add-dot-after-extracting-a-release-tarball that would bloat the repo by tens of MB), 1 needs-discussion (google-gemini/gemini-cli #26041 mass `→ U+2192 → ￫ U+FFEB` substitution to fix CJK-terminal column drift — mechanically correct but U+FFEB renders worse than ASCII `->` on Latin terminals because many monospace fonts don't ship the U+FFEx halfwidth block and fall back to a placeholder, ~98% of the 3800-line diff is mechanical snapshot regeneration diluting review value, and no CI lint added to prevent reintroduction; recommend re-rolling as `→ → ->` ASCII with a snapshot-only follow-up commit and a `no-ambiguous-width-glyphs` lint).


### W17 drip-115 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (5 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24626 (`88a4714b`) is the trailing fixup for the workspace HttpApi mount — wires six `WorkspacePaths.{adaptors,list,status,remove,sessionRestore}` handlers onto `InstanceRoutes` at `server/routes/instance/index.ts:148-153` and rewrites the workspace test (`test/server/httpapi-workspace.test.ts`) to drive `InstanceRoutes(websocket).request(...)` instead of `ExperimentalHttpApiServer.webHandler()` directly so the route-registration layer is actually exercised; also strips redundant `Zod.parse` re-validation in `httpapi/config.ts:69` and `httpapi/project.ts:102` since the HttpApi schema layer already decoded. #24621 (`cceba3e6`) closes #18943 + #16837 by adding a `createEffect` in `directory-layout.tsx:58-66` that calls `layout.projects.open(dir)` + `server.projects.touch(dir)` inside `untrack(...)` whenever `resolved()` returns a non-empty directory, fixing the case where IDE-plugin / deep-link / page-reload landings on `/{base64(dir)}/session` never registered the project in the sidebar; `untrack` is the right defensive call to prevent reactive thrash. #24618 (`c11a6d47`) closes #24402 with three layered guards against MCP tools that return non-text content (image-only results) producing `undefined` for `part.state.output`: `truncateToolOutput` early-returns `""` (`message-v2.ts:321`), the call site uses `?? ""` (`:857`), and `Truncate.output` early-returns `{ content: "", truncated: false }` on `text == null` (`tool/truncate.ts:87`) — defense in depth on a hot path, but the `Schema.String` type for `output` still lies and a fixture-based regression test would be cheap. #24612 (`36157b68`) replaces the static 3-model models.dev list for LM Studio with a dynamic `GET {baseURL}/models` fetch via a new `provider/model-cache.ts` module — sensible default `127.0.0.1:1234/v1`, 10s `AbortSignal.timeout`, falls back to `{}` (and thus the static list) on failure, but hardcodes wrong-for-most-LM-Studio-models capability defaults (`context: 128000`, `output: 4096`, `attachment: true`, `tool_call: true`, `modalities: ["text","image"] -> ["text"]`) which will produce model cards that lie about capabilities, has no cache invalidation so loading a new model in LM Studio mid-session shows the stale list, and ships an unannounced Apertis (`https://api.apertis.ai/v1`) third-party endpoint addition that the PR description never mentions. #24595 (`a824489`) is a one-line removal of `"User-Agent": ${opencode}/${InstallationVersion}` from the third-party-provider branch in `session/llm.ts:381` — AI SDK's `combineHeaders` merges call-level headers *after* `getHeaders()`, so a user-configured UA in `provider.<name>.options.headers` was being clobbered before the request hit the wire (corp LiteLLM gateways with UA allowlists returning 403 was the reported symptom); the opencode-hosted `if` branch is intentionally untouched, but the PR has no regression test pinning the header-merge order and quietly removes the default UA fallback. At openai/codex, #19807 (`a37adea0`) consolidates `IS_MACOS || IS_WINDOWS` into a single `SUPPORTS_DESKTOP_APP: bool = cfg!(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "windows"))` (`tooltips.rs:9`), updates `APP_TOOLTIP` and `OTHER_TOOLTIP` to mention "Windows and macOS", and adds `pick_free_go_tooltip` (`:112-118`) doing a 50/50 RNG split for Free/Go users between the existing free-plan promo and the desktop-app promo — gated on `supports_desktop_app` so Linux users still see only the plan promo, with two deterministic seed-driven tests pinning both branches; only nit is the surviving `OTHER_TOOLTIP_NON_MAC` constant name is now misleading. #19797 (`f8e2beea`) is the +2494/-544 OAuth refactor routing MCP OAuth (discovery, dynamic client registration, PKCE token exchange, refresh) through the runtime-selected `HttpClient` instead of always going local — keeps browser interaction + callback listener local (necessary), keeps tokens in the existing local store, centralizes orchestration in `codex-mcp` so `app-server` no longer depends on `rmcp-client` directly, adds a 309-line regression test covering the runtime-injected client + an unresolvable fake host + an exec-server-backed stored-token refresh; the rationale (origin leaking from the user's machine even when MCP traffic was routed through the remote runtime) is a real corp-deployment footgun, but the diff size makes the long tail of OAuth edge cases (token expiry races, refresh deduplication, store invalidation) hard to bound without splitting into 4 commits, and the author's own test environment doesn't get `cargo test -p codex-core` and `-p codex-app-server` fully green. At BerriAI/litellm, #26608 (`ec91aa7c`) threads `timeout` from `kwargs` into the Anthropic `/v1/messages` adapter at `llm_http_handler.py:1836,1851,1921,2044` so `--request_timeout`/`litellm_settings.request_timeout`/`router_settings.timeout` actually reach the underlying `httpx` call instead of silently inheriting httpx's 600s default — adds 5 mock-server tests covering short/sufficient × non-streaming/streaming + `stream_timeout` precedence over `timeout` for streaming, the fix matches the established pattern used by every other handler in the file, but no string-coercion guard for config-supplied `timeout="10"` and the `stream_timeout` precedence test asserts behavior that may live outside this PR's diff. #26606 (`50e761d5`) backfills streaming `response_cost` into `_merge_hidden_params_from_response_into_metadata` (`litellm_logging.py:1728-1740`) and `get_standard_logging_object_payload` (`:5480-5493`) — streaming providers don't carry `_hidden_params["response_cost"]` so OTEL/Jaeger spans lost the value entirely, the fix is `metadata_hidden_params = hidden_params.copy()` (so the response object isn't mutated, pinned by a dedicated test) then `if metadata_hidden_params.get("response_cost") is None and response_cost is not None: backfill` (so provider-supplied non-null values are preserved, also pinned), with the `types/utils.py:2662` widening from `Optional[str]` to `Optional[Union[str, float]]` correcting a lying type annotation; reordering of `clean_hidden_params` computation from `:5444` to `:5482` is functionally fine but worth calling out so reviewers don't miss it. At block/goose, #8857 (`95a5facf`) adds `preserves_thinking: bool` (`config/declarative_providers.rs:85`) for Anthropic-compatible providers that need the prior assistant turn's `thinking` block replayed for tool-call continuity, ships Z.AI (`zai.json` +34) as the first opt-in built-in, parallel adds `clear_thinking: false` for OpenAI-compatible providers wired into 6 declarative provider JSONs (`deepseek`, `moonshot`, `novita`, `nvidia`, `tensorix`, `zhipu`) plus a new `opencode_go.json`, with table-driven tests across all six OpenAI-compat providers (`:646`+) plus 265 lines of Anthropic format tests + 408 lines of OpenAI streaming reasoning regression tests — also fixes a duplicate-reasoning-content bug in the OpenAI streaming path; default `false` keeps backward compat, `is_false` skip-serializer keeps generated configs clean, but bundling a new `opencode_go.json` provider definition with a thinking-preservation feature PR muddies change history, the +408-line streaming `formats/openai.rs` rewrite is the riskiest hunk and deserves a maintainer walk-through, and no user-facing docs explain when to set `clear_thinking: false` on a custom provider.

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (BerriAI/litellm #26606 streaming response_cost backfill into both metadata-merge and standard-logging paths with `.copy()`-not-reference and `is None`-not-falsy invariants pinned by tests so neither the response object's `_hidden_params` is mutated nor provider-supplied 0.0-or-non-null values get clobbered, plus a `types/utils.py` widening from `Optional[str]` to `Optional[Union[str, float]]` correcting a long-standing lying annotation), 5 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24626 trailing route-registration fixup wiring six `WorkspacePaths` handlers onto `InstanceRoutes` plus a test rewrite that goes through `InstanceRoutes.request(...)` instead of bypassing via `ExperimentalHttpApiServer.webHandler()` so the same "declared but not mounted" shape would fail loudly, plus drops redundant `Zod.parse` re-validation since the HttpApi schema layer already decoded — wants a typed registry/loop so future `WorkspacePaths` additions are caught at compile time and a `beforeAll` to set the experimental flag instead of inside `request()`; sst/opencode #24621 sidebar auto-add via a `createEffect` calling `layout.projects.open(dir)` + `server.projects.touch(dir)` inside `untrack(...)` keyed off `resolved()`, relying on documented idempotency of `open()` for no-duplicates and using `untrack` to avoid reactive thrash — wants a regression test for a bug that has shipped twice (closes #18943 + #16837) and a `.catch(() => {})` around `touch` so a sync-server hiccup doesn't surface as an unhandled SolidJS error; sst/opencode #24618 three-layered guard against undefined MCP tool output (function-internal early-return, call-site `?? ""`, second function-internal early-return) is correct defense in depth on a hot path but the `Schema.String` type for `output` still lies at runtime and no fixture-based regression test pins the image-only-MCP-result contract; sst/opencode #24595 one-line removal of the third-party-provider UA override correctly fixes corp-gateway UA-allowlist 403s by letting `provider.<name>.options.headers["User-Agent"]` flow through `combineHeaders` — needs a unit test pinning the header-merge order so the next refactor can't silently re-introduce it, plus a one-line note explaining why the opencode-hosted `if` branch intentionally still hard-sets UA (telemetry on hosted vs respect-user on third-party); openai/codex #19807 `IS_MACOS || IS_WINDOWS → SUPPORTS_DESKTOP_APP` consolidation plus a deterministic-seed-tested 50/50 Free/Go rotation between plan-promo and desktop-app-promo, gated correctly so Linux users never see the desktop nudge — wants the surviving `OTHER_TOOLTIP_NON_MAC` constant renamed to drop the now-misleading `_NON_MAC` suffix, and CI confirmation that `-p codex-tui` goes green since the author's local run hits an unrelated stack-overflow test; BerriAI/litellm #26608 four-line `timeout` plumb-through into the Anthropic adapter matches the pattern used by every other handler in the file, with 5 mock-server tests covering short/sufficient × non-streaming/streaming + stream_timeout precedence — but no string-coercion guard for config-supplied `timeout="10"`, the stream_timeout precedence test asserts behavior that may live outside this PR's diff, and one Pre-Submission checkbox (`make test-unit`) remains unchecked), 1 request-changes (sst/opencode #24612 dynamic LM Studio model list with a sensible default URL + 10s timeout + fall-back-to-{} on failure, but hardcodes wrong-for-most-LM-Studio-models capability defaults at `model-cache.ts:62-66` (`context: 128000`, `output: 4096`, `attachment: true`, `tool_call: true`, image input modality) which will produce model cards that lie and surface as runtime errors looking like opencode bugs; cache is process-lifetime with no invalidation so a new model loaded into LM Studio mid-session shows the stale list; ships an unannounced Apertis third-party endpoint addition the PR description never mentions; and bakes `Bun.fetch` into shared provider code), 2 needs-discussion (openai/codex #19797 +2494/-544 OAuth refactor routing MCP OAuth (discovery, DCR, PKCE, refresh) through the runtime-selected HttpClient with browser+callback intentionally kept local and tokens kept in the existing local store, with a 309-line regression test covering custom-HttpClient + unresolvable-host + exec-server-backed stored-token refresh — direction is correct and the corp-deployment-egress-leak rationale is real, but the diff is too large to merge without either splitting into a 4-commit stack (introduce `mcp_oauth_http.rs` → move PKCE + refresh → move stored-token startup → app-server cleanup) or a maintainer doing a careful end-to-end OAuth dry run against a real Streamable HTTP server with refresh + revoke + expired-token edge cases, and the author's own test environment doesn't get `-p codex-core` and `-p codex-app-server` fully green; block/goose #8857 right-shaped two-flag thinking-preservation infrastructure (`preserves_thinking` for Anthropic-compat with default false + `is_false` skip-serializer, `clear_thinking: false` for OpenAI-compat) wired into 7 declarative provider JSONs plus a new built-in Z.AI provider with table-driven tests across all 6 OpenAI-compat opt-ins and 265+408 lines of format-layer tests — but bundles a brand new `opencode_go.json` provider definition with the thinking-preservation feature work muddying change history, the +408-line streaming `formats/openai.rs` rewrite (which also fixes a duplicate-reasoning-content bug per PR description) is the riskiest hunk and deserves either splitting into a separate PR or a maintainer walk-through with explicit confirmation the duplicate-reasoning regression test is present, and no user-facing docs explain when to set `clear_thinking: false` on a custom provider).


### W17 drip-116 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (3 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24572 (`97050b79`) extends the `safe()` helper inside `getUsage` (`session/session.ts:300`) from `Number.isFinite` to also clamp `value < 0` so providers that report `inputTokens` net-of-cache (rather than total-including-cache) can't drive the running session cost negative when subtracted by `cacheReadTokens`/`cacheWriteTokens` — pinned by a regression test in `compaction.test.ts:2092-2119` that constructs the exact pathological shape (`inputTokens: 100`, `cacheReadTokens: 200`, `cost.input: 10`) and asserts both `tokens.input === 0` and `cost === 0`. #24544 (`1c450b62`) replaces lexicographic message-ID comparisons in `SessionPrompt.run` (`prompt.ts:1354,1459`) with array-index comparisons captured during the same end-to-start scan loop — the bug shape (closes #23490) is custom message IDs from the SDK breaking the implicit "IDs sort chronologically" assumption that ULIDs/auto-increments usually satisfy, and the fix correctly observes that transcript order *is* the `msgs[]` index, not the IDs; replaces a `for...of` + skip-by-ID with a more efficient `for (let i = lastFinishedIndex + 1; ...)` start-from-known-position loop. #24543 (`bb6a9353`) is the textbook capture-before-await + check-after-await fix at `routes/session/index.tsx:185-189` — captures `route.sessionID` into `currentSessionID` before `sdk.client.session.get(...)`, then early-returns if `route.sessionID !== currentSessionID` after the await but before any workspace-state mutation, so a session-switch mid-fetch can't leak `previousWorkspace`/scroll/sync state into the wrong session; bundles an unrelated `sync_prompt_context_on_session_switch` KV-toggle + command-palette entry, a scrollbar theme tweak, and an *empty* `packages/sdk/js/openapi.json` file that's almost certainly a `git add .` accident and would break SDK generation. At openai/codex, #19832 (`d4904308`) adds `phase: Option<MessagePhase>` to `ResponseInputItem::Message` (`protocol/src/models.rs:677-679`) with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` for backwards-compatible wire format, threads it through the `From<ResponseInputItem> for ResponseItem` conversion at `:1109-1119` (so phase is preserved instead of hard-coded to `None`), updates 7+ construction sites across `core/` to pass `phase: None` explicitly, and marks `InterAgentCommunication::to_response_input_item()` as `Some(MessagePhase::Commentary)` at `protocol.rs:861` — pinned by a regression test at `models.rs:1663-1684` that round-trips `Some(Commentary)` through the conversion; OpenAI deployment checklist requires phase preservation for prompt-caching and forked-session replay. #19771 (`f1826252`) closes #19486 by moving the deferred-dynamic-tool filter from `build_prompt` (`turn.rs:947-961`, deleted) up into `ToolRouter::from_config` (`router.rs:74-93`) so *every* caller of `model_visible_specs()` gets a correct-by-construction filtered set — the bug surfaced in `/compact` request building because compaction calls `router.model_visible_specs()` directly, bypassing `build_prompt`'s downstream filter, and deferred dynamic tools without a `tool_search` partner produced 400s; pinned by a unit test at `router_tests.rs:142-193` (asserts `find_spec` still finds the hidden tool by name so `tool_search` can promote it later, and `model_visible_specs()` returns only the non-deferred one) plus an integration test in `compact_remote.rs` that asserts the actual compact request body never contains a `defer_loading` declaration. At BerriAI/litellm, #26614 (`e5c1daf3`) supersedes #26285 against `litellm_internal_staging` after the previous base branch was deleted — fixes leaked `</think>` prefixes in non-streaming text blocks (`anthropic/chat/transformation.py:1670-1671` splits on first `</think>` and `.lstrip()`s, *after* `reasoning_content` accumulation so reasoning isn't lost) plus preserves `reasoning_content` in tool-call flows including the no-thinking-block tool-call case and the thinking-only case; 229 tests pass on the rebuilt branch across three Anthropic test files; the fix also includes follow-up review iterations preserving empty post-`</think>` text blocks as `text: ""` and making `_sanitize_think_tag_text_blocks()` side-effect-free; ~370 of the 388 added lines are mechanical PEP-585/PEP-604 type-hint modernization (`Dict → dict`, `Optional[X] → X | None`, `Union → |`) in `experimental_pass_through/adapters/transformation.py` that should ship as a separate "chore: modernize type hints" PR. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26040 (`3184b993`) auto-detects `windows-native` sandbox on win32 (`sandboxConfig.ts:104-107` — peer to `sandbox-exec` in the precedence chain ahead of docker/podman) and adds `sandboxType !== 'windows-native'` to the `isContainerSandbox` predicate at both `browserManager.ts:590` and `registry.ts:280` so the browser-agent path doesn't try container-style networking for the Windows native case; pinned by tests at `sandboxConfig.test.ts:157-168` and `browserManager.test.ts:1346-1365`. Bundles a cross-platform `disableUserInput` default flip from `true` to `false` at `settingsSchema.ts:1320` and `config.ts:3620` (predicate inverted from `!== false` default-on to `=== true` default-off) that the title only attributes to Windows but actually applies to all platforms — macOS/Linux upgraders silently lose the input-blocker default; PR description body is the unfilled template with no platform-validation checkboxes ticked. At block/goose, #8854 (`7c60c1b3`) removes the `defaults.yaml` mechanism from `config/base.rs` (-117 lines: the `defaults_path` field, `find_workspace_or_exe_root` discovery, `Config::new_with_defaults` constructor, `load_defaults` / `merge_missing_defaults` helpers, and the four `defaults_path: None` test-constructor inits) and replaces it with a security-specific `set_security_defaults()` (`security/mod.rs:21-32`) plus a `set_default_if_not_exist(config, key, default_env)` helper that seeds two specific env-var-backed keys at `SecurityManager::new()` and `goose-server::commands::agent::run` boot — correct architectural critique (one-consumer mechanism with a 5:1 plumbing-to-payload ratio), correct user-wins precedence (early-return if key already set), but deletes three automated tests pinning env > config > defaults precedence, fallback-not-persisted invariant, and key-only-in-defaults behavior, replacing them with "manual testing locally" — coverage regression that should be reversed by adding equivalent unit tests against `set_default_if_not_exist`.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is, 5 merge-after-nits, 0 request-changes, 0 needs-discussion. Detail: 3 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24572 one-line `value < 0` clamp in `getUsage`'s `safe()` helper preventing negative session cost when providers report `inputTokens` net-of-cache, with a regression test pinning the exact pathological shape from #22618; openai/codex #19832 surgical protocol extension adding `phase: Option<MessagePhase>` to `ResponseInputItem::Message` with the right `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` for backwards-compatibility, threaded through the `From` conversion so `Some(Commentary)` round-trips, with a regression test pinning the *Some* case at the exact conversion boundary the bug crossed; openai/codex #19771 architectural move of deferred-dynamic-tool filter from `build_prompt` downstream call site up into `ToolRouter::from_config` source so every `model_visible_specs()` caller gets correct-by-construction filtering — fixes the `/compact` 400 because compaction was bypassing the old downstream filter, with two-layer test coverage: unit test asserting `find_spec` still works + integration test asserting the actual compact request body has no `defer_loading` declarations), 4 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24544 message-position-not-ID loop-exit fix is correct framing of the custom-message-ID bug from #23490 — wants a regression test with non-monotonic IDs because without it the next refactor silently re-introduces the bug, and a one-line `// IDs are not ordered; use index for transcript order` comment; sst/opencode #24543 textbook capture-before-await + check-after-await race fix at the session route — but bundles an unrelated KV-toggle, a scrollbar theme tweak, and an empty `packages/sdk/js/openapi.json` that's almost certainly a `git add .` accident; should split scope; BerriAI/litellm #26614 correct `</think>` strip + reasoning-content preservation in tool-call flows fixing two real Anthropic regressions with 229 tests passing — but ~370 of 388 added lines are mechanical PEP-585/PEP-604 type-hint modernization that should be a separate "chore" PR so the bug fix is reviewable in isolation, plus one Pre-Submission `make test-unit` checkbox unchecked; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26040 correct win32 auto-detect + container-carve-out for `windows-native` sandbox with paired tests on both `sandboxConfig` and `browserManager` — but bundles a cross-platform `disableUserInput` default flip the title only attributes to Windows, plus the PR description body is the unfilled template with no platform-validation checkboxes ticked; block/goose #8854 correct architectural cleanup removing the `defaults.yaml` 5:1-plumbing-to-payload mechanism, with the right user-wins precedence in `set_default_if_not_exist` — but deletes three automated config-layer tests replaced with manual testing, a coverage regression that should be reversed by porting the env > config > defaults precedence, fallback-not-persisted, and seed-from-env-on-absent invariants to unit tests against `set_default_if_not_exist`).

### W17 drip-117 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). At sst/opencode, #24645 (`43f18a1a`) is a two-line guard inside `getCustomThemes()` (`tui/context/theme.tsx:500-504`) that runs each user-supplied theme JSON through the existing `isTheme(...)` validator before landing it in `result`, closing the *valid JSON, wrong shape* gap that was crashing the TUI at startup when malformed local theme files (e.g. `{}`, partial drafts) bypassed `Filesystem.readJson`'s syntactic check and reached downstream code that assumed every record entry had `scheme`/`palette`/etc. #24642 (`90c50fc2`) extends the existing `toolStreaming = false` carve-out at `provider/transform.ts:847-856` from just `@ai-sdk/google-vertex/anthropic` to also cover `@ai-sdk/anthropic` when the model id does not contain `"claude"` — i.e. third-party or proxied non-Claude models routed through the Anthropic SDK shape that don't implement streamed tool-use blocks the way real Claude does (closes #24627); the `includes("claude")` substring check is soft and not lowercased. At openai/codex, #19841 (`015d9276`) is a pre-stabilization removal of `FileSystemSpecialPath::CurrentWorkingDirectory` from the experimental `PermissionProfile` API — drops the variant from `protocol/src/permissions.rs` (+39/-39 with helper rename to `legacy_workspace_write_template()` to flag symbolic-not-resolved), removes the cwd-only session-update branch at `core/src/session/session.rs:+3/-37` so cwd bumps no longer secretly rebind permissions, and mechanically deletes the `CurrentWorkingDirectoryFileSystemSpecialPath` oneOf-branch from 18 regenerated JSON schema files (~270 lines); the rationale (one symbolic anchor not two) is correct but the regenerated schemas dropped trailing newlines and there's no `BREAKING:` callout for external consumers holding `kind: "current_working_directory"` payloads. #19839 (`79391f0f`) closes the rollout-trace-completeness gap where the inference node was left in `Running` whenever the consumer dropped `ResponseStream` before the provider sent `response.completed` — wires a `CancellationToken` between `ResponseStream` and the mapper task with a `Drop` impl on `ResponseStream` that fires `consumer_dropped.cancel()`, turns the mapper's `while let Some(event) = api_stream.next()` into a `loop { tokio::select! { _ = consumer_dropped.cancelled() => record_cancelled(...); event = api_stream.next() => event } }` (`client.rs:1635-1739`), and adds a `record_cancelled(reason, items_added)` payload variant that preserves partial output items observed before the drop; pinned by reducer tests across all three terminal cases plus an end-to-end `dropped_response_stream_traces_cancelled_partial_output` integration test that builds a real `TraceWriter`, drops the stream mid-flight, then `replay_bundle`s and asserts the `Cancelled` status, reason string, and partial items round-trip. At BerriAI/litellm, #26621 (`500b2a49`) closes the false-unhealthy ghost in the proxy UI for image/embedding/rerank/audio/OCR/search/moderation deployments — the health check was unconditionally injecting `max_tokens` via `_resolve_health_check_max_tokens` for every deployment but those non-chat endpoints reject `max_tokens` with a 400, so the UI marked working models as permanently unhealthy; fix is a 10-mode `_NON_CHAT_MODES` set membership check at `proxy/health_check.py:380-388` that gates the existing injection block, with a parametrized test covering each mode (fixes #26406). At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26042 (`82068989`) bundles a legitimate copy-mode fix (synthetic `HistoryItem` injection at the tail of the augmented history list when `inputState.copyModeEnabled` is true, plus hiding the static composer to free terminal rows, in `MainContent.tsx:+43/-6` and `Composer.tsx:+3/-3`) with a destructive global character substitution across 80+ unrelated files replacing U+2192 (`→`) with U+FFEB Halfwidth Rightwards Arrow (`￫`) in user-facing logs, code comments, and 40+ snapshot baselines — *and* a U+25CF (`●`) → U+2219 (`∙`) swap in the same snapshots, with the regression assertions updated to *match* the change rather than *reject* it; the Unicode swap belongs in sibling PR #26041 not bundled here. At block/goose, #8866 (`1ff16001`) is a two-line workflow glob fix in `.github/workflows/release-goose2.yml:166,184` changing `release/*.dmg` → `release/**/*.dmg` so the GitHub Release artifact upload step actually picks up the macOS DMG which is produced one directory deeper at `release/dmg/*.dmg` rather than flat in `release/` — without this the release run finished green with no DMG attached, classic empty-payload CI failure. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3682 (`7635f8c1`) backs out two `stripThoughtsFromHistory()` calls (one in `slashCommandProcessor.ts:649` `/load_history` branch, one in `Config.switchModel` at `config.ts:1426`) and flips the corresponding regression-test assertions from "called once" to "not called", citing maintainer decision in #3579 that strip-on-event was the wrong fix direction; observation about DeepSeek requiring reasoning preservation across turns is real, but the PR's own Risk section admits "Strict provider wire-format compatibility is not handled by history mutation anymore" and points at "provider-specific request-boundary sanitization work is not part of this PR" — meaning the fix is half-done and #3304 (the original 422-on-strict-OpenAI-compat bug that motivated the strip in the first place) will regress unless the request-boundary filter lands alongside.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24645 two-line `isTheme(...)` validator guard in `getCustomThemes()` closing the valid-JSON-wrong-shape startup crash; openai/codex #19839 `CancellationToken` + `Drop`-fires-cancel + `tokio::select!`-in-mapper architecture for partial-output-preserving cancel path with three-case reducer + integration test coverage; BerriAI/litellm #26621 10-mode `_NON_CHAT_MODES` set membership gate around `_resolve_health_check_max_tokens` injection with parametrized per-mode test; block/goose #8866 two-line `release/*.dmg` → `release/**/*.dmg` workflow glob fix), 3 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24642 non-Claude-on-Anthropic-SDK carve-out is correctly shaped but the `includes("claude")` substring check should be lowercased and a regression test should pin #24627; openai/codex #19841 cwd-special-path removal is correct with `legacy_workspace_write_template()` rename surfacing the previously-implicit "symbolic, resolve before use" contract — but regenerated JSON schemas dropped trailing newlines and the release notes need an explicit `BREAKING:` callout for external consumers holding `kind: "current_working_directory"` payloads), 1 request-changes (google-gemini/gemini-cli #26042 bundles a legitimate copy-mode synthetic-history fix with a destructive global U+2192 → U+FFEB Unicode swap across 80+ unrelated files including user-facing logs, code comments, and 40+ snapshot baselines updated to *match* the change rather than reject it; should split into two PRs and coordinate the glyph migration with sibling #26041), 1 needs-discussion (QwenLM/qwen-code #3682 backs out `stripThoughtsFromHistory()` from `/load_history` and `switchModel` to fix DeepSeek reasoning-preservation but the PR's own Risk section admits the original #3304 422-on-strict-OpenAI-compat regression isn't addressed at the request-boundary; the durable shape is per-provider serialization-time filtering with history kept canonical, not history mutation as wire-format shim).


### W17 drip-118 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24641 (`c1639f48`) is a two-line `await refreshDirs(target.directory)` before `canOpen` inside `openSession()` (and the same for the SDK-resolution branch) at `packages/app/src/pages/layout.tsx:1298,1311` — closes a Windows-CI race where `project.sandboxes` is populated asynchronously by `worktree.list` + `fsmonitor` but `navigateToProject` runs before that completes, so the workspace dir isn't in the cached `dirs` set, `canOpen` returns false, `openSession` bails silently, and the function falls through to navigate to the wrong URL (caught by `toHaveURL` in `projects-switch.spec.ts:90`). #24638 (`9cd9587b`) replaces `children().flatMap((x) => sync.data.permission[x.id] ?? [])` with `descendants().flatMap(...)` in the TUI session view's permissions and questions memos at `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/routes/session/index.tsx:160-165`, where the new `descendants` memo (`:136-156`) does an iterative DFS over `sync.data.session` building a `childrenByParent: Map<string, string[]>` index then walking from `session()?.parentID ?? session()?.id` — fixes the closes-#13715/#7654 bug where a subagent that spawns its own subagent that needs (e.g. bash) permission was hanging forever because the prompt was only being collected one level deep. Bundles a recursive `Task` subtree renderer with per-session stats (`taskSubtree`, `sessionStats`, `formatStats` at `:1989-2046`) and a `subagentLabel()` derivation in the permission prompt (`permission.tsx:429-444`) that strips a trailing `(@xxx subagent)` suffix from `session().title` so the user sees which subagent in the tree is requesting permission. At openai/codex, #19836 (`3bdb43a0`) is a small but security-meaningful tightening of `lookup_mcp_tool_metadata` so the `openai/fileParams` meta key (which makes a tool input parameter behave as a file-upload field on the Responses-API side) is honored *only* when the MCP server is the trusted built-in `CODEX_APPS_MCP_SERVER_NAME` — closes a quiet privilege-escalation shape where a malicious or sloppy custom MCP could otherwise advertise `openai/fileParams: ["arbitrary_param"]` and have codex silently treat it as a file-upload field, with a unit test at `mcp_tool_call_tests.rs:184-201` pinning both sides of the gate. #19848 (`2b511ae3`, DRAFT) is the third PR in a 5-PR stack that adds a preflight layer to the agent shell handler and `codex debug sandbox` — before exec, parses the bash/zsh `-lc` script via tree-sitter and rejects up-front if any literal redirection target or write-creating command (`mkdir`, `touch`, `cp`, `mv`) resolves under a preserved agent metadata path per the new `FileSystemSandboxPolicy` (with the new `parse_shell_lc_command_word_prefixes` helper at `shell-command/src/bash.rs:122-176` whose docstring is honest that it's "intentionally more permissive... use only for conservative deny checks, not for allow-listing"); reviewer-focus correctly punts security to the platform sandbox layers (this is UX), but the diff also bundles a substantial Linux signal-forwarding subsystem (`LINUX_SANDBOX_FORWARDED_SIGNALS`, `wait_for_debug_sandbox_child`, `recv_linux_sandbox_forwarded_signal`, `signal_debug_sandbox_child`, the `prctl(PR_SET_PDEATHSIG, SIGTERM)` + `getppid()` race-close in a `pre_exec` block) that's not preflight code and not mentioned in the title. At BerriAI/litellm, #26628 (`d22ffd94`, DRAFT) closes a real router-fallback bug where falling back from `gemini-2.5-flash` (429) to `gemini-2.5-pro` was forwarding `thinkingBudget: 0` to Pro, which Pro rejects with `400 INVALID_ARGUMENT — The model does not support setting thinking_budget to 0`, torching the fallback and surfacing as a hard error to the user even though both deployments are healthy — fix is a 4-line carve-out in `_map_thinking_param` at `vertex_and_google_ai_studio_gemini.py:1015-1020` gated by `thinking_budget > 0 or VertexGeminiConfig._model_supports_thinking_budget_zero(model)` (the helper at `:925-931` returns `"flash" in model.lower()` with the right `None`-guard), pinned by a 95-line test that asserts both the translation-layer behavior (Pro: no `thinkingBudget`; Flash: `thinkingBudget == 0`) and the Router-layer end-to-end fallback (`call_count == 2`, response content roundtrips, original `thinking` param preserved on the kwargs hitting Pro). #26627 (`2d5163e6`) is a one-line predicate widening to `is_anthropic_claude_model()` at `anthropic/experimental_pass_through/adapters/transformation.py:719-722` adding `or ("arn:" in model_lower and "bedrock" in model_lower)` so Bedrock Application Inference Profiles (ARN-shaped model identifiers) get correctly classified as Claude when routed through the `/v1/messages` adapter — without this, the existing substring check returned false for ARNs, the adapter silently stripped `cache_control`, and prompt caching never activated, with the PR's CloudWatch numbers making the cost impact concrete: `cacheWriteInputTokens` 0 → 34,004 on the same workload. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26063 (`5001f493`) tightens permissions on every directory and file written under `~/.gemini/` to `0o700`/`0o600` across 12 production files — closes #24743 by going beyond the reporter's named `ChatRecordingService` to fix a structural race where `ProjectRegistry` creates per-project slug dirs at `projectRegistry.ts:367` *before* the previously-patched `Storage.ensureProjectTempDirExists()` runs, and because `mkdir({recursive: true, mode})` is a no-op when the dir already exists, the later patches were silently ineffective for those parents (only leaf `.jsonl` files got `0o600`); end-to-end Docker-based verification with `stat -c "%a %n"` and an `alice` unprivileged user demo confirming `Permission denied` after the patch vs full-history dump before. At block/goose, #8788 (`02cc44e4`) fixes #8748 — every ACP-based provider was rendering streamed responses as many separate message bubbles in the desktop UI rather than one progressively-filling assistant message because `AcpProvider::stream` was yielding each chunk as a fresh `Message::assistant()` (which mints `id: None` + a fresh `Utc::now()` timestamp per call, and the desktop coalescing check requires both `lastMsg.id` truthy AND matching the incoming id); fix at `crates/goose/src/acp/provider.rs:339-525` introduces a `let mut text_run: Option<(String, i64)> = None` at the top of the `try_stream!` block, lazily allocates `(uuid, timestamp)` on the first text chunk via `text_run.get_or_insert_with(fresh_text_run).clone()`, attaches both to every `Text` and `Thought` yield, and resets `text_run = None` on every `ToolCallStart`/`ToolCallComplete`/`PermissionRequest` arm so post-tool text gets a fresh timestamp (the comment correctly names the `SessionManager` `created_timestamp`-ordered reload as the consumer that depends on this).

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (openai/codex #19836 server-allow-list at the lowest layer that handles file-param metadata with a unit test pinning both sides of the gate, exactly the right "capability-narrowing change that gets cheaper the earlier it lands" shape; BerriAI/litellm #26628 textbook router-fallback bug where translated request shape isn't portable across sibling models, fixed at the per-model translator with 3-layered test pinning translator behavior + end-to-end fallback path + user-supplied param preservation; block/goose #8788 30-line streaming coalesce fix using `Option<(String, i64)>` with `get_or_insert_with(fresh_text_run)` for lazy run allocation + reset on every tool/permission arm boundary, with a paragraph comment naming the `SessionManager` `created_timestamp`-ordered reload invariant that motivates the post-tool timestamp reset), 4 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24641 two-line `await refreshDirs(target.directory)` before `canOpen` inside `openSession()` for the Windows-CI workspace-dir race — closes #22051/#21172/#14461 but adds no regression test pinning the precondition and turns the cheap `canOpen` shortcut into a guaranteed network round-trip on cache miss without a comment naming the no-op invariant; sst/opencode #24638 BFS over descendants in the TUI permissions/questions memos closes the deep-nested-subagent-permission-prompt-hangs-forever bug, mirrors what web/desktop already does via `sessionTreeRequest()` — but `descendants()` rebuilds the full child index on every memo invalidation (O(n) over all sessions on every status update), `taskSubtree` glues subtask lines into one big text block rather than per-node JSX nodes, `sessionStats` only sums assistant tokens and per-tool counts double-count tools that appear in both assistant and tool messages, and the new `descendants()` traversal has no unit test; BerriAI/litellm #26627 one-line predicate widening adding `or ("arn:" in model_lower and "bedrock" in model_lower)` to `is_anthropic_claude_model()` correctly fixes the cache_control strip on Bedrock Application Inference Profiles with concrete CloudWatch numbers — but the new clause has no unit test, the docstring isn't updated to mention the ARN+Bedrock branch, and the predicate trusts that any `arn:...bedrock...` is Claude (true today via the `/v1/messages` routing invariant living in callers, not enforced by the predicate); google-gemini/gemini-cli #26063 substantive security fix with end-to-end Docker `stat -c "%a %n"` verification + `alice → Permission denied` demo across 12 production files — but `mode:` only applies on creation (existing installs need a chmod recipe in release notes), Windows is a no-op (NTFS uses ACLs), and only `ChatRecordingService` got intent-pinning test assertions while the other 8 modules use real temp dirs in tests so future fs.* writes there could silently drop the mode flag), 1 needs-discussion (openai/codex #19848 DRAFT preflight layer is a defensible UX shape but bundles a substantial Linux signal-forwarding subsystem under a "preflight" title without explanation, the parser is admittedly incomplete in ways the user-facing rejection string doesn't reflect — `bash -c "$(echo mkdir .codex)"` slips through but the message says "blocked because writes to protected path" — and there's no table-driven test suite for `parse_shell_lc_command_word_prefixes` covering write verbs and redirection forms named in the diff). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).

### W17 drip-119 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24653 (`2a119434`) adds an `ignore_instructions?: boolean` flag on `AgentInfo` (`packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts`) plumbed through `Session.chat()` so a designated agent (e.g. a benign summarizer or planner) can skip the per-message instructions block injection — narrow surface, opt-in default-false, exactly the right shape for a capability flag. #24652 (`daf72e46`) replaces a `Glob.scan` brace-expansion (`{config,agent,command}/**`) plus implicit ordering with three explicit `Glob.scan` calls and a `paths.sort()` at `packages/opencode/src/config/resource.ts:67-89` — closes a real cross-platform determinism gap (Bun's brace-expansion order isn't spec'd and differed between macOS and Linux CI), with the sort making resource-load order reproducible regardless of FS readdir order. At openai/codex, #19855 (`fa64a935`) is a one-line continue inside the `process_sse` event loop at `core/src/client.rs` so when a `response.output_item.done` arrives with `Reasoning` or `Commentary` content the loop keeps draining the stream rather than treating it as a terminal event — fixes a real "stream cuts off mid-reasoning" bug visible to users on long Commentary-heavy responses, with the right test pinning both Reasoning and Commentary cases. #19840 (`b1307c25`) introduces a `hooks/config/write` MCP tool plus the corresponding `HookConfigWriteParams` / `Response` types in `protocol/`, wiring through `core/src/hooks/` to persist hook config edits to disk via the existing `HookConfig` serializer; correct end-to-end shape but the new tool uses positional keys in the request schema (no `name` field on the entries array, just index-as-identity) and the impl has a `// TODO: support named keys` that should be resolved before the tool stabilizes since renaming/reordering hooks becomes a breaking change. At BerriAI/litellm, #26632 (`2eb9ffd1`) emits an `assistant` role on the first chunk of an SGLang streaming response even when the upstream sends `[DONE]` immediately with no content tokens — closes a hang where the OpenAI-compat client was waiting for a role assignment that never came, with a unit test pinning the empty-stream → `assistant` + empty-content shape. #26617 (`11f6a1c7`) forwards the deployment-level `use_chat_completions_api` from `litellm_params` through `Router._get_async_openai_client` to the request layer at `router.py:2847-2861` — closes #26580 where DB-stored model definitions weren't honoring this flag (only YAML-config models were), fix is the right plumbing but the new branch has no test pinning DB-loaded vs YAML-loaded parity and the param-forward could be folded into the existing `_get_litellm_params_to_pass_through` helper rather than a sibling carve-out. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26068 (`6060719b`) makes `mcp ping` optional in the `mcp list` command and routes ping timeout to the user-configured `mcpServers.<name>.timeout` rather than a hardcoded 5s — closes #26041-style hangs where slow-but-healthy MCP servers were being marked unhealthy in `mcp list` output, with the right two-prong fix (skip ping entirely with `--no-ping`, or honor configured timeout when pinging). At block/goose, #8867 (`e355740d`) adds an "Ocultar PII Refinery" extension entry to `documentation/src/pages/extensions/data/index.ts` pointing at a self-hosted PII-redaction MCP server — author is the same person as the extension owner (self-submission), entry includes a working `command` and `description` but no demonstration of provenance, no link to source, no security review of what data the extension exfiltrates; goose extensions catalog historically requires either community vetting or explicit maintainer sign-off for self-submissions, and the PR has neither.

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24653 narrow opt-in `ignore_instructions` agent capability flag with default-false safe semantics; openai/codex #19855 one-line continue in `process_sse` for Reasoning/Commentary `output_item.done` events with both-case test coverage; BerriAI/litellm #26632 assistant-role emission on empty SGLang stream closes the OpenAI-compat client hang with empty-stream shape test; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26068 `--no-ping` flag + configured-timeout routing for `mcp list` closes the slow-but-healthy-MCP false-unhealthy bug), 3 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24652 explicit three-call `Glob.scan` + `paths.sort()` correctly fixes the cross-platform brace-expansion ordering nondeterminism but should add a regression test pinning sorted order on a fixture with mixed config/agent/command paths; openai/codex #19840 `hooks/config/write` MCP tool end-to-end shape is right but uses positional keys with a `// TODO: support named keys` that turns rename/reorder into a breaking change once consumers depend on the index-as-identity contract; BerriAI/litellm #26617 deployment-level `use_chat_completions_api` forward closes the DB-vs-YAML parity gap but the new branch has no test pinning both load paths and the param-forward could be folded into `_get_litellm_params_to_pass_through` rather than living as a sibling carve-out), 1 needs-discussion (block/goose #8867 self-submitted Ocultar PII Refinery catalog entry has working command + description but no provenance link, no source-code link, and no security review of what PII data the extension processes/exfiltrates; goose extensions catalog historically requires community vetting or maintainer sign-off for self-submissions and this PR has neither — author should provide source link + security disclosure or a maintainer should explicitly sign off). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).

### W17 drip-120 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24662 (`64aae7e0`) is a small but real Zed-context-disappears-when-terminal-active bug fix at `tui/context/editor-zed.ts:8-130` — relaxes `editor_id` to nullable, adds `item_kind` to the row schema, switches the SQL `inner join editors` to `left join editors` and reads `i.workspace_id` not `e.workspace_id`, then classifies in TS so non-`Editor` active items return `unavailable` (the discriminant the consumer correctly treats as "keep prior context") instead of `empty` (which clobbered it). New type guard `isZedActiveEditorRow` narrows back to `ZedActiveEditorRow & { item_kind: "Editor"; editor_id: number }` so `queryZedEditorContents`'s parameter type statically guarantees non-null editor id, and a regression test pins the `unavailable` discriminant on a `itemKind: "Terminal", editor: false` fixture. #24659 (`e524a2c6`) adds two-line `process.on("SIGINT"|"SIGTERM", () => exit())` registration alongside the existing `SIGHUP` handler in `tui/context/exit.tsx:58-59` so systemd/Docker `kill -TERM` and parent-driven SIGINT trigger the same on-exit teardown — the issue is that the TUI input layer almost certainly already handles interactive Ctrl-C as a soft-cancel, and adding a process-level `SIGINT → exit()` handler races against that with no idempotency guard on `exit()` itself. At openai/codex, #19852 (`87b8a740`) materializes the `is_preserved_path_name` contract from the wider preserved-path stack into the Linux bwrap argv — introduces `SyntheticMountTarget` (empty file or directory bind-mounted RO over a real preserved location) and `ProtectedCreateTarget` (tombstone bind preventing creation) primitives in `linux-sandbox/src/bwrap.rs:104-225`, plumbs them through `BwrapArgs`, and has `linux_run_main` materialize/cleanup with a `FileIdentity { dev: u64, ino: u64 }` capture-and-compare so cleanup never deletes a host file whose inode no longer matches the pre-scan capture; ~270 lines of test cover missing/existing-empty/missing-dir cases plus the inode-mismatch skip path. #19849 (`5f6cf039`) closes a "runtime sandbox override silently doesn't take effect" bug in `tui/src/app/thread_routing.rs:530-617` — the turn-start was sending the new sandbox policy in the protocol envelope but leaving `permission_profile` from the stale pre-override config untouched, so the receiver derived the wrong profile; new helper `runtime_permission_profile_for_turn_start(runtime_override, sandbox_policy)` returns `None` when no override is set (let the receiver fall back), `None` for `ExternalSandbox` (external host owns the contract), and `Some(PermissionProfile::from_legacy_sandbox_policy(...))` otherwise, with the `app_server_session.rs:1107-1144` `turn_start_permission_overrides` simultaneously rewritten from a four-arm match to a clean predicate-then-branch form. At BerriAI/litellm, #26612 (`ebeec24d`) updates `model_prices_and_context_window.json` for the Snowflake Cortex REST API — bumps `claude-3-5-sonnet` 18k → 200k input / 8k → 16k output with full Anthropic feature flags (`supports_function_calling`, `supports_vision`, `supports_prompt_caching`, `supports_system_messages`, `supports_response_schema`) and per-token + cache-read pricing matching Anthropic's published rates, bumps `deepseek-r1` 32k → 128k input, and adds previously-missing per-token pricing on the Llama 3.1 family; indentation drift on the touched entries (4-space → 6-space on several opening braces) and no regression test pinning the new numbers. #26610 (`fd3eadbd`) bumps the chat-default `_resolve_health_check_max_tokens` fallback from 5 → 16 in `proxy/health_check.py:319-353` closing #23836 — Anthropic newer models reject very low caps with 400 and OpenAI-compat reasoning gateways pad reasoning tokens against the cap before producing visible content so 5 yielded empty completions that flipped deployments to `unhealthy`; pinning tests updated, plus an incidental test tightening from `not in updated_params or != 1` to the strictly stronger `"max_tokens" not in updated_params`. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26069 (`c29089fa`) closes a yargs-array-mishandling bug where `--model` passed twice produced `string[]` that downstream `resolveModel`/`isCustomModel` either threw on or silently coerced via `String([a,b])` to `"a,b"` and routed to a wrong-identifier custom-model fallback — fix is two-layer defensive flatten at the CLI boundary (`config.ts:818-826`) and inside `resolveModel` (`models.ts:109-119`), with `Array.isArray ? at(-1) : typeof !== string ? String() : trim()` semantics and the critical `undefined → undefined` carve-out preserved so the no-flag-passed default-fallthrough path still works; tests pin last-wins on dual `--model` and a non-string boolean coercion case. At block/goose, #8857 (`95a5facf`) introduces a `preserves_thinking: bool` capability flag on `DeclarativeProviderConfig` (default false, `skip_serializing_if = "is_false"` to keep diff-clean manifests) and turns it on for DeepSeek, Moonshot, Z.AI, Novita, NVIDIA, Tensorix, Zhipu, OpenCode-Go — these providers require prior-turn `<think>...</think>` blocks survive into subsequent assistant turns or they error/degrade on multi-turn; introduces `AnthropicFormatOptions` carrier and `create_request_with_options` so the Anthropic formatter is tunable per-provider, with `AnthropicProvider::format_options` using `#[serde(skip)]` so it doesn't pollute persisted config; only the Anthropic engine is wired through though, OpenAI-engine providers (the majority of the JSON additions) advertise the flag without anything in `providers/openai_compatible.rs` reading it.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24662 LEFT-JOIN + item_kind classification preserves Zed context when active pane is non-Editor with the right `unavailable` vs `empty` discriminant for the consumer; openai/codex #19849 `runtime_permission_profile_for_turn_start` helper closes the "runtime sandbox override silently doesn't take effect" bug with the right `None`-on-no-override + `None`-on-ExternalSandbox + `Some(from_legacy_sandbox_policy)` carve-outs and a clean four-arm-match → predicate rewrite at `app_server_session.rs:1107-1124`; BerriAI/litellm #26610 5 → 16 `_resolve_health_check_max_tokens` chat default closing #23836 with mechanical test updates plus an incidental `!= 1` → `not in` assertion-strength fix), 4 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24659 process-level `SIGINT`/`SIGTERM` → `exit()` registration races against the TUI input layer's interactive Ctrl-C cancel handler and lacks an idempotency guard on `exit()` for two-near-simultaneous-signals; openai/codex #19852 architecturally correct preserved-path bwrap enforcement with `FileIdentity { dev, ino }` capture-and-compare on cleanup but missing `mtime` in the identity tuple leaves a TOCTOU window with recycled inodes, and cleanup-skip uses `eprintln!` rather than a structured warning; BerriAI/litellm #26612 Snowflake Cortex pricing data is correct against the public rate page but indentation drifts 4-space → 6-space on several entries and no test pins the new numbers; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26069 yargs-array defensive flatten is correct two-layer fix but the boolean coercion test pins an unreachable shape and the two coercion sites should share a `coerceToModelString` helper to avoid future drift), 1 merge-after-nits-bordering-discussion (block/goose #8857 `preserves_thinking` capability flag is the right shape with default-false + diff-clean serialization but the field name describes an upstream *requirement* not a Goose-side *behavior* — should be `requires_thinking_in_history` — and the JSON additions advertise the flag on OpenAI-engine providers without anything in `providers/openai_compatible.rs` reading it, so either the OpenAI-compat formatter needs the same `_with_options` overload or the OpenAI-engine JSON additions should be deferred to a follow-up that lands with the formatter wiring). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).

### W17 drip-121 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24671 (`c3d10af7`) introduces an `omitUndefined<S>` Schema combinator at `packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/session.ts:45-58` that wraps `Schema.optionalKey(schema)` with a `decodeTo(Schema.optional(schema), {decode: passthrough({strict:false}), encode: transformOptional(Option.filter(v => v !== undefined))})` so on encode `Some(undefined)` collapses to absent rather than `null`, then rebuilds `SessionInfoResponse` via `Session.Info.mapFields(Struct.evolve({workspaceID: () => omitUndefined(WorkspaceID), parentID: () => omitUndefined(SessionID)}))` and swaps `success: Schema.Array(Session.Info)` → `Schema.Array(SessionInfoResponse)` at `:140` for `session.list` only — preserves the legacy session-list JSON shape (`parentID`/`workspaceID` omitted, not `null`) that strict-optional-key SDK consumers depend on; bundled with a 225-line `bun:test` → effect-test-helper migration of `httpapi-session.test.ts` that splits reviewer attention. #24666 (`e80afb35`) defines a new `"model.before"` plugin Hook at `packages/plugin/src/index.ts:240-259` with `(input: { sessionID, agent, model, message }, output: { providerID: string, modelID: string }) => Promise<void>` mirroring `chat.params`'s convention so the runtime pre-fills `output` with original IDs and treats plugin-non-mutation as no-op, dispatched in `packages/opencode/src/session/llm.ts:86-108` between the existing `l.info("stream", …)` log and `Provider.getLanguage`/`getProvider` resolution; if `rewrite.providerID/modelID` differ, re-resolves via `provider.getModel(rewrite.providerID as any, rewrite.modelID as any)` and reassigns `input.model = replaced` so all downstream logic sees the new model — enables hybrid local/cloud routing without a proxy in front of the agent, but the `as any` brand-cast and missing regression test are real gaps. At openai/codex, #19875 (`8cf1249c`) closes a privilege-escalation/UI-spoofing surface where custom (non-trusted) MCP servers could spoof `connector_*` provenance metadata to make the agent display `(via Gmail)` on tools they implement; new `sanitize_tool_connector_metadata(server_name, tool, connector_id, connector_name, connector_description)` (`rmcp_client.rs:369-382`) is a single-arm `if server_name == CODEX_APPS_MCP_SERVER_NAME { return ...; } strip_untrusted_connector_meta(tool); (None, None, None)` gate called from inside `list_tools_for_client_uncached`'s per-tool `.map(|tool| { ... })` (`:323-336`) *before* `normalize_codex_apps_callable_name`/`namespace` so normalized names can never embed stripped connector_name; `is_connector_meta_key` (`:390-393`) uses `key.get(..CONNECTOR_META_PREFIX.len()).is_some_and(|prefix| prefix.eq_ignore_ascii_case("connector"))` for UTF-8-safe case-insensitive ASCII prefix matching that catches `connector_id`, `connectorDescription`, `connectorFutureField`, `CONNECTOR_UPPERCASE`, etc., while preserving non-connector keys like `openai/fileParams` and arbitrary user `custom: "kept"` metadata; pinned by `custom_mcp_connector_metadata_is_stripped` test at `:425-441` constructing a tool with seven connector-flavored keys + two unrelated keys, asserting all three returned `Option<String>`s are `None`, iterating `meta.0.keys()` to assert no remaining connector keys, and asserting `meta.0.contains_key("openai/fileParams")` survives. #19859 (`a7674d66`) closes the visibility gap from sibling #19705 (which discovered bundled plugin hooks but didn't surface them in `/plugins`) — adds `PluginHookSummary { eventName: HookEventName, handlerCount: u32 }` to `app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2.rs:+9` and threads it through 14 files (3 generated JSON schemas, 1 generated TS binding, core plugin manager + `manager_tests.rs` + `app-server/tests/suite/v2/plugin_read.rs` end-to-end test, app-server processor, hook_config public surface, and a new `Hooks` row in the TUI `/plugins` detail popup); `PluginDetail` `required` array gains `"hooks"` in all three schema files which is a **breaking change for external strict-schema v2 consumers** with no `BREAKING:` callout in the PR title or body, and the `HookEventName` enum (`["preToolUse","permissionRequest","postToolUse","sessionStart","userPromptSubmit","stop"]`) has no inline reminder to regenerate JSON schemas after adding a variant. At BerriAI/litellm, #26634 (`1b0ef5f9`) closes a real silent-tool-drop bug where `SemanticToolFilterHook.async_pre_call_hook` was passing all tools (MCP + native) to `self.filter.filter_tools()` whose `SemanticRouter._tool_map` is built exclusively from the MCP registry at startup; native tools have no `_tool_map` entry so `_get_tools_by_names()` silently dropped them, the LLM responded in prose or upstream returned 400, and the tool-count stat read `7→5` looking like normal filtering; fix at `hook.py:126-141` is `_is_mcp_tool(tool: Any) -> bool` calling `self.filter._extract_tool_info(tool)` and checking `name in self.filter._tool_map` — docstring NOTE block names the load-bearing invariant that `_extract_tool_info` returns `""` for OpenAI-format `{"type":"function","function":{"name":...}}` dicts because the name is nested under `function`, not top-level, so empty-string-not-in-`_tool_map` is what classifies native function tools as native; `:236-289` partitions `native_tools` and `mcp_tools`, runs filter only on `mcp_tools` (skipped entirely if empty), returns `native_tools + filtered_mcp_tools`, and gates response-header metadata (`litellm_semantic_filter_stats`/`tools`) behind `if mcp_tools:` so all-native requests don't emit spurious `N->N` stats; pinned by `test_semantic_filter_hook_preserves_native_tools` (mixed 5+2) and `test_semantic_filter_hook_all_native_tools` (all-native pass-through). #26633 (`a64416b6`) fixes a crash-and-credential-leak in `MaskedHTTPStatusError.__init__` where rebuilding the request with the secret URL masked accessed `original_error.request.content` on a multipart streaming body (`image[]` + `mask` BytesIO streams from image-edit requests) raising `httpx.RequestNotRead` — the masking helper crashed and the original `HTTPStatusError` with the unmasked URL containing the API key in the query string propagated up; new `_build_masked_request(original_request, masked_url)` helper at `http_handler.py:348-365` wraps `original_request.content` in `try/except httpx.RequestNotRead` substituting `b""` and stripping `content-length`/`transfer-encoding` headers (load-bearing — without the strip the rebuilt `httpx.Request` would claim a body it doesn't have and crash again on later inspection); pinned by `test_handles_unread_multipart_request_content` at `test_credential_leak_prevention.py:139-159` building an actual `httpx.Request` with `files=[("image[]", ("image.png", BytesIO(b"image"), "image/png")), ("mask", ...)]` and asserting no-crash + URL mask + `content == b""` + `content-length == "0"` in one test, plus parallel `test_transform_image_edit_request_with_bytesio_mask_list` covering the request-build side that creates the unread-stream condition. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26074 (`d31b0158`) closes #26010 — `robustRealpath` (`packages/core/src/utils/paths.ts`) only special-cased `ENOENT`/`EISDIR`, so when a user pasted a JSON blob containing a long hex string or `@email.com` token the at-mention regex matched it as `atPath`, `checkPermissions` called `resolveToRealPath`, `fs.realpathSync` raised `ENAMETOOLONG`, the error rethrew unhandled, and the UI surfaced `CRITICAL: Unhandled Promise Rejection`; fix extracts a `hasErrorCode(e: unknown, codes: readonly string[])` helper at `:415-422` (`!e || typeof e !== 'object' || !('code' in e) ? false : typeof e.code === 'string' && codes.includes(e.code)`), refactors the two existing inline checks at `:432, :443`, and adds a new branch at `:451-458` for `['ENAMETOOLONG', 'ENOTDIR']` returning `p` unchanged — critically separate from the `ENOENT`/`EISDIR` branch because the parent-walk semantics (`return path.join(robustRealpath(parent, visited), path.basename(p))`) would be wrong when the basename itself is garbage; deferred to downstream `fileExists`/`fs.stat` callers (already handle `ENOENT` gracefully per `atCommandProcessor.ts:301`); pinned by paired regression tests at `paths.test.ts:564-580` (`ENAMETOOLONG` with 4096-char path) and `:583-595` (`ENOTDIR` with `/tmp/file.json/extra`). At block/goose, #8870 (`2f60a434`) fixes a token-under-reporting bug in `goose run --output-format stream-json` where the `complete` event was emitting `Session::total_tokens` (the *last turn's* context size, overwritten on every LLM round-trip and reset to summary output count after compaction) instead of `Session::accumulated_total_tokens` (the true running sum); downstream eval harnesses and billing dashboards interpret `total_tokens` as cumulative session cost and under-report by an order of magnitude — author's eval session that streamed thousands of chunks reported `total_tokens=17099` (just the final-turn context); fix at `session/mod.rs:67-78` extends `JsonMetadata` and at `:91-105` extends `StreamEvent::Complete` with `input_tokens`/`output_tokens: Option<i32>` (both `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` for wire compat); at `:1281-1296` and `:1299-1318` swaps the read source from `session.total_tokens` → `session.accumulated_total_tokens.or(session.total_tokens)` with the `.or()` fallback handling legacy/empty sessions where `accumulated_*` was never populated; struct docstring explicitly names the bug source ("`Session::total_tokens` ... is the last-turn context size and gets overwritten / reset on compaction — see `update_session_metrics` in `goose/src/agents/reply_parts.rs`") guarding against future "simplify by reading total_tokens directly" PRs.

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (openai/codex #19875 server-allow-list at the lowest layer that handles tool-definition ingest with case-insensitive ASCII-prefix `connector*` strip catching the whole namespace, paired test pinning both the strip and the preservation of unrelated meta keys; BerriAI/litellm #26634 root-cause partition fix with the right gate on response-header emission, paired regression coverage for both mixed and all-native cases, and a docstring naming the load-bearing `_extract_tool_info` empty-string-return invariant; BerriAI/litellm #26633 surgical fix at the exact crash site with the right header-reconciliation step (strip `content-length` + `transfer-encoding`) so the substituted empty body doesn't trigger a second crash, three layers of test coverage; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26074 correct root-cause analysis from paste → at-mention regex → `realpathSync` → `ENAMETOOLONG` → unhandled rejection, fix correctly scoped as separate branch from `ENOENT`/`EISDIR` because the parent-walk semantics differ for garbage-basename cases, helper extraction is a real readability win), 4 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24671 correct schema-encoding fix at the route layer but bundled with 225-line `bun:test` → effect-test-helper migration that splits reviewer attention plus `list`-only carve-out without justifying why `get`/`create` don't need the same `parentID`/`workspaceID` omit-not-null treatment; sst/opencode #24666 design is right (mirrors `chat.params`, narrow surface, opt-in default-passthrough) but the brand-cast `as any` should use `ProviderID.make()`/`ModelID.make()` and a regression test should pin the no-op-on-unchanged-IDs short-circuit; openai/codex #19859 symmetric end-to-end visibility fix with right test coverage at unit + integration layers but `required: ["hooks"]` schema change should be called out as `BREAKING:` for external v2 consumers and the `HookEventName` enum should grow a "regenerate schemas after adding a variant" comment; block/goose #8870 fix is correct (right field source, right `.or()` fallback for migration, right `skip_serializing_if` for wire compatibility) but `cargo test -p goose-cli` is unchecked in the Pre-Submission checklist and there's no serde-roundtrip unit test on the new `Complete` shape — for a fix that touches a public CLI output format, both gaps should close before merge). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).

### W17 drip-122 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24680 (`d6dc7179`) extracts a `matchAgentName(name, agents) → string | undefined` helper at `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/agent-match.ts:5-12` that prefers exact-match then falls back to a case-insensitive scan and returns the *canonical* name (not the user input) so downstream code keeps operating on the registered ID; both `--attach` and local agent-resolution branches in `run.ts:586-628` are rewritten symmetrically to thread `matched` through and the test trio at `test/cli/agent-match.test.ts:5-16` pins exact + case-mixed (`Build`, `PLAN`) + miss. The `get(name)` → `list()` swap on the local resolution path trades one server lookup for one list round-trip but is the only way to do case-insensitive matching against user-supplied IDs without a server-API change. #24673 (`7d48e354`) handles the stale `/session/:id` route after switching projects/servers — `isSessionNotFoundError(error, sessionID)` walks `error.cause` to handle the SDK's wrapping and tolerates string/`Error`/plain-object shapes (the three SDK error forms historically observed), then on the foreground catch path shows a toast and `navigate("../", { replace: true })` (the `replace: true` is load-bearing — without it the user's "back" button would re-navigate to the missing session); the scheduled-refresh path silences the same error class via `console.debug` so backgrounded stale tabs stop emitting console noise. Three-times-repeated match predicate at `:97-99,109-111,122-124` is a maintenance hazard. At openai/codex, #19880 (`6502bbed`) closes the gap where Guardian/proxy network denials inside the Windows sandbox capture path could only terminate by waiting for the timeout — adds a `WindowsSandboxCancellationToken` predicate primitive at `windows-sandbox-rs/src/lib.rs:11-35` (closure-based contract keeps the crate independent of `tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken`), threads it through `ExecExpiration::cancellation_token()` at `core/src/exec.rs:225-233` → both elevated and non-elevated capture entry points, and `spawn_cancel_writer` at `elevated_impl.rs:118-145` spawns a parker-thread that polls every 50ms via `thread::park_timeout` and writes a `Message::Terminate` framed message to the runner pipe on detection. The capture loop at `:246-285` is rewritten from `?`-propagating `read_frame(...)?.ok_or_else(...)?` to `match read_frame { Ok(Some) => ... | Ok(None)/Err => break Err }` so the cancel-writer cleanup at `:286-291` (`done.store(true); cancel_handle.thread().unpark(); cancel_handle.join(); drop(pipe_write)`) always runs even on read error; without this restructure a `?` short-circuit would have leaked the cancel thread. Regression test pins that cancellation is not reported as timeout. #19867 (`0f697bc4`) is a one-line `#[ignore = "TODO: legacy non-tty cmd.exe fails with ERROR_DIRECTORY in CI"]` on `legacy_non_tty_cmd_emits_output` at `windows-sandbox-rs/src/unified_exec/tests.rs:139` — preserved-path stack invalidates the test target through its protocol dependency, the test stops coming from cache and exposes a previously-cached `CreateProcessAsUserW` error 267, adjacent legacy cmd.exe tests already carry the same marker; correctly split out from the policy work so reviewers can land CI stabilization independently. At BerriAI/litellm, #26642 (`b953bfe2`) closes #26413 — clients sending Ollama-native `"think": false` in a chat-completion request body had it filtered out by `pre_process_non_default_params` (because `think` isn't in `DEFAULT_CHAT_COMPLETION_PARAM_VALUES`) so reasoning-capable models still ran reasoning and the response carried `reasoning_content`/`thinking_blocks`/non-zero `reasoning_tokens`; fix is `_think_to_reasoning_effort` helper at `utils.py:3683-3759` plus an early `_think_value = special_params.pop("think", None)` capture at `:4067` (load-bearing — `pop` not `get` so the field can't leak to providers that pass kwargs through unchanged) and a late translation at `:4150-4160` (positioned after `supported_params` is resolved so the gate can check `"reasoning_effort" in supported_params`, before `_check_valid_arg` so the translated value survives the validator). The `_PROVIDERS_SUPPORTING_REASONING_DISABLE` allowlist (`gemini`, `vertex_ai`, `vertex_ai_beta`, `ollama`, `ollama_chat`) is the critical correctness gate — Anthropic/Bedrock/OpenAI o-series advertise `reasoning_effort` as supported but only accept `low|medium|high|minimal|none|None` and would `BadRequestError` on `"disable"`; on those providers `think:false` is silently dropped with a debug log. Allowlist set is rebuilt per-call inside the helper body (should be hoisted to module-level frozenset) and dead truthy-string parse generates only a debug log. #26604 (`cfe0b774`) closes #26406 permanent-unhealthy-status bug for `image_generation`/`video_generation`/`embedding`/`rerank`/`audio_speech`/`audio_transcription`/`ocr`/`responses`/`batch` deployments — `_filter_model_params` was only stripping `messages` so `max_tokens` (also injected unconditionally) flowed through to handlers that don't accept it, OpenAI's `dall-e-2`/`dall-e-3`/`gpt-image-1` reject unknown params with `400 Unknown parameter: 'max_tokens'`, other providers silently dropped it masking the bug; fix introduces module-level `_NON_CHAT_HEALTH_CHECK_STRIP_KEYS = {"messages", "max_tokens"}` constant at `health_check_utils.py:5-12` and uses set membership in `_filter_model_params`, with the docstring naming the load-bearing invariant that `litellm.acompletion` is the only mode handler bypassing this filter so chat health checks remain unaffected. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26075 (`fc950075`) is a low-quality bounty-spam submission — claims to close #20569 (Conflicting privacy info for Google AI Pro re: model training) but the entire diff is a new file `tos-privacy.html` at the repository root containing 8 lines of *Markdown* (not HTML) reading like an automated bounty template (`# Bounty Contribution`, `## Task: ...`, `**Reward: $20**`, `**Source: GitHub-Paid**`, `**Date: 2026-04-28 04:35:34.749868**`); PR body is in Chinese declaring `$20 GitHub-Paid` reward auto-generated by "the bounty hunter system + Feishu notification" (`由赏金猎人系统自动生成 + 飞书通知`); sibling #26076 from same author is a duplicate submission. File is misnamed (`.html` extension with markdown body), at the wrong location (repo root not `docs/`), and contains zero actual content addressing the privacy-docs conflict — should be closed not merged. At block/goose, #8869 (`2efc7957`) fixes a "WSL2 user gets a Windows .exe installed into a Linux bin directory" install bug at `download_cli.sh` — was using `$PWD =~ ^/mnt/[a-zA-Z]/` as a signal that the user "wanted the Windows build" even though `uname -s` correctly reported `Linux`, so a developer running `cd /mnt/c/users/me/proj && curl ... | bash` got `goose-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip` installed into `$HOME/.local/bin/` followed by config attempts against Windows `AppData\Roaming` paths that don't exist on Linux; fix collapses the 13-line WSL detection branch (with `powershell.exe`/`cmd.exe` interop heuristics that were pure WSL-false-positive generators since WSL ships these on PATH) to a 3-line `OS="linux"` block with an inline comment naming the invariant ("WSL is a Linux environment regardless of the current working directory. The PWD does not change the kernel"); preserves the Git Bash heuristic (`/[a-z]/` PWD + `/c` or `/d` exists) which is legitimately needed for actual-Windows Git Bash environments. Net 27-line deletion of broken heuristic.
