Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24680 case-insensitive `--agent` resolution helper that returns the canonical name not the user input so downstream lookups keep working, 3-test coverage; openai/codex #19867 single-line `#[ignore]` with explicit reason for a legacy cmd.exe test exposed by parallel preserved-path-stack cache invalidation, follows existing pattern in the file; BerriAI/litellm #26604 module-level `_NON_CHAT_HEALTH_CHECK_STRIP_KEYS` set with set-membership filter at `_filter_model_params`, docstring names load-bearing "acompletion is the only unfiltered consumer" invariant; block/goose #8869 net 27-line deletion of PWD-based WSL→Windows misdetection with inline invariant comment "PWD does not change the kernel"), 3 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24673 SDK-error-cause-walk + foreground-toast + scheduled-refresh-silence is correctly scoped but the triple-repeated `(name, message)` match predicate is a maintenance hazard, `return ""` discards the resource value without comment, and the `navigate("../", { replace: true })` parent-route assumption isn't named; openai/codex #19880 cross-process cancel writer with parker-thread + atomic done flag + capture-loop restructure to `match-and-break-with-result` is correctly designed to keep cancel-writer cleanup reachable on every exit path, but the 50ms poll interval is a magic number, the unpark-then-join sequence isn't commented, and the `let _ = write_frame` swallow loses diagnostic signal; BerriAI/litellm #26642 capture-early translate-late shape with provider-allowlist gate is correct and `pop`-not-`get` is load-bearing, but the allowlist set is rebuilt per-call inside the helper body — should be hoisted to module-level frozenset — and the dead truthy-string parse only fires a debug log), 1 request-changes (google-gemini/gemini-cli #26075 bounty-spam orphan `.html` file with markdown body at repo root, claims to close #20569 but contains zero actual content addressing the privacy-docs conflict, sibling #26076 is a duplicate submission from the same author, classic "automated bounty harvester" three-signal pattern: foreign-language body describing the bounty system, file content that is metadata about the bounty rather than a fix, and a duplicate sibling PR). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).

### W17 drip-123 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). At sst/opencode, #24682 (`cd472620`) is the test-side complement to last drip's #24671 schema fix — adds nested-undefined-omit coverage at `test/session/session-schema.test.ts:167-188` (proves `summary.diffs` and `revert.{partID,snapshot,diff}` survive `Schema.encodeUnknownSync(Session.Info)` as omitted keys, not explicit `null`s) plus a new HTTP-API parity harness at `test/server/httpapi-json-parity.test.ts:1-148` that boots the route stack twice (with `Flag.OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_HTTPAPI` toggled), seeds parent+child sessions + one user message + one text part via the `Session.Service` Effect layer, and walks seven endpoints (`session.list?roots=true`, `session.list`, `session.get`, `session.children`, `session.messages`, `session.message`, experimental `session`) asserting `expect({label,body}).toEqual({label,body:legacy})` on each. Three foot-guns avoided: `Flag` reset in `afterEach`, sequential `.reduce(promise, ...)` chain at `:143-146` instead of `Promise.all` (which would race seven `Instance.provide` calls against shared tmpdir state), and `pathFor` at `:51-53` reduces template `:sessionID`/`:messageID` placeholders before request-time so failures point at actual URLs not templates. The `{label, body}` envelope-on-`toEqual` is a quietly-clever bun:test ergonomic that gives endpoint-labeled diff output without a custom matcher. #24678 (`c338facc`) refactors the desktop Electron auto-updater gate from a flat `app.isPackaged && CHANNEL !== "dev"` ternary to a pure 4-arg function `isUpdaterEnabled({isPackaged, channel, platform, appImage})` at the new file `packages/desktop-electron/src/main/updater-support.ts:7-13`, and rewrites `constants.ts:11-15` to call it with `{appImage: process.env.APPIMAGE}` — the `APPIMAGE` env-var sniff is the canonical Linux-AppImage detection signal (set by the AppImage runtime stub before exec, unset for `.deb`/`.rpm`/Flatpak/Snap) so the previous "auto-updater attempts an update on every Linux install regardless of packaging format and ends in a confusing 'cannot find update channel' error" failure mode is closed for non-AppImage Linux installs. Function extraction makes all 4 branches testable in `updater-support.test.ts:32-51` (unpackaged → false, dev channel → false, packaged win32/prod → true, packaged linux/prod with `appImage: undefined` → false, packaged linux/prod with `appImage: "/tmp/OpenCode.AppImage"` → true) which the prior module-load-time ternary couldn't be. At openai/codex, #19884 (`111edb60`) is the textbook minimum-surface feature-flag registration: enum variant `Feature::EnableMcpApps` at `codex-rs/features/src/lib.rs:147-148` placed alphabetically between `Apps` and `ToolSearch`, `FeatureSpec { stage: Stage::UnderDevelopment, default_enabled: false }` at `:836-841` (the conservative pairing — lowest visibility stage + opt-in even on bleeding-edge), plus mechanical `"enable_mcp_apps": {"type": "boolean"}` injections at the same alphabetical slot in both schema sections of `config.schema.json` (`:400-411` per-project + `:2607-2618` global). Zero behavioral surface — no `is_enabled(Feature::EnableMcpApps)` call anywhere in the diff, so the only user-visible change is "users can write `enable_mcp_apps = true` without an unknown-key warning". The split-from-consumer-wiring cadence is the right one for any flag whose first consumer is more than a few lines: lets the registration be a no-op-by-construction PR (verifiable by grep), lets the consumer PR focus reviewer attention on actual logic. #19882 (`7656221d`) is the broader companion to last drip's #19859 plugin-hooks-panel — adds a top-level TUI `/hooks` browser plus a new `is_managed: bool` field on `HookMetadata` that distinguishes plugin-shipped hooks from user-authored ones. Three layers: protocol bump at `app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2.rs:4490-4493` + four mirrored JSON schemas + regenerated TS type (the new field lands in the `required` array in all three schema mirrors, which is the strict version-bump signal — every external `hooks/list` consumer fails schema validation on missing `isManaged`); engine layer at `hooks/src/engine/mod.rs:74-77` + `discovery.rs:453-456` populates from `source.is_managed` (correct location because `is_managed` is a property of *how the hook entered the engine*, not how the UI displays it, so CLI/JSON consumers get it for free); TUI layer at `tui/src/app/background_requests.rs:92-100` + `:212-225` adds `fetch_hooks_list` (UUID-stamped `ClientRequest::HooksList` per cwd, dispatches `AppEvent::HooksLoaded { cwd, result }`) and `set_hook_enabled` (issues `write_hook_enabled`, dispatches `AppEvent::HookEnabledSet { result }`). Engine unit test at `mod_tests.rs:122-130` calls public `crate::list_hooks` API not engine internals, so any future regression where the field populates correctly in `HookListEntry` but is lost on the way to API surface fails this test. Adds `EnumIter` to `HookEventName` derive at `protocol/src/protocol.rs:1610` (used by the new browser to enumerate event-name display groups). Schema-break callout missing from PR description (same nit as #19859), `EnumIter` derive needs comment naming load-bearing TUI consumer, and `AppEvent::HooksLoaded { cwd, result }` carries no request ID for de-duplication of rapid-cwd-switch in-flight requests. At BerriAI/litellm, #26648 (`44923f26`) closes the bug where the Guardrail Monitor admin endpoints (`/guardrails/usage`, `/guardrails/usage/<id>`, `/guardrails/usage/logs` at `litellm/proxy/guardrails/usage_endpoints.py`) only queried Prisma so users with YAML-defined guardrails saw an empty Monitor UI even though the guardrails were running and logging metrics. Fix unions DB+in-memory sources at `:286-301` with `seen_ids` dedup-by-`guardrail_id` (DB wins on collision, matching proxy convention "DB is live source of truth, YAML is bootstrap default"), detail-endpoint fallback at `:340-343` falls back from `find_unique` to `IN_MEMORY_GUARDRAIL_HANDLER.get_guardrail_by_id` and only 404s if both miss. Two new helpers consolidate prior repetition: `_get_guardrail_field(g, field)` abstracts dict-vs-Prisma-row polymorphism via `g.get(field)` for dicts + `getattr(g, field, None)` for rows; `_to_dict(value)` correctly handles `LitellmParams` via `model_dump(exclude_none=True)` (load-bearing because `IN_MEMORY_GUARDRAIL_HANDLER.list_in_memory_guardrails()` returns dicts whose `litellm_params` field is a `LitellmParams` instance, not a plain dict like the Prisma `Json` column, so the prior `isinstance(g.litellm_params, dict)` check would have silently coerced to `{}` and lost the `guardrail` provider key). Fix uses `if guardrail is None` (not the prior `if not guardrail`) which fixes a latent edge case where a row with all-default-falsy fields would have wrongly tripped the old branch. `_to_dict` only handles `LitellmParams` not the general `BaseModel` case, no new test coverage for the YAML-visibility path. #26636 (`2e3da159`) closes the silent-tool-call-drop bug for `mlx_lm.server` and any other OpenAI-compatible provider that emits multiple parallel function calls in one delta — the downstream converter `_translate_streaming_openai_chunk_to_anthropic_content_block` at `AnthropicStreamWrapper` indexes `tool_calls[0]` (assumes one tool_call per delta), so chunks with N tool_calls had calls 2..N silently dropped without an error visible to the Anthropic-format consumer. Fix introduces `_MultiToolCallSplitter` at `:30-86` that wraps the upstream stream and emits at most one tool_call per delivered chunk by splitting any `delta.tool_calls`-length-> 1 chunk into N single-call chunks via `copy.deepcopy`. Three things right: dual-protocol lazy binding at `:60-86` (sync `__iter__/__next__` + async `__aiter__/__anext__`, lazily binds on first call because upstream may expose both protocols and binding at construction breaks whichever is unused); `copy.deepcopy` of *both* the chunk *and* each `one_tc` at `:133-138` is load-bearing (without inner deep-copy of `one_tc`, mutations downstream on `sub.choices[0].delta.tool_calls[0]` like argument-delta accumulation in subsequent chunks would alias across split chunks producing confusing "the second tool call's arguments got appended to the first" bugs visible only on long argument streams); separate-attribute storage at `:111` (`self._completion_stream_splitter` not overwriting `self.completion_stream`) preserves upstream attribute access and keeps static analyzers happy. `chunk == "None"` literal-string check at `:119` is unusual (suggests upstream serialization bug papered over here, should be commented or fixed at source), no unit test for `_split_chunk_by_tool_calls` static surface. At block/goose, #8868 (`3f6fb45b`) migrates the canonical project-scoped writable skills location from `<project>/.goose/skills` to `<project>/.agents/skills` (composing with Claude `.claude/skills` and the broader `.agents/skills` convention), makes `.agents/skills` the highest-priority project source so it shadows legacy `.goose/skills` on collision. `crates/goose/src/skills/mod.rs:8-13` rewrites `project_skills_dir`, `:20-25` reorders `all_skill_dirs` so `.agents/skills` is searched first then `.goose/skills` then `.claude/skills`. Strongest piece is the collision-precedence test at `crates/goose/src/sources.rs:75-113` (`project_sources_prefer_agents_directory_over_legacy_goose`) that constructs same-skill-name in both directories and asserts listed result has length 1, points to `.agents/skills`, has the "preferred" description not "legacy", and the exported source contains agents-directory content — pins the precedence semantic through the public `list_sources` + `export_source` API not just the directory-walker function in isolation. UI side bumps `SkillsView.tsx` file-size exception to 620 lines with justification naming five distinct concerns ("centralizes list/detail state, project-aware skill hydration, category/source filtering, import/export flows, and detail-page action wiring pending a later decomposition") — code-smell escape hatch since the "pending a later decomposition" hedge suggests the author knows. No migration story for legacy `.goose/skills` users (no startup deprecation log), no tracking issue linked for the SkillsView decomposition. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3683 (`4c7994dd`) is a bulk SHA-bump sweep across roughly a dozen workflow files: `actions/configure-pages` v5→v6, `actions/upload-pages-artifact` v3→v5, `actions/deploy-pages` v4→v5, `actions/create-github-app-token` v2→v3.1.1, `docker/setup-qemu-action` v3→v4, `docker/setup-buildx-action` v3→v4, `docker/metadata-action` v5→v6, `docker/login-action` v3→v4.1.0, `docker/build-push-action` v6→v7, `dorny/test-reporter` v2→v3.0.0, `thollander/actions-comment-pull-request` v3→v3.0.1, `google-github-actions/run-gemini-cli` v0→v0.1.22. Right cadence (all ratchet-tracked actions in one PR — splitting 12-ways would 12x review overhead with no safety gain since actions are independent) and the SHA-pinning convention (`owner/repo@<full-40-char-SHA>`) is preserved throughout (the security-relevant supply-chain mitigation). Blocking nit: comment-format drift — existing convention is `# ratchet:owner/repo@vN` (literal `ratchet:` prefix is the parser anchor for the ratchet tool's "is this action up to date?" detection), new lines use `  # vN.M.P` (no `ratchet:` prefix, double-space indent, exact version string). The next time `actions/checkout@v5` ships v6 the tool won't know to bump `actions/configure-pages` because its line no longer matches the parser regex. Inconsistency is the worst outcome — half the lines parse, half don't. Also two major Docker-action bumps (`setup-buildx-action` v3→v4 drops Node 16 runner, `build-push-action` v6→v7 changes default Buildx version + cache-export shape) need a successful end-to-end CI run on a test branch.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is / 5 merge-after-nits / 0 request-changes / 0 needs-discussion. 3 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24682 nested-undefined-omit test + 7-endpoint HTTP-API JSON parity harness with `{label,body}`-on-`toEqual` envelope and sequential `.reduce` against shared `Instance` state; sst/opencode #24678 pure-function extraction of Electron auto-updater gate with `APPIMAGE` env-var sniff for Linux AppImage detection and 4-branch test coverage; openai/codex #19884 minimum-surface feature-flag registration with `Stage::UnderDevelopment + default_enabled: false` conservative pairing and zero downstream consumers in the diff), 5 merge-after-nits (openai/codex #19882 top-level `/hooks` browser + `is_managed: bool` propagation through engine + protocol + TUI layers correctly populated at discovery from `source.is_managed` so CLI/JSON consumers get it for free, but the schema `required` bump should carry a `BREAKING:` callout and the new `EnumIter` derive on `HookEventName` needs a load-bearing-consumer comment; BerriAI/litellm #26648 DB+YAML guardrail union with `seen_ids` dedup and `_to_dict` polymorphic-shape helper handling `LitellmParams` via `model_dump(exclude_none=True)`, but `_to_dict` only handles `LitellmParams` not the general `BaseModel` case and no new test coverage for the YAML-visibility path; BerriAI/litellm #26636 `_MultiToolCallSplitter` with dual-protocol lazy binding + correct deep-copy of both chunk and tool-call to prevent argument-delta aliasing across split chunks, but `chunk == "None"` literal-string check at `:119` should be commented or fixed at source and no unit test for `_split_chunk_by_tool_calls` static surface; block/goose #8868 `.goose/skills` → `.agents/skills` canonical-path migration with strong collision-precedence test pinning the semantic through public API, but no startup deprecation log for legacy `.goose/skills` users and `SkillsView.tsx` file-size exception bumped to 620 lines is a code-smell escape hatch with no decomposition tracking issue linked; QwenLM/qwen-code #3683 bulk ratchet-tracked SHA bump across 12+ workflow files preserving SHA-pinning supply-chain mitigation but breaking the `# ratchet:owner/repo@vN` comment-format parser anchor on every bumped line — half the file parses, half doesn't, future ratchet automation silently turns off until the comment format is reconciled, blocking nit on comment-format consistency). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).

### W17 drip-124 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (2 sst/opencode, 3 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli). At sst/opencode, #24674 (`ba5f4c70`) adds a single-line guard to the `renderer.console.onCopySelection` callback at `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/app.tsx:296` returning early when `Flag.OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_DISABLE_COPY_ON_SELECT` is set, restoring terminal-native selection for users on tmux/Windows Terminal where opencode's auto-copy was racing the terminal's own selection-buffer commit and visually clearing the highlight mid-drag — guard placement is correct (after the empty-text early-return at `:295`, before `Clipboard.copy(text)`), the `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_` prefix correctly signals "behavior may change without deprecation", and reading the flag at every callback invocation rather than caching at assignment time is the right choice for env-var-backed flags. #24646 (`1c5c6935`) appends `opencode-honcho` (Honcho-backed long-term memory plugin from plastic-labs, the same org shipping Honcho itself) as a single new row in the Plugins table at `packages/web/src/content/docs/ecosystem.mdx:55` — first-party plugin from the upstream memory product, padding/alignment consistent with surrounding rows, description fits the one-sentence-capability convention. At openai/codex, #19881 (`258951b6`) collapses the bifurcated `mcp_tools` / `deferred_mcp_tools` data flow into a single `HashMap<String, McpToolInput>` where each entry carries explicit `defer_loading: bool` (`mcp_tool_input.rs:483-486`), `ToolRouter` now registers specs for *both* direct and deferred tools (closing the discrepancy where dynamic deferred tools could be invoked but their specs were missing from `router.specs`), and renames `router.specs()` → `router.specs_including_deferred()` for clarity — the rename is the load-bearing signal forcing every call site through review, the fast-path (small candidate set) at `mcp_tool_exposure.rs:38-50` flat-maps with `defer_loading: false` for every entry, the slow path at `:54-76` correctly removes direct tools from the candidate set before iterating remaining → reinserting with `defer_loading: true` so no tool ends up double-mapped. Asymmetry between input-side `bool` and registry-side `Option<bool>` (where `None` means non-MCP / always visible) reads as inconsistent — needs a comment to prevent a future cleanup PR from collapsing them and accidentally hiding non-MCP specs. #19878 (`963fcb88`) adds an end-to-end stderr-span-telemetry ingest path so a local stdio MCP server (specifically `node_repl`) can emit `@codex-telemetry`-prefixed JSON spans on stderr and have them reconstructed inside codex's OTel pipeline as children of the `node_repl.js` tool-call span: request-side `_meta` augmentation at `mcp_tool_call.rs:723-776` only fires for `server == "node_repl" && tool_name == "js"` (`:730`) and pulls `codex_otel::current_span_w3c_trace_context()` for the active span — bails if no traceparent is present so requests outside an active OTel span produce no telemetry meta at all rather than emitting placeholder zeros; `mcp_connection_manager.rs:113,1639-1655` gates telemetry sink registration with early `None` return for non-node_repl servers (correct narrow scoping — allowing arbitrary MCP servers to emit OTel spans into codex's pipeline would be a trust escalation since any local MCP could forge spans claiming to be parented under any trace); `stderr_span_telemetry.rs:544-548,630-670` enforces strict validation (`v` must be `1` else `UnsupportedVersion`, `type` must be `"span"` else `UnsupportedType`, span name must be in an allowlist) with `UnsupportedVersion` / `UnsupportedType` deliberately separated from generic `InvalidField` errors so the caller at `mcp_connection_manager.rs:53-65` can demote them to `tracing::debug!` while treating malformed records as `warn!` — correct because a future v2 schema rolling out from the node_repl side shouldn't spam the codex warning channel. `CODEX_TELEMETRY_STDERR_PREFIX = "@codex-telemetry "` (with trailing space) prevents partial-match misclassification. #19870 (`cbd37741`) refactors live assistant TUI streaming from queue-based transient-cell-run with `ConsolidateAgentMessage` walk-back to a single source-backed active markdown cell from the first delta, then commits that same cell to history on `ItemCompleted` — eliminates the consolidation-event walk-back entirely (`AppEvent::ConsolidateAgentMessage` removed at `app_event.rs:392-404`, handler arm removed at `app/event_dispatch.rs:206-230`), simplifies `should_mark_reflow_as_stream_time` from four conditions to two (active plan stream OR trailing `ProposedPlanStreamCell` run, agent-stream conditions gone because the active cell commits atomically on completion), `controller.active_cell()` returns a fresh `AgentMarkdownCell` constructed from accumulated `markdown_source` + `cwd` so resize during stream produces correctly-wrapped output instead of fixed-width-relict lines. Test at `app/tests.rs:3914-3978` pins both invariants — feeds an `AgentMessageDelta` and asserts no `InsertHistoryCell` is emitted yet (deltas update the active cell in place), then feeds an `ItemCompleted` and expects exactly one `InsertHistoryCell` whose underlying type is `AgentMarkdownCell`, renders at width 100 vs 28 to assert the cell re-flows, and critically asserts the draft text is *not* present in the final render (pinning that the final commit replaces the draft, doesn't append to it). At BerriAI/litellm, #26651 (`ea0ce944`) halves the per-token costs for `gpt-5.5-pro`, `gpt-5.5-pro-2026-04-23`, and the `azure/` namespaced variants of both in `model_prices_and_context_window.json` and the bundled `litellm/model_prices_and_context_window_backup.json` — prior values were exactly double the rates published on OpenAI's pricing page (so every billing/cost calculation against these IDs was over-reporting by 2x), the consistent halving across all 8 cost fields (cache_read, input, input_above_272k, output, output_above_272k for all entries plus flex/batches for non-azure entries) is consistent with "the original entry doubled the per-1M-token rate when computing per-token", both the canonical and bundled fallback files updated together (must move together because the bundled copy is the offline fallback when the canonical can't be fetched), and `test_llm_cost_calc_utils.py:241,275-278` test fixtures and parametrized cases updated symmetrically so the suite validates the new prices not the prior wrong ones. #26645 (`6d6be44b`) adds three opt-in fields to `GenericAPILogger.__init__` — `max_retries: int = 0`, `retry_delay: float = 1.0`, `timeout: Optional[Union[float, httpx.Timeout]] = None` — and threads them through `LoggingCallbackManager._add_custom_callback_generic_api_str` so they're configurable via `litellm.callback_settings[name]`; new `_post_with_retries` helper at `generic_api_callback.py:262-285` wraps the prior bare `async_httpx_client.post` with an attempt loop that retries transient errors (litellm timeouts, httpx transport errors, HTTP 5xx) with exponential backoff and re-raises non-retriable errors (4xx, etc.) immediately — `_should_retry_exception` at `:240-247` is the load-bearing classifier (`isinstance(exception, (litellm.Timeout, httpx.TransportError))` covers the full transient-network set since `TransportError` is the parent of `ConnectTimeout`/`ReadTimeout`/`ConnectError`/`PoolTimeout`, and `HTTPStatusError` only retries when `response.status_code >= 500` so 4xx auth/permission/payload errors short-circuit immediately — correct policy because retrying a 401 on a callback endpoint will never recover and just delays the visible failure). `:256-285` builds `post_kwargs` once outside the loop including `timeout` only if configured (so `None` doesn't accidentally override the httpx client's default), `is_last_attempt = attempt == self.max_retries` with `if is_last_attempt or not should_retry: raise` re-raises the original exception unchanged on the last attempt or any non-retriable error preserving stack traces, defaults preserve backward compatibility (`max_retries=0` means exactly one attempt with no retries), cache key includes the new fields at `logging_callback_manager.py:246-248` so a config change forces logger re-instantiation. Test coverage covers all three classifier branches (`_retries_timeout_then_succeeds`, `_retries_5xx_then_succeeds`, `_does_not_retry_4xx` asserting `mock_post.assert_called_once()`). At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26078 (`d683bbca`) fixes the DevTools fetch interceptor at `packages/cli/src/utils/activityLogger.ts:patchGlobalFetch` (`:302-330`) so it correctly preserves headers, method, and body when the caller passes a `Request` object as the first argument to `fetch` rather than splitting metadata into the `init` parameter — prior implementation only read from `init` so any caller using the Request form (notably `ky` for update checks against private npm registries that require auth) had its `Authorization` header silently stripped at the interceptor and rejected `403` upstream; new precedence is `init.method || inputMethod || 'GET'` matching the actual semantics of `new Request(input, init)` where `init` overrides when both are provided, headers extract `inputHeaders` from `Request.headers` first as the base layer then layer `init.headers` on top via `forEach((value, key) => headers.set(...))` — `set` (not `append`) is correct because per-`init`-override semantics require replacement not stacking; body-extraction guard at `:329-333` is intentionally *not* extended to read `input.body` (Request bodies are streams that once read can't be re-read; pulling `await input.text()` to log the body would consume it before the real `originalFetch(input, newInit)` call) — correct conservative choice since body logging is best-effort and dropping it for Request-form callers is better than breaking the request. Test builds an actual `Request` with `Authorization: 'Bearer test-token'` + `method: 'POST'` and asserts the mocked underlying fetch saw both the auth header *and* the activity-id header *and* the POST method on `calledInit`.

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24674 surgical 1-line guard at the exact callback site with correct ordering relative to the existing empty-text early-return and opt-in via explicitly experimental flag; sst/opencode #24646 minimal docs append for a real first-party plugin from credible upstream with no behavior changes; BerriAI/litellm #26651 straightforward pricing correction with right 1-to-1 test update covering all four affected model IDs and both files updated together; BerriAI/litellm #26645 opt-in feature with `max_retries=0` default preserving prior behavior, conservative classifier policy retrying only transient errors, test coverage exercising all three classifier branches plus config-plumbing path, and cache-key update preventing stale-logger reuse), 4 merge-after-nits (openai/codex #19881 correct refactor closing the deferred-spec discrepancy with right data-shape collapse to single map with explicit per-tool flag, but `Option<bool>` vs `bool` asymmetry between input and spec sides needs a comment and `model_visible_specs()` behavior on `defer_loading: None` specs needs regression test; openai/codex #19878 right design with narrow scope to one named server + one named tool, strict version/type validation with demotable error variants, and W3C trace context propagation via standard `_meta` channel, but allowlisted-name set needs top-level documentation, sink callback exhaustiveness needs explicit marker, and end-to-end test would close contract; openai/codex #19870 substantial simplification removing a class of race conditions by construction with well-targeted test coverage, but `BREAKING:` callout missing for dropped `AppEvent` variant, `controller.active_cell()` allocation cost contract needs documenting, and plan-stream-vs-assistant-stream pattern asymmetry in `should_mark_reflow_as_stream_time` needs a one-line comment; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26078 correct fix closing the auth-header-stripping bug with body-extraction guard correctly *not* extended to avoid consuming the Request body, but missing test for `Headers` layering precedence between Request and init, no test for `URL`-input case, and the `init`-overrides-Request precedence rule needs a WHATWG fetch-spec reference comment). Repo coverage: 4 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli).


### W17 drip-125 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 1 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24693 (`8f8a976c`) closes the parity gap between the experimental HttpApi session routes and legacy `/session` around query-string boolean parsing — prior `roots`/`archived` typed as `Schema.Literals(["true","false"])` rejected `?roots=1`/`?roots=false`/empty values with 400 while legacy accepted "any present non-empty value as truthy"; fix widens both schemas to `Schema.optional(Schema.String)` at `experimental.ts:54-66` + `session.ts:39-49` and routes through a shared `legacyQueryBoolean(value) => value ? true : undefined` helper (the `undefined`-on-falsy branch is load-bearing because `Session.list` interprets `false` differently from omitted), with the helper duplicated locally rather than imported (defensible isolation choice but needs a `// keep in sync` cross-reference comment), `before` guard tightened from `!== undefined` to truthy at `:476-479` (subtle: `?before=` empty now routes through the no-cursor path instead of 400 — pinned by new `session.messages empty before` parity-harness row at `httpapi-json-parity.test.ts:135-139`), and three new parity-harness rows at `:107-138` exercising `?roots=false`/`?roots=1`/`?before=` for both routes via the existing `expectJsonParity` envelope. #24691 (`cd9645d5`) adds an opt-in `order: PositiveInt` field to agent config so users can pin Tab-cycling order — runtime `Info.order: Schema.Number` at `agent.ts:36` (asymmetric with config-side `PositiveInt` at `config/agent.ts:38`, intentional but worth a comment), comparator chain at `agent.ts:288-294` is the load-bearing piece with three sort keys (default-agent-first, then `(x) => x.order ?? Infinity` ascending, then alphabetical tiebreaker) and the `?? Infinity` sentinel correctly partitions ordered-first then alpha-second, `"order"` added to `KNOWN_KEYS` at `:67` so unknown-key warning doesn't fire, two new tests at `test/agent/agent.test.ts:420-491` covering both default-agent-first invariant (default `build` with `order: 2` lands at index 0 because default-agent comparator slot wins over `order`) and mixed ordered+unordered partitioning. At openai/codex, #19860 (`a650996b`) is the second installment of the memories-package split — `Cargo.lock` shape reverses the dep edge so `app-server`/`exec` gain `codex-memories-write` while `core` loses both `codex-memories-write` and `codex-secrets`, new `SessionSource::Internal(InternalSessionSource::MemoryConsolidation)` variant added across 4 enum exhaustive matches (`agent-identity/src/lib.rs:325`, `analytics/src/reducer.rs:109`, `app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2.rs:1998-2002` mapping `Internal(_) => SessionSource::Unknown` to elide internal sources from the wire), `clear_memory_roots_contents` import moves `codex_core` → `codex_memories_write` at `codex_message_processor.rs:317`, `StartThreadWithToolsOptions` → `StartThreadOptions` rename signals options-shape generalization to include `session_source: Option<...>`, `auth_manager` added to `ListenerTaskContext` so the new `start_memories_startup_task(thread_manager, auth_manager, thread_id, thread, memory_config, session_source)` call at `:2689-2696` can fire in the background after the config_snapshot is sent. Every regenerated JSON file lost its trailing newline (`\ No newline at end of file`) — `ts-rs` generator glitch worth a post-step fix to silence one-byte-per-regeneration diff noise. At BerriAI/litellm, #26654 (`e43ad465`) extends the cross-pod Redis spend-counter pattern to `spend:end_user:*` and `spend:tag:*` and adds matching in-process cache-update paths for team-membership and org budget objects — end-user budget check at `auth_checks.py:656-672` now reads via `get_current_spend(counter_key=f"spend:end_user:{user_id}", fallback_spend=...)` so multi-pod fleets stop letting end_users run N×budget before any single pod tripped the check, `_tag_max_budget_check` at `:3820-3845` applies the same shape per-tag with the import correctly hoisted outside the loop, reseed at `spend_counter_reseed.py:104-114` adds two new prefix branches with right Prisma table targets (`litellm_endusertable.find_unique` keyed on `user_id`, `litellm_tagtable.find_unique` keyed on `tag_name`), `increment_spend_counters` at `proxy_server.py:1816-1937` gains `end_user_id` and `tags` kwargs with tag writes built as `asyncio.gather`'d coroutines (correct concurrency choice for independent per-tag work), defensive `if tag_name and isinstance(tag_name, str)` filter drops `None`/empty/non-string tags from user metadata, two new cache-update closures `_update_team_membership_cache` and `_update_org_cache` at `:2206-2273` (the org cache walks both `org_id:{id}` *and* `org_id:{id}:with_budget` keys because the budget-bearing variant is independently cached and would diverge otherwise), all gated on `user_id+team_id` and `org_id` respectively to prevent `None`-key writes. #26643 (`abbe5d7f`) rewrites `/config/update` for *targeted per-section writes* — prior shape did `proxy_config.get_config()` (loaded full YAML+DB merged view) → mutated requested sections → `proxy_config.save_config(new_config)` (serialized whole merged view back to DB), persisting pre-existing YAML values to DB as a side effect of any unrelated update; new shape introduces inline `_read_section`/`_upsert_section` closures and writes only the sections present in the request, drops the blanket `if store_model_in_db is not True: raise 500` guard (correct: none of the four sections this endpoint writes are part of the model-store), preserves the legacy *existing-wins* merge for `litellm_settings` (load-bearing — admin UI updates intentionally don't clobber YAML-seeded callback config except via the explicit `success_callback` union path) but uses *request-wins* for `router_settings` (the asymmetry is policy-deliberate but needs comments naming why), `general_settings` keeps the `alert_to_webhook_url` side-effect of auto-enabling slack alerting now applied to DB-loaded `existing` not the merged view (subtle YAML-blindness worth naming). Test rewrite at `test_proxy_server.py:2764-2815` drops the `store_model_in_db = True` setattr and the `MockProxyConfig.get_config`/`save_config` machinery, asserts `upserted["litellm_settings"]["success_callback"]` directly — sharper assertion that pins the section-targeted invariant by structural shape (only modified sections appear in `upserted`, not all four). New 241-line test file at `tests/test_litellm/proxy/management_endpoints/test_update_config_endpoint.py` introduces a reusable `FakeLitellmConfig` fixture. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26073 (`0438c09b`) is a multi-part follow-up to a prior generalist-profile refactor with three load-bearing pieces — `ContextManager.assemble()` at `contextManager.ts:175-195` now passes its rendered history through a new `hardenHistory(...)` step that appends a `"Please continue."` user-role sentinel turn whenever history would otherwise end on a model turn (closing the gap where the LLM saw its own last turn as the "expected response" slot rather than as prior context); `ContextGraphBuilder` at `toGraph.ts:43-118` is rewritten from instance-state-bearing (`this.episodes`/`currentEpisode`/`pendingCallParts`/`pendingCallPartsWithoutId`) to a *pure* `processHistory(history) -> ConcreteNode[]` method with all working state in locals, `clear()` and `getNodes()` removed, mapper at `mapper.ts:32-40` reduced to just `return this.builder.processHistory(event.payload)` so the prior `CLEAR`/`SYNC_FULL` event-type branches that drove `clear()` calls become no-ops because every call rebuilds from scratch (eliminates a class of state-leak bugs by construction); `pendingCallPartsWithoutId` legacy fallback for tool-call/response pairing on missing-ID parts is removed entirely, with `parseToolResponses` at `:144-156` logging structured `MISSING_CALL` errors via `debugLogger.error` and `parseModelParts` at `:243-251` silently dropping ID-less function-call parts (correct policy decision but should also surface to telemetry not just debug log). `contextManager.barrier.test.ts:73-84` updated to assert the sentinel at `[length-1]` and walk back two extra slots for the original last user/model — correctly pins the hardener invariant alongside the barrier invariant. The `debugLogger.log(...)` summary that JSON-pretty-prints every assembled history will be very high-volume — needs gating on a verbose level. #26066 (`435e4a6c`) closes #25898 ("selected model silently degrades to fallback Flash on a single transient API failure") — prior `transient` state-transition policy was `terminal` (one transient error → walk the chain), conflating rate limits/503s/network blips with permanent unavailability; fix changes 14 `stateTransitions.transient` fields (7 chain entries × 2 files: executable defaults at `defaultModelConfigs.ts` + IDE settings-schema mirror at `gemini-cli-vscode-ide-companion.json`) from `"terminal"` → `"sticky_retry"`, with the dual-copy markdownDescription-vs-default-object kept in sync (correct: drift means IDE settings UI shows users a stale default they can't actually get). The change applies uniformly including to `isLastResort: true` Flash entries — sticky-retrying a last-resort entry on transient errors is a different policy than sticky-retrying a primary entry (last-resort means the user has *already* exhausted the chain, so transient failures there could now produce invisible-retry-storm UX) and deserves a deliberate decision rather than uniform substitution. Pre-merge checklist's "Updated relevant documentation" and "Noted breaking changes" both unchecked despite this being a user-visible behavior change (workflows that depended on silent-degrade-to-Flash semantics will now stick on Pro). At block/goose, #8808 (`fe8cc369`) is a single-file blog post (95-line markdown + one PNG cover image) introducing goose's new built-in local-inference path (llama.cpp embedded in-process, GGUF model download, no Ollama dependency) — well-framed user-impact-first opening, ten-row comparison table vs Ollama (Setup/Server/Model format/Model management/Tool calling/Vision/Config rows) is the most load-bearing piece and is honestly framed (explicitly notes built-in uses GGUF from HuggingFace vs Ollama's own registry, and that built-in tool calling is "Native (Gemma 4) or emulated"), the "What to expect" section sets honest expectations with concrete numbers ("first request takes 30–120 seconds", "4K–8K tokens of context vs 128K+ for Claude/GPT"), names `~/.local/share/goose/models/` as the model storage location (discoverable when users grep for missing downloads). Three editorial nits: "As at the time of this writing" on the featured-models list is a maintenance hazard (link to canonical catalog instead), the privacy claim "No telemetry, no API calls, no data sharing" should be scoped to the inference path (not blanket the whole product if goose desktop has any background telemetry/update-checks), and the last featured-model bullet is truncated mid-sentence ("for users").

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24693 surgical schema-widening that closes a real parity gap with load-bearing helper rebuilt at the right shape for the underlying `Session.list` API plus three new parity-harness rows whose endpoint-labeled diff catches future divergence by construction; BerriAI/litellm #26654 extends well-established counter-first/cached-fallback/reseed-aware pattern to the two remaining entity kinds with all write/read/reseed paths wired symmetrically and the dual-cache-key org update correctly handled), 6 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24691 right feature design with opt-in field and conservative `PositiveInt` constraint at config boundary plus default-agent invariant preserved by sort-key precedence and two real tests covering both happy paths, but runtime-vs-config schema asymmetry and missing `order: 0` rejection test should close before merge; openai/codex #19860 dep-graph reversal correctly executed across the workspace with compiler-enforced exhaustive matches making the change visible and the new `Internal` source kind appropriately scoped, but every regenerated JSON file lost trailing newline as `ts-rs` glitch and the `Internal → Unknown` protocol shim should debug-log the conversion; BerriAI/litellm #26643 targeted-write rewrite is right architectural fix for the side-effect bug and test rewrite sharpens assertions to pin the new contract structurally, but `litellm_settings` existing-wins vs `router_settings` request-wins merge-policy asymmetry needs comments and the `store_model_in_db` gate removal needs a release-notes line; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26073 three pieces individually well-motivated (hardener closes real history-shape bug, builder rewrite removes state-leak bugs by construction, dropping ID-less fallback is right policy decision for provider conformance) but diagnostic-logging volume needs gating, unmatched-call signals should reach telemetry not just debug log, missing-behavior guard should fail loudly in dev, and `ContextGraphBuilder` API removal needs external-caller sweep; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26066 fix is right shape (single transition policy update applied uniformly via configuration not code change) and dual-copy default kept in sync, but the change has user-visible behavior implications needing release notes/docs and the last-resort-entry interaction needs a deliberate decision rather than uniform substitution; block/goose #8808 solid honest blog post setting realistic expectations for hardware-dependent feature with the right comparison-with-Ollama positioning, but editorial nits remain — scope the privacy claim, fix truncated featured-model bullet, drop or re-anchor "as at the time of this writing", add image alt text, note model licensing). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).

### W17 drip-126 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (2 sst/opencode, 3 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24692 (`6da33466`) is a 2-line correctness fix at `packages/opencode/src/project/project.ts:199-200` swapping the `worktree: "/"` / `sandbox: "/"` defaults for non-git projects to the actual resolved `directory` — the `"/"` default was a real footgun where any tool whose access list checks `worktree` (file gates, sandbox bind mounts, pwd-relative shell exec) would have allowed reads anywhere on the host filesystem treating the entire host as in-scope. Placement is right (inside the `if (!dotgit)` branch after `directory` is settled, before the `ProjectID.global` return) and `fakeVcs` is unaffected. Worth a regression test asserting `worktree === directory` for the no-`.git` branch since the wrong-state was silent. #24666 (`e80afb35`) adds `model.before` plugin hook firing inside the LLM dispatch path at `packages/opencode/src/session/llm.ts:86-108` with output pre-filled with `{providerID, modelID}` so a non-rewriting plugin is a no-op; change-detection at `:103` avoids re-resolving when no-op. The `as any` casts at `:104` rely on `getModel` for runtime validation — fine but should be commented to prevent a future cleanup PR from tightening them and breaking the hook. `input.model = replaced` mutation at `:108` is OK only because no other reader has captured the old reference yet, but a non-mutating local would survive a future telemetry refactor. Plugin contract docstring at `packages/plugin/src/index.ts:243-259` is good but missing the multi-plugin ordering semantic — without that two routing plugins will silently conflict. At openai/codex, #19895 (`ac0b9f51`) adds external-agent session detection/import/compaction with a wire-protocol break: schema renames `MigrationDetails` → `ExternalAgentConfigImportMigrationDetailsParams` and `ExternalAgentConfigMigrationItem` → `ExternalAgentConfigImportMigrationItem` at `app-server-protocol/schema/json/ClientRequest.json:811-879`, removes `MigrationDetails` entirely, adds `SESSIONS` enum variant correctly placed last so existing variants don't shift. Rename hostility to schema diff tools earns a `BREAKING:` callout requirement. Compaction-before-first-follow-up is the most dangerous piece — needs explicit determinism test (same source session → same compacted rollout) so the import ledger dedup works. #19875 (`8cf1249c`) is a real security fix at `codex-rs/codex-mcp/src/rmcp_client.rs:369-392` — untrusted MCP servers can no longer forge `connector_*` metadata to get their tools classified as belonging to a trusted connector (Gmail/GDrive/etc.). `sanitize_tool_connector_metadata` is the single-purpose gate (trusted server passes through; untrusted has connector triple forced to `None` and `strip_untrusted_connector_meta` runs). Stripping is case-insensitive prefix-match (`prefix.eq_ignore_ascii_case("connector")`) catching `connector_id`, `connectorDescription`, `CONNECTOR_UPPERCASE`, `connectorFutureField`. Allowlist test enumerates exactly the trick-key shapes — the test is the spec for the policy. The hoist of `let mut tool_def = tool.tool` *before* the sanitize call so meta mutation operates on the same object that's later moved into `LoadedTool`, plus rebinding `connector_id`/`connector_name`/`connector_description` from sanitized return values not original struct fields, is load-bearing — without that reorder the stripped meta and the unstripped struct fields desync and the trusted-connector-name normalization at `:332-336` still fires on untrusted inputs. #19855 (`fa64a935`) is a 10/-1 line fix at `codex-rs/core/src/session/turn.rs:1899-2157` adding `should_continue_sampling_assistant: bool` carried alongside `needs_follow_up` set per output item (`Reasoning` always continues, `Message{role: assistant, phase: Some(Commentary)}` continues, everything else doesn't), OR'd into `needs_follow_up` at the break point — closes the gap where the model emits Commentary-phase or Reasoning items without immediately producing tool calls and the sampling loop would terminate early. Per-iteration assignment at `:2032` is **last-write-wins** not OR-accumulating — subtle policy choice that's probably right (Final-phase is terminal even after Commentary) but uncommented and the variable name doesn't hint at it; a future "fix" to `|=` would silently change semantics. `MessagePhase::Commentary` match guard at `:2011` is the load-bearing classifier; if the model can produce a runaway `[Commentary, Commentary, ...]` sequence, this introduces a new infinite-loop risk at the sampler level. No test added — at minimum a unit test feeding a single Reasoning item asserting `needs_follow_up == true` would pin the new behavior. At BerriAI/litellm, #26653 (`26064667`) fixes embedding-cache loss of `prompt_tokens_details` for multimodal providers (Bedrock Titan, Nova, Vertex AI) — extends `_convert_to_cached_embedding` to thread `prompt_tokens_details` through all three serialization branches at `litellm/caching/caching.py:653-685`, adds `_get_per_item_prompt_tokens_details` helper at `:687-731`, calls helper at `:745-749`. Distribution policy at `:719-728` is right shape for arithmetic correctness — integers use `divmod(value, num_items)` with remainder distributed to first `remainder` items so summing reconstructs the original (avoids the "9/4 = 2 each, total 8 ≠ 9" undercount), floats use even division (precision loss bounded by float epsilon × num_items), non-numeric pass through. Single-item short-circuit at `:715-716` is load-bearing for the *primary* fix target. `else: return None` at `:707` for unsupported types silently drops without a debug log — for an obscure provider this means caching silently loses details with no log signal. No test for the float-distribution branch. #26650 (`6c62c5ca`) closes the bug where mixed requests (MCP refs + native OpenAI function tools) silently lost all native tools after MCP expansion because `_expand_mcp_tools` only returned expanded MCP tools and `_parse_mcp_tools` discarded everything else. Fix at `litellm/proxy/hooks/mcp_semantic_filter/hook.py:266-276` materializes `native_tools_before_expand` *before* expansion via shape-match, then concatenates `data["tools"] = native_tools_before_expand + expanded_tools` (native-first ordering consistent with non-expand path). New `_is_mcp_tool` classifier at `:126-150` is shape-first (always-native if `dict + "function" + "type"`) then name-lookup fallback — pre-expanded MCP tools also match the OpenAI shape and would otherwise be misclassified, which the docstring correctly defers to the caller's `tools_expanded_from_mcp` flag. New `_emit_filter_metadata` at `:160-198` scopes header counts to MCP-only and skips emission entirely for purely-native requests (avoids misleading `5->5` headers). Native-tool preservation list-comp at `:271-275` duplicates the `_is_mcp_tool` shape check — should be extracted to `_is_native_openai_tool(tool)` before drift on adding a third tool shape. At block/goose, #8870 (`2f60a434`) is a 36/-5 line surgical fix at `crates/goose-cli/src/session/mod.rs` switching the `total_tokens` field reported in JSON-output and stream-json `Complete` events from `session.total_tokens` (last-turn context size, **gets reset on compaction**) to `session.accumulated_total_tokens.or(session.total_tokens)` — closes a real bug where downstream consumers tracking cost/usage saw a value bearing no relation to actual session usage (compaction would zero it mid-session). The `.or(...)` fallback is load-bearing for sessions that predate the cumulative field. Adds matching `input_tokens` / `output_tokens` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` so the JSON shape extension is purely additive. The block comment at `:67-71` explicitly names the trap with cross-reference to the function responsible for the reset (`update_session_metrics` in `goose/src/agents/reply_parts.rs`) — exactly the kind of comment that prevents the bug from being reintroduced by a well-intentioned cleanup PR.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24692 surgical 2-line correctness fix closing a silent over-broad sandbox default for non-git projects with right placement and obviously correct semantics; openai/codex #19875 real security fix gating connector-metadata forgery from untrusted MCP servers via case-insensitive prefix-strip plus single-purpose sanitize gate plus load-bearing struct-field rebinding from sanitized return values, with the test enumerating exactly the trick-key shapes serving as the spec for the policy; block/goose #8870 surgical reroute of `total_tokens` from compaction-resetting last-turn field to cumulative-accumulator field with backward-compat `.or` fallback, additive JSON shape extension via `skip_serializing_if`, and a load-bearing block comment naming the trap with cross-reference to the resetting function), 5 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24666 right-shape `model.before` plugin hook with output pre-filled for no-op behavior and change-detection avoiding re-resolution, but `as any` casts need a "runtime-validated" comment, in-place mutation of `input.model` should become a non-mutating local, and multi-plugin ordering semantic is missing from docstring; openai/codex #19895 broad external-agent session import with right enum-extension cadence and `null` branch preserved in `details` shape, but the `MigrationDetails` rename is a wire-break needing `BREAKING:` callout and the compaction-before-first-follow-up needs explicit determinism test for ledger dedup correctness; openai/codex #19855 surgical `should_continue_sampling_assistant` flag closing the Commentary/Reasoning early-termination gap, but last-write-wins assignment policy is uncommented and a future `|=` "fix" would silently change semantics, runaway-Commentary infinite-loop risk needs reasoning, and no test pins the new behavior; BerriAI/litellm #26653 right arithmetic distribution with `divmod`-and-remainder for integers preserving sum-reconstruction invariant plus single-item short-circuit for the primary fix target, but unsupported-type silent-drop needs a debug log and no test for float-distribution; BerriAI/litellm #26650 correct shape-first/name-fallback classifier with native-first concatenation order matching non-expand path and MCP-only filter-stats scoping avoiding misleading `5->5` headers, but native-tool list-comp duplicates the shape check and should be extracted to `_is_native_openai_tool` before drift). Repo coverage: 4 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, block/goose).

### W17 drip-127 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (2 sst/opencode, 3 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24702 (`be8dae4e`) is a surgical 1-line fix at `packages/opencode/src/session/session.ts:611` that closes a real cross-session-reference bug when forking sessions containing `compaction`-type parts — every other field on the cloned part was being remapped through `idMap` (so `messageID`/`sessionID`/`id` all resolved into the new session's ID space) but `tail_start_id`, the field compaction parts use to point at where the post-compaction tail begins, was being silently inherited from the source. Conditional spread `...(part.type === "compaction" && part.tail_start_id && { tail_start_id: idMap.get(part.tail_start_id) })` is the right shape (type-narrows on variant, guards on truthiness, uses the existing `idMap` so the remapped target is guaranteed to resolve in the cloned session). #24680 (`d6dc7179`) extracts agent-name resolution out of `RunCommand` into a pure helper at `cli/cmd/agent-match.ts:5-11` with a two-pass (exact-then-case-folded) lookup — exact-first ordering is load-bearing because two agents legitimately differing only by case (`Build` vs `build`) must still resolve to the typed casing when it matches exactly; the load-bearing piece of the fix is `return matched` (canonical name) at both call sites in `cli/cmd/run.ts:586-628`, replacing prior `return name` (user's typed casing) — without this, downstream session creation/agent lookup/event emission would silently bifurcate state by keying on different casings of the same logical agent. The second call site swaps `svc.get(name)` for `svc.list()` + linear scan — small cost regression worth either a `getCaseInsensitive` API or a comment. At openai/codex, #19880 (`6502bbed`) wires `CancellationToken` through to the Windows sandbox spawn path, resolving a `TODO(iceweasel-oai)` and closing the gap where network-denial inside the sandbox would hang until timeout instead of unwinding immediately on outer cancel. New `ExecExpiration::cancellation_token()` accessor at `core/src/exec.rs:225-233` extracts the inner token from the two carrying variants with platform-scoped `#[cfg_attr(not(target_os = "windows"), allow(dead_code))]` to silence Linux/macOS dead-code lints, and exhaustive match arms ensure compiler-enforced visibility on future variant additions. Cancel-writer thread at `windows-sandbox-rs/src/elevated_impl.rs:118-148` is the load-bearing translation: polls a 50ms `park_timeout` loop, on cancellation writes a `Message::Terminate` framed message into the sandbox IPC pipe (fire-and-forget — silent on write failure since a broken pipe means the sandbox is probably already gone). The closure-wrap `WindowsSandboxCancellationToken::new(move || token.is_cancelled())` keeps the sandbox crate decoupled from `tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken` (correct design — sandbox shouldn't pull in tokio just for a should-cancel signal). #19901 (`38e6abc4`) adds a "Create a plan?" composer-footer nudge with `plan_mode_nudge_visible: bool` field on `ChatComposer` at `chat_composer.rs:352`, setter `set_plan_mode_nudge_visible` at `:994-1000` returning `bool` indicating whether visibility *changed* (load-bearing for callers to skip redundant repaints — a per-keystroke classifier would otherwise trigger unconditional re-renders), and render-path chain at `:3978-3989` correctly ordered (history-search-footer first as it's a transient modal, then the plan nudge, then default). The `magenta()` color choice for "Create a plan?" differentiates the soft nudge from regular dimmed footer hints — good UX signaling. +261/-0 with no test changes despite a new user-visible UI element with a non-trivial state machine — at least a snapshot test for narrow-width truncation and a toggle test for the change-detection contract should land before merge. #19900 (`393a21dc`) introduces a colon-prefix sigil for built-in permissions profiles (`default_permissions = ":workspace"`) — `:` is not a valid TOML key character so it's an unambiguous namespace separator vs user-defined profiles. `config_toml.rs:117-119` doc-comment plus mirrored `core/config.schema.json:2497` description prevents schema/doc drift. Three new tests cover the policy-layer matrix: explicit `:workspace` without `[permissions]` table at all (`:1192-1218`), implicit `:workspace` fallback for trusted projects (`:1224-1264`) with a deliberate Windows-stays-read-only assert pinning the not-yet-shipped Windows sandbox so a future rollout requires intentional test update, and an undocumented-but-named `empty_config_defaults_to_builtin_read_only_without_trust_decision` covering the strictest fallback. The `.git`/`.codex` metadata-carveout asserts are the load-bearing pieces — a buggy implementation that rubber-stamped `:workspace` with full FS access would pass the positive write-allowed asserts but fail these. At BerriAI/litellm, #26660 (`dc4b206b`) adds DeepSeek V4 support across three concerns: routing branch at `litellm_core_utils/get_llm_provider_logic.py:381-384` (`elif model in litellm.deepseek_models: custom_llm_provider = "deepseek"`) closes the unprefixed-name routing gap; multi-turn thinking fix at `llms/deepseek/chat/transformation.py:78-99` is the load-bearing piece — DeepSeek V4 thinking mode requires `reasoning_content` on *every* assistant message in history, with detection heuristic `any(msg.get("role") == "assistant" and "reasoning_content" in msg for msg in messages)` opting in by presence of the field on at least one prior turn (which the API itself emits when thinking is active — non-thinking conversations stay untouched), injection fills missing values with `""` not `None` because V4 schema is "string field, optionally empty" and `None` would fail server-side validation. The `# type: ignore[typeddict-unknown-key]` at `:96` is a real concern — extending `AllMessageValues` with `reasoning_content: NotRequired[str]` would catch a future field rename at type-check time. Four new model-registry entries (`deepseek-v4-flash`, `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`, and the V4-pro pair) with prefixed/unprefixed pairs sharing identical pricing creating a maintenance trap (a future price update has to touch both). #26652 (`d1cef71d`) is the right kind of vendor-bug fix for issue #26548 — Volcengine Ark and similar providers return response headers containing non-ASCII characters (Chinese text in `x-tos-expiration`'s `rule-id="180day自动清理"` and `x-ark-message: "视频生成成功"`), aiohttp decodes those into Python `str` happily, but `httpx.Headers.__init__` calls `value.encode("ascii")` on every value and raises `UnicodeEncodeError` crashing the *entire* request. Sanitize loop at `aiohttp_transport.py:351-360` tries `value.encode("ascii")` per header and on `UnicodeEncodeError` appends `(key, value.encode("utf-8"))` (bytes) — httpx accepts list-of-tuples where values can be `str | bytes` and storing bytes bypasses the str→ascii encoding step entirely. The inline comment at `:342-350` is exactly the kind of documentation that prevents reintroduction (names upstream cause, why httpx blows up, why bytes is the workaround, and the user-visible "garbled but works" tradeoff which is the right call since these headers are metadata not load-bearing for callers). Test at `tests/test_litellm/llms/custom_httpx/test_aiohttp_transport.py:564-635` uses Volcengine's actual header names and Chinese phrasings as fixtures (self-documenting), pins all the right invariants — request doesn't crash, ASCII-safe headers preserved as `str`, non-ASCII present, ASCII portions of mixed headers preserved through bytes-encoding round-trip, and raw UTF-8 bytes assertable via `raw_headers[b"x-tos-expiration"]` so callers wanting to manually decode have a recoverable path. At block/goose, #8869 (`2efc7957`) is a +4/-31 simplification of `download_cli.sh` removing a tangle of WSL detection branches that all tried to second-guess the OS based on `$PWD` heuristics (`[[ "$PWD" =~ ^/mnt/[a-zA-Z]/ ]]`, presence of `/c`/`/d`/`/e`, presence of `powershell.exe`/`cmd.exe`). Replacement at `:97-99` names the right invariant: "WSL is a Linux environment regardless of the current working directory. The PWD does not change the kernel — always install Linux." Load-bearing realization: prior code conflated *filesystem location* with *runtime ABI* — a user `cd`-ing into `/mnt/c/foo` is still running a Linux ELF binary on the WSL2 Linux kernel, and installing the Windows `.exe` build there would either fail to execute under Linux or, worse, succeed via WSL's binfmt_misc and produce a confusing mixed installation. The asymmetry between "drop PWD detection for WSL" and "keep PWD detection for Git Bash" at `:108-110` is exactly right because the underlying environments differ — WSL has a Linux kernel, Git Bash has a Windows kernel with POSIX-emulation shell. Removed `command -v powershell.exe` branch at `:103-104` correctly stops misclassifying Linux-with-pwsh-on-linux as Windows.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24702 surgical 1-line fix closing a real cross-session-reference bug in compaction-part forking with the conditional spread shape correctly type-narrowing on variant and guarding on truthiness; BerriAI/litellm #26652 vendor-bug fix with right shape using bytes pass-through to bypass httpx's strict ascii encoding plus an inline comment naming the upstream cause and the garbled-but-works tradeoff plus a self-documenting test using Volcengine's actual header fixtures pinning all the right invariants including raw UTF-8 byte recoverability; block/goose #8869 net-deletion simplification removing PWD-based WSL→Windows misclassification with the right invariant named "WSL is Linux regardless of PWD" and the asymmetric retention of PWD detection for Git Bash exactly matching the underlying ABI difference), 5 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24680 correct two-pass exact-then-case-folded resolver with canonical-name return value preventing downstream state bifurcation, but `svc.list()` + linear scan replaces prior O(1) `svc.get` so a `getCaseInsensitive` API would close the regression and a collision-test pinning array-order-determinism is missing; openai/codex #19880 right shape with platform-scoped accessor and exhaustive match plus closure-wrapped cancellation token decoupling sandbox crate from tokio, but no test for the cancellation-during-network-denial path that the PR title targets and the cancel-writer's silent fire-and-forget terminate-frame should at least debug-log on write failure; openai/codex #19901 well-shaped UI nudge with change-detection setter and correct precedence in render chain, but no test for the new state machine despite +261/-0 and the classifier trigger cadence is missing from setter docstring; openai/codex #19900 colon-prefix sigil is the right namespace separator with three-cell test matrix covering explicit/trusted-implicit/no-trust paths and load-bearing metadata-carveout asserts pinning that built-ins are not full-FS rubber stamps, but the precedence between user-defined profile named `workspace` and built-in `:workspace` needs a positive shadowing test and shell-eval-expansion concerns deserve a doc note; BerriAI/litellm #26660 right routing branch placement and load-bearing thinking-mode opt-in heuristic with empty-string injection matching V4 schema, but `# type: ignore[typeddict-unknown-key]` should be replaced by extending `AllMessageValues` with `NotRequired[str]` and prefixed/unprefixed pricing-entry duplication needs parity enforcement). Repo coverage: 4 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, block/goose).

### W17 drip-128 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (3 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24706 (`44397fb5`) restores legacy MCP OAuth error-body parity for the experimental HttpApi router by introducing a typed `Schema.ErrorClass` `UnsupportedOAuthError` with `{ error: Schema.String }` and `httpApiStatus: 400` at `packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/mcp.ts:23-26`, mirroring the legacy zod schema + OpenAPI block at the Hono router so both surfaces emit identical `McpUnsupportedOAuthError` typedefs in the regenerated SDK; the load-bearing test at `test/server/httpapi-mcp.test.ts:167-187` constructs both `app(false)` (legacy) and `app(true)` (HttpApi) in one body, asserts each individually equals the canonical `{status:400,body:'{"error":"MCP server demo does not support OAuth"}'}` then asserts `httpapiResponse === legacyResponse` — that final equality is the regression anchor against future drift on either branch. #24704 (`3ca3849f`) adds a nullable `path` column to the `session` SQLite table storing the worktree-relative CWD, with the additive migration at `packages/opencode/migration/20260428004200_add_session_path/migration.sql:1` (single `ALTER TABLE`), the cross-platform-safe helper `sessionPath` at `src/session/session.ts:129-131` (`path.relative(...).replaceAll("\\", "/")`), and proper schema wiring across `Info` (`optionalOmitUndefined` so JSON stays compact), `UpdatedInfo` (`Schema.NullOr` so callers can clear the column), `fromRow`/`toRow`, and the regenerated SDK + OpenAPI — but `sessionPath` has no visible call site in this PR so it lands as dead code, no test pins the `\\` → `/` normalization that's the whole reason the helper exists, and no test pins the omit-vs-null clear-vs-overwrite semantics on `UpdatedInfo`. #24691 (`cd9645d5`) adds an optional `order: PositiveInt` field for agent Tab-cycling, closing #7372 — `?? Infinity` sentinel at `src/agent/agent.ts:293` makes unordered agents collate after every ordered agent in stable alphabetical order, sort-key sequence is correct (default-pin → order-asc → name-asc), the merge rule at `:260` follows the surrounding last-non-undefined-wins pattern, and `PositiveInt` (not generic `Number`) at the config schema layer rejects the ambiguous `0` case; two new tests cover ordered-only and mixed-ordered/unordered, with the original alphabetical test renamed (not deleted) as the no-order regression anchor. At openai/codex, #19907 (`03fc0b7e`) splits guardian approval-prompt rendering on the `GuardianApprovalRequest` variant at `core/src/guardian/prompt.rs:177-211` so the `NetworkAccess` arm emits a dedicated "Network approval context:" block redirecting the auto-review LLM to assess the **triggering command** rather than the network connection itself ("The user does not need to have explicitly authorised this exact network connection, as long as the network access is a reasonable consequence of the triggering command"), with a fail-safe trigger-missing branch that doesn't pretend to approve-by-default; the `_` arm preserves byte-identical legacy rendering for every other variant. The test at `core/src/guardian/tests.rs:850-855` is a complete spec — six positive-content asserts pin the new wording verbatim plus three negative asserts pin that "Planned action JSON:", "Retry reason:", and the misleading blocked-by-policy retry string are absent. #19904 (`8e394faf`) adds a `Option<String>` `agent_identity_authapi_base_url` config knob to `ConfigToml` (`config/src/config_toml.rs:283-284`) and `ConfigProfile` (`profile_toml.rs:42`), regenerates the JSON schema (top-level + ConfigToml definition), and threads the value through every `AuthManager` constructor and loader across 18 files with a consistent `/*agent_identity_authapi_base_url*/ None,` comment convention — the right shape for OSS-friendly staging-URL configurability without baking internal URLs in source, but every test call site passes `None` so there is zero test coverage proving a `Some("...")` value actually reaches `AgentIdentity` task registration; two adjacent positional `Option<String>` parameters (`chatgpt_base_url`, `agent_identity_authapi_base_url`) are easy to swap and would benefit from typed-newtypes; merge-precedence between profile and root config field is implicit and undocumented. At BerriAI/litellm, #26664 (`7c1ffaf0`) is a textbook three-bug compounding diagnosis — frontend `useProjects.ts:78-81` `enabled` gate excluded non-admins so the API never fired; backend `_types.py:668-669` omitted `/project/list` and `/project/info` from `internal_user_routes` so even if it fired it 403'd; backend `project_endpoints.py:912-928` queried stale scalar `members`/`admins` columns that `team_member_add` no longer populates, fix replaces with O(1) reverse-index lookup on `LiteLLM_UserTable.teams` plus `WHERE team_id IN (...)` against the team_id-indexed projects table (real perf win, not just correctness), and `project_info` 403-guard at `:857-869` switches to iterating `team.members_with_roles` with both dict + pydantic accessor shapes handled, short-circuiting on first match — but the PR adds zero new tests despite the three changed query patterns being distinct from the prior code paths, and the malformed-JSON branch on `members_with_roles` fails open rather than fail-closed which is wrong shape for a 403-guard. #26662 (`468cfe0c`) closes a real prompt/response leak path where `str(BadRequestError)` embedded the upstream candidate JSON containing model output ("text": "secret content"), bypassing the `store_prompts_in_spend_logs` guardrail by leaking through `error_information.error_message` instead — fix at `litellm/proxy/spend_tracking/spend_tracking_utils.py:141-152` checks `_should_store_prompts_and_responses_in_spend_logs()` and on false replaces the dict with `{**error_info, "error_message": None}` (preserves `error_code`/`error_class`/`llm_provider`/`traceback` which are operationally useful and don't carry response content); the 5-test matrix at `tests/test_litellm/proxy/spend_tracking/test_spend_tracking_utils.py:1521-1583` covers all four cells of the (storage on/off) × (error present/absent) truth table plus the field-preservation invariant — that fourth test (`test_other_error_fields_preserved_when_message_redacted`) is the regression anchor catching future over-redaction. At block/goose, #8857 (`95a5fac`) closes #7363 (multi-turn reasoning collapse on Z.AI/Zhipu/DeepSeek/Moonshot/Novita/Nvidia/Tensorix/OpenCode-Go) by adding a per-provider declarative `preserves_thinking: bool` flag with `serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "is_false")` keeping every existing custom-provider config file deserializing unchanged and round-trip JSON byte-equal for unaffected providers; new built-in declarative providers `zai.json` and `opencode_go.json` opt in, six existing OpenAI-engine providers (`deepseek`, `moonshot`, `novita`, `nvidia`, `tensorix`, `zhipu`) flip the flag to `true`; Anthropic-format path at `crates/goose/src/providers/anthropic.rs:154-160` derives `AnthropicFormatOptions` with two distinct knobs (`preserve_unsigned_thinking`, `preserve_thinking_context`) leaving room to refine if a provider needs only one — but `AnthropicProvider::new()` silently ignores config (uses `default()`) while `from_config()` reads it, asymmetry deserves a comment, and the 6-provider parametric test at `:638-672` only asserts the **field deserializes** not that the OpenAI-format streaming path actually consumes it across multi-turn requests, leaving the load-bearing reasoning-preservation contract unpinned end-to-end.
