Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24706 surgical typed-error parity fix with regenerated SDK and a load-bearing parity test pinning byte-equal responses across legacy and HttpApi branches; sst/opencode #24691 backwards-compatible additive `order: PositiveInt` field with correct `?? Infinity` sentinel placement and stable alphabetical fallback plus two purpose-built tests with the original alphabetical test preserved as the no-order regression anchor; openai/codex #19907 right-shaped variant-split prompt template with dedicated network-access arm redirecting assessor focus to triggering command and a complete spec test pinning six positive content asserts plus three negative asserts including the regression anchor against the misleading retry string; BerriAI/litellm #26662 closes a real prompt/response leak via `str(exception)` embedding upstream candidate content with operator-policy-aware gate and field-preserving redaction shape plus a complete 5-test (storage × error) truth-table matrix), 4 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24704 right-shaped additive nullable migration with `optionalOmitUndefined`/`Schema.NullOr` schema wiring and cross-platform-safe `sessionPath` helper, but the helper has no visible call site so lands as dead code, no test pins the `\\`→`/` normalization that's the helper's whole purpose, and no test covers omit-vs-null-vs-overwrite semantics on `UpdatedInfo.path`; openai/codex #19904 right `Option<String>` config knob threaded through 18 files with consistent comment convention but every test call site passes `None` leaving zero coverage proving a configured value actually flows to AgentIdentity task registration, two adjacent positional `Option<String>` params invite swap mistakes that typed-newtypes would catch, and profile-vs-root precedence is undocumented; BerriAI/litellm #26664 textbook three-bug compounding diagnosis with O(1) reverse-index query path replacing full table scan but adds zero tests for the three changed query patterns and the malformed-JSON branch on `members_with_roles` fails open rather than fail-closed in a 403-guard; block/goose #8857 right declarative `preserves_thinking` flag with backward-compat serde defaults and two-knob `AnthropicFormatOptions` shape leaving room to refine, but `AnthropicProvider::new()` silently ignores config asymmetry deserves a comment and the 6-provider parametric test only pins field-deserialization not that the OpenAI-format streaming path actually consumes the flag across multi-turn requests). Repo coverage: 4 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, block/goose).

### W17 drip-129 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 2 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24716 (`5ae2ba3d`) closes a real validation-parity gap between the legacy Hono `/sync` zod schema and the experimental Effect HttpApi schema where the latter's `seq` field used a bare `Schema.Number` and silently accepted negatives and fractionals — fix introduces a single named `Seq = Schema.Int.check(Schema.isGreaterThanOrEqualTo(0))` brick at `packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/sync.ts:16` reused at `ReplayEvent.seq` (`:21`) and `HistoryPayload`'s record-value (`:32`), with `Schema.Int` being the load-bearing piece (rejects `1.5` as a separate failure class from `>= 0`); adds `error: HttpApiError.BadRequest` to both endpoints (`:63`, `:74`) so validation failures surface as documented 400s instead of generic 500s; the parity test at `test/server/httpapi-sync.test.ts:84-127` parameterizes `app(httpapi=true|false)` from the same flag the production code reads, exercises the (history, replay) × (negative, fractional) cell matrix, and asserts both `httpapi.status === legacy.status` and `=== 400` so a future "both surfaces accept -1" regression doesn't pass the parity check while silently weakening validation everywhere — but no positive-boundary test for `seq: 0` to pin the inclusive lower-bound semantic. #24717 (`964101d0`) is the stacked-on-top documentation parity follow-up — adds `error: HttpApiError.BadRequest` declarations to the four payload-validating TUI endpoints (`appendPrompt`, `executeCommand`, `publish`, `selectSession`) at `tui.ts:64/117/138/149` so the OpenAPI surface declares the 400 response, with `selectSession` getting the union-error syntax `error: [HttpApiError.BadRequest, HttpApiError.NotFound]` to preserve its existing 404 declaration alongside the new 400; the introspective test at `test/server/httpapi-tui.test.ts:43-50` walks all four `TuiPaths` and asserts both `Server.openapi()` (legacy) and `OpenApi.fromApi(TuiApi)` (Effect) declare 400 — but doesn't pin that `selectSession`'s 404 survived the union rewrite, so a future "simplification" dropping the `NotFound` half could regress silently. At openai/codex, #19914 (`0f37c757`) promotes remote plugin install from "register-only" to "fetch-validate-download-extract-cache" via a new 776-line `core-plugins/src/remote_bundle.rs` module with `validate_remote_plugin_bundle` (sync, no IO, parse-don't-validate shape, scheme allowlist `{http, https}` not denylist, returns typed `ValidatedRemotePluginBundle` whose fields are all post-validation) followed by async `download_and_install_remote_plugin_bundle` that off-loads tar extraction to `spawn_blocking` and stages into `tempfile::Builder::tempdir_in(&staging_root)` for atomic install — but the custom 512-byte-block tar parser at `extract_plugin_bundle_tar` instead of the audited `tar` crate is a real maintenance and security risk (path-traversal defense at `checked_tar_output_path` correctly rejects `ParentDir`/`RootDir`/`Prefix` Components but the visible typeflag match only handles regular file `b'\0'`/`b'0'` and directory `b'5'` with no symlink rejection, which is the CVE shape if symlinks aren't explicitly denied), the cloud `install_remote_plugin` runs *before* the local download so a successful-cloud-but-failed-local sequence leaves a half-installed state with no rollback, the bundle-on-disk app-IDs are now trusted over the catalog (means a malicious bundle can declare connectors the catalog never advertised — needs explicit "must appear in catalog" enforcement), and the test matrix is missing the security-critical cells (path-traversal rejection, scheme-rejection, version-validation negative tests). #19899 (`5fca8f2f`) marks `permissionProfile` as experimental on five v2 message shapes by combining (a) `inspect_params: true` macro flag on `OneOffCommandExec` at `protocol/common.rs:584` so the runtime experimental-API gate catches stable clients sending the field with a typed `command/exec.permissionProfile` reason string (test at `:2052-2078` is the spec), (b) mechanical schema regeneration removing the field from 11 JSON files and 9 TS files (additive removal — backwards-compat for stable clients that never used it), and (c) a comment-aware fix to the TS-filter scanner at `export.rs:891-934` extending `ScanState` with `block_comment`/`line_comment`/`previous_char` tracking and renaming `in_string()`→`in_ignored_syntax()` to close a pre-existing real bug where JSDoc apostrophes (e.g. `user's`) were corrupting the scanner's string-state tracking and silently leaking experimental fields into stable TS — the test at `:2731-2810` reproduces the exact JSDoc-with-apostrophe failure shape and pins both positive (field stripped) and negative (unrelated `sandboxPolicy` preserved) outcomes against future over-stripping. At BerriAI/litellm, #26663 (`73bd5978`) is a three-fix bundle whose title only describes one — (1) the titled `auth_checks.py:2695-2710` reordering correctly converts `access_group_ids` from a fallback (only checked when `_can_object_call_model` raised a `ProxyException`, never reached because empty `models` evaluates as all-access and returns True) into a precondition early-exit when `access_group_ids and not valid_token.models`, with the original except-fallback path preserved unchanged for the `models=[...]` denied case and a 4-cell test matrix covering (key, team) × (model in group, model not in group); (2) an undeclared bonus at `db_spend_update_writer.py:511-518` adds a `team_id is not None: return` early-exit guard in `_update_user_db` so team-key calls no longer accumulate spend on `LiteLLM_UserTable.spend` causing false `BudgetExceededError` after Redis restart; (3) another undeclared bonus at `zai/chat/transformation.py:11-100` adds a `_transform_messages` override flattening multi-part `content: [{"type": "text", "text": "..."}]` to plain string for `tool` and `assistant` messages because GLM's Jinja template does `m.content is string` and silently drops list-format content. Three independent fixes with three independent test files, three independent root causes, three independent blast radii — strongly recommend splitting before merge. #26658 (`69dcf549`) refactors the CrowdStrike AIDR guardrail hook to replace `dict[str, Any]` guard-input with typed Pydantic models (`_TextContentPart`/`_ImageUrlContentPart` use `ConfigDict(extra="forbid")` rejecting unrecognized block shapes at construction with informative discriminator-based errors), threads a new `_normalize_content` helper through five branches (str/None passthrough, non-list non-str → `json.dumps` silent stringification, list → walk and coerce known types, drop unknown) — but the response-side rewrite at `_build_guard_input_for_response` has two undocumented semantic changes that aren't called out in the PR description: the `choices` array shape in the AIDR wire payload is removed and replaced with a flat `messages` array bolted with `assistant` rows (either AIDR accepts both shapes and PR author tested or this is a wire break), and the three-location input-messages fallback chain (`request_data["body"]["messages"]` → `request_data["messages"]` → `logging_obj.model_call_details["messages"]`) collapses to checking only `request_data["messages"]` which weakens response-side context if other call paths populate the body location. At block/goose, #8866 (`1ff16001`) is a 2-line release-workflow fix at `.github/workflows/release-goose2.yml:166/184` swapping `release/*.dmg` for `release/**/*.dmg` because the macOS build step writes to `release/dmg/<arch>/Goose-<version>.dmg` (nested) while every other artifact build step writes at the top level — both symmetric upload blocks updated consistently so draft and published releases stay in sync; the asymmetry between recursive `**/*.dmg` and top-level `*.exe`/`*.msi`/`*.deb` admits "build steps write to whatever path they want" which is brittle for a future Windows-installer build step changing to `release/msi/x64/`. #8844 (`1a18189f`) closes issue #8437 (Ollama/OpenAI/LiteLLM timeout values written quoted by the desktop UI as `OLLAMA_TIMEOUT: '1200'` silently failed `u64` deserialization) via a surgical `match`-on-error fallback at `config/base.rs:735-748` — first arm `Ok(value) => Ok(value)` preserves String-typed reads (no round-trip through env-parser that would normalize `"300"` to number), fallback arm only activates for the narrow "string scalar that the caller wanted as a number" case via `value.as_str()` guard, fallback uses `Self::parse_env_value` so YAML-quoted scalars and env-var scalars now parse through identical code paths (real consistency win), and fail-safe error mapping returns the original `yaml_err` class on JSON-deserialize failure (preserves caller's "I'm reading YAML" mental model) — 4-test matrix at `:1175-1224` covers numeric YAML → u64 (no-op), quoted-numeric → u64 (the fix), quoted-numeric → String (preserves String-typed), invalid quoted → u64 (negative path).

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (openai/codex #19899 well-shaped per-field experimental marker bundle with the load-bearing `inspect_params: true` runtime gate and a comment-aware TS-scanner fix that closes a pre-existing real bug where JSDoc apostrophes corrupted the string-state tracking and silently leaked experimental fields, plus a test reproducing the exact failure shape with both positive-stripping and negative-preservation asserts; block/goose #8844 surgical `match`-on-error fallback at `config/base.rs:735-748` correctly scoped to the narrow "string-typed YAML value, caller wanted a number" cell with fail-safe error mapping preserving the caller's YAML mental model and 4-cell test matrix pinning both new branches and the String-typed-read regression anchor), 1 merge-as-is-tiny (block/goose #8866 minimal 2-line workflow glob fix recursing into `release/**/*.dmg` to match the nested macOS DMG output path with both symmetric upload blocks updated consistently), 3 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24716 named-schema-brick `Seq = Schema.Int.check(...)` shape with both endpoints declaring `HttpApiError.BadRequest` and a 4-cell parity matrix asserting both `status === legacy.status` and `=== 400`, but missing positive-boundary test for `seq: 0` to pin inclusive lower-bound; sst/opencode #24717 introspective OpenAPI parity test correctly walking both legacy and Effect docs across four TUI paths and asserting 400 declarations exist with the right union-error syntax for `selectSession`, but missing assert that the existing 404 declaration survived the union rewrite so a future simplification dropping `NotFound` would regress silently; openai/codex #19899 also handled in merge-as-is bucket above), 1 request-changes (openai/codex #19914 substantial new attack surface — 776-line module downloading and extracting untrusted tarballs over HTTPS — without security-critical test cells for path-traversal/scheme-rejection/invalid-version, custom 512-byte-block tar parser instead of the audited `tar` crate with no visible symlink-typeflag rejection, half-install rollback semantics where successful-cloud-but-failed-local leaves an observable broken state, and bundle-vs-catalog app-ID trust boundary needing an explicit "must appear in catalog" enforcement against malicious bundles declaring undeclared connectors; BerriAI/litellm #26663 three logically independent fixes (auth precondition reordering + spend-writer team-key guard + ZAI content flatten) bundled in one PR with the description only describing one of them, strongly recommend splitting), 1 needs-discussion (BerriAI/litellm #26658 typed-Pydantic refactor and `_normalize_content` consolidation are net wins but the response-side `_build_guard_input_for_response` rewrite has two undocumented semantic changes — removal of `choices` array shape and collapse of three-location input-messages fallback to a single source — that risk either breaking the AIDR wire contract or weakening response-side guardrail context; need maintainer to confirm AIDR API spec). Repo coverage: 4 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, block/goose).

### W17 drip-130 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 2 block/goose). At sst/opencode, #24725 (`678357e2`) is a 1-line correctness fix replacing the two-tier `(updatedDay, createdMs)` comparator at `dialog-session-list.tsx:113-118` with a single full-millisecond `b.time.updated - a.time.updated` sort — the prior comparator truncated `time.updated` to midnight via `setHours(0, 0, 0, 0)` then fell back to `time.created` as the intra-day tiebreaker, so a session touched 30s ago ended up beneath a same-day session that was *created* later in the day, the exact opposite of "sort by recency of activity" and the bug users actually filed. The numeric subtraction shape is also faster (no `Date` allocations per comparison vs the prior two `Date` objects per pair), and the day-grouped headers downstream are unaffected because grouping is done by the renderer not by the sort key. #24720 (`be1f828c`) adds bounded exponential backoff to the global SDK SSE reconnect loop at `packages/app/src/context/global-sdk.tsx:130-206` — declares `let reconnectDelay = RECONNECT_DELAY_MS` and `MAX_RECONNECT_DELAY_MS = 10000` inside the `start()` closure but outside the `while`-loop so backoff state survives across iterations but is freshly re-initialized on `stop()`/`start()` cycles, resets on the *first event arrival path* at `:158` (right discrimination — a "connect, get one event, disconnect" cycle resets but a "connect, get nothing, disconnect" cycle does not because handshake-then-no-data is still pathological), and the actual backoff at `:205-206` doubles after wait so the first retry uses the unmodified base delay preserving prior latency for the common single-blip case — but `MAX_RECONNECT_DELAY_MS` is a magic number redeclared on every `start()` call (hoist next to `RECONNECT_DELAY_MS` for symmetry), no jitter so many clients reconnecting against the same backend after a deploy bounce synchronize at the 10s ceiling (add `* (1 + Math.random() * 0.25)` to break the herd), and no test pinning the doubling progression. At openai/codex, #19920 (`dfe51b10`, fixes #19837) raises tungstenite's WebSocket frame/message cap from the 16 MiB default to 128 MiB so single-frame `thread/resume` JSON-RPC responses carrying long saved sessions deserialize instead of being rejected at the transport layer — `remote.rs:59` introduces `REMOTE_APP_SERVER_MAX_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_SIZE: usize = 128 << 20` with a one-line justification comment ("remote resume responses can legitimately carry large thread histories") and `:175-189` switches `connect_async` → `connect_async_with_config` with explicit `disable_nagle: false` preserving prior TCP behavior; the *second* fix in the same PR is the more interesting one — `remote.rs:215` introduces `let mut worker_exit_error: Option<(ErrorKind, String)> = None` with three assignment sites at `:255` (write-failed → `BrokenPipe`), `:382` (rejection-write-failed → `BrokenPipe`), and `:395` (invalid JSON-RPC → `InvalidData`), correctly inverting the prior "drain pending requests with a generic channel-closed error and lose the cause" shape so callers now see the concrete transport reason. The regression test at `lib.rs:1399-1450` uses padding `(17 << 20) + 1024` = 17 MiB + 1 KiB deliberately above the prior 16 MiB cap so any revert of the config fails loudly. PR description honestly calls out "this isn't a perfect fix. It really just moves the limit to a much larger value" and that's the right honest framing — but 128 MiB is large enough to be a DoS knob if a hostile/buggy server emits an oversized frame, and the fix masks the underlying server-side bug (`thread/resume` should page large histories rather than ship in one response). #19917 (`9cd0e96c`) is a 5-line UX fix moving `Title` and `Statusline` from the "unavailable during task" arm into the implicit-allow default at `slash_command.rs:196-200` — both commands mutate purely client-side display state (window title via OSC 0/2, TUI-rendered statusline string) and don't interact with the running turn's protocol or model-state, so disabling them while a turn was in-flight was a UX regression with no underlying correctness reason. The test rename at `:251-254` from `goal_command_is_available_during_task` → `certain_commands_are_available_during_task` extending with two new asserts is correct because the test's invariant is now "the set of during-task-available commands includes these three" not "Goal specifically"; only nit is the surviving `Theme => false` arm deserves a one-sentence comment naming why (likely: theme switch causes full TUI re-layout that races against streaming output). At BerriAI/litellm, #26667 (`a3d496ad`) is three layered fixes for a real UX freeze on large teams — root-cause analysis names ~2000 members × 6 columns = 12,000 linear scans per render — replacing six per-row `Array.find()` calls over `team_memberships` with a single `useMemo`-built `Map<user_id, TeamMembership>` at `TeamMemberTab.tsx:34-42` (with the right defensive `.filter((tm) => tm.user_id)` guard against null user_id collisions), extending `MemberTable` with optional `withPagination` prop wiring antd `<Pagination>` controls and slicing rendered rows at `MemberTable.tsx:42-62` (dual `internalPage`/`controlledPage` shape so existing call-sites are unaffected via uncontrolled default, and the load-bearing `safePage = Math.min(page, totalPages)` clamp at `:54` correctly handles the "user is on page 5, deletes enough rows to reduce total to 20" race), and adding email/user-id search plus admin/non-admin role select threaded through a `useMemo`-derived `filteredMembers` at `TeamMemberTab.tsx:44-58` that correctly applies the role filter *before* the text filter and short-circuits with `if (!q) return true` so role-only filtering doesn't pay the `.toLowerCase().includes()` cost; `countDisplay` correctly switches to "X of Y Members" via `totalMembers !== undefined && total !== totalMembers` discrimination preventing `0 of 0` confusion when the prop isn't passed. Five new realistic-userEvent tests cover email search, admin role filter, non-admin role filter, search-clear behavior, and filtered count display. Nits: page-size options cap at 100 even though the bug names "several hundred" members (8 page flips at default 50), no test pins the `safePage` clamp behavior, and the "member exists in `members_with_roles` but not in `team_memberships`" asymmetry deserves a comment. #26659 (`7be66dda`) closes a real "non-admin sees empty projects list" bug rooted in a schema-vs-code mismatch — team membership is written to `LiteLLM_UserTable.teams` by `team_member_add` but `/project/info` and `/project/list` were querying `LiteLLM_TeamTable.members`/`.admins`, columns the membership-write path *never populates* — so every non-admin user got `is_team_member = False` and `team_ids = []` regardless of actual membership. Fix at `project_endpoints.py:855-862` and `:911-920` queries the user-table side via `litellm_usertable.find_unique({"user_id": ...})` then checks `project.team_id in user_row.teams`, with a load-bearing two-line code comment naming the data-flow asymmetry (the right kind of comment for this class of bug — tells the next reader *why* the obvious code shape is wrong); `_types.py:697-700` adds `/project/list` and `/project/info` to `LiteLLMRoutes.self_managed_routes` (without this registration, non-admin callers are rejected at the framework routing layer with 401 before the per-endpoint membership check ever runs); `useProjects.ts:78-82` removes the client-side `all_admin_roles.includes(userRole)` gate so the dashboard actually fires the request for non-admins. The behavior-pinning test `test_list_projects_internal_user_queries_user_table_teams` *asserts* `mock_prisma.db.litellm_teamtable.find_many.assert_not_called()` — the strongest possible regression protection because a future "let's also fall back to team-table for safety" change fails this test loudly. At block/goose, #8872 (`087d65a6`) is a workspace `1.32.0 → 1.33.0` version bump with 6 files / +2207/-1259 lines, but the bulk of the diff is `canonical_models.json` churn co-shipped with the version bump — three classes of model-data changes raise concrete questions: (1) name canonicalization is *inconsistent* (some entries strip date suffixes from names, e.g. `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` → `claude-haiku-4-5` at `:200`, but `claude-opus-4.5` at `:318` goes the *other* direction and adds `-20251101`); (2) knowledge-cutoff date downgrade for DeepSeek R1 at `:6175` from precise `2025-05-28` (the "0528" snapshot) to `2025-01-01` is a metadata fidelity regression that downstream cost/capability comparisons may depend on; (3) net-new `abliteration-ai/abliterated-model` entry at `:4501-4528` with `"open_weights": true` and image-input multimodal — "abliterated" is the term-of-art for safety-stripped models and is a *category* not a model, listing it under a single `id` with a single cost line implies a single specific model which doesn't match reality (dozens of "abliterated" models from different authors). #8815 (`3b712d87` per repo PR list) is a clean `lazy_static = "1.5.0"` → `std::sync::LazyLock` migration that drops the dep from `crates/goose/Cargo.toml` and migrates the two visible call-sites — `security/patterns.rs:1-3` swaps `use lazy_static::lazy_static;` for `use std::sync::LazyLock;` and rewrites at `:355-363` to `static COMPILED_PATTERNS: LazyLock<HashMap<...>> = LazyLock::new(|| {...})` (idiomatic std shape, semantically identical), and `tests/providers.rs:93-95` migrates `TEST_REPORT: Arc<TestReport>` and `ENV_LOCK: Mutex<()>` with `LazyLock::new(TestReport::new)` using the function-pointer shorthand. Behavior-preserving by construction; `rust-version = "1.91.1"` workspace pin makes the `LazyLock` (stable since 1.80) safe to bottom out on std.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24725 surgical 1-line activity-recency comparator fix faster than the bug it replaces with no behavioral surprise; openai/codex #19917 minimal 5-line UX fix promoting Title/Statusline to during-task availability with positive-test coverage and the right invariant rename; BerriAI/litellm #26659 textbook schema-vs-code mismatch fix with the right data-flow code-comment, the strongest possible behavior-pinning test asserting `teamtable.find_many.assert_not_called()`, and the right three-piece shape of backend-query-fix + route-registration + UI-gate-removal; block/goose #8815 clean dep-reduction `lazy_static` → `std::sync::LazyLock` migration that's behavior-preserving by construction with the right function-pointer shorthand for `TestReport::new`), 3 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24720 right-shaped exponential-backoff fix preserving prior latency for the common single-blip case via doubling-after-wait and resetting on first event arrival not first connect, but `MAX_RECONNECT_DELAY_MS` is a magic number declared inside the closure not hoisted with the base, no jitter so clients synchronize at the ceiling after deploy bounces, and no test pinning the doubling progression; openai/codex #19920 honestly-framed 16 MiB → 128 MiB tungstenite cap raise with a load-bearing regression test at `(17 << 20) + 1024` deliberately above the prior cap plus a separate worker-exit-error preservation refactor that's a real quality improvement, but 128 MiB is a DoS knob if a hostile server emits oversized frames and the fix masks the underlying "thread/resume should page" server-side bug; BerriAI/litellm #26667 substantial real perf fix with the right algorithmic shape O(n²)→O(n) via Map lookup plus opt-in pagination plus search/role filter with five realistic-userEvent tests, but page-size options cap at 100 for "several hundred" member teams and no test pins the `safePage` clamp behavior), 1 needs-discussion (block/goose #8872 version bump itself is fine but co-shipped canonical_models.json data has three concrete questions — date-suffix canonicalization inconsistency in opposite directions, knowledge-cutoff date precision regression for DeepSeek R1, and `abliteration-ai/abliterated-model` policy question of whether safety-stripped model categories belong in a canonical registry — easiest path is to split into two PRs so release-cutting doesn't block on resolving them). Repo coverage: 4 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, block/goose).

### W17 drip-131 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). At sst/opencode, #24728 (`f343c95a`, rektide) adds `opencode session move` and `opencode session detached` CLI subcommands closing #24708 — `move` supports `--from-id` (multiple), `--from-dir` (with `cwd`/glob `*`/`?` wildcards), `--from-project` selectors and `--to-directory`/`--to-project` setters with `keep` sentinel for unchanged values, while `detached` walks each session's stored `directory` resolving via cached `.git/opencode` then `git rev-list --max-parents=0 HEAD | sort | head -1` to detect (a) directories that no longer exist and (b) project_id mismatches; the architectural concern is the **direct-SQL bypass** at `:144-149` (`Database.use((db) => db.update(SessionTable).set(set).where(where).run())`) sidestepping every invariant `Session.update()` enforces (cache invalidation, observers, `updated_at` touch, child-session-tree handling), the unsafe glob-to-LIKE translation at `:118` (`dir.replace(/\*/g, "%").replace(/\?/g, "_")` doesn't escape literal `%`/`_` already present in POSIX paths so `--from-dir "/home/user/100%proj"` matches unintended directories), missing child-session-tree migration so sessions with `parent_id` pointing at moved rows keep their old `directory`/`project_id`, and uncapped `Promise.all` over all sessions in `detached` shelling out to `git` per directory which hits per-process FD limits on macOS for users with thousands of sessions. #24726 (`53dba048`, Alchuang22-dev) takes the **opposite architectural path** for the same orphan-recovery problem from #23250 — adds `Session.listGlobal()`, `Session.listOrphans()`, `Session.migrate()` (correctly going through `Session.Service.use((svc) => svc.migrate(input))` at `:213-215` so all service-layer guarantees including child-tree migration are preserved), HTTP routes `GET /session/history`/`GET /session/orphans`/`POST /session/:sessionID/migrate`, JS SDK regeneration, and three CLI subcommands (`session list --all`, `session orphans`, `session migrate`/`rebind`); the `Row = Session.Info | Session.GlobalInfo` union type at `:223` with the narrowing `if (!("project" in session)) return ""` at `:227-230` and the conditional column-width math at `:235` that only allocates the Project column when `all=true` is the right shape preserving byte-identical non-`--all` table output for existing scripts; nits are missing `--cursor` flag on `session list --all` despite the HTTP API supporting cursor-paged history per the PR body, the single-element-array JSON output `formatSessionJSON([session])` at `:218-220` for the single-result `migrate` command which contrasts with other single-result endpoints in opencode emitting bare objects, and missing focused fixture for the "global session's directory becomes a known-project worktree" orphan-class named in the PR body. **The two PRs solve overlapping problems with incompatible primitives and one must be closed before either lands** — #24726's service-layer route is the correct canonical mutator. At openai/codex, #19919 (`33fafff1`, euroelessar) adds the v2 server notification `remoteControl/environment/updated` with `environmentId: string | null` payload distributed through `RemoteControlHandle` via a Tokio `watch` channel rather than adding a new transport-event variant — the `watch`-channel choice is correct because it only delivers the *latest* value so a client initializing during enrollment churn doesn't replay every intermediate state, the replay-on-initialize path correctly filters to non-null ids only (replaying `null` post-init would falsely signal cleared enrollment), the single-arm `Option<String>` payload distinguishes "not enrolled" (`null`) from "enrolled with id X" (`Some(...)`) without inventing a tagged enum, and all generated artifacts (`ServerNotification.json` legacy + v2, `codex_app_server_protocol.schemas.json`, `v2.schemas.json`, `typescript/v2/RemoteControlEnvironmentUpdatedNotification.ts`, `typescript/v2/index.ts`, the union in `ServerNotification.ts`) are kept in sync in one PR with no half-regeneration risk; the TUI no-op match arm preserves exhaustive-match safety by signaling deliberate ignore rather than future-developer confusion about a missing handler. #19905 (`f0503819`, eternal-openai, originated from external #19060 contribution by vincentkoc) adds `PreCompact` and `PostCompact` to the `HookEventName` enum threading them across hook config/discovery/dispatch/app-server-protocol/generated-schemas/analytics-event-name-mapping/TUI-rendering — wires `PreCompact` *before* both local and remote compaction with `manual`/`auto` trigger discriminator and `custom_instructions` in the input enabling independent matchability of user-initiated `/compact` vs. automatic context-pressure compaction (very different security/audit surfaces), supports `PreCompact` blocking via exit code 2 stderr or JSON `{"decision":"block","reason":"..."}` which is the only useful policy-gate semantics (post-fact blocking would just be logging), wires `PostCompact` after success with `compact_summary` in the input enabling summary-aware audit hooks; the `v2_enum_from_core!` macro at `protocol/v2.rs:436-440` adding both variants in one place forces every downstream `match` to update or stop compiling — verified analytics mapping at `analytics/src/events.rs:671-677` and schema regeneration both got both variants, no half-add risk; pinned by `manual_pre_compact_hook_blocks_compaction` and `compact_hooks_respect_matchers_and_post_receives_summary` integration tests covering the matcher-filtering and blocking-behavior paths which are the right cells since a future "let's simplify by always running on both manual and auto" change would silently violate the documented independence; the `ManagedHooksRequirements` schema at `v2.rs:972-980` gets two new required fields (`PreCompact`, `PostCompact`) which is **a breaking change for external strict-schema v2 consumers** with no `BREAKING:` callout in title/body. At BerriAI/litellm, #26657 (`57d6500d`, ishaan-berri) is **a duplicate fix for the same root cause as drip-129's already-reviewed #26659** — both PRs identify the schema-vs-code mismatch where `team_member_add` writes to `LiteLLM_UserTable.teams` while `/project/list`+`/project/info` were querying `LiteLLM_TeamTable.members`/`.admins`, both flip the same query at `project_endpoints.py:855-862` and `:911-920` to read `user_row.teams` and check `project.team_id in user_row.teams`, and both add `/project/list`+`/project/info` to a routes allowlist; the differences are #26657 places routes in the bare `internal_user_routes` membership while #26659 places them in `self_managed_routes` (the latter classification matches the read-your-own-projects intent better), #26659 has the strongest possible regression test asserting `mock_prisma.db.litellm_teamtable.find_many.assert_not_called()` while #26657 has zero tests despite its own pre-submission checklist marking "Adding at least 1 test is a hard requirement" as a hard requirement, and the entire pre-submission checklist on #26657 is unchecked including the Greptile-4/5-confidence requirement suggesting draft-quality submission; the `is_team_member` collapse at `:855-862` drops the admin-vs-member distinction silently (if downstream code branches on `is_team_admin`, this loses the signal). At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26088 (`1713aca8`, Gitanaskhan26) is a 3-line `keyBindings.ts:399-432` fix adding `F10` as an additional `KeyBinding` alongside `Shift+Tab` for `CYCLE_APPROVAL_MODE`, `UNFOCUS_SHELL_INPUT`, and `UNFOCUS_BACKGROUND_SHELL` closing #22738 — uses the established `KeyBinding[]` pattern that already exists for `Command.RESTART_APP`, the choice of F10 is well-motivated (F-keys reported as DECKPAM-style escape sequences are reliable across Windows console hosts where modifier-key sequences for `Shift+Tab` get mangled, and F10 specifically is rarely bound by modern terminal emulators with F11=fullscreen being the more common collision), and the symmetry across all three commands is correct because they all share `Shift+Tab` as their primary binding so adding F10 to only `CYCLE_APPROVAL_MODE` would create the surprising state where Windows users could cycle approval but couldn't escape shell focus with the same fallback; nits are unverified F10 menu-activation collision on macOS/Linux (testing matrix shows only Windows checked), missing one-cell test asserting all three Commands include F10, and unrefreshed user-facing keyboard-shortcuts documentation that still says only `Shift+Tab`. #26087 (`530ec448`, Ultron09) bundles **two unrelated bug fixes** — settings persistence (adds `skipSave` option to `LoadedSettings.setValue()` at `settings.ts:447-486`, refactors `migrateDeprecatedSettings()` to return `LoadableSettingScope[]` instead of `boolean`, batches per-scope saves at the end of `_doLoadSettings()`) and OAuth URL display (disables alternate-screen-buffer + ANSI screen-clearing during OAuth flow so the login URL survives in scrollback for SSH/narrow-terminal users) — bundling forces reviewers to understand both surfaces simultaneously and a request-changes on either blocks both; the `migrateDeprecatedSettings` return-type change is **breaking** for any external/test caller because public function signature went from `boolean` to `LoadableSettingScope[]` (eight test sites updated only by adding `expect.anything()` as the new fourth arg which passes the type checker but doesn't actually validate the new `options.skipSave` parameter behavior); the `originalSettings` vs `settings` swap at `:914` changes which object the migration reads from with a subtle behavior change (a user who had `${ENV_OVERRIDE}` resolving to a deprecated value would previously have had it migrated, now preserved as-is — probably correct intent but no test pinning the new invariant); `resolveEnvVarsInObject(valueToSet)` added at `:464-469` in the runtime-settings update path resolves env vars on every `setValue` call previously only resolved at load time which is a behavior change without explicit acknowledgment; new public `save(scope)` method at `:485-491` has no docstring and only one caller; comment-only Unicode glyph artifact at `:973` (`→` → `￫`) is exactly the kind of "removed unrelated change" the PR body claims to have eliminated. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3685 (`56a35023`, doudouOUC) adds a 330-line GitHub Actions workflow `.github/workflows/release-sdk-python.yml` plus `packages/sdk-python/scripts/get-release-version.js` to build/validate/publish `qwen-code-sdk` to PyPI via trusted publishing supporting stable/preview/nightly release types with PyPI/GitHub conflict checks — the trusted-publishing pattern via `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1` is the right modern shape (no API token in repo secrets, OIDC-based auth via `id-token: 'write'` permission), `concurrency: group: '${{ github.workflow }}' cancel-in-progress: false` at `:34-36` correctly serializes release runs without canceling an in-flight publish (a canceled `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish` mid-upload could leave PyPI partial), action ratchet pinning to commit SHAs is consistent across `actions/checkout`/`actions/setup-node`/`actions/setup-python` with `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1` as the only un-SHA-pinned action which should be pinned for consistency, the `if: github.repository == 'QwenLM/qwen-code'` guard correctly prevents fork CI from attempting to publish, and PEP 440 version derivation in JS for nightly (`0.1.1.dev20260428023154`)/preview (`0.1.1rc0`)/stable (`0.1.0`) all produce valid PyPI-acceptable versions; the `force_skip_tests` input default `false` is correct but production releases should additionally gate `force_skip_tests=true` behind `dry_run=true` so a real publish can never skip the smoke test, and a local-wheel `pip install` smoke step before publish would catch packaging-metadata bugs before the real publish.

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (openai/codex #19919 architecturally sound `watch`-channel + replay-on-init shape correctly handles both broadcast-during-churn and bootstrap-race semantics with all generated artifacts in sync and TUI no-op arm preserving exhaustive-match safety), 4 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24726 right-shaped service-layer-routed `Session.migrate()` preserving cache invalidation/observers/child-tree handling with correct `Row` union type and conditional Project-column allocation but missing `--cursor` flag on `session list --all`, single-element-array JSON output for the single-result `migrate` command, and missing focused fixture for global-session-becomes-project orphan class; openai/codex #19905 right-shaped lifecycle gate where `PreCompact` runs before context rewrite and can block via exit-code-2-stderr or JSON `{"decision":"block"}`, exhaustive-match propagation through the `v2_enum_from_core!` macro forces every downstream match to update with no half-add risk, and integration tests pin matcher-filtering and blocking-behavior cells but the two new required fields in `ManagedHooksRequirements` are a breaking change without `BREAKING:` callout; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26088 well-motivated `F10` fallback using the established `KeyBinding[]` pattern with correct symmetry across all three Shift+Tab-using commands but unverified macOS/Linux menu-activation collision and missing structural test; QwenLM/qwen-code #3685 right modern trusted-publishing pattern with conservative `cancel-in-progress: false` concurrency, ratchet-pinned actions, fork-CI guard, and valid PEP 440 version derivation for all three release types but `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish` should be SHA-pinned for ratchet consistency, `force_skip_tests=true` should be gated behind `dry_run=true`, and a local-wheel `pip install` smoke step before publish would catch packaging-metadata bugs), 1 request-changes (google-gemini/gemini-cli #26087 bundles two unrelated bug fixes (settings persistence + OAuth URL display) with no shared root cause forcing reviewer attention split, the `migrateDeprecatedSettings` return-type change from `boolean` to `LoadableSettingScope[]` is silently breaking with eight test sites updated only via `expect.anything()` rather than actually validating the new `options.skipSave` parameter, the `originalSettings`-read swap and `resolveEnvVarsInObject`-on-setValue are undocumented behavior changes, and a comment-only Unicode glyph artifact `→` → `￫` at `:973` is exactly the kind of "removed unrelated change" the PR body claims to have eliminated), 2 needs-discussion (sst/opencode #24728 and BerriAI/litellm #26657 both **duplicate-of-existing-PR cases** — #24728 solves the orphan-recovery problem from #23250 with incompatible direct-SQL primitives vs. #24726's service-layer route in the same drip; #26657 is the same schema-vs-code mismatch fix as already-reviewed drip-129 #26659 with subtly different allowlist classification choices and no tests vs. #26659's `teamtable.find_many.assert_not_called()` regression test — in both cases one PR must be closed before either lands and #24726/#26659 are the canonical paths). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, QwenLM/qwen-code).

### W17 drip-132 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 1 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). At sst/opencode, #24707 (`a03a08ab`, kitlangton) introduces a new internal `@opencode-ai/effect-drizzle-sqlite` package that monkey-patches Drizzle ORM's SQLite query-builder prototypes so existing query-builder objects are *also* yieldable as `Effect.Effect<T, EffectDrizzleQueryError>` — call sites that previously did `await db.select()...` can now do `yield* db.select()...` inside `Effect.gen` blocks, with errors automatically wrapped carrying SQL text and parameter list, plus `db.withTransaction` taking an Effect, running it inside a synchronous Drizzle transaction via `runSyncExit`, and propagating failures as a sentinel `TransactionFailure` to roll back; the shape correctly preserves the existing fluent Drizzle API verbatim with no fork or wrapper, lets call sites incrementally adopt `yield*`, and the error-class carries `query`/`params`/`cause` so failed queries are debuggable instead of opaque, and the 237-line test file exercises the load-bearing cells (select/insert/update/delete, relational queries, nested transactions, defects rolling back, query-error wrapping); concerns are the prototype-monkey-patching shape itself (mutates `BaseSQLiteSelectQueryBuilder.prototype`/`SQLiteInsertBuilder.prototype`/etc. on import side-effect, so any other code in the same Node process loading `drizzle-orm/sqlite-core` afterward sees a different prototype — fragile and load-order-dependent), `withTransaction` uses `runSyncExit` requiring the inner Effect be fully synchronous (any accidental `Effect.async`/`Effect.promise` deep in the call chain crashes the transaction silently with a defect), and the migrated call sites lose the visual distinction between `await`-style and `yield*`-style mid-function which hurts grep-ability. #24724 (`7e60df51`, KTibow) is a pure docs sync regenerating the Go and Zen subscription-tier pricing/usage-limit tables from upstream models.dev — closes #24723, notable corrections include Kimi K2.6 now showing ~3× more requests-per-period than the stale value, all `MiMo-V2-*` model display names normalized from hyphenated to space-separated form, and the API-endpoints table loses two `@ai-sdk/*` rows for MiniMax/Qwen because those models now go through chat-completions with the openai-compatible adapter; mechanical regeneration from upstream with no code paths affected and no behavioral-regression risk. At openai/codex, #19929 (`579adb18`, etraut-openai, closes #19814) replaces the per-separator incremental timing scheme (legacy of #9599 from before turns had protocol-native `duration_ms`) with a direct read of the new `TurnCompleteEvent.duration_ms` / `TurnCompleted.turn.duration_ms` field, with a fallback to the existing status-indicator elapsed-time only when the protocol value is unavailable, and removes the now-unused `last_separator_elapsed_secs` state and `worked_elapsed_from()` helper; mid-turn dividers between assistant text segments lose their `Worked for ...` clock and become plain visual dividers — textbook cleanup-after-protocol-uplift, the original incremental-timer was correctly comment-described as a workaround for the pre-`duration_ms` era so removing it once the upstream signal is reliable is the right lifecycle, the new code is shorter with fewer pieces of mutable per-turn state, the snapshot test pins the load-bearing UI string, and the fallback-to-status-indicator at `chatwidget.rs:2814-2818` is the right amount of conservatism for sessions started before `duration_ms` existed. At BerriAI/litellm, #26644 (`c7d46971`, emerzon) adds support for the `gpt-image-2` family in two surfaces — provider detection (both `get_llm_provider` and `validate_environment` recognize any `model.startswith("gpt-image")` as the OpenAI provider) and cost routing (the `gpt-image-1`-specific substring guards in `route_image_generation_cost_calculator` widen to `"gpt-image" in model_lower` so future revisions auto-route to the token-based calculator instead of falling through to the pixel-based DALL-E path) — adds four registry entries (`gpt-image-2`, `gpt-image-2-2026-04-21`, and the two `azure/`-prefixed siblings) with token-based pricing in both the primary registry JSON and the `_backup.json` copy, and three end-to-end tests pin the routing and cost math; the change from a hard-coded version-suffix check to a family-prefix check is the correct generalization for what is clearly going to be a multi-version family, the pricing-registry entries are symmetrically applied across both files (no risk of the load-bearing primary registry and the bundled backup falling out of sync), and the `test_gpt_image_provider_detection_covers_existing_family` test is the right kind of regression anchor pinning that the old `gpt-image-1*` strings still detect — but no symmetric `azure/gpt-image*` prefix in the provider-detection chain (worth confirming Azure detection falls back gracefully for unregistered Azure variants), tests don't cover the cache-read paths despite the registry declaring `cache_read_input_image_token_cost`, and the `"supports_pdf_input": true` flag on an image-generation model is suspicious copy-paste from `gpt-image-1.5`. #26640 (`8248f424`, skgandikota, closes #26403) closes a real crash in `/sso/callback` where the IdP redirects back with `?error=access_denied&error_description=...` instead of the expected `?code=...` (e.g. user clicked "Cancel" on the consent screen), prior to which `auth_callback` proceeded into the normal token-exchange path and crashed non-gracefully trying to parse the absent `code` — fix adds a precondition check at the top of the handler that converts an `error` query param into an `HTTPException(401)` with the OAuth error class and description embedded in the detail string; textbook fail-fast shape with the check at the top of the function before any state validation or token-exchange code runs, the `isinstance(oauth_error, str) and oauth_error` guard correctly rejects both the missing-key case (`None`) and the empty-string case (`""`), the description-formatting handles the optional-description case without producing the literal substring `"None"` in the user-visible detail, and the test file covers the three cells that matter (error-with-description, error-without-description, no-error-passes-through) with the no-error case having a strong "doesn't fire and downstream proceeds normally" assertion mocking the cli-callback path; nits are the detail string interpolating attacker-controlled IdP values into the response body without truncation/HTML-escaping. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26092 (`f05946cb`, Gitanaskhan26) is a 1-line addition to `KEY_INFO_MAP` in `KeypressContext.tsx` mapping the escape sequence `OM` to the canonical `enter` key — this sequence is what xterm-class emulators in DECKPAM "application keypad" mode emit when the user presses the *numeric-keypad* Enter key instead of the main Return key, without which that keystroke either does nothing or is rendered as the literal characters `OM` in the input box; exactly the right shape (a single surgical entry in the same `KEY_INFO_MAP` table where the other keypad/cursor escape sequences live), one line, zero blast radius beyond "users with numeric keypads can now press Enter," matches the established pattern, the discriminator (`O` vs `[` after `ESC`) correctly distinguishes from mouse-tracking sequences, and the `OM` sequence is well-documented in xterm DECKPAM/DECKPNM control documentation — not a guess; follow-ups for the same DECKPAM family include keypad F1-F4 (`OP`-`OS`) and keypad arrows (`OA`-`OD`) which may also be silently swallowed on terminals booting into application-mode by default. #26084 (`35589570`, gemini-cli-robot) adds a hard 128-tool ceiling at *two* layers — `ToolRegistry.getFunctionDeclarations()` invokes a new `smartLimitTools()` that prioritizes built-in tools first then non-MCP discovered tools then fairly distributes remaining slots round-robin across MCP servers, plus `GeminiChat` defensively re-truncates `config.tools` inside `generateContent()` if any caller still constructs a `tools` array exceeding 128 — the two-layer enforcement is the right shape (registry-level smart prioritization is the canonical place but the chat-level last-line-of-defense covers the "library consumer constructs config.tools manually" case where the registry isn't on the path), the smart-limit prioritization is well-motivated (built-in tools are higher signal-to-noise than the 95th MCP tool from a third-party server), and the round-robin distribution is correct fairness semantics with a clean walking-server-by-server-picking-index-th-tool implementation verified by a test asserting `s1Count === 49 && s2Count === 49`; nits are the two `MAX_TOOLS = 128` magic numbers (one in registry, one in chat — should be hoisted to a shared constant), no MCP-floor when built-in + discovered alone saturate (a future user with 130 built-in tools gets zero MCP slots even when MCP servers are explicitly enabled), and the truncation order for the chat-layer safety net loses both the built-in and round-robin guarantees. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3684 (`6e4b9300`, doudouOUC) is Phase C of a multi-PR feature stack adding a new built-in `monitor` tool that runs a long-running shell command in the background, intercepts its stdout, and streams throttled event notifications back into the agent context — adds a new `MonitorRegistry` service mirroring the existing background-task/shell registry shapes with `setNotificationCallback`/`setRegisterCallback` hooks for both interactive and non-interactive call paths, plus a `sleep`-interception path that *blocks* `sleep N` for `N >= 2` and tells the agent to use the monitor tool instead; substantial new surface (a new tool, a new registry singleton, a new shell-command interception layer, and a non-trivial change to the non-interactive command-completion logic) shipped without an architecture-level review trail, and the harder concerns are the `sleep`-interception blocking a *fundamental shell command* with no opt-out and a magic-number 2s threshold (sleep is the canonical CI debug pause, the canonical rate-limit, and the canonical test setup primitive — replacing it everywhere with "use the monitor tool" assumes monitor is always the right answer which it isn't), the monitor-registry abort-and-drain protocol mixing two registries' completion conditions in a way that admits a race between "monitor finalized" and "notification enqueued" causing dropped notifications, the new tool registered in the global `tool-names.ts` enum without visible permission-gating despite effectively being a wrapper around `run_shell_command`, the `monitor.test.ts` integration test using model-as-judge introspection ("Reply with just yes or no") which is flaky on weaker models and gives false-pass on strong models that hallucinate "yes," and callback-leak in the abort path where cleanup only runs on normal-exit not abort-exit.

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24724 mechanical docs regeneration from upstream models.dev with no code paths affected and a real factual K2.6 ~3× correction; openai/codex #19929 textbook cleanup-after-protocol-uplift removing the pre-`duration_ms`-era incremental timer in favor of the protocol-native field with the right fallback conservatism for old sessions; BerriAI/litellm #26640 textbook fail-fast shape for OAuth callback handler with three-cell test matrix and the strong `"None" not in detail` regression assertion; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26092 surgical 1-line addition matching the established `KEY_INFO_MAP` pattern with zero blast radius and a well-documented xterm-spec basis), 3 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24707 right-shaped Effect/Drizzle bridge preserving the fluent API verbatim with debuggable error-class carrying SQL+params and a 237-line test file covering load-bearing cells but the prototype-monkey-patching shape is fragile/load-order-dependent and `withTransaction` requires fully-synchronous inner Effects with silent defect-on-async; BerriAI/litellm #26644 correct family-prefix generalization with symmetric registry application across primary+backup files and a strong regression-anchor test pinning existing `gpt-image-1*` detection survives but missing symmetric Azure prefix in provider-detection chain, no cache-read path test coverage, and suspicious `supports_pdf_input` copy-paste artifact; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26084 correct two-layer enforcement with well-motivated built-in-prioritization and round-robin MCP fairness verified by `49 && 49` assertion but two `MAX_TOOLS = 128` magic numbers that must be hoisted to one shared constant, no MCP-floor when built-in tools saturate, and the chat-layer safety net loses both prioritization guarantees), 1 request-changes (QwenLM/qwen-code #3684 substantial new surface — new tool + new registry singleton + new shell-command interception layer + non-trivial non-interactive completion change — with three blocking concerns: `sleep`-interception blocks a fundamental shell command with no opt-out and an unjustified 2s threshold replacing the canonical CI debug pause / rate-limit / test setup primitive, monitor-registry abort-and-drain protocol mixes two registries' completion conditions admitting a race that drops notifications, and the new tool is registered in the global enum without visible permission-gating despite being a `run_shell_command` wrapper; also an integration test using model-as-judge natural-language introspection that's flaky on weak models and false-passes on strong models hallucinating "yes"). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, QwenLM/qwen-code).

### W17 drip-133 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (1 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). At sst/opencode, #24701 (`c4b937d9`, workdocyeye, closes #18567) is a +3/-64 pure-deletion fix on the share page: removes the desktop-only `MessageNav` sidebar layout (every entry was labeled the generic "New Message" so users couldn't distinguish ticks, and the hover tooltip vanished as the cursor moved toward the sidebar) and lets the mobile continuous-scroll `turns()` view render at all viewport widths, with the load-bearing observation that `turns()` is unchanged so the verification claim ("same function already working in production for mobile viewports") is literally true from the diff; the `wide()` signal now appears dead on this route and `SessionReview` (diffs panel) is now mobile-tab-only on desktop, both are acceptable intermediate states with follow-ups. At openai/codex, #19933 (`bd324edc`, addresses #9274) adds a top-level `codex update` subcommand replacing the rough edge where typing it started an interactive session with `update` as the first prompt — reuses the existing `run_update_action()` and `get_update_action()` from the TUI's `/update` flow, with `cfg(debug_assertions)` correctly bailing on debug builds matching the existing `tui/src/lib.rs:172` `update_versions` gating, the visibility widening at `tui/src/update_action.rs:62` (`pub(crate)` → gated `pub`) only applies in release builds, and dispatch correctly routes through `reject_remote_mode_for_subcommand`; the marketplace_upgrade test edit (`unexpected argument 'upgrade'` → `unrecognized subcommand 'upgrade'`) is the natural clap message change from adding the new enum variant. #19931 (`4721d365`) pushes local-mode `cwd` filtering for `/resume` and `/fork` pickers down from the TUI (which was scanning loaded rows after the fact) into the `thread/list` app-server call, consolidating the three-cell decision (`show_all` × `is_remote` × `local`) into a new `picker_cwd_filter()` helper with the subtle correctness improvement of using `config.cwd` (the captured-at-startup project root) instead of `std::env::current_dir().ok()` (which races with any tool that ran `cd`); the new test pins `params.cwd == Some(ThreadListCwdFilter::One("/tmp/project"))` which is exactly the right load-bearing assertion. At BerriAI/litellm, #26670 (`4d2e13c9`, closes #25840) closes a real observability gap — `/v1/responses` (Responses API) calls were emitting OTel spans missing `gen_ai.output.messages`, `gen_ai.system_instructions`, and `gen_ai.response.finish_reasons` because the attribute-population code only knew about the `choices`-shaped chat-completions response — with three independent fixes folded into one PR: an `or`-chain coalescing `system_instructions`/`instructions`/`system` kwarg names with separate string-vs-list handling, a new `_transform_responses_api_output_to_otel()` emitting the same `{role, parts}` envelope as the chat-completions transformer (so downstream consumers stay shape-agnostic) handling both `type="message"` and `type="function_call"` items, and a `status`-as-finish-reason extraction wrapped in the canonical list-of-one shape; the 459-line test file with 21 cells in `TestOpenTelemetryResponsesAPI` covers the load-bearing positive cases and uses a clean `_get_attr` helper that decouples assertions from `set_attribute` call ordering. At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26054 (`d622a078`, closes #21803) is a +4/-4 pure typo fix renaming local variable `adminControlsListner` → `adminControlsListener` at four call sites in `packages/cli/src/gemini.tsx` (the function `setupAdminControlsListener` it returns from was already correctly spelled, so the misspelling was never load-bearing) — zero blast radius because the variable is function-local with no module-boundary crossings. #26048 (`4c08d4b2`, related #26047) is explicitly labeled by the author as a GSoC-proposal prototype "NOT intended for merging" — implements Behavioral Tip Personalization with a `Tip[]` interface refactor (text/relatedFeature/weight) tagging ~150 tips, a `featureUsageCounts` persistent state addition, weighted selection in `usePhraseCycler`, and instrumentation hooks at three call sites; the conceptual model (show users tips for unused features) is sound but free-form `relatedFeature` strings will drift between instrumentation and tip declarations, persistent-state schema change ships with no migration plan, no test coverage, and no measurement hook to evaluate whether the algorithm beats uniform random — should be split into (1) data model + migration, (2) instrumentation behind a flag, (3) selection algorithm with stated cold-start/power-user behavior, (4) measurement. At block/goose, #8790 (`7de9ad31`, closes #8373) fixes a real "configure exits silently with code 2 right after `Model fetch complete`" bug on macOS 26.4/kitty/Terminal.app caused by `cliclack`'s spinner render-thread outliving `spin.stop()` for a few ms and racing the next prompt's `interact()` for raw-mode stdin — the +5-line fix at `configure.rs:743-747` does the smallest correct thing (`drop(spin); tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;`) with a comment that names the race, names the affected next operation, and references the issue, which is exactly the right amount of consumer-side workaround documentation; the 50ms is a magic number worth promoting to a named constant with a `TODO(cliclack)` cleanup signal. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3519 (`02e181eb`, closes #3518) adds three new image-attachment paths (data URL paste, raw base64 paste with magic-byte sniff for PNG/JPEG/GIF/WebP/BMP/TIFF, drag-and-drop including the macOS Terminal.app burst-of-keystrokes case detected by a `<10ms` inter-key threshold + 150ms debounced tail-scan) and unifies all four paths (including existing Cmd+V) under a single `[Image #N]` placeholder UX with monotonic per-input counter and submit-time substitution to `@<relative path>`; the load-bearing structural choice is unification at the *output* layer (one placeholder format, one attachment state) rather than at the input layer, the two-bucket submit logic (`refByPlaceholder` for placeholder-having attachments, `prependRefs` for legacy paths) is correct backwards-compat shape, the magic-byte sniff for raw base64 correctly prevents JWT/hash-shaped text from hijacking the large-paste flow, and the test coverage includes the right negative fallthrough cells; concerns are three magic numbers with no env-var override for field debugging, silent path-pasted-as-image substitution that may surprise users, no end-to-end integration test with a real fixture image, and the 150ms debounce flicker (path text appears then is replaced with `[Image #N]`).

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24701 +3/-64 pure deletion of broken desktop sidebar with `turns()` unchanged so the mobile-already-working invariant carries the entire correctness argument; openai/codex #19931 right-factored push-down of cwd filtering from TUI to `thread/list` with the subtle `config.cwd` vs `std::env::current_dir()` correctness improvement and a load-bearing test pinning `ThreadListCwdFilter::One`; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26054 +4/-4 pure typo fix with zero blast radius), 4 merge-after-nits (openai/codex #19933 thin-shim `codex update` subcommand correctly reusing existing TUI `get_update_action`/`run_update_action` with the right `cfg(debug_assertions)` gating and `reject_remote_mode_for_subcommand` dispatch but the not-detected error string now duplicated between CLI and TUI; BerriAI/litellm #26670 three independent OTel-Responses-API fixes folded with the right `or`-chain coalescing and shape-parity transformer but missing status normalization to canonical OTel finish-reason values, no `reasoning` output-item handling, and no chat-completions regression assertion alongside the new tests; block/goose #8790 +5-line consumer-side workaround for cliclack spinner-vs-picker race with the right comment naming the race + issue + affected operation but the 50ms magic number lacks a named constant and a `TODO(cliclack)` cleanup signal; QwenLM/qwen-code #3519 four-input-paths-one-placeholder-UX with correct unification at the output layer, magic-byte sniffing that prevents JWT-hijacking, and right negative fallthrough tests but three burst-detection magic numbers without env-var override and no end-to-end integration test with a real fixture image), 1 needs-discussion (google-gemini/gemini-cli #26048 GSoC-proposal prototype the author explicitly says is NOT intended for merging — sound conceptual model but free-form `relatedFeature` strings, persistent-state schema change with no migration, no tests, and no measurement hook; should be split into 4 sequential PRs starting with data model + migration). Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).

### W17 drip-134 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). At sst/opencode, #24740 (`9d41bf66`, closes #24739) batches `/vcs/diff`'s per-file `git show HEAD:<file>` calls into one `git cat-file --batch` process via a new `Git.showMany()` at `git/index.ts:196-271`, with the right "batch is a perf optimization, never a correctness primitive" shape — every structural check failure (header line not ending at `\n`, regex mismatch on `^[0-9a-f]+ blob (\d+)$`, non-integer/negative size, missing trailing newline byte at predicted offset, trailing garbage post-loop) bails to `return undefined` and the outer `(yield* batch()) ?? (yield* fallback())` at `:270` correctly degrades to the per-file `show()` chain at concurrency 8; the binary-safe NUL sniff at `:255` returns `""` for blobs containing zero bytes which may diverge from the per-file `show()` path that returns raw output (worth a parity test); UI-side debounce + git-metadata filter at `session.tsx:621-622` and `helpers.ts:174-177` correctly handle the four cells that matter (bare `.git`, `/.git` suffix, `.git/` prefix, `/.git/` substring) with the critical negative `src/.github/workflows/test.yml` pinned in `helpers.test.ts:172`. #24738 (`cc8441d9`) is a single-file 8-line surgical regression fix identifying commit `6002500bc` as having dropped the `childStore.icon` fallback in `enrich` causing different subdirectories of the same git repo to share the DB-keyed worktree icon — fix at `layout.tsx:391-401` does `{ ...base.icon, override: childStore.icon }` so other `icon` subfields survive the partial-override merge, returns `base` unchanged in the no-override branch with zero allocation overhead, and the `if (childStore.icon)` truthy check correctly handles both `undefined` and empty-string. At openai/codex, #19939 (`f2d62613`, fixes #19925) is a one-line conditional flip at `chatwidget.rs:4949-4952` changing the `MessagePhase::FinalAnswer | None` arm of `pending_status_indicator_restore` from unconditional `false` to `!self.pending_steers.is_empty()` so the working spinner restores after a streamed final answer when a user steer was queued mid-turn — `pending_steers` is the right signal (narrower than `is_task_running()` which would over-restore in the no-steer case), and the test at `status_and_layout.rs:960-1016` pins all four cells of the lifecycle (task started → spinner visible, deltas → spinner hidden but task running, steer submitted → `pending_steers.len() == 1` with `Op::UserTurn` content asserted, final-answer completion → spinner *visible* + task running which is the new invariant, then steer-completion → `pending_steers.is_empty()` and spinner stays visible). #19914 (`4a56f048`) caches remote plugin bundles on install with the right architectural shape — `plugins.rs:421-485` reorders to validate→download→install→notify-backend→load-apps-from-materialized-bundle so a corrupt local bundle never reaches the backend install POST, the new `fetch_remote_plugin_detail_with_download_urls` returns `bundle_download_url` and `release_version` for cache-keying as `cache/<marketplace>/<name>/<version>`, the test `plugin_install_writes_remote_plugin_to_cloud_and_cache` at `plugin_install.rs:185-274` exercises the full happy path and verifies four cells post-install (install-POST count, bundle-GET count, two `installed_path.join(...).is_file()` files including a `.tar.gz`-extracted `skills/plan-work/SKILL.md`, and the negative cache-key-uses-display-name pin), and `CODEX_TEST_ALLOW_HTTP_REMOTE_PLUGIN_BUNDLE_DOWNLOADS` env-gating cleanly separates production HTTPS-only from test mock `http://`; nits are the `remote_plugin_bundle_install_error_to_jsonrpc` helper collapsing every variant into a generic `internal_error` (losing user-vs-operator distinction the symmetric catalog-error helper preserves with a `match`), the `=0.4.45` exact-pin on `tar` worth confirming as deliberate vs. accidental, and 848 lines in one new file is hard to review-split. At BerriAI/litellm, #26675 (`3ca98545`) is a 7-line deletion in `vertex_ai/common_utils.py:710-716` removing the block that stripped `items` from `anyOf` array branches whose `items` was empty `{}`, with the load-bearing observation in the PR body that downstream `process_items()` already converts empty `items: {}` → `items: {"type": "object"}` so the removed code was redundant cleanup that produced a Vertex-rejected schema — the unit-test snapshot update at `test_vertex_ai_common_utils.py:225-232` (codified-broken-shape replaced with codified-correct-shape) is the strongest possible regression anchor, the de-flake conversion of `test_gemini_tool_calling_not_working` at `test_amazing_vertex_completion.py:3569-3705` from a real-Vertex network call to a `MagicMock`-patched HTTPHandler test asserts the request body sent to Vertex contains `items` on the array branch of `callbacks.anyOf` (mirroring commit `eddae66697`'s in-repo idiom), and the PR body's "Confirmed both tests fail when the production fix is reverted" is the right reverse-the-fix-and-watch-the-test-fail validation. #26663 (`39f008bc`, fixes #26656) bundles **three unrelated fixes** — the named auth `access_group_ids` enforcement (correctly shaped early check at `auth_checks.py:2693-2710` resolving group models when `models=[]` instead of falling through to all-model access, symmetrically applied to teams at `:2770-2787` with 4 unit tests covering the right cells), an unannounced `_update_user_db` carve-out at `db_spend_update_writer.py:507-541` adding an early return when `team_id is not None` with a preserved end-user-spend-still-enqueued sub-cell (subtle invariant change that means `LiteLLM_UserTable.spend` understates personal+team aggregates with no test for the carve-out cell and no PR body mention), and an unrelated ZAI provider message-content-flattening fix at `zai/chat/transformation.py:11-99` for GLM chat templates dropping list-format content (well-attributed to vllm-project/vllm#39614 with 114 lines of test coverage but should be its own PR). At block/goose, #8869 (`2efc7957`, relates #8672) is a `+4/-31` pure-deletion fix in `download_cli.sh:97-100` replacing a 27-line tangle of PWD-based `[[ "$PWD" =~ ^/mnt/[a-zA-Z]/ ]]` heuristics nested inside the WSL detection branch (plus a `command -v powershell.exe`/`cmd.exe` probe that always fired in modern WSL2 because Windows interop ships callable from Linux processes by default) with a single `OS="linux"` assignment when `/proc/version` matches the WSL kernel marker — the new comment names the load-bearing invariant correctly ("WSL is a Linux environment regardless of the current working directory"), the three concrete failure modes named in the PR body (`.zip` of Windows `.exe`, install into `$HOME/.local/bin/`, `AppData\Roaming` config attempts) are all downstream consequences of the same upstream `OS="windows"` misclassification eliminated by the fix, the native Windows `[[ "${WINDIR:-}" ]] || [[ "${windir:-}" ]] || [[ "$OSTYPE" == "msys" ]] || [[ "$OSTYPE" == "cygwin" ]]` check at `:96` is preserved unchanged so Git Bash/Cygwin/native Windows-shell installs still resolve to `OS="windows"`, and the Git-Bash-with-Windows-style-mount-points elif at `:103-105` is correctly left alone (real distinct codepath without `/proc/version`). At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3677 (`f18cc05c`, fixes #3669/#3228/#3387) adds a MiniMax OpenAI-compatible provider that opts into tagged thinking parsing only when the endpoint matches `*.minimaxi.com` — provider-gating travels through one `responseParsingOptions.ts` config struct rather than scattered conditionals, the new `taggedThinkingParser.ts` (+80) is a standalone module that carries partial-match state across SSE chunk boundaries (load-bearing for the streaming-tag-split cell where `<think>` arrives as `<thi` + `nk>` across chunks), and the CLI display fix at `useGeminiStream.ts:108-111` adds `stripLeadingBlankLines` applied via `if (newGeminiMessageBuffer.trim().length === 0) return newGeminiMessageBuffer;` early-returns at `:773` and `:856` symmetrically for both agent-message and thought paths preventing the empty-`✦`-row symptom by not creating the pending history item until there's actual content; tests at `useGeminiStream.test.tsx:1208-1278` and `:1336-1406` pin both branches with the right cells (leading `\n\n` chunk → `pendingHistoryItems` stays empty, then content chunk → exactly one pending item with the post-strip text); three closed issues raise confidence the bug had multiple independent reports; nits are the duplicated `trim().length === 0` predicate across the two early-return sites (worth a shared `shouldStartNewPendingItem(buffer)` helper) and the `*.minimaxi.com` substring check that should be tightened to exact-suffix or hostname-parse to rule out the substring-false-positive risk.
