Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24738 surgical 8-line regression fix with named blamed commit and correct partial-override merge shape; openai/codex #19939 one-line conditional flip with `pending_steers` as the correct narrower-than-`is_task_running` signal and a 4-cell test pinning the full lifecycle including the new invariant; BerriAI/litellm #26675 7-line production deletion with the strongest-possible regression-anchor snapshot update plus hermetic mock conversion of the previously-flaky network test asserting the array branch carries `items`; block/goose #8869 +4/-31 pure-deletion replacing a 27-line PWD-heuristic tangle with a one-line "WSL is Linux regardless of cwd" assignment correctly preserving the native-Windows and Git-Bash-mount-point branches), 3 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24740 right-shaped batch-as-perf-optimization-never-correctness-primitive with all five structural-check failures bailing to per-file fallback and load-bearing UI debounce + git-metadata filter test cells, but the binary NUL sniff may diverge from per-file `show()` semantics for true binaries and `100`ms debounce is a magic number; openai/codex #19914 right validate→download→install→notify ordering with 4-cell post-install verification and env-gated HTTP-allowed test isolation, but the bundle-install error helper collapses every variant losing user-vs-operator distinction and 848 lines in one new file is hard to review-split; QwenLM/qwen-code #3677 right provider-gated `*.minimaxi.com`-only thinking-tag parsing with standalone parser module carrying partial-match state across SSE chunks and symmetric agent-message + thought early-returns preventing empty `✦` row, but duplicated `trim().length === 0` predicate across two sites and substring `*.minimaxi.com` check should tighten to exact-suffix), 1 request-changes (BerriAI/litellm #26663 bundles three unrelated fixes — auth `access_group_ids` enforcement (correctly shaped + tested), unannounced `_update_user_db` team-id carve-out with subtle invariant change and no carve-out test, and unrelated ZAI message-content flattening for GLM chat templates — must be split into 3 PRs with their own root causes named, and the spend-tracker change needs a dedicated test cell pinning the "team key + end_user_id still enqueues END_USER SpendUpdateQueueItem" carve-out plus PR-body acknowledgment of the personal+team aggregate understatement). Repo coverage: 4 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm + block/goose + QwenLM/qwen-code) — pushing to 5 if the litellm+goose+qwen single-PR-each repos are counted distinctly per the dispatch convention.

### W17 drip-135 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 2 QwenLM/qwen-code). At sst/opencode, #24745 (`a3ccb821`, kzekiue, closes #24744) is a `+8/-6` two-file fix for a real "edited project name/icon never shows up in the sidebar" symptom: the layout reducer at `context/layout.tsx:386-396` was returning `{ ...metadata, ...project }` from server `globalSync.data.project` only, dropping the locally-saved `childStore.projectMeta` for any project whose server row is missing or is the catch-all `id === "global"` bucket; new condition at `:393` (`!metadata || metadata.id === "global"`) routes the local meta into a six-source merge with explicit per-subfield `icon`/`commands` partial-overrides at `:394-395` mirroring the same pattern landed in drip-134 #24738; companion fix at `avatar.tsx:33-47` swaps the cached-once `const src = split.src` for inline `split.src` reads at four sites so Solid's reactive accessor re-evaluates `<Show when=>` on upload (the prior behavior left the fallback initials rendered until full remount), with the author's "did this so i can zero it out to test fallback" comment correctly removed since the test gymnastic stops being needed once `split.src` is the canonical accessor; nits are the six-spread merge expression worth naming an intermediate `merged` const, the `local.commands` truthy-check (vs key-count) replacing rather than merging when the user clears all commands, and no test for the "global metadata row + local override" cell. #24734 (`c4481d9b`) is a +3/-1 conditional flip in `provider/provider.ts:1361-1363` wrapping the existing `model.variants = mapValues(ProviderTransform.variants(model), (v) => v)` assignment in `if (!model.variants || Object.keys(model.variants).length === 0)` so an upstream-API-supplied variants object isn't clobbered by the derived value — but ships with no PR body, no linked issue, no test for a load-bearing path that runs on every refresh, and the immediately-following `configVariants` merge at `:1364-1370` implicitly assumes canonical-shape variants so any byte-incompatibility between API-returned and `ProviderTransform.variants`-derived shape is now a partially-shaped production-first bug per provider — needs (1) PR body naming the failure mode, (2) two-cell unit test pinning empty-API-variants→derived-applied and non-empty-API-variants→user-supplied-survives-and-downstream-merge-still-works, (3) confirmation the affected provider's API shape is byte-compatible with `ProviderTransform.variants` output. At BerriAI/litellm, #26673 (`02e92113`, AinzRimuru) is a 15-line registry add for `openrouter/zai/glm-5.1` to `model_prices_and_context_window.json:27209-27223` with all twelve consumer-checked fields self-consistent (`litellm_provider: "openrouter"` matching the `openrouter/` prefix routing convention, `mode: "chat"`, the four capability flags, the four cost fields with `cache_read_input_token_cost = 5.25e-07` exactly half of `input_cost_per_token = 1.05e-06` matching documented "cached read = 50% of input" for GLM-5.1, `max_input_tokens: 202752` aligned to OpenRouter's 256-token-boundary publishing convention, `max_output_tokens: 65535` matching the standard GLM-5.x family cap), `source: "https://openrouter.ai/z-ai/glm-5.1"` field included as the reviewer's verification anchor; nits are no `_backup.json` mirror update (drip-132 #26644 honored the "both files in lockstep" convention — needs PR-body confirmation of whether backup is auto-regenerated or hand-maintained), missing `supports_pdf_input`/`supports_response_schema`/`supports_system_messages` flags relative to sibling `openrouter/zai/glm-4.6`, and base branch is `litellm_internal_staging` (the canonical landing branch for vendor-team registry adds, not main). #26671 (`8540a78b`, cy-jhs) closes a real silent-no-op bug end-to-end where `client.responses.retrieve(response_id, stream=True, starting_after=N)` through the proxy was returning `200 OK` with empty body because the proxy GET handler ignored query params, the core `aget_responses` had no `stream`/`starting_after` parameters, and provider transforms didn't surface them — the PR walks all three layers in one pass: proxy reads `request.query_params.get("stream")` and `"starting_after"` at `endpoints.py:530-549` with defensive `int(...)` inside `try/except ValueError` raising `HTTPException(400)` with the literal `query_starting_after!r` echoed back, core dispatcher `responses/main.py:1368-1369`/`:1448-1449` threads them through, and OpenAI/Azure provider transforms put them into the returned `data: Dict` so the HTTP layer attaches them as query params (`openai/.../transformation.py:463-466`, `azure/.../transformation.py:273-277`), with `Manus` and `Volcengine` getting the new args added to their `transform_get_response_api_request` signatures with explicit "accepted for signature parity, silently ignored" comments — the HTTP-layer streaming wiring at `http_handler.py:489-512` uses the canonical httpx `build_request("GET", ...) ; client.send(req, stream=True, ...)` idiom with the right caller-contract docstring naming the `aiter_lines()` iteration + `aclose()` connection-release responsibility, and `async_get_responses` at `llm_http_handler.py:2782-2811` wraps the streamed response in the same `ResponsesAPIStreamingIterator` used for streaming POSTs so parsing/hooks/logging stay uniform across stream-on-POST and stream-on-retrieve paths; 175-line test file with 6 cells covers OpenAI transform with/without resume args, Azure transform with resume args, Volcengine + Manus signature-parity (assert `data == {}` despite passing args), and the integration cell monkeypatching `get_async_httpx_client` and triple-asserting `isinstance(result, ResponsesAPIStreamingIterator)` + `captured["stream"] is True` + `captured["params"] == {"stream": "true", "starting_after": 6}` — the exact wiring contract; nits are no proxy-FastAPI-TestClient test for the `endpoints.py::get_response` query-param parsing (the actual entry point of the bug), the `azure/.../transformation.py:271` string-`"true"`-vs-bool-`True` inconsistency worth a one-line comment, no symmetric `stream` kwarg added to the sync `HTTPHandler.get` (callers invoking sync with `stream=True` would hit `TypeError`), and the empty-string `model=""` at `:2803` worth a comment naming "resume call doesn't know model upfront." At google-gemini/gemini-cli, #26071 (`ee347a76`, gemini-cli-robot) fixes the `gh issue list --limit 1000`-induced metric cap in `tools/gemini-cli-bot/metrics/scripts/{open_issues,open_prs}.ts` by swapping to `gh api "search/issues?q=repo:${repo}+is:issue+is:open" --jq .total_count` (and `is:pr` for PRs) which returns the true server-side count without paging, with `GITHUB_REPOSITORY` env-var fallback to `${GITHUB_OWNER}/${GITHUB_REPO}` constants for local-vs-Actions runtime parity, and the output format change from CSV `open_issues,${count}\n` to structured JSON `{ metric, value, timestamp }` via `MetricOutput` typed import — error path now uses `process.stderr.write` with `err.message` instead of `catch {}` swallow with the `err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)` non-Error-thrown handling and a JSON-shaped fallback so the downstream pipeline doesn't break on errors; nits are 95% file duplication between `open_issues.ts` and `open_prs.ts` (should share a `runGhCountQuery(query, metricName)` helper to prevent drift), the `gh api search/issues` endpoint's separate 30 req/min rate limit (worth caching for ≥2 minutes or catching 403 with a sentinel `value: -1`), the un-encoded `+` in the query string fragile against future repo-name extensions, no Vitest unit test stubbing `execSync`, and the output-format change from CSV→JSON is a *consumer-breaking change* not mentioned in the PR body — either a prior PR updated the consumer or this needs a co-landing follow-up to avoid a metrics-pipeline outage. At block/goose, #8873 (`958c7989`, matt2e) is a `+60/-35` three-file dedup eliminating one of two startup `_goose/providers/list` RPCs by merging `loadProviders` and `loadProviderInventory` into a single `loadProvidersAndInventory` at `useAppStartup.ts:48-72` that runs the inventory fetch once and *derives* the provider list from the same response via the new `discoverAcpProvidersFromEntries` helper at `acp.ts:46-51` — `acpApi.ts:43-57` extracts `buildProviderListFromEntries(entries)` from the inline body of `listProviders` so both consumers (the existing RPC-fetching `listProviders` and the new entries-receiving `discoverAcpProvidersFromEntries`) route through the same `DEPRECATED_PROVIDER_IDS` filter + `DEFAULT_PROVIDER` prepend, killing the drift class; private `resolveProvidersCatalog` in `acp.ts:53` factors out the seen-set dedup so both `discoverAcpProviders` and `discoverAcpProvidersFromEntries` use identical dedup semantics; loading-state lifecycle correctly merged with both `setProvidersLoading(true)` and `inventoryStore.setLoading(true)` set before the await and both cleared in the same `finally`; error message updated to "Failed to load providers and inventory on startup" matching the merged operation, `perfLog` reports both `entries=` and `providers=` counts; `Promise.allSettled` shape preserved (array shrinks by one, the downstream `.then((entries) => refreshConfiguredProviderInventory(entries))` chain is preserved verbatim), `DEPRECATED_PROVIDER_IDS` and `DEFAULT_PROVIDER` cleanly widened from `const` to `export const` for the helper. At QwenLM/qwen-code, #3694 (`f3af25b3`, tanzhenxin) is a 0-additions / 477-deletions test cleanup removing 8 flaky TUI input tests across `AuthDialog.test.tsx`, `InputPrompt.test.tsx`, and `StandaloneSessionPicker.test.tsx` based on mining the last 60 failed `Qwen Code CI` runs and observing each removed test fails on **2-10 distinct unrelated SHAs** with low fails-per-SHA — the textbook signature of a timing flake (real bugs are reproducible on a single SHA), with the top offender (`AuthDialog > should trigger OpenRouter OAuth from API key options`) failing on 10 distinct SHAs across 3 matrix legs; common shape across all 8 is `stdin.write(...)` followed by a fixed `setTimeout(50-350ms)` waiting for React state to settle (the textbook "race fixed-delay against real async event" anti-pattern), same root cause as the existing `itWhenTuiInputReliable` guard at `AuthDialog.test.tsx:728` which already skips Windows + CI Node v20 but isn't broad enough now that the flake reproduces on Ubuntu 24.x as well, and `InputPrompt.test.tsx`/`StandaloneSessionPicker.test.tsx` have no equivalent guard at all; coverage-loss bounded with the surviving wizard review-screen test pinning the load-bearing review/save path; concerns are 2 of the 8 deletions had genuine regression-anchor value (the `InputPrompt` "microtask race" test at `:232-246` whose deleted comment explicitly named "Enter path must NOT call buffer.insert — it passes text directly to handleSubmitAndClear. Calling insert would re-fill the buffer after it was already cleared (the microtask race bug)" pinned a real bug not just a UX cell, and the `StandaloneSessionPicker` Space-to-preview/Esc-to-close cell tested an open/close transition not covered by the surviving in-preview Enter-fires-onSelect test) and the minimum-cost alternative — widening the `isUnreliableTuiInputEnvironment` predicate at `:725-727` to detect Ubuntu 24.x and converting deleted tests to `itWhenTuiInputReliable(...)` — would preserve coverage on environments where they pass while skipping on flaky ones, worth at least mentioning in the PR body why "delete" was preferred over "expand the skip predicate." #3692 (`d4211c25`, qqqys, refs #3681) is a textbook "missed-symmetry-after-prior-PR" fix routing `SessionService.countSessionMessages` through `jsonl.parseLineTolerant` so the listSessions count matches the resume-time record count — `sessionService.ts:323-336` (pre-diff) had its own readline loop with inline `JSON.parse(trimmed)` inside `try/catch { continue }` that silently dropped any line that wasn't perfect-JSON, including the `}{`-glued lines from interrupted appends (the canonical #3606 corruption shape) that PR #3606's read-side recovery now handles via `parseLineTolerant`; fix widens `parseLineTolerant` from private `function` to `export function` at `jsonl-utils.ts:115` with the docstring at `:109-114` updated from "Mirrors the silent skip in `countSessionMessages`" (the prior framing was the bug) to "Use this from any streaming reader that walks JSONL line-by-line and wants the same recovery semantics as `read()` / `readLines()`" — that docstring rewording is load-bearing as the canonical-entry-point announcement, and the replacement at `sessionService.ts:329-335` swaps inline `JSON.parse` for `for (const record of jsonl.parseLineTolerant<ChatRecord>(trimmed, filePath)) { ... }` preserving the `user`/`assistant` filter and `uniqueUuids: Set<string>` semantics verbatim with the only behavior diff being "well-formed yields 1, glued yields 2" instead of "well-formed yields 1, glued yields 0"; the "why streaming, not `jsonl.read()` + dedupe" rationale in the PR body correctly rejects the issue's alternate suggestion because `countSessionMessages` is on the `listSessions` hot path (default page size 20) with active sessions holding thousands of records, so full-loading would change O(uuid-set memory) to O(record-count memory) per listed session; 3 unit tests at `jsonl-utils.test.ts:84-104` cover the load-bearing cells (well-formed → `[{a:1}]`, `}{`-glued → `[{uuid:'a'},{uuid:'b'}]`, unrecoverable `'not-json'` → `[]` with the third being the most important regression anchor against future "silent-skip becomes hard-crash" drift); Item 2 from #3681 (write-side `fsync`/atomic-rename durability) intentionally deferred per the issue's own "Open for discussion — no immediate action required" guidance.

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (block/goose #8873 right-shaped startup-RPC dedup with shared `buildProviderListFromEntries` helper killing the filter-drift class, private `resolveProvidersCatalog` keeping dedup semantics identical across both entry points, and correctly merged loading-state lifecycle + error message + perf telemetry; QwenLM/qwen-code #3692 textbook missed-symmetry-after-prior-PR fix routing `countSessionMessages` through the existing `parseLineTolerant` helper with the load-bearing docstring rewording announcing the canonical streaming-reader entry point, preserved O(uuid-set) memory profile vs the rejected `jsonl.read()+dedupe` O(record-count) alternative, and the unrecoverable-input → `[]` regression anchor against silent-skip-becomes-hard-crash drift), 5 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24745 right diagnosis with the `!metadata || metadata.id === "global"` condition correctly firing for both no-server-row and global-bucket cells plus per-subfield `icon`/`commands` partial-overrides mirroring drip-134 #24738, and the `avatar.tsx` cached-once → inline reactive-accessor swap is the minimal correct fix for the upload-doesn't-show symptom but the six-spread merge expression should name an intermediate `merged` const and no test for the global-row + local-override cell; BerriAI/litellm #26673 self-consistent 15-line registry add with `cache_read_input_token_cost` correctly half of `input_cost_per_token` matching the documented 50% cached-read pricing and `source` field as reviewer anchor but no `_backup.json` mirror update per the drip-132 #26644 lockstep convention and missing capability flags relative to sibling `openrouter/zai/glm-4.6`; BerriAI/litellm #26671 closes the silent-no-op `responses.retrieve(stream=True)` bug end-to-end across all three layers (proxy query-param parsing with defensive `int()` and 400 echoing the bad value, core dispatcher signature widening, provider transforms with explicit "signature parity, silently ignored" comments for non-supporting providers) with the canonical httpx `build_request + send(stream=True)` idiom and the 6-cell test file triple-asserting iterator-type + stream-flag + params, but missing FastAPI-TestClient test for the actual entry-point query-param parsing, no symmetric `stream` kwarg on the sync `HTTPHandler.get`, and the string-`"true"` vs bool-`True` inconsistency at `azure/.../transformation.py:271` worth a comment; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26071 right diagnosis of the `gh ... --limit 1000`-induced metric cap with the right `gh api search/issues?q=...&jq .total_count` swap and `GITHUB_REPOSITORY` env-var fallback for local-vs-Actions parity but 95% file duplication between `open_issues.ts` and `open_prs.ts` should share a helper, the search-API's 30 req/min separate rate-limit is unhandled, and the CSV→JSON output-format change is a *consumer-breaking change* not mentioned in the PR body needing co-landing consumer update; QwenLM/qwen-code #3694 right methodology mining 60 failed CI runs to identify the 2-10-distinct-SHAs flake signature with bounded coverage-loss but 2 of 8 deletions had genuine regression-anchor value (the `InputPrompt` microtask-race test whose deleted comment explicitly named the bug it pinned, and the `StandaloneSessionPicker` Space-to-preview/Esc-to-close transition not covered by the surviving in-preview-Enter test), and the minimum-cost alternative — widening `isUnreliableTuiInputEnvironment` to detect Ubuntu 24.x and converting deletions to `itWhenTuiInputReliable(...)` — would preserve env-conditional coverage and should at least be mentioned in the PR body), 1 needs-discussion (sst/opencode #24734 surgical 2-line conditional flip in a hot path with no PR body, no linked issue, no test for a load-bearing variants-sync code path that runs on every refresh, and the immediately-following `configVariants` merge implicitly assumes canonical-shape variants so any byte-incompatibility between API-returned and `ProviderTransform.variants`-derived shape is now a partially-shaped production-first bug per provider — needs PR body naming the failure mode, two-cell unit test pinning empty-API-variants → derived-applied and non-empty-API-variants → user-supplied-survives, and confirmation the affected provider's API shape is byte-compatible). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).

QwenLM/qwen-code #3699 textbook "schema said required but field always had a sensible default" fix touching all three layers (interface signature, JSON-schema `required` list, validator gate) with the validator now accepting `undefined || boolean` while still rejecting wrong types, two-cell positive-omit + negative-wrong-type test; QwenLM/qwen-code #3693 two-line additive token-limit pattern for deepseek-v4 family above the `^deepseek` catchall in both `PATTERNS` and `OUTPUT_PATTERNS`, with `LIMITS['384k']: 384_000` constant added and inline comment naming the vendor-declared decimal vs binary-power convention; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26074 right refactor-then-feature shape extracting `hasErrorCode(e, codes)` helper from three duplicate predicate sites then adding the `ENAMETOOLONG`/`ENOTDIR` arm at the end with load-bearing comment naming the at-mention probe as the use case and "return input unchanged so downstream rejects normally" as the intentional contract), 2 merge-after-nits (openai/codex #19847 closes the "writable-root grant silently shadows protected metadata" hole with per-root `protected_metadata_names` computation gated on `can_write_path_with_cwd` so operator overrides survive, regex shape correctly anchored with root + name escaping and root-`/` special case, three call sites updated symmetrically with full-disk paths emitting empty Vec while only the policy-derived writable-roots path populates names — nits are `PROTECTED_METADATA_PATH_NAMES` iteration order being part of the test assertion (one-line constant comment would prevent trap), no regression test for the policy-grants-metadata-write-back case where the `can_write_path_with_cwd` short-circuit allows tooling profiles to keep `.git` writable, and the `_ = dot_agents_canonical` test rebind is a code-style artifact; block/goose #8870 right-shaped cumulative-vs-per-turn token-count fix using `accumulated_*.or(*)` fallback at both `JsonMetadata` and `Complete` event sites with symmetric `input_tokens`/`output_tokens` field expansion under `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`, error-path emitting `None` for all three counts, and stream-json branch refactored to one `get_session` read + unified match — nits are the fallback semantics should be locked down with a unit test pinning `accumulated.or(per_turn)` priority, no CHANGELOG for the new fields, and `skip_serializing_if` makes absence ambiguous between "lookup failed" and "no input/output split tracked"; litellm #26637 opt-in `include_model_info` query param flag wiring through three call sites in `proxy_server.py` and into `create_model_info_response` with `try/except Exception` fail-quiet on the per-row router lookup so a single bad model can't 500 the whole listing, `model_info: {}` sentinel for unknown/wildcard/router-error/no-router cells preserving consistent response shape, five-cell test matrix covering all failure modes — nits are no FastAPI-TestClient end-to-end test, response-size widens substantially for deployments with hundreds of model groups (worth an `expand` value option), and the `Optional[bool] + or False` coercion repeated 3× could share a helper). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos.

### W17 drip-137 (2026-04-28)

openai/codex #19880 right cancellation-token plumbing through `ExecExpiration::cancellation_token()` accessor and `WindowsSandboxCancellationToken` IPC poll-thread sending `Message::Terminate` on the runner pipe with the load-bearing post-loop join ordering, but `try_clone()` failure is silent regression to no-cancellation, 50ms `park_timeout` is magic number, and `Message::Terminate` write error is `let _ = ...` swallowed; BerriAI/litellm #26678 closes the DeepSeek V4 multi-turn-tool-call rejection by preserving the `reasoning_content` slot via the `tool_calls` truthy-guard at `types/utils.py:1224` and injecting empty default at three transform sites plus two raw-API-call sites, with the `requires_deepseek_v4_reasoning_content` predicate handling the `responses/` prefix and the load-bearing negative test cell pinning DeepSeek-reasoner is not patched, but the `get_supported_openai_params` `"deepseek"` substring widens beyond V4 and may 400 V3/R1, no integration test against real V4 endpoint, and the `tool_call_id` arm in the role-assistant filter at `:289` is likely dead code), and 0 needs-discussion / request-changes. Includes one borderline merge-after-nits (gemini-cli #26069) where the defense-in-depth coercion at both CLI ingestion (`config.ts:818-830`) and core resolver (`models.ts:111-117`) correctly handles yargs's tri-state `string | string[] | boolean | undefined` argv shape with last-wins semantics for arrays, three load-bearing test cells `(['a','b'])→'b'`, `(true)→'true'`, `(null)→''`, and the negative `isCustomModel(['a','b'])→true` cell locking the symmetric behavior — main concern is duplicated coercion logic across two files that should share a `normalizeModelInput(input, {keepUndefined})` helper. Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos.

openai/codex #19956 mature operational doctrine for an installable capacity-analysis skill with right Core Rules ordering (collapse-VMs, Pacific-time, risk-forward forecast, decision-by-cushion-percent), correctly-sequenced Workflow at `:31-69`, deterministic Decision Rubric at `:67-71`, and unusually well-specified evidence-link discipline at `:75-92` — but description sentence ordering is wrong for skill-discovery indexing, hardcoded Datadog widget IDs at `:88-92` will drift, and Slack-vs-Codex output-surface ambiguity at `:97`; BerriAI/litellm #26676 right cron-to-PR conversion with deterministic branch name `compat-matrix/<litellm>-<claude>-<date>` driving idempotent re-runs, empty-input ValueError closing the silent-collision hole, four-cell test matrix pinning the same-input-collide / any-change-diverge / byte-identical-no-PR / empty-input-rejected invariants, and load-bearing column-order assertion `feature_line.index("anthropic=") < feature_line.index("bedrock_invoke=")` catching dict-iteration-order silent reorders — but no branch-name length sanitization, fragile `gh pr create` "already exists" string-match, and `--force-with-lease` keying not visible in this diff sample; QwenLM/qwen-code #3701 right diagnosis of `/export` arrow-key flow gap with `EXPORT_FORMAT_COMPLETIONS` carve-out plus refs-not-state pattern at `InputPrompt.tsx:210-211` for transient interaction state, and four-cell test matrix at `:745-907` covering no-arrow-Enter, arrow-Enter, multi-arrow-cycling, and integration-with-real-buffer cells with the load-bearing negative `mockInputHistory.navigateDown not called` assertion — but format list duplicated between constant and inline regex at `:78`, hardcoded list belongs in the slash-command tree not the UI component, and Windows-only manual validation is the same blind spot mined by drip-135 #3694), 1 needs-discussion (openai/codex #19949 protocol-widening for three new ExternalAgentConfig migration types Subagents/Hooks/Commands lands schema-first across six JSON files in lockstep with the protocol enum, `Cargo.toml:77` adds `serde_yaml` (a non-trivial trust boundary expansion that previously had RCE-class CVEs in the alias-bomb class), and the symmetry of the importer wiring at `external_agent_config.rs:189-243` is correct — but the PR ships with an empty body, a 1155-line new implementation file `external_agent_config_migration.rs` not visible in any review-time summary, the `merge_missing_toml_values` call at `:295` has subtle detect-only-not-write semantics that aren't documented at the call site, and the `#[path]`-inlined-submodule pattern at `:24-25` is an unusual structural choice with no inline rationale; needs PR body naming the conflict-resolution strategy, the trust-boundary assumptions for serde_yaml, the not-tested-here cells, and the rationale for the inlined-submodule pattern), and 1 request-changes (BerriAI/litellm #26680 vendor-solicited "MseeP.ai security assessment badge" PR submitted by the badge vendor's own bot account targeting `litellm_internal_staging` (not main) with a 2-line README-top diff that gives `mseep.net` referer-leak/pixel-tracking on every README impression and implies a third-party endorsement relationship the project did not initiate — recommend close-as-decline-by-policy or escalate to maintainer for explicit decision; if accepted, at minimum move below the existing `<h1>` and request versioned-URL badge stability commitment from vendor). Must be in X.Y.Z-preview.N format`, with `gh run view 25032972161` as the failure anchor; QwenLM/qwen-code #3703 one-character `--merge`→`--squash` swap at `release-sdk.yml:408` matching the repo policy `mergeCommitAllowed:false, rebaseMergeAllowed:false, squashMergeAllowed:true, viewerDefaultMergeMethod:SQUASH` which made the previous workflow categorically unrunnable, with `gh run view 25036315088` as the failure anchor and `gh repo view --json mergeCommitAllowed,...` as the policy evidence), 2 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24746 single-row ecosystem-table addition at `ecosystem.mdx:43` clustered correctly underneath `opencode-supermemory` with a description that names the two specific contexts state evaporates ("compactions and sessions") rather than the vaguer "persistent memory" framing — but linked-repo trust check is the load-bearing pre-merge gate not visible in the diff, description is ~95 chars and may push column-width on narrow renders, and self-submission pattern (`sdwolf4103` matches both PR author and linked-repo owner) is standard but worth confirming repo is non-malicious; BerriAI/litellm #26685 right-shape "widen base contract → sibling no-op forwarders → only Bedrock consumes" sequencing across 12 transformers with the load-bearing `deepcopy` at `bedrock/vector_stores/transformation.py:222` preventing in-place mutation of caller's `extra_body`, dual-key acceptance (`retrievalConfiguration` or `retrieval_configuration`), and `verbose_logger.debug` override-with-warning pattern at `:226-235` and `:243-251` for `max_num_results`/`filters` precedence — but 11-place mechanical addition is fragile (every new sibling transformer is a forget-point; needs base-class kwarg discipline or lint rule), inconsistent debug-format between the two override sites, both-keys-set silent-precedence hole, and `cast` at `:255-257` gives up the typed-construction safety the prior code had), 1 needs-discussion (openai/codex #19896 bot-authored single-field default-flip at `models.json:114` changing `gpt-5.4`'s `default_reasoning_level` from `xhigh` to `medium` — every user who selects `gpt-5.4` without explicit override gets a different latency/cost/quality profile from the next release, supported_levels list unchanged so capability ceiling preserved but out-of-the-box UX shifts materially; PR body is literally "Automated update of models.json" with zero rationale, no eval link, no before/after numbers, no release-note hook, and no inter-model consistency check against sibling 5.x defaults), and 1 merge-after-nits with structural-fix recommendation (QwenLM/qwen-code #3698 right diagnosis of "ACP `Session` bypassed `GeminiClient.sendMessageStream`'s compression pre-flight by calling lower-level `GeminiChat.sendMessageStream` directly" — same shape as several W9/W13 two-surfaces-one-backend bugs — with the correct fix shape: extract `#sendMessageStreamWithAutoCompression(promptId, message, abortSignal)` helper at `Session.ts:786-802` that runs `tryCompressChat` then re-fetches `geminiClient.getChat()` (load-bearing because compression may replace the chat instance, pinned by test at `:339-368`), removed all cached `chat` references including the stale-prone `chat: GeminiChat` parameter on `#handleStopHookLoop` at `:556-562`, and four-cell test coverage at `:308-440` covering ordered-invocation (`invocationCallOrder` assertion), instance-replacement, tool-response-follow-up sends, and the negative slash-command-no-send cell — but `tryCompressChat(promptId, false, abortSignal)` hard-codes `force=false` without a comment, `#getCurrentChat()` and inline `geminiClient.getChat()` are now two ways of saying the same thing, and no test for compression-AbortError-propagation). ### W17 drip-142 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (1 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 block/goose, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "discrimination at the boundary" — Warp+Win32 alt-screen carve-out via env-var double-discriminator (TERM_PROGRAM + WARP_SESSION_ID belt-and-suspenders), workspace-metadata directory protection promoted from existence-conditional subpath list to first-class `protected_metadata_names: Vec<String>` on WritableRoot closing the "create `.git` post-hoc to bypass" gap, marketplace add+remove TUI flow with misleadingly-titled "remove marketplace" PR scrubbing `source.display()` from user-facing errors (load-bearing regression for support workflows), websearch_interception agentic-loop dynamic credentials propagation closing a real 401-cooldown-cascade (issue #26389), `_success_handler_helper_fn` dict/list dispatch unification routing previously-bypass-path inputs through `_response_cost_calculator` (silent cost-surface widening), Goose2 adaptive theme picker rewrite with system-fallback / accent-suppression-in-system-mode / oklch color-mix shadcn-token bridge, **hostile fork-PR injecting `process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY` exfiltration POC into package.json npm scripts**, and CLI startup keystroke-loss fix via `earlyInput.ts` raw-stdin buffering + `KeypressContext` mount-time replay closing the lossy window between binary entry and Ink REPL mount.

### W17 drip-143 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code, 1 block/goose). Theme is "activate dormant protections + remove redundant work" — SSE idle-timer turned on by defaulting `chunkTimeout` to 60s (the wrap was already shipped, never armed), per-file `git show` fan-out collapsed to single `git cat-file --batch` with per-file fallback for malformed input, TUI `Worked for ...` separator switched from per-chunk incremental workaround to protocol-native `duration_ms`, app-server v2 notification `remoteControl/environment/updated` with Tokio-watch + initialize-replay so late subscribers reconcile state, vector-store `extra_body` threaded through 7 transformers + Bedrock `retrievalConfiguration` deep-copy/merge with optional_params-wins precedence, DeepSeek V4 multi-turn `reasoning_content` injection (with a load-bearing source-corruption bug: helper inserted inside `OpenAICredentials` NamedTuple class body breaking imports), `parseLineTolerant` exported and routed into `countSessionMessages` to fix the count vs resume discrepancy on `}{`-glued JSONL lines, and goose2 startup `_goose/providers/list` RPC dedup via shared `buildProviderListFromEntries` helper.

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24740 batch `git cat-file --batch` collapsing N processes to 1 with malformed-input fall-through-to-per-file safety net + 100ms watcher-event coalescing + Windows-backslash-normalized `.git`-metadata path filter pinned by both positive cells `.git/HEAD`/`/repo/.git/worktrees/feature/HEAD`/`C:\repo\.git\worktrees\feature\HEAD` and the load-bearing negative cells `src/git/index.ts`/`src/.github/workflows/test.yml` that prevent silent over-filtering of legitimate user file events; block/goose #8873 single source of truth `buildProviderListFromEntries` extraction lets startup eliminate the duplicate `_goose/providers/list` RPC by deriving providers from already-fetched inventory entries, paired-store atomicity preserved across `loadProvidersAndInventory`, perf-log line correctly merges to `entries=N, providers=M` preserving observability, error semantics correctly fold from two independent failures to one since the underlying call is now also one), 5 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24762 one-line `?? 60_000` activation of already-shipped SSE wrap protection at `provider.ts:1449` + new `case e.message === "SSE read timed out"` arm at `message-v2.ts:1118-1126` mapping to `APIError(isRetryable: true, metadata: { code: "SSE_TIMEOUT" })` plus dual round-trip + direct-classification test cells at `retry.test.ts:233-244`/`:332-339` — but 60s is hard-coded magic number, no test pins `chunkTimeout: 0` disables the timer, brittle exact-string match on `"SSE read timed out"` substring will silently regress on rename; openai/codex #19929 protocol-native `duration_ms` replaces per-separator incremental workaround with right-shape `or_else` fallback chain `duration_ms.and_then(try_from i64→u64).map(/1_000).or_else(status_widget.elapsed_seconds)`, snapshot test pinning `─ Worked for 2m 05s ─` line, `had_work_activity` reset moved from emit-time to turn-start fixing a subtle interrupted-turn carryover bug — but no test for negative-`duration_ms` fallback to status-widget, no test for sub-second turn `/ 1_000 = 0` rendering as `Worked for 0s`, mid-turn-deliberate-no-clock at `:5050` needs inline comment naming why; openai/codex #19919 v2 notification `remoteControl/environment/updated` with `environmentId: string | null` + Tokio-watch channel (chosen over transport event so late subscribers see the latest non-null id via `borrow()`) + initialize-time replay of current non-null id, six-surface schema symmetry held across schema/v2-schema/v2-file/ts-import/ts-union/everything-else, TUI exhaustive-match no-op carve-out keeping `cargo check -p codex-tui` green — but verify all four publication trigger points are tested (load/create/clear-for-account-change/backend-invalidate), confirm initialize-replay path explicitly skips `None` send, no in-tree TUI consumer beyond no-op is a smell that the contract is under-tested end-to-end; BerriAI/litellm #26685 `extra_body` threaded through 6 transformers + 3 handler callsites + base contract + async-defaults-to-sync forwarding, Bedrock-specific merge correctly `deepcopy`s before mutation and detects conflicts via verbose_logger.debug, camelCase + snake_case dual-key acceptance with short-circuit `or` — but undocumented "optional_params wins over extra_body" precedence (opposite of usual extra_body-as-escape-hatch in litellm), `cast(BedrockKBRetrievalConfiguration, ...)` at `:251-253` widens the unvalidated-passthrough surface vs the previous explicit field-by-field copy, no negative-cell test for matching `numberOfResults`, PR checklist tests-added box unchecked; QwenLM/qwen-code #3692 surgical export of `parseLineTolerant` from `jsonl-utils.ts:115` + flip of `countSessionMessages` from inline `try { JSON.parse } catch { continue }` to `for (const record of jsonl.parseLineTolerant<ChatRecord>(...))` so `}{`-glued lines contribute both uuids instead of neither, three-cell test matrix (well-formed/glued-recovery/unrecoverable) at `jsonl-utils.test.ts:85-103`, scope-discipline rejecting issue's "use jsonl.read() + dedupe" suggestion with O(uuid-set) memory-profile rationale, write-side durability deferred to follow-up per issue guidance — but no `sessionService.countSessionMessages`-level test pinning the integration end-to-end, only the helper layer is directly verified), and 1 request-changes (BerriAI/litellm #26678 right-shape `requires_deepseek_v4_reasoning_content` discriminator + `patch_deepseek_v4_reasoning_messages` mutator + `Message.__init__` slot-preservation when tool_calls present + four-cell test matrix V4-positive/V4-tool-calls-injection/reasoner-negative/types-utils-slot — but the diff at `litellm/llms/openai/common_utils.py:258-296` corrupts the source by inserting the new helper *between two fields* of the `class OpenAICredentials(NamedTuple):` body so the helper becomes a method scope, the imports `from .common_utils import patch_deepseek_v4_reasoning_messages` at `gpt_transformation.py:59` and `openai.py:60` cannot resolve as written, and the dangling `organization: Optional[str]` annotation at `:296` lands on the wrong scope; required: move the helper out of the NamedTuple class body to module scope before merge, with secondary nits to `deepcopy` mutation, tighten substring matcher to avoid `deepseek-v40`/`deepseek-v4o` false positives, narrow the `"deepseek" in model.lower()` widening at `:172-174` to V4 only). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos.

### W17 drip-144 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 block/goose, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "missing-invariant-at-the-boundary" — `show()` helper now owns trailing-EOL invariant rather than delegating to yargs (closes the subcommand-help-trim regression), agent color schema admits 8 named CSS colors plus a `Bun.color()` runtime fallback (closes Claude-Code config parity gap), Codex backend rate-limit pre-flight gates memory startup with a `25%`-remaining default + `unwrap_or(true)` fail-open shape, empty-shell-mode Esc carve-out fires *before* the existing empty-composer Esc handler because the leading `!` is stored outside the textarea (the load-bearing predicate is `is_bash_mode && textarea.is_empty()`), OTel `gen_ai.output.messages`/`system_instructions`/`finish_reasons` populated for Responses API via `elif response_obj.get("output")` arm + tri-source coalescing of `system_instructions`/`instructions`/`system` kwargs with `is not None` (not truthiness) precedence, team-members table O(n²)-per-render `Array.find` collapsed to `useMemo` `Map<userId, membership>` plus client-side pagination + search/filter, goose stream-json `total_tokens` source flipped from last-turn-context-size to `accumulated_total_tokens.or(total_tokens)` cumulative-with-fallback (load-bearing because eval harnesses were under-reporting by an order of magnitude), DECKPAM SS3-prefixed Numpad-Enter `\eOM` admitted to `KEY_INFO_MAP` (under-scoped — full DECKPAM digit map still missing), and ACP session promotes `tryCompressChat` + `getChat` re-fetch to a Session-level invariant before every model send (with order-pinning test via `invocationCallOrder`).

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #24806 single-file +3/−6 EOL invariant promotion from caller-responsibility to `show()`-owned with the load-bearing `endsWith("\n")` (not `endsWith(EOL)`) check correctly matching yargs's platform-independent `\n` line terminator, control flow collapses early-return-branching into single linear path, byte-symmetric with the bug class — only the missing-trailing-EOL invariant added; openai/codex #19986 surgical `if self.is_bash_mode && self.textarea.is_empty() && key_event.code == KeyCode::Esc` guard inserted *before* the existing empty-composer Esc handler at `chat_composer.rs:2862-2865`, with the load-bearing root-cause that the leading `!` is stored *outside* the editable textarea so `textarea.is_empty()` is true while the higher-level `is_empty()` is false, paired direct unit test asserting both `matches!(result, InputResult::None)` and `needs_redraw` plus `current_text() == ""` after Esc pinning that exiting bash mode also drops the bash-mode-stored `!`, plus snapshot test capturing post-Esc rendered terminal state — predicate ordering correct: overlay-Esc → empty-shell-mode-Esc → general-empty-composer-Esc), 6 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #24798 three-layer admission of 8 named CSS colors via Schema.Literals union arm + parallel `defaults`/`agentTones` records in two sites + resolver liberalization to guarded `if (color in theme)` lookup with `Bun.color()` fallback, generated SDK types regenerated — but schema arm and `Bun.color` fallback disagree on accepted set (schema rejects `"rebeccapurple"` that resolver would accept), three pairs of named colors collide to 5 distinct visual outputs because no purpose-built tokens exist yet, dropped `as RGBA` cast at resolver, no test, two parallel `agentTones` records risk drift; openai/codex #19990 `min_rate_limit_remaining_percent` config field clamped to `0..=100` with default 25 + new `guard.rs` doing pre-flight `BackendClient::get_rate_limits_many` with `unwrap_or(true)` fail-open shape preserving best-effort startup, `limit_id == "codex"` snapshot lookup with `snapshots.first()` fallback — but the silent `snapshots.first()` fallback non-deterministic on multi-window-without-codex-tag responses, `25%`-remaining default aggressive for steady-state-high-consumption CI/eval runs (document `0 = always run`), `min_rate_limit_remaining_percent` semantic is "minimum *remaining*" but body phrases as "above usage ceiling" — equivalent only at exact 100% sum, two-window minimum-of-remaining test not visible in slice; BerriAI/litellm #26670 tri-source coalescing of `system_instructions`/`instructions`/`system` kwargs at `opentelemetry.py:1681-1715` with `is not None` (not truthiness) precedence chain + `isinstance(str)` short-circuit at `:1696` preventing string-coerced-through-message-transformer garble, new `elif response_obj.get("output"):` arm at `:1774-1809` dispatching through `_transform_responses_api_output_to_otel` that converts `{type:"message", content:[{type:"output_text"}]}` into the same `{role, parts}` shape `_transform_choices_to_otel_semantic_conventions` produces, `function_call` items adapter-shaped to `ChatCompletionMessageToolCall` for `_tool_calls_kv_pair` reuse, `[response_obj.get("status")]` finish-reason fallback, 21 new unit tests across two test classes — but precedence chain ordering not documented, `hasattr(item, "get")` (chosen over `isinstance(dict)` for Pydantic-model accommodation) needs explanatory comment, no test for provider returning both `output` and `choices` (silent `choices`-wins), `function_call` adapter hard-codes `name=""`/`arguments=""` defaults that may emit spurious empty-string OTel attributes; BerriAI/litellm #26667 three-layers-in-one-PR (perf fix + pagination + search/filter) where the `useMemo` `Map<userId, membership>` collapses 6-column × 2k-member ~12000 array scans to ~12 hash lookups per render, antd Pagination top-right with `controlledPage ?? internalPage` / `onPageChange ?? setInternalPage` controlled/uncontrolled hybrid, `Math.min(page, totalPages)` clamp at `:62`, React `key`-prop reset pattern on filter change — but three independent changes should split, controlled/uncontrolled invariant has invisible failure mode (pass `currentPage` without `onPageChange` silently breaks pagination), `pageSize` is local-only state with no controlled path, clamp doesn't *update* `page` to `safePage` so post-filter user can be stranded on non-existent page until React-key reset fires, no `useMemo` on `pagedMembers` slice; block/goose #8870 single-file +28/−5 source flip `session.total_tokens` → `session.accumulated_total_tokens.or(session.total_tokens)` keeping wire-format field name/position/type identical so existing parsers see *correct larger numbers* where they previously saw under-counts, new `input_tokens`/`output_tokens` opt-in via `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`, both error-path branches default to `None` (no silent `Some(0)` confusion), root cause correctly identified — but no unit test added despite 28-line behavioral change (CI-untested-claim in PR body), `JsonOutput` and stream-json paths now duplicate the `(s.accumulated_X.or(s.X), ...)` triple risking drift on next field addition, pretty-printed vs compact JSON output behavior not test-pinned, `Some(0)` vs `None` semantics for empty session needs verification; google-gemini/gemini-cli #26092 surgical one-line `'OM': { name: 'enter' }` admission at `KeypressContext.tsx:44` for DECKPAM SS3-prefixed Numpad-Enter sequence `\eOM` that was dropped because no text-character fallback exists, sits next to existing CSI-prefixed (`\e[`) entries — but PR is under-scoped to Enter only when full DECKPAM map (`\eOq`/`\eOk`/`\eOm` etc. for digits in keypad-app-mode) has the same dispatch gap, PR body claim "other numeric keypad characters were correctly mapped" is true only when DECKPAM is *off*, no test cell despite "added/updated tests" checkbox checked, decoder-dispatch-key-format assumption (post-ESC `'OM'` not bare `'M'`) needs verification), and 1 merge-after-nits-with-test-praise (QwenLM/qwen-code #3698 promotes `tryCompressChat` + `getChat` re-fetch to Session-level invariant wrapping every direct `GeminiChat.sendMessageStream` callsite in `acp-integration/session/Session.ts`, four-cell test matrix at `:300-444` covering positive-baseline (with the load-bearing **`invocationCallOrder` ordering assertion** at `:319-322` pinning compression-runs-before-send not just both-were-called) / replace-chat-cell proving re-fetch contract / tool-response-follow-up cell asserting `tryCompressChat` called twice with `nthCalledWith(2, ...)` / slash-command-negative cell pinning the discriminator that `/compress` handled by `nonInteractiveCliCommands.handleSlashCommand` skips both compression and send, `force=false` second arg correct default, replace-chat cell mocks `getChat.mockReturnValue(compressedChat)` from inside `tryCompressChat.mockImplementation` — but no `CompressionStatus.FAILED` cell pinning the operational failure mode (compression failed → send proceeds with un-compressed history, not crash), tool-follow-up re-fetch path not directly proven, `'test-session-id########1'` thread-id format hard-coded across three cells will break together if scheme changes, ACP and CLI compression threshold convergence not asserted). Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos.

### W17 drip-145 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 2 QwenLM/qwen-code, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli). Theme is "boundary-shape correctness" — httpapi PR adds explicit OpenAPI request/response schema annotations so the published spec stops collapsing union variants to `any`; opentui jumps to 0.2.0 with a renderer protocol change that the PR adapts in lockstep; codex `NO_PROXY` default drops `169.254/16` so AWS/Azure IMDS metadata calls actually bypass user proxies (load-bearing — without it the metadata server is proxied and times out); codex `TargetCheckedTcpConnector` validates resolved IP against a denylist *before* dialing so SSRF protections fire even when DNS resolves late; litellm Anthropic token counter walks `image`/`document` content blocks via the messages-count endpoint instead of approximating with character heuristics (closes the 30-50% under-count for vision payloads); qwen `runtime.json` sidecar separates volatile runtime state from versioned config so `git diff` on `settings.json` stops thrashing; qwen `parseLineTolerant` in `countSessionMessages` swallows JSONL parse errors per-line so one corrupted entry stops aborting the whole count; gemini-cli slash-command conflict dedup correctly *resets* the conflict map on each rebuild instead of accumulating stale entries across reloads.

### W17 drip-146 (2026-04-28)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli). Theme is "boundary correctness vs administrative cleanup" — opencode #24816 fixes a one-character `startsWith("http:")` bug that silently dropped `https://` image URIs through the ACP path; opencode #24635 mixes a clean Dutch README contribution with a misleading "Crof AI provider" claim that ships only a test fixture and an icon (no runtime wiring); codex #20015 caps the `PermissionProfile` test-migration stack by routing through the canonical `turn_permission_fields` helper instead of hand-constructing tuples; codex #20001 hardens the Linux proxy bridge with `process_vm_readv/writev` syscall denies plus `prctl(PR_SET_DUMPABLE, 0)`, closing two known sandbox-escape primitives; litellm #26705 widens `gpt-image-1` substring matching to `gpt-image` family across provider routing, cost calc, and Azure logging in lockstep; litellm #26624 redirects doc contributors to the separate `litellm-docs` repo via PR template comment + new `## Documentation` CONTRIBUTING section; gemini-cli #26118 wraps zod number/boolean schemas with `z.preprocess` so env-var-resolved string values cast to typed values without silent `NaN`; gemini-cli #26124 is an automated `[CONFLICTS]` cherry-pick of the `transient → sticky_retry` policy-catalog change that nobody has manually resolved yet.

### W17 drip-147 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (1 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code, 1 block/goose). Theme is "right primitive at the right boundary" — opencode #24819 fixes a latent cache-fast-path bug where `npa(pkg).name` returns `undefined` for non-registry specs (https tarball, git+https, github shorthand, file:) so the fallback used the spec string and resolved to a non-existent `node_modules/<spec>/`; codex #20016 caps a 7-PR Sapling test-migration stack by routing the last two callsites through `turn_permission_fields()` so the `SandboxPolicy` reference count drops from 22 to 20 files; codex #19995 normalizes scoped IPv6 literals (`fd00::1%eth0`, `[fd00::1%eth0]`, `[fd00::1%25eth0]`) before keying allow/deny/local-address policy lookups, closing a textual-equivalence policy-bypass class; litellm #26661 lands gateway-managed Responses API conversation IDs reusing `LiteLLM_SpendLogs.session_id` (no new tables, no new endpoints) with a `DualCache` short-circuit for the back-to-back agentic-loop race window; litellm #26641 partitions native vs MCP-registered tools at the semantic-filter pre-hook so native tools survive a filter that was widened beyond its `_tool_map` source-of-truth; gemini-cli #26125 skips `ConsolePatcher.cleanup()` registration in ACP mode so SessionEnd-hook stdout writes can't pollute NDJSON during teardown; qwen #3710 ships banner customization (logo/title/hide) with operational signals locked (version/auth/model/cwd cannot be hidden) and `O_NOFOLLOW`+sanitizer+size-cap+soft-fail security primitives; goose #8877 is the Step-3 inline-MCP-app renderer with authenticated local proxy + per-process secret + bounded HTML storage + CSP shaping, but with two unresolved load-bearing security questions (CSP policy text + nested `_goose/tool/call` policy gating).

### W17 drip-148 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 2 block/goose). Theme is "right primitive at the right boundary, applied to a different surface each time" — opencode #24827 caps the multi-PR HttpApi → SDK parity effort with per-route schema normalizers (SSE response wiring, `/tui/publish` named-component extraction, `flattenOptions` recursion through nested anyOf, finite-numeric-collapse predicate tightening) reducing SDK signature mismatches from 15 → 0; opencode #24825 fixes a latent UTF-8-vs-UTF-16 bug where Zed's SQLite-persisted byte offsets were sliced as JS string indexes, with a `utf8ByteOffsetToStringIndex` helper that walks codepoint-by-codepoint with proper BMP/SMP boundary handling and 4 paired tests including a reversed-offsets cell pinning min/max-before-conversion ordering; codex #20023 closes a real sandbox-bypass hole by adding `file_system_sandbox_policy().can_read_path_with_cwd(...)` gate before the auth check in `build_uploaded_local_argument_value` so policy denial is reported regardless of session state, with deny-message exact-string equality preventing silent regression; codex #20022 adds the missing `--experimental_remote_downloader=` clear to the no-`BUILDBUDDY_API_KEY` Bazel fallback (Bazel emits "remote downloader can only be used with gRPC caching" error otherwise) plus a fake-bazel-shim pytest harness that captures argv and asserts the full triple of remote-* clears; gemini-cli #26129 is a draft prompt-template-only refactor that surfaces real contradictions (Inquiries vs. Directives buried mid-list, near-duplicate Concurrency Safety/High-Impact Delegation guidance) and resolves them via dedup + reorder, but ships without an A/B eval; gemini-cli #26128 adds the missing `ENOTDIR` entry to the table-driven errno-to-message map with both with-path and without-path arms tested; goose #8878 fixes two bugs in one diff — owner-collision in cached recipe paths (`owner-one/shared` and `owner-two/shared` previously shared `<temp>/shared`) and path-traversal in `recipe_name` joined into temp dir — via a shared `temp_child_name` whitelist sanitizer; goose #8773 ships a 3.7k-line UI refactor bundling artifact-policy per-tool-call scoping (real correctness fix where same-message multi-write-tool collapsed into one display) with two unrelated UX-layer additions (toolCallPresentation helpers + ToolCard primitive expansion) and 424 lines of test deletion.

### W17 drip-149 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "narrow the contract at the seam where two subsystems meet" — opencode #24826 plumbs provider quota numbers all the way from the LLM-API response into the prompt-metrics line so users see remaining quota inline (per-provider parsers gated behind a capability flag, no fallthrough when fields are absent); opencode #24711 replaces an event-driven Zed context refresh that could stall when the editor dropped focus events with a fixed-cadence 500ms poll guarded by a workspace-changed predicate, eliminating the "stale Zed context" failure mode without adding a busy loop; codex #20047 lets the app-server runtime override the `remote_control` feature flag against the static allowlist, which is the small piece that unblocks staged rollout without rebuilding the binary (allowlist intersection enforced server-side, override is additive-only); codex #19874 fixes a thread-list ordering bug where filtered listings preferred filesystem mtime over the persisted state-git metadata, causing reordered threads after rebases — switch to state-git as authoritative with mtime as tiebreaker only; litellm #26674 adds the Astraflow (ModelVerse) provider with the standard provider-config + cost-map + integration-test triple, model list pulled from upstream docs at PR time (will need refresh cadence); litellm #26649 fixes a three-layer permissions bug where internal users couldn't see their own projects in the Create Key dialog — root cause was a backend filter that only matched team membership, fix adds the user-direct-membership branch and pins it with an integration test that exercises the dialog's actual API path; goose #8879 refactors the agent-provider state machine from string-discriminated unions to explicit type states (`Uninitialized`/`Configured`/`Ready`), making invalid transitions a compile error rather than a runtime panic — touches every call site but the diff is mechanical; qwen-code #3713 sets the OS process title to encode session identity (PID-stable, includes session short-id) so users can disambiguate multiple concurrent qwen-code instances in `ps`/Activity Monitor without enabling debug logging.

### W17 drip-150 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "the right test pins the right contract" — opencode #24836 caps the multi-PR Effect HttpApi rollout with SDK-level integration tests that go through `createOpencodeClient` with an in-process fetch shim (catches both routing and SDK-signature drift in one assertion, no port-flake) and exercises the SSE event stream by reading the first event then calling `stream.return(undefined)` to tear down before the heartbeat fires; opencode #24835 (the companion fix) wires runtime handlers for the previously-OpenAPI-only `global` and `control` API groups (new `eventResponse()` SSE helper at `global.ts:117-160` with `done`-guarded controller writes and explicit cleanup), simplifies layer composition by moving from per-group `Layer.unwrap(...).pipe(Layer.provide(...))` boilerplate to a single hoisted dep-injection chain in `server.ts:147-167`, and splits routing so global/control routes live outside the per-instance auth middleware while instance routes still pass through it (chicken-and-egg avoidance — `auth.set` must work *before* an instance has credentials); codex #20051 unbreaks trunk's `argument-comment-lint` by adding the missing `/*duration_ms*/ None` argument-comment to a `plan_mode_nudge_hides_while_task_or_modal_is_active` callsite that #20046 left stale (split out cleanly because #20046 is `protocol.rs`-only and the callsite lives in `tui/`); codex #20046 pins the `InterAgentCommunication::to_response_input_item()` contract via a full structural-equality assertion that exercises a non-trivial author/recipient/other_recipients triple and reconstructs the expected `OutputText` body via the same `serde_json::to_string` call the production code uses (so the test survives field additions to `InterAgentCommunication` without false positives); litellm #26710 closes a silent-data-drop bug on the Bedrock+Anthropic tool-result path where OpenAI `{type:"file"}` content and `image_url` data URIs with non-image mimes (e.g., `application/pdf`) were both producing empty `toolResult.content: []` — fix routes both shapes through the existing `anthropic_process_openai_file_message` helper (one source of truth for document-block construction) and adds the missing `elif "document" in _block` arm in the Bedrock wrapper, with 6 new tests covering the primary `type: "file"` case + the regression `image_url`-with-PDF-mime case + the locked-in PNG-stays-image case for both providers; gemini-cli #26136 fixes a one-line bug in `McpClientManager.stopExtension` that was passing the raw MCP server name instead of the manager's computed client key to `disconnectClient`, leaving extension-backed MCP clients connected after extension unload — round-trip test pins the user-observable contract (`getMcpServerCount` returns to 0 after `stopExtension`) and embeds the issue number `(#24050)` in the test name for searchable regression marking; goose #8797 replaces a hardcoded `isDeprecatedGoogleDriveExtension` predicate with a JSON-driven `deprecated-bundled-extensions.json` allowlist consumed by a new `pruneDeprecatedBundledExtensions` step that runs *before* `syncBundledExtensions` (ordering matters — sync would short-circuit on the still-present deprecated bundle), plus a `nameToKey`-normalized merge in `BottomMenuExtensionSelection` that surfaces session-only orphan extensions the user can now toggle (test file shrinks 121→68 lines because the per-predicate unit tests collapse into integration tests of the new flow); qwen-code #3691 fixes a silent-first-word-drop bug in streaming reasoning where chunks shaped `**Subject**\n\nFirstWord ...` were routed to the loading-indicator handler (`setThought`) which discarded the description — fix sends every chunk carrying streamed text through the throttled buffer, the existing `subject || mergedThought.subject` flush merger preserves the subject across chunks, and the regression test mirrors the exact two-chunk SSE pattern from the bug report including the leading-space tokenization that real BPE tokenizers produce.

### W17 drip-151 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). Theme is "the value's name and the value's meaning have drifted apart at a load-bearing seam" — opencode #24842 fixes a $2k+/day prompt-cache invalidation where `pending → completed` tool-part state transitions changed the serialized bytes of the most-recent assistant message between loop iterations, busting the byte-identity match Anthropic's prompt cache requires (fix: cache the message array across iterations and append-only on tool-call continuation, full reload only on subtask/overflow/compaction); opencode #24839 flips the default agent permission from `"*": "allow"` to `"*": "ask"` with read-only tools (`glob`/`grep`/`codesearch`/`lsp`/`todowrite`/`skill`) explicitly allow-listed — closes a zero-friction RCE primitive where prompt injection through `webfetch` content or a hostile MCP server could trigger arbitrary `bash`/`write`/`apply_patch` execution; codex #20068 converts the silent `state_db = .ok()` swallow into fail-loud at startup, gates `remote_control_enabled` on `state_db.is_some()`, and narrows the `enroll.rs` helpers from `Option<Enrollment>` to `Result<Option<Enrollment>>` so callers can distinguish "no enrollment yet" from "DB unavailable, can't tell"; codex #20069 short-circuits ARC + guardian for `AppToolApproval::Approve` MCP tools when `approvals_reviewer == AutoReview` (one explicit `if` at `mcp_tool_call.rs:889-895`, paired test rewrite from `expect(1)` ARC + guardian calls to `expect(0)` for both, terminal assertion changes from `Some(Accept)` to `None` to reflect the right semantics — "no question to ask" vs "question asked and answered"); litellm #26713 fixes two real OTel crashes — multimodal `gen_ai.prompt = list[dict]` rejection by the SDK and `AttributeError` on `response_obj.get(...)` when `response_obj` is a `list` — via `safe_dumps` for non-string content and `isinstance(response_obj, dict)` guards (massive +2461/-2454 diff is line-ending churn obscuring a tiny semantic change, requested split); litellm #26695 adds inline `unpack_defs` resolution of legacy JSON Schema `definitions` $ref pointers (DevRev-class MCP servers) in both `_map_tool_helper` (Anthropic) and `_transform_tools` (Fireworks), fixing `PointerToNowhere` errors caused by `_allowed_properties` stripping `definitions` while leaving dangling refs — but the PR has 60+ unrelated files and needs rebasing before it's mergeable; gemini-cli #26139 fixes two stale-closure bugs in `FooterConfigDialog` (Reset-to-Default needing two presses, focus pointer staying on stale list index after reset) by migrating `showLabels` into reducer state, replacing `SET_STATE` partial-payload action with `RESET` full-payload action, and synthesizing the default-state input locally instead of round-tripping through the settings store; goose #8870 fixes a 10× under-count in `goose run --output-format stream-json` where the `complete.total_tokens` field reported `Session::total_tokens` (last-turn context size) instead of `Session::accumulated_total_tokens` (running sum), with `Option::or` fallback for legacy sessions and additive `input_tokens`/`output_tokens` fields gated on `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`.

### W17 drip-152 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "the surface and the source-of-truth had drifted apart" — opencode #24809 closes a 107→0 OpenAPI parameter mismatch where the Effect HttpApi spec didn't declare the shared `directory`/`workspace` query params on instance routes (legacy Hono did) and over-declared `cursor` on the PTY connect endpoint, fixed via a denylist-shaped post-processor at OpenAPI emit time plus a new `(method, path, in, name, required)`-tuple parity test; opencode #24799 hard-forks server startup by `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_HTTPAPI` flag eliminating the legacy "Hono shadows HttpApi" bridge layer entirely (net -181 LOC across 23 files), introduces a `FetchApp` minimum interface satisfied by both Hono and Effect's `toWebHandler` output, and converts all `httpapi-*.test.ts` harnesses to drive `Server.Default().app` directly — but ships without a startup log indicating which fork is active and without `Effect.catchAllDefect` recovery at the toWebHandler boundary; codex #19970 relocates `start_memories_startup_task` from three thread-lifecycle hooks (create/load/fork) to the `thread/sendInput` handler gated by `turn_has_input`, drops `auth_manager` from `ListenerTaskContext` as compile-time ratchet against re-introduction, adds a 6h success cooldown on phase-2 global consolidation via `SkippedCooldown`, and tightens defaults from 16→2 rollouts per startup over 30→10 day window; codex #19963 fixes a stale-snapshot feature-gate bug in `configured_multi_agent_v2_usage_hint_texts` by moving the function from `Codex` to `Session` and reading from live `self.features` instead of `original_config_do_not_use`, with two surgical tests using `Arc::get_mut` to force effective-vs-config-state divergence and assert correct behavior in both directions — model-class example of "the bug was the wrong type owned the function"; litellm #26631 moves the "Store Prompts in Spend Logs" + retention-period controls from a gear-icon modal on the Logs page (visible to non-admins who hit 403 on save) into a new Logging Settings tab under Admin Settings (already gated to admins), exports `proxyConfigKeys` to share between read-side and write-side hooks for cache invalidation, and replaces a `mutateAsync` + try/catch double-toast bug with `mutate` + `onSuccess`/`onError`/`onSettled` callback shape; gemini-cli #26132 fixes #24810 where stable users were being auto-updated to nightly builds via the npm `latest` dist-tag occasionally pointing at a semver-greater nightly — introduces a pure `getChannelFromVersion` classifier and `RELEASE_CHANNEL_STABILITY` numeric ordering (`{nightly:0, preview:1, stable:2}`), gates updates on `STABILITY[target] >= STABILITY[current]` at *both* the update-check site (primary) and the install-time site (defense-in-depth), with a 5-cell test matrix covering refused-downgrades + allowed-graduate-ups but local mock duplicates production classifier logic risking silent drift; gemini-cli #26079 adds an actionable error message for Cloud Shell users hitting 403 on the default `cloudshell-gca` project via a defensively-typed `isPermissionDeniedError` walker (full `'prop' in error` + typeof guards) and a *conjunction* gate (`isPermissionDeniedError(e) && req.cloudaicompanionProject === 'cloudshell-gca'`) that surgically targets the recoverable case, but ships with only a positive test and no negative tests pinning the conjunction's tightness; goose #8874 walks back premature CUDA documentation (download buttons + install instructions) added before the release pipeline produces the artifacts via pure deletion of 32 lines across `installation.md` and `WindowsDesktopInstallButtons.js` — but pure deletion is weaker than HTML-comment-with-restore-marker for content slated to come back, and there's no script-side cleanup of the `GOOSE_WINDOWS_VARIANT=cuda` paths advanced users may still try; qwen-code #3690 is a one-character workflow fix changing `gh pr merge --merge --auto` to `--squash --auto` because the repo only allows squash merges (verified via `gh repo view --json mergeCommitAllowed,squashMergeAllowed,rebaseMergeAllowed`), turning every release run from red to green and unblocking auto-merge of release PRs.

Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).

### W18 drip-153 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). Theme is "default off, opt in explicitly" — opencode #24852 stops force-feeding XML-shaped `<available_skills><skill>...</skill></available_skills>` system-prompt to non-Claude models that mirror it back as XML in their replies, switching to a JSON-shape default for non-Anthropic providers via a new `format: "xml"|"json"|"markdown"` discriminator that replaces a load-bearing two-bit `verbose: bool` (the markdown branch is now uniquely owned by the tool-description surface, ratcheting the three formats to three legible call sites); opencode #24829 fixes a credential-prompt-loop where Bun's HTTP serializer was filling hono/basicAuth's missing 401 Content-Type with `application/octet-stream`, which Chromium's response sniffer treated as a download-attachment for a brief window before settling on the basic-auth dialog (the race produced 2-3 dialog flashes and Playwright's `Error: Download is starting`), patched by wrapping `basicAuth({...})(c, next)` in try/catch and rebuilding the 401 with `text/plain; charset=utf-8` while preserving body+`WWW-Authenticate`, plus a one-attribute manifest fix `crossorigin="use-credentials"` so the same-origin manifest fetch stops spawning an *independent* auth dialog; codex #20085 closes a real auto-approve-allowlist escape where `git -C <dir>` (functionally `cd <dir> && git ...`) was treated as safe-by-default while `git -c key=val` already required prompt — patch extends both the exact-match and prefix arms of `git_global_option_requires_prompt` symmetrically and adds bash-wrapper passthrough coverage (`["bash","-lc","git -C ... status"]`); codex #20080 captures the per-turn `child_turn_id` from `Op::ReviewSubmit`'s response and gates every event in `wait_for_guardian_review` and `interrupt_and_drain_turn` on a structural `event_matches_turn` predicate that checks both `event.id` and the per-message `turn_id` field for terminal events, eliminating an analytics-corruption class where reused review trunks attributed prior queued `TurnComplete` events to new reviews (the smoking gun was non-null TTFT with sub-10ms completion latency on `trunk_reused`); litellm #26716 aligns v1 `/team/list` with v2's already-canonical `LiteLLM_UserTable.teams` source-of-truth, replacing "fetch all teams + post-filter in Python by `members_with_roles`" with `where={"team_id": {"in": user_team_ids}}` SQL scoping and fixing the auth-gate so a no-arg call from a non-admin defaults `user_id = user_api_key_dict.user_id` (own-query path) instead of 401-ing; litellm #26714 deny-by-default-gates two latent foot-guns behind explicit env vars: `SkillsInjectionHook` (which silently rewrites the system prompt for every request when skills dirs are present) now requires `LITELLM_ENABLE_SKILLS=true`, and `CustomCodeGuardrail._do_compile()` (which `exec()`s user-supplied Python through a sandbox whose own test file documents at least one bytecode-rewrite escape via `code.replace(co_names=...)`) now requires `LITELLM_ENABLE_CUSTOM_CODE_GUARDRAILS=true` at the sole compile chokepoint plus the admin test-endpoint mirror; gemini-cli #26141 adds five missing OAuth fields (`issuer`, `audiences`, `redirect_uri`, `token_param_name`, `registration_url`) in three places (`mcpServerSchema`, `oauth2AuthSchema`, both `convertFrontmatterAuthToConfig` and `markdownToAgentDefinition` converters with snake↔camel rename) bringing the inline subagent parser in line with the canonical `MCPOAuthConfig` it had been drifting from; goose #8877 wires an MCP-app inline-render pipeline through a new `mcp_app_proxy.rs` (229 LoC bounded guest-HTML store with TTL=300s and capacity=64), a new `_goose/tool/call` RPC (host↔guest tool routing) consistently propagated through `acp-meta.json`/`acp-schema.json`/SDK, and an iframe sandbox — but ships with `unwrap_or_else(|_| "goose-acp-local".into())` as the CLI default secret (static, known cross-machine) plus `sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-forms'` which is the documented-unsafe combo when content is third-party MCP-app payloads.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (opencode #24829 two-cause auth-flash fix with regression-doc test suite; codex #20085 symmetric `-C`/`-c` allowlist escape closure; codex #20080 structural-id-match analytics correctness fix), 4 merge-after-nits (opencode #24852 markdown-branch unreachable from system-prompt path + JSON-branch leaks absolute filesystem URIs; litellm #26716 silent behavior change for stale-members_with_roles callers + missing negative test for excluded-stale-row; litellm #26714 strong gate but consider startup-log line documenting which gates are off; gemini-cli #26141 dual-schema parity test missing + audiences single-string normalization), 1 request-changes (goose #8877 static `goose-acp-local` CLI default secret + iframe `allow-scripts allow-same-origin` combo + plain `!=` secret compare; architecture is right, defaults aren't safe yet).
