### W18 drip-154 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (1 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 3 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). Theme is "the platform's not the heuristic — stop guessing what kernel you're on" — codex #20089 expands the `inherit=Core` Windows env-var allowlist from 3 entries (`PATHEXT`/`USERNAME`/`USERPROFILE`) to 22 (adding `SYSTEMROOT`, `COMSPEC`, `LOCALAPPDATA`, `APPDATA`, `PWSH`, etc. — without `SYSTEMROOT` cmd.exe itself can't resolve `kernel32.dll` and bare-Core inheritance produced unspawnable child shells), splits the `Core` arm by `#[cfg(target_os)]` so the linear-scan-with-case-fold cost only runs on Windows where it's needed, and consolidates a near-duplicate list out of `rmcp-client/src/utils.rs` via a `pub` re-export of `WINDOWS_CORE_ENV_VARS` (the two lists had already drifted — `rmcp-client`'s was missing `SHELL` and `TMPDIR`); codex #20081 generalizes `TurnMetadataState`'s single-purpose `turn_started_at_unix_ms: Arc<RwLock<Option<i64>>>` field into an `additional_metadata: Arc<RwLock<BTreeMap<String, Value>>>` plus a `RESERVED_DYNAMIC_METADATA_KEYS` registry array, then uses the new pattern to record `plugin_ids_used` per turn (with `BTreeSet` dedup so consecutive same-plugin tool calls collapse to one entry, and explicit `remove(KEY)` on empty input so absent-key vs empty-array distinction is preserved); litellm #26721 swaps deprecated bedrock `claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219-v1:0` for `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-v1:0` across 16 test files (including the load-bearing `_update_litellm_params_for_health_check` regression test for issue #15807) without touching `constants.py` or `model_prices_and_context_window.json` — correctly preserves customer-facing model-string compatibility while keeping the test suite alive against AWS deprecation; litellm #26712 fixes three v2-migration-resolver pain points in one PR: rewrites the `_warn_if_db_ahead_of_head` copy to call out `_baseline_diff` suffix as the benign developer-branch-leftover case (vs the actually-dangerous newer-deployment case), passes `DIRECT_URL` as `DATABASE_URL` to `prisma migrate deploy` when set (sidestepping advisory-lock orphaning on PgBouncer/Neon-pooler transaction-mode connections), and adds a P1002-advisory-lock catch with 5-15s randomized back-off and a terminal-error message that hands the operator three actionable remediations including the `pg_terminate_backend(pid)` SQL to clear orphan locks; litellm #26708 closes the #20499 silent-no-email-on-default-config UX paper cut by routing `send_invite_email is None` through a new tri-state `_should_send_user_invitation_email` evaluator that auto-detects via either v2 enterprise email loggers or v0 `general_settings.alerting=["email"]`, with an `enterprise_email_sent` flag preventing double-send when both paths are configured and 7 new tests pinning every cell of the truth table including the critical "preserved old behavior when no email infra configured" case; gemini-cli #26134 fixes #26133 — Esc-to-cancel-mid-stream now also drains the pending steering-hint buffer via a one-line `consumeUserHint?.()` call inside `cancelOngoingRequest` of *both* `useGeminiStream` and `useAgentStream` (symmetric across the Gemini and ACP-protocol code paths so non-Gemini-default users get the fix too), tested through both the direct-call surface and the full keypress-event surface using a never-resolving stream to force the streaming-active branch; sst/opencode #24858 adds an identical 11-line `## Long-running processes` block to all 8 built-in system prompts (`anthropic.txt`, `beast.txt`, `codex.txt`, `default.txt`, `gemini.txt`, `gpt.txt`, `kimi.txt`, `trinity.txt`) steering the model away from foreground `npm run dev`-style invocations through the bash tool (which gets `SIGTERM`'d at turn boundary, leaving "I started the server" in the transcript and nothing actually running), with a carve-out for genuinely-detached `docker compose up -d`/`brew services start`/`systemctl start` and a new `test/session/system-prompt.test.ts` enforcing cross-prompt parity so the next single-file edit can't drift one variant out of sync; goose #8869 deletes 31 lines of net-negative WSL-OS-detection heuristics (`PWD =~ ^/mnt/[a-zA-Z]/`, `command -v powershell.exe`, layered guards) that were causing WSL2 users sitting in `/mnt/c/...` to download `goose-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip` despite running on a Linux kernel — the fix is "WSL is Linux regardless of cwd" (4 added lines, comment included), preserving only the genuinely-correct Native Windows + Git Bash arms.

### W18 drip-155 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). Theme is "the abstraction lies about its lifetime" — opencode #24861 traces a 3.7 GB external-memory leak in the bash permission scanner to dropped `web-tree-sitter` `Tree` handles whose underlying WASM/Emscripten allocations are never freed because the JS shim has no FinalizationRegistry hook, fixed by wrapping the parse in `Effect.acquireRelease`/`Effect.scoped` so `tree.delete()` runs on success/failure/interruption with rootNode reads strictly inside scope; opencode #24857 fixes the DeepSeek/NVIDIA-NIM `sequence item N: expected str instance, list found` 500 by collapsing all-text user-message content arrays back into a single string scalar for text-only models in a new `mergeTextParts()` step that runs after `unsupportedParts()`, capability-gated so multimodal models are untouched and adding DeepSeek V4 Flash/Pro to the test fixture; codex #20092 routes auth-refresh failure to `Ok(None)` so the caller can redirect to login but conflates transient (503/network) and permanent (revoked/401) failures into the same `None` lossy-semantic, with an unrelated README joke commit (`255d497e`) that should be split out before merge; codex #20086 isolates plugin-list workspace-settings tests by setting both `HOME` and `USERPROFILE` via a new mcp-process helper that combines managed-config isolation with env overrides, addressing the canonical "passes on CI, fails on my laptop" hermeticity bug surfaced by host-marketplace leakage; litellm #26722 centralizes the live Bedrock Anthropic model ID behind `$BEDROCK_LIVE_ANTHROPIC_MODEL` set via job-level `environment:` blocks in `.circleci/config.yml` for the three relevant CI jobs, with a Sonnet 4.5 in-code fallback for local dev — scope-correct (only live tests; mocked transformation tests keep hardcoded IDs because the model string is opaque there) but invites future EOL drift between the YAML and the in-code default; litellm #26706 fixes the cached-OAuth-stale-token failure mode for the github_copilot provider where post-sleep/wake refresh retries reused a dead disk token forever, by detecting 401 on `_refresh_api_key()`, deleting the cached file, and running device-flow re-acquisition via a new `_invalidate_and_reacquire_token()` helper gated by a `token_invalidated` once-per-call flag, with a 30-test matrix (11 new) covering the full state space and a `get_api_key()` second-order catch for `GetAccessTokenError`; gemini-cli #26143 phase-1 ACP refactor splits the monolithic `acpClient.ts` (and its 2294-line test file) into 5 focused modules (`acpStdioTransport.ts`, `acpRpcDispatcher.ts`, `acpSessionManager.ts`, `acpSession.ts`, `acpUtils.ts`) plus 3 new test files totaling 1187 lines (vs old 2294) — half the test mass raises a coverage-parity question, plus the redundant `acp` filename prefix on every file in the `acp/` dir, plus only macOS validation despite the change touching the transport that Zed/JetBrains integrations use; goose #8887 collapses goose2's parallel desktop+core credential systems into ACP-only via 4 new `_goose/providers/config/{read,status,save,delete}` methods, deleting the entire Tauri-side `services/goose_config.rs` (-452) + `commands/credentials.rs` (-50) + `services/provider_defs.rs` (-150), batching secret writes/deletes in `config/base.rs` to dedupe keychain prompts, and hardening provider-inventory refresh with capture-identity-at-plan-time + reject-stale-credential-refresh — but ships without documented keychain credential migration for existing installs, without a stated atomicity guarantee on partial batched-secret-write failure, and with localization updates for only `en` + `es`.

Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).

### W18 drip-156 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). Theme is "exception/lifetime semantics that pass type-checks but lose data" — opencode #24866 traces a process-hang bug (1000 iterations of `McpOAuthCallback.ensureRunning(...)` ⇒ `isRunning() === true` and parent process can't exit) to the OAuth callback HTTP server never being shut down on completion/failure/cancellation; fix hoists a single named `stopOAuthCallback = Effect.tryPromise(() => McpOAuthCallback.stop()).pipe(Effect.ignore)` and attaches it at four lifecycle points via `Effect.ensuring(...)` (which runs on success, failure, *and* interruption — the right primitive for a one-shot OAuth dance) plus the service finalizer and `removeAuth` site; opencode #24863 fixes a sibling-prefix instruction-leak (`/tmp/project-sibling/file.ts` was inheriting `AGENTS.md` from `/tmp/project-sibling` while editing files notionally under `/tmp/project`) where the load-bearing predicate at `instruction.ts:201` was a substring `current.startsWith(root)` check, replaced with the now-correctly-implemented `AppFileSystem.contains(root, current)` whose underlying `relativePath`/`isContainedRelativePath` decomposition correctly handles Windows drive boundaries (`C:\` vs `D:\` no longer match), `..c`-prefixed names that aren't actually parent traversals, and POSIX/win32 separator drift; codex #20098 closes a credential-exfiltration primitive (a malicious `.codex/config.toml` in a freshly-cloned repo could silently route every request to `https://attacker.example/v1` via project-local `openai_base_url`/`chatgpt_base_url`/`model_provider`/`model_providers`/`profile`/`profiles`/`experimental_realtime_ws_base_url`) by adding a 7-key `PROJECT_LOCAL_CONFIG_DENYLIST` applied at the loader boundary in `sanitize_project_config`, with an `ignored_project_config_keys: Vec<String>` per-layer record that lets the loader emit a startup warning and lets tests assert exactly what was filtered without resurrecting denied keys downstream; codex #20091 (DRAFT) converts `tool_suggest` from a flat `ToolSpec::Function` to a `ToolSpec::Namespace` containing one `ResponsesApiNamespaceTool::Function` child, generalizing `configured_tool_supports_parallel` from "namespaced calls always return false" (`if tool_name.namespace.is_some() { return false; }`) to a structural `Namespace` arm that matches when both the call's namespace AND child name match the registered namespace's name + child tool name (with `Function`/`Freeform` arms gaining `if tool_name.namespace.is_none()` guards to prevent false-positive cross-shape matches); litellm #26723 fixes silent per-request routing breakage on multipart endpoints where `user_config`/`litellm_metadata` arrived as raw JSON strings instead of dicts (the existing decoder only handled `metadata`) — fix hoists a `_JSON_STRING_FORM_FIELDS = ("metadata", "litellm_metadata", "user_config")` tuple consumed by a single helper, plus a gated `tags` decode that only fires when the string starts with `[` (preserving the legitimate `tags=single-string` contract); litellm #26719 model-class fix for the `GeneratorExit is BaseException, not Exception` trap across all three Bedrock pass-through streaming sites (`_async_streaming`, `_sync_streaming`, `chunk_processor`) — Starlette's `aclose()` on client disconnect raises `GeneratorExit` at the suspended `yield`, the `try/except Exception:` wrapping the chunk loop didn't catch it, and the post-loop spend-tracking flush was silently skipped on every interrupted stream; fix moves the flush into a `finally:` block guarded by a `flush_scheduled` flag and a `raw_bytes`-non-empty gate to enforce "exactly once, only if data buffered," with `verbose_logger.exception` on scheduling failure so original `GeneratorExit` propagates cleanly; gemini-cli #26148 (DRAFT) fixes a missing-top-border bug in `ToolGroupMessage` when an `update_topic` (non-compact, non-bordered) tool sat between two normal tool groups — the `isFirstProp = isFirst ? (borderTopOverride ?? true) : prevIsCompact` predicate didn't recognize topic-class predecessors as "no bottom border to share," fixed symmetrically at both render call sites by adding `prevIsTopic = ... && isTopicTool(prevGroup.name)` and changing the predicate to `prevIsCompact || prevIsTopic`, with a snapshot test pinning the rendered char grid; goose #8886 (DRAFT) is a wide copy/content sweep across goose2 (~22 files, ACP `New Chat`→`New chat` normalization at the ingestion boundary via single-source `normalizeAcpTitle` is the only behavior change, plus delete-modal restructuring with item-name-in-title, Doctor "Run fix" command moved into a styled `<code>` block, error-message normalization "Unable to..."→"Couldn't...", and a literal JSON syntax-error fix in `sessions.json` EN+ES) — but ships with three commented-out section description blocks (`{/* <p>...</p> */}`) which is dead-code-as-comments, an English-only `replace(/ \(Default\)$/, "")` regex in an i18n-aware codebase, and an undocumented removal of `variant="outline" size="sm"` on the Doctor confirm button.

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (litellm #26719 model-class `GeneratorExit`-vs-`Exception` fix with structurally-impossible-to-double-flush `flush_scheduled`+`raw_bytes` invariant pair across all three pass-through sites + 6-cell test matrix), 5 merge-after-nits (opencode #24866 document the `ensureRunning`/`stop` refcount-idempotency assumption near `stopOAuthCallback` + `Effect.catchAll(log.debug)` over `Effect.ignore`; opencode #24863 dedupe the duplicated `core/src/filesystem.ts` and `opencode/src/util/filesystem.ts` copies + Windows-host POSIX-input fallthrough comment + `D:\file.ts`-with-`C:\project`-root walker terminator test; codex #20098 confirm startup-warning emission site fires at user-visible log level + comment "top-level only" semantics + release-note callout for silent behavior change; litellm #26723 comment intentional "proxy fields only" scope on `_JSON_STRING_FORM_FIELDS` + `tags` `[`-prefix-sniff assumption + release-note for newly-decoded `litellm_metadata`; gemini-cli #26148 comment "extend this when introducing other unboxed prev-group types" near `prevIsTopic` declarations + take out of DRAFT), 1 needs-discussion (codex #20091 wire-format compatibility for in-flight `(plain, "tool_suggest")` calls during rollout + MCP-side parallel-support helper untouched intentionally? + draft state), 1 request-changes (goose #8886 three commented-out section description `<p>` blocks must resolve to delete-or-restore not the third state + locale-parity audit beyond just EN+ES + confirm Doctor confirm-button styling change is intentional). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).

### W18 drip-157 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). Theme is "the wrong primitive looked right until you ran it through a second mode" — opencode #24864 fixes a one-line `withTimeout` cleanup leak where the cancel-the-pending-`setTimeout` call lived inside `.then(value => { clearTimeout(timeout); return value })`, so a rejecting inner promise propagated correctly but stranded the timer on the event loop until it fired (`.then` runs only on fulfillment), swapped to `.finally(() => clearTimeout(timeout))` which is the structurally-correct primitive and drops the now-redundant `return result` synchrony; opencode #24849 adds a third axis to the session-list filter (`?path=<workspace-relative>` alongside the existing `?directory=<abs>` and a new `?scope=project` enum), exposes a TUI System-palette toggle (`session_directory_filter_enabled`, default true), wires it through `sync.tsx`'s new `sessionListQuery()` helper that computes `path.relative(worktree, directory).replaceAll("\\", "/")` and falls back to `{scope:"project"}` on missing-worktree, and adds an integration test that mounts the full provider stack with a fake-fetch URL recorder to assert the `path=` and `scope=` query params per filter state — but the fallback for legacy rows where `SessionTable.path IS NULL` only triggers when callers pass *both* `path` and `directory` and the TUI helper passes only `path`, so the migration safety net never fires from the TUI; codex #20100 follows up #20088's structural-race fix for the plugin-hook env test by bumping that test's `python3 ...` handler `timeout_sec: Some(5)` → `Some(10)` because cold-start `python3 -c "import json; ..."` was tight against a 5 s budget on slow CI runners, but the doubling lacks the calibration comment that makes the next bump principled; codex #20097 redesigns the issue-digest skill output around three explicit modes (`default` summary-only ending with a "want details?" prompt, `details-upfront`, `follow-up details`), mandates a *single-line headline* as the first non-blank line under `## Summary` (canonical quiet-day literal `No major issues reported by users.`), inlines two example markdown blocks at SKILL.md:55-77 so the consumer has a literal template, halves the attention-marker thresholds 10/20 → 5/10 (script_version 2 → 4) without an attached historical replay, adds explicit `sort=updated`+`order=desc` to the GitHub search call which makes the existing reaction-handling caveat *more* load-bearing not less, and fixes a per-query pagination cap by renaming `len(numbers) >= limit` → per-query `seen_for_query >= limit` counter (the original short-circuited on combined-across-queries which starved later labels under multi-label invocations); litellm #26727 is a clean mechanical revert of #26534's lazy-feature-router registry — deletes `litellm/proxy/_lazy_features.py` (-307), restores ~30 eager `from litellm.proxy.X import router as Y` imports at the top of `proxy_server.py`, removes the `attach_lazy_features(app)` call site, deletes the test-side force-load shim in `test_proxy_routes.py:39-53` that iterated `LAZY_FEATURES`, and removes the entire `TestLazyFeatureRegistry`/`TestLazyFeaturesNotImportedAtStartup`/`TestLazyFeatureMiddleware` block (~250 lines) including the subprocess-isolated `test_heavy_modules_absent_at_startup` that pinned the now-dropped property, but ships without a captured post-mortem of *why* the lazy approach was reverted (prefix-collision regression? OpenAPI-schema-omits-routes-until-warm? first-request-latency tax?); litellm #26622 surfaces uvicorn ≥0.37.0's `timeout_worker_healthcheck` knob as a new `--timeout_worker_healthcheck` click option (env: `TIMEOUT_WORKER_HEALTHCHECK`) gated on `running_uvicorn = run_gunicorn is False and run_hypercorn is False` (the explicit `is False` is defensive against `None` truthiness — load-bearing but uncommented), with a capability probe via `inspect.signature(uvicorn.Config.__init__).parameters` that gracefully degrades on older uvicorn with a yellow-ANSI `print(...)` warning (which lands on stdout instead of through the structured `verbose_proxy_logger` that the rest of the file uses, breaking JSON-log shipping pipelines), and pins both the helper-shape and the click-shape with two new tests — the first patches `uvicorn.Config` with a fake whose `__init__` accepts the kwarg, which is the cleaner technique than monkey-patching `inspect`; gemini-cli #26130 fixes SEA (Single Executable Application) relaunch by extracting four primitives into a new `processUtils.ts` module (`isStandardSea` discriminating *initial* SEA from *relaunched* SEA via `argv[0] !== argv[1]`, `getScriptArgs` returning `slice(1)` vs `slice(2)` accordingly, `isSeaEnvironment`, `getSpawnConfig(nodeArgs, scriptArgs)` returning `{spawnArgs, env}` with the SEA branch packing node flags into `NODE_OPTIONS = "${existing} ${new}".trim()` and pushing `[process.execPath, ...scriptArgs]` where the `execPath` is a *deliberate placeholder* for the `argv[1]` slot so the child's slice-from-2 lines up correctly), hardens against complex shell-escape via a regex `[\s"'\\]` reject-with-throw guard since `NODE_OPTIONS` round-tripping arbitrary quoted args would be brittle, and pins each helper independently with a 173-line test surface — but the `argv[0] !== argv[1]` discriminator is fragile when the binary is invoked via a symlink (Node 18+ preserves the symlink path in `argv[0]` while `execPath` resolves to realpath), the `NODE_OPTIONS` ordering puts new flags *after* user flags (CLI memory-bound wins on Node's last-wins semantics — likely intentional but uncommented), and the placeholder-execPath trick is load-bearing across the `getSpawnConfig`/`isStandardSea`/`getScriptArgs` triangle without a cross-reference comment; goose #8881 introduces the four-surface (mention `@`, slash command, Skills-view "start chat with skill" button, on-reload chip restoration) skill-application flow for goose2 with the right split-shape contract — chip in the user bubble (`MessageMetadataChip` + `messageChips` slice), assistant-only prompt block in the agent stream — pinned by the load-bearing test `it("stores user-visible chips separately from the agent prompt")` at `useChat.skillChips.test.ts:150` that asserts `message.metadata.chips` is computed from the same input as `message.body` but rendered to different consumers, with `buildSkillSendPayload(...)` separating user-visible `messageText` from agent-visible `skill_invocations: skillChips.map(c => ({ name: c.label }))` and per-session draft persistence + replay reconstruction via `acpSkillReplayChips.ts` — but ships with locale parity broken (only `en` + `es` updated when `de`/`fr`/`ja`/`pt-BR`/`zh-CN` are part of the i18n contract), no audit affordance for the assistant-only injected prompt (no tooltip, no debug expander, no "see full skill content" link — load-bearing because skills are user-installable), `skill_invocations` is name-only not ID-pinned (collision-fragile across user/system/ACP-installed skill sources), and no test for the replay path's "skill no longer installed since send" fallback rendering.

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (opencode #24864 minimal `.then→.finally` cleanup-primitive correction with bit-identical fulfillment behavior; litellm #26727 mechanical revert of #26534 with consistent test-deletion of the now-orphaned negative-shape assertions), 5 merge-after-nits (opencode #24849 missing windows-drive-boundary guard + empty-string-at-worktree-root test + legacy-row safety net unreachable from TUI helper; codex #20100 doubling-without-calibration-comment + sibling-timeout audit + follow-up-issue for cold-start cost; codex #20097 threshold-halving without historical replay + sort=updated tightens existing reaction-handling caveat + commit-splitting between sort-add and per-query cap fix; litellm #26622 cache the inspect.signature probe + route warning through structured logger + comment on `is False` defensive-truthiness; gemini-cli #26130 symlink-resolution fragility in `argv[0]!==argv[1]` discriminator + comment NODE_OPTIONS ordering precedence + cross-reference comment between placeholder-execPath and slice-from-2 contract), 1 request-changes (goose #8881 broken locale parity beyond en+es + no audit affordance for assistant-only injected prompt + name-only `skill_invocations` not ID-pinned + missing replay test for uninstalled-since-send skill). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).

### W18 drip-158 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 openai/codex, 2 sst/opencode, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "shrink the disagreement surface" — codex #20083 promotes the Goals feature from `Stage::UnderDevelopment` to `Stage::Experimental { name: "Goals", menu_description: "Set a persistent goal Codex can continue over time", announcement: "" }` in `codex-rs/features/src/lib.rs:949-957`, keeping `default_enabled: false` so the only behavioral delta is panel-visibility; codex #20106 collapses the linux-sandbox helper's three-input shape (`--sandbox-policy` + `--file-system-sandbox-policy` + `--network-sandbox-policy`) into a single canonical `--permission-profile`, deletes the ~140-line `resolve_sandbox_policies` resolver and its 4-variant `ResolveSandboxPoliciesError` enum (including `MismatchedLegacyPolicy { provided, derived }` — a class of bug that simply cannot exist in the new shape), renames `apply_sandbox_policy_to_current_thread` → `apply_permission_profile_to_current_thread` with the helper deriving `(file_system_sandbox_policy, network_sandbox_policy) = permission_profile.to_runtime_permissions()` internally at `linux-sandbox/src/landlock.rs:46-50`, and renames the exported `create_linux_sandbox_command_args_for_policies` → `..._for_permission_profile` — but drops the direct `SandboxPolicy::WorkspaceWrite { ... }` literal tests without a replacement that pins the `permission_profile → file_system_sandbox_policy` derivation produces the same `has_full_disk_write_access()` answer for each pre-existing fixture; opencode #24871 closes a silent-death regression (issue #3523) where MCP tool keys exceeding OpenAI's 64-char `tools[].function.name` limit caused a 400 that never reached the TUI — fix hoists `buildToolName(clientName, toolName)` at `packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts:118-134` that on overflow takes a content-derived `Hash.fast(combined).slice(0, 8)` and packs `combined.slice(0, 64 - 9) + "_" + hash`, with a `log.warn` at truncation time and a 79-line test surface that pins `keys.length === 2`, `key.length <= 64`, key uniqueness, and the `/_[0-9a-f]{8}$/` suffix regex against two prefix-colliding tools under a 35-char server name; opencode #24865 adds `cors?: string | string[]` to `ServerOptions` in both `packages/sdk/js/src/server.ts:34-38` and the v2 mirror, threading it as one or more `--cors=<origin>` args to the spawned `opencode serve` — handles `cors: ""` (falsy, dropped) and `cors: []` (loop runs zero times) correctly but ships without input validation/escaping or TSDoc; litellm #26725 gates the admin-UI static mount in `_try_populate_ui_directory` (`proxy_server.py:1454-1469`) on `str_to_bool(os.getenv("DISABLE_ADMIN_UI", "false")) is True`, replacing `app.mount("/ui", StaticFiles(...))` with two stub handlers `@app.get("/ui/{path:path}")` and `@app.get("/ui")` returning `admin_ui_disabled()` on the disabled branch — but doesn't appear to suppress the parallel `/ui/_next` mount and uses `@app.get` only (POST/PUT/etc. would 405-leak the path); litellm #26718 fixes a `httpx.RequestNotRead` crash inside `MaskedHTTPStatusError.__init__` at `litellm/llms/custom_httpx/http_handler.py:387-394` that previously broke credential-masking for every multipart upload (image edits, audio transcription, file uploads — exactly where API-key-in-URL leaks are most common) by wrapping the `original_error.request.content` access in `try/except httpx.RequestNotRead` with a `b""` fallback, plus a `test_handles_streaming_request_content` case that builds an `httpx.Request("POST", "https://api.openai.com/v1/images/edits?key=SECRET_KEY", stream=httpx.ByteStream(b"multipart-data"))` and asserts no-crash + `SECRET_KEY` is still scrubbed from `masked.request.url`; gemini-cli #26147 is a comment-only +85/-0 backfill that adds uniform `@group X / @scenario y / @maintainer agent-team` JSDoc to existing `evalTest('USUALLY_PASSES', { ... })` cases across `evals/{ask_user, automated-tool-use, frugalReads, plan_mode}.eval.ts` — `automated-tool-use.eval.ts:14-19, 106-111` correctly *appends* tags to existing prose blocks, the others insert fresh blocks; qwen-code #3717 adds a session-scoped `FileReadCache` keyed by `(dev, ino)` (correct primitive for `mv`-restore, atomic-rename-on-write, rm-and-recreate) at `packages/core/src/services/fileReadCache.ts` (75 LOC + 420-LOC test surface), wires it via `Config.getFileReadCache()` (one cache per Config so subagents start empty — explicitly documented at `config.ts:2498-2506`), and short-circuits at `tools/read-file.ts:130-167` where `isFullRead = offset === undefined && limit === undefined && pages === undefined` plus `cache.check(stats).state === 'fresh' && lastReadWasFull && lastReadCacheable && (lastWriteAt === undefined || lastReadAt > lastWriteAt)` returns an `unchangedResult(absPath)` placeholder whose llmContent is `[File <rel> unchanged since last read in this session — content was provided earlier in this conversation. ...]` — the cacheability gate `result.originalLineCount !== undefined` correctly excludes binary/image/audio/PDF/notebook reads but couples to a `processSingleFileContent` impl detail, and external-mutation blind spot for shell-tool writes is acknowledged only in the placeholder prompt rather than in code.

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (codex #20083 canonical stage promotion with no behavioral change beyond panel visibility; gemini-cli #26147 uniform comment-only annotation backfill with consistent `@group/@scenario/@maintainer` schema), 6 merge-after-nits (codex #20106 missing replacement test for `permission_profile → file_system_sandbox_policy.has_full_disk_write_access()` derivation parity + grep external consumers of dropped `compatibility_sandbox_policy_for_permission_profile`; opencode #24871 add comment near `Hash.fast(combined)` explaining "hash full name not prefix" collision-avoidance contract + UTF-16-vs-bytes `.length` comment even though `sanitize` makes it ASCII-safe; opencode #24865 add lightweight origin validation `^https?://[^\s,]+$` + TSDoc on the new `cors` field; litellm #26725 confirm `/ui/_next` mount is gated alongside `/ui` + change `@app.get` to `@app.api_route(..., methods=["*"])` to prevent 405-leak + add a `TestClient` assertion under `DISABLE_ADMIN_UI=true`; litellm #26718 add `assert masked.request.content == b""` to pin the body-dropping fallback shape against future "preserve body" refactors + one-line comment at the `try/except` site documenting the security/observability tradeoff; qwen-code #3717 swap `result.originalLineCount !== undefined` cacheability proxy for an explicit `result.cacheable` producer flag + document the same-mtimeMs/same-size content-equal external-write blind spot in `FileReadCache` JSDoc + file follow-up issue for `FileOperationEvent` cache-hit telemetry schema). Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (openai/codex, sst/opencode, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, QwenLM/qwen-code).

### W18 drip-159 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). Theme is "the wrong default leaks state across boundaries" — opencode #24877 closes a session-routing bug where every Hono session route extracted the *request's* `directory` query param and ran handlers under that instance, so a TUI on `/projB` continuing a session created against `/projA` silently moved the session's emitted sync events to the wrong workspace bus, fixed in `server/workspace.ts:42-71` by renaming `getSessionWorkspace`→`getSession`, looking up the saved `session.directory` first, and wrapping `next()` in `Instance.provide({ directory: session.directory, init: ... })` when no `workspaceID` is set; companion deletion at `cli/cmd/tui/context/event.ts:12-30` rips out three layers of in-TUI directory/workspace event filtering on the theory that the server now tags events correctly — but that's a much wider blast radius than the title implies and the PR test only exercises the server path, not the TUI handler-dispatch invariant; opencode #24852 fixes the devstral:24b skill-format regression (50-trial measurement: 68% pass on XML, 86% on JSON) by adding a `skills.format: "xml"|"json"|"markdown"` config knob plus an `isAnthropicModel(model)` predicate at `session/system.ts:36-90` that defaults non-Anthropic models to JSON and Anthropic models to XML, with the load-bearing risk being the substring sniff `model.api.id.includes("claude")` that will misclassify look-alike model ids on Ollama or self-hosted endpoints; codex #20113 closes the heredoc-file-redirect sandbox-bypass primitive opened by #10941 — pre-PR `cat <<'EOF' > /some/important/folder/test.txt\nhello\nEOF` collapsed to executable prefix `cat` and a `prefix_rule(pattern=["cat"], decision="allow")` then approved a write outside the sandbox, fixed by splitting the two semantics rather than narrowing one: `parse_shell_lc_single_command_prefix` now early-returns `None` on `file_redirect` descendants (`bash.rs:131-138`), `is_allowed_heredoc_attachment_kind` drops `"file_redirect"` from the allowlist (line 257), and a new sibling helper `shell_lc_contains_file_redirect()` is consulted by `commands_for_exec_policy` to force the *full* command vec into policy evaluation when a file redirect is present, with three new test cases covering the forbidden-prefix-still-applies, sandbox-catches-without-escalation, and escalation-required legs; codex #20096 (+747/-53) consolidates plugin-cache invalidation + MCP-refresh queueing behind a single `effective_plugins_changed_callback(&self, config: Config) -> Arc<dyn Fn() + Send + Sync>` (`codex_message_processor.rs:725-733`) that's threaded into all four lifecycle handlers (`plugin_install`, `plugin_install_remote`, `plugin_uninstall`, `plugin_uninstall_remote`) plus the startup warmup at `message_processor.rs:317-326`, fixing the bug where remotely-installed `chatgpt-global` plugins didn't show up in `skills/list` until restart, with a 217-line integration test that pre-seeds a real on-disk plugin layout and mocks the GLOBAL+WORKSPACE pages — but the asymmetric `plugin_uninstall_remote` callback firing only when `clear_remote_installed_plugins_cache()` returns true (line 691-697) is a subtle observability gap, and the `tokio::spawn`-fire-and-forget shape has a race window between cache clear and refresh queue that should be commented for idempotency; litellm #26730 closes the well-known AWS-NAT-Gateway-vs-OpenAI/Azure-slow-response failure mode (NAT idle 350s, provider tail 600s) by wiring opt-in TCP `SO_KEEPALIVE` into both the in-process httpx-on-aiohttp transport and the proxy's shared aiohttp session via a one-time `inspect.signature(TCPConnector.__init__).parameters` capability probe at `_AIOHTTP_SUPPORTS_SOCKET_FACTORY` (aiohttp ≥3.10) and a `_build_aiohttp_keepalive_socket_factory()` helper that returns a callable wiring `SO_KEEPALIVE=1` plus platform-conditional `TCP_KEEPIDLE`/`TCP_KEEPALIVE`/`TCP_KEEPINTVL`/`TCP_KEEPCNT`, with a 4-test surface that pins kwarg-omitted-when-disabled, kwarg-attached-when-enabled, kwarg-skipped-on-old-aiohttp, and the exact `setsockopt` triple-tuples the factory issues — but the default `AIOHTTP_TCP_KEEPIDLE=60` is aggressive (probes during the still-warm `keepalive_timeout=120` window) and the test surface only covers the transport path, not the proxy-bootstrap path; litellm #26714 flips two security-class default-on behaviors: removes `SkillsInjectionHook` from the unconditional `PROXY_HOOKS` dict and re-adds it only when `LITELLM_ENABLE_SKILLS=true`, plus gates `exec()` in `CustomCodeGuardrail._do_compile` and the matching admin endpoint behind `LITELLM_ENABLE_CUSTOM_CODE_GUARDRAILS=true` — but `_require_custom_code_enabled()` raises the same `CustomCodeCompilationError` class as legitimate compile failures (collapses two distinct operational signals), the endpoint returns `200 OK` with `success=False` while the hook raises (asymmetric disable shape), no startup-warning is emitted for operators relying on skills today, and there's no test for the disabled-by-default leg of either gate; gemini-cli #26139 fixes the FooterConfigDialog two-press-reset stale-closure bug — `setSetting(... 'ui.footer.items', undefined)` followed immediately by `resolveFooterState(settings.merged)` saw the *render-time* `settings.merged` not the post-write value, so the "new" state was the old state — by rewriting `handleResetToDefaults` to build a synthetic `defaultFooterSettings` object purely locally, dispatch a renamed `RESET` action (full-replace; was `SET_STATE` partial-merge), and defer the persistence write to `handleSave`, plus reordering two `useEffect` blocks in `useSelectionList.ts:310-353` so the items-changed initialization runs *before* the focusKey-changed effect (the load-bearing fix for the active-row-stays-at-old-index half of the bug), pinned by an `useSelectionList` regression test that asserts focusKey follows item `C` from index 2 to index 3 across a rerender — but the deferred-commit semantics of reset (no longer immediate-persist) is a subtle UX shift not addressed in the diff; goose #8877 (DRAFT, +2120/-52) turns goose2 into a real inline MCP-Apps host with a brand-new local axum proxy at `crates/goose/src/acp/mcp_app_proxy.rs` (+229) serving a 141-line CSP-shaped iframe wrapper plus guest-HTML store (TTL=300s, max=64), a new ACP method `_goose/tool/call` with `GooseToolCallRequest{session_id, name, arguments}` for app→Goose nested tool calls without going through chat, and a 412-line `McpAppView.tsx` host bridge routing six app-originated channels (`ui/message`, `ui/get-context`, `tools/call`, `resources/read`, `ui/open-link`, `ui/notifications/size-changed`) — but ships with the local proxy on HTTP (not HTTPS) authenticated by a default-shared `GOOSE_SERVER__SECRET_KEY="goose-acp-local"` query-string secret on both GET and POST endpoints (CSRF-vulnerable, secret-in-history-and-logs), a flat-string `extension__toolName` namespacing scheme on the new wire protocol that's hard to walk back if two extensions register colliding double-underscore names, and continued en+es-only locale parity (now a three-PR pattern with #8881 and #8886 from the prior two drips).

Verdict mix: 0 merge-as-is, 7 merge-after-nits (opencode #24877 server fix is well-targeted but TUI-side mass-deletion of `useEvent` filtering needs a rationale comment + cross-cwd subscriber test; opencode #24852 narrow the substring `claude` sniff or hoist a provider allowlist + fix the brittle preamble-counting test parser; codex #20113 the boolean overload in `commands_for_exec_policy`'s return tuple now carries two opposite semantics depending on which branch produced it + missing `>>` append-redirect test + brittle exact-reason-string assertion; codex #20096 the asymmetric `plugin_uninstall_remote` callback only firing when `clear_remote_installed_plugins_cache()` returns true + race-window comment between async cache clear and refresh queue + signature-change audit for `effective_skill_roots_for_layer_stack`; litellm #26730 default `AIOHTTP_TCP_KEEPIDLE=60` probes during the warm `keepalive_timeout=120` window + missing test for the proxy-bootstrap path symmetry + PR template fully unchecked; litellm #26714 collapsed exception class for compile-error-vs-policy-disable + asymmetric 200-OK-vs-raise disable shape across endpoint and hook + missing startup warning for operators relying on skills today + missing disabled-leg tests; gemini-cli #26139 deferred-commit semantics for reset (no longer immediate-persist) is a subtle UX shift not addressed + missing showLabels-reset test + react-effect-ordering load-bearing comment), 1 request-changes (goose #8877 default-shared local-proxy secret on HTTP with query-string auth and no CSRF protection on the POST store-guest-html endpoint must be addressed structurally not commented away as future hardening + flat-string `extension__toolName` namespacing on the new ACP wire protocol is harder to walk back than to fix now + continued locale parity drift). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).


### W18 drip-160 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). Theme is "the right primitive in the wrong place leaks state through a side channel" — opencode #24869 promotes the existing config-only `experimental.disable_paste_summary` knob into a TUI palette toggle backed by `kv` at key `paste_summary_enabled`, with a load-bearing `kv.get("paste_summary_enabled") ?? !sync.data.config.experimental?.disable_paste_summary` fallback at `app.tsx:301-303` for the signal initializer — but the actual paste-time gate at `prompt/index.tsx:1213` reads `kv.get("paste_summary_enabled", true)` (default `true`), so a user with `experimental.disable_paste_summary: true` who never opens the palette will silently get summaries on the first paste of every fresh kv-state session, contradicting their config; opencode #24867 replaces the `meta.limit >= store.limit` cache-reuse predicate with a four-input `canReuseRootSessionCache({cachedRootCount, fetchedLimit, requestedLimit, total})` whose third clause `total <= cachedRootCount` correctly handles the "asked for 40, server only has 20 roots, all 20 cached" branch the old check mishandled (pinned by `global-sync.test.ts:107-115`), and bumps the per-directory `SESSION_ROOT_PAGE_SIZE` from 5 to 20 — the `cachedRootCount` derivation `store.session.filter((s) => !!s?.id && !s.parentID && !s.time?.archived).length` is a hot-path linear scan; codex #20117 adds `--permissions-profile <NAME>` to all three `codex sandbox <macos|linux|windows>` debug subcommands, plumbed by appending `("default_permissions", TomlValue::String(name))` onto `cli_overrides` *before* `build_debug_sandbox_config` folds the stack — and as a side cleanup hoists seatbelt-only state (`allow_unix_sockets`, `log_denials`) into the `SandboxType::Seatbelt { ... }` variant so invalid combos (Landlock with allow_unix_sockets) become representationally impossible — but the three identical clap-arg blocks across `SeatbeltCommand`/`LandlockCommand`/`WindowsCommand` lack a shared derived struct or stay-in-sync comment, the parse test only covers macos, and the precedence interaction with `-c default_permissions=bar` is neither tested nor documented; codex #20111 bounds the `system_bwrap_warning_for_path` startup probe with a 500ms wall-clock + 50ms poll loop via `spawn` + `try_wait` rather than the unbounded `Command::new(...).output()`, with timeout-fail-open returning `true` (no warning) to preserve the "warn iff we *know* namespaces are broken" contract, pinned by `bwrap_tests.rs:49-65` which uses a 10ms timeout against a 1s sleeper to assert both correctness and bounded duration; litellm #26733 introduces an opt-in `LITELLM_USE_SHORT_MCP_TOOL_PREFIX` mode that swaps the historical alias/server_name prefix for a deterministic `SHA-256(server_id)[:8] → base62 → first 3 chars` derivation to keep tool names under the 60-char Anthropic-class limit, with mixed-version client tolerance via a new `iter_known_server_prefixes(server)` iterator that registers every prefix form (current, alias, server_name, server_id, short-ID) into `tool_name_to_mcp_server_name_mapping` — but the `prefix_to_server.setdefault(...)` collision-handling at `mcp_server_manager.py:2613-2617` silently picks first-registered on hash collision (62³=238328 namespace), the lookup dict is rebuilt on every `_get_mcp_server_from_tool_name` call instead of cached, and the `server.py:2034-2039` change widens `_get_mcp_server_from_tool_name` from "unprefixed-only" to "always, overwriting parsed prefix" without acknowledging that as a behavioral change in its own right; litellm #26729 closes a DB-amplification surface where every invalid `sk-...` request triggered a full `combined_view` join, by adding an `InvalidVirtualKeyCache` preflight (sk-shape check → `invalid_vk:{hashed_token}` negative cache → cheap single-table `litellm_verificationtoken` probe → record-miss with TTL 3600s default) and an invalidation hook at `generate_key_helper_fn:3248-3252` so a previously-bad-then-created key doesn't stay 401'd for an hour — but `check_invalid_token` returns only a bool not the hashed token so the caller re-hashes 2-3 times per rejected request, the malformed-key path silently shifted from `AssertionError` to `HTTPException(401)` without a behavioral-change test, and other key-creation paths (`/key/import`, undelete) lack parallel invalidations; gemini-cli #26149 adds an atomic `<projectTempDir>/<sessionId>/runtime.json` sidecar via the canonical tmp+`fsync`+`rename` shape with `O_CREAT|O_EXCL` (`'wx'`) defense-in-depth against pre-placed symlinks at the temp path, mode `0o600`, `crypto.randomUUID()` temp suffix, snake_case on-disk schema (`schema_version`, `pid`, `session_id`, `work_dir`, `hostname`, `started_at`, `gemini_cli_version`), strict reader at `runtimeStatus.ts:191-233` that refuses to coerce `null`/array/object/non-fatal-UTF-8/wrong-typed-fields into the type, and a `getSessionTempDir()` that throws on missing-session-id (caller wraps in best-effort try/catch at `gemini.tsx:622-636` after the `--list-*`/`--delete-session` early exits) — but the on-disk schema is forward-compatible while the reader uses `schemaVersion !== 1` equality, so a v2 producer would silently invisibilize itself to v1 readers, the no-cleanup-on-exit decision lacks accumulation-rate discussion, and the 304-line test surface omits Windows rename-replace atomicity and POSIX permission-bit assertions; goose #8870 fixes an under-reporting bug in `--output-format json` and `--output-format stream-json` where the completion event read only `session.total_tokens` (current turn) instead of `session.accumulated_total_tokens.or(session.total_tokens)` (cumulative-preferred), and adds the previously-missing `input_tokens`/`output_tokens` fields to both `JsonMetadata` (lines 67-70) and `StreamEvent::Complete` (lines 91-94) with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` for wire-format backward compatibility.

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (codex #20111 minimal `spawn`+`try_wait` poll-with-deadline replacement of unbounded `Command::output()` with fail-open-on-timeout preserving the existing "warn iff we *know* namespaces broken" contract, pinned by 10ms-vs-1s-sleeper test; goose #8870 surgical `accumulated_X.or(X)` cumulative-preferred fix to under-reporting bug across both json and stream-json emitters with `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"` wire-format backward compat for new input_tokens/output_tokens fields), 6 merge-after-nits (opencode #24869 two-site asymmetry between signal initializer using `??` against legacy config and gate site using `kv.get(..., true)` ignoring it — fix by either writing resolved initial value back to kv at startup or extending gate to consult the same `??` chain; opencode #24867 cachedRootCount filter is hot-path O(n) linear scan + page-size 5→20 deserves cold-load perf note + third test name should explicitly say "even when historical fetch over-asked"; codex #20117 three identical clap-arg blocks need shared struct or stay-in-sync comment + parse test only covers macos + precedence with `-c default_permissions=bar` neither tested nor documented; litellm #26733 missing startup-time short-prefix collision check across registered servers + `prefix_to_server` rebuilt on every lookup instead of cached + `server.py:2034-2039` widens resolution scope from "unprefixed only" to "always overwriting parsed prefix" without docstring acknowledgment; litellm #26729 `check_invalid_token` should return `(reject, hashed)` to avoid 2-3x re-hash per rejected request + invalidation hook only on `generate_key_helper_fn` not on `/key/import`/undelete + malformed-key path silently shifted from `AssertionError` to `HTTPException(401)` without behavioral-change test; gemini-cli #26149 reader `schemaVersion !== 1` equality vs forward-compatible on-disk schema asymmetry + no-cleanup-on-exit accumulation-rate discussion missing + Windows rename-replace atomicity not tested), 0 needs-discussion, 0 request-changes. Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).

### W18 drip-161 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 openai/codex, 2 sst/opencode, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "delete the cleanly-replaceable shortcut, then keep the receipts" — codex #20120 removes the legacy `--full-auto` shortcut from all three `codex debug sandbox <macos|linux|windows>` subcommands now that `--permissions-profile` (#20117) and replay-JSON (#20119) cover the same ground, deleting the `full_auto: bool` field from `SeatbeltCommand`/`LandlockCommand`/`WindowsCommand` and the `DebugSandboxConfigOptions` struct, the `pub fn create_sandbox_mode(full_auto: bool) -> SandboxMode` helper at `cli/src/debug_sandbox.rs:599-607`, and the `if full_auto { anyhow::bail!("...is only supported for legacy `sandbox_mode` configs; choose a writable `[permissions]` profile instead") }` guard at `:817-828` — but the bail's user-facing guidance ("use `--permissions-profile` instead") evaporates with the option, so users on permission-profiles configs hit clap's generic `unrecognized argument` instead of the helpful redirect, and the `pub` visibility on the deleted helper means any out-of-tree fork that imported it for parity gets a hard break without a release-note callout; codex #20110 repairs the `apply_patch_cli` event-stream race that #20040 introduced (its `harness.submit(...)` waits for `TurnComplete`, which drains the same stream the tests assert against) by adding a parallel `submit_turn_without_wait_*` family in `core/tests/common/test_codex.rs` that mirrors the existing `submit_turn_with_*` family signature-for-signature so the test edits become 1:1 method-name substitutions, with both families converging on a shared private `submit_turn_op_with_context(...)` so op-construction has one source of truth — but the new `_without_wait_` naming is ambiguous at call sites without a docstring ("does not wait for what?"), and the long-standing swallowed-timeout in `wait_for_event_with_timeout(... TurnComplete ..., SUBMIT_TURN_COMPLETE_TIMEOUT)` (no `?`/`expect`) was the moment to either propagate or comment; opencode #24887 adds a built-in `hash` tool for file checksums (closes #24886) — `node:crypto.createHash` streamed off `createReadStream`, defaults to `sha256` with `sha512`/`sha1`/`md5` for vendor-manifest compat, optional `expected` hex for verification with `metadata.matches: true|false|undefined` tristate, schema-default applied at decode time via `Schema.withDecodingDefault(Effect.succeed("sha256" as const))` so partial model input doesn't leak `undefined` into `createHash`, and a TUI inline render arm next to the existing `Skill` arm — but `normalizeHex` (`s.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-f0-9]/g, "")`) doesn't length-check against the algorithm's expected digest length so a truncated `expected` silently produces `matches: false` instead of a clear "expected 64 hex for sha256, got 32" diagnostic, the `createReadStream` `signal: signal as any` cast is unnecessary on Node ≥ 15.4, and there's no test surface for the five branches (default algo, explicit algo, expected match, mismatch, abort-during-stream); opencode #24883 introduces server-side **workflow trace** (per-HTTP-request structured JSON to `~/.local/share/opencode/trace/trace_<request_id>_<iso>.json`) capturing HTTP metadata, `events` timeline, and `chat` block (`user_input`/`model_stream_text`/`part_deltas`/`assistant_output`/`model`), threaded through Effect via a new `WorkflowTraceSessionRef` (ALS can't traverse Effect runtime boundaries) and defaults to writing only for `POST /session/:id/{message,prompt_async,command,shell}` — notably ships with a self-postmortem `WORKFLOW-TRACE-PERF.md` documenting an O(n²) string-concat regression caught during dev (`model_stream_text = model_stream_text + delta` on every stream event was saturating the event loop), fixed by `string[].push` + single `join` plus deferred `setImmediate` for `materialize + JSON.stringify + fs.write` so the HTTP path doesn't block on persistence — but the `setImmediate` callback is fire-and-forget without `try/catch` so any thrown `materialize` error becomes an unhandled rejection, the trace dir lacks any rotation/retention story (default-on for four interactive routes accumulates files indefinitely), and the design-pivot from `llm_stream` shape to `part_deltas` shape (correctly aligned with the UI bus) leaves residual question marks about whether `traceRecordLlmStreamEvent` is a backward-compat alias or live duplicate; litellm #26735 stops `app/page.tsx`'s teams-prefetch from stomping `OldTeams`'s own paginated reads on every parent re-render — the production fix is two prop drops at `:533-538` (`teams={teams}` and `setTeams={setTeams}`), making `OldTeams` the sole owner of its paginated state via the existing `teamListCall` + `useTeams` hook, with the test side migrating consistently via a new `mockTeamsResponse(teams)` helper at `OldTeams.test.tsx:134-143` that injects `{teams, total, page:1, page_size:10, total_pages: ...}` shape per test — but the helper uses `mockResolvedValueOnce` so two-refetch tests (delete + reload) silently fall through to the un-mocked default, and the `total_pages: teams.length > 0 ? 1 : 0` test convenience never exercises the actual bug class (`total_pages > 1` parent-stomp) so the 30/30-pass regression test surface only proves "renders 1 page", not "paginates correctly across 3 pages without parent stomp"; litellm #26726 threads `stream` through `get_optional_params_transcription` and the OpenAI-whisper / hosted_vllm transformations so audio-transcription endpoints emit SSE chunks (`text/event-stream`) instead of buffering into one `TranscriptionResponse` — adds a new `transform_audio_transcription_streaming_chunk(chunk: bytes) -> bytes` hook on `BaseAudioTranscriptionConfig` (default pass-through for OpenAI-SSE-compatible providers), a `_build_audio_transcription_streaming_response(...)` on `BaseLLMHTTPHandler` with sync+async iterator paths each `try: yield ... finally: response.aclose()/close()`, `"stream"` added to `OPENAI_TRANSCRIPTION_PARAMS` in `litellm/constants.py:671`, and a verbose_json-skip when `stream=True` (incompatible with streaming) preserving the override otherwise so cost calc still works — but the chunk-translation hook docstring doesn't clarify whether `chunk` is one full SSE frame or an arbitrary byte slice (matters for provider override authors who might parse JSON inside their override and choke on keep-alive frames), and the new `TranscriptionStreamingResponse` public type needs `litellm.__all__` export confirmation for SDK type consumers; gemini-cli #26153 closes a privacy-class bug where four telemetry event classes (`ApiRequestEvent.toLogRecord` `request_text`, `ConsecaPolicyGenerationEvent` `user_prompt`/`trusted_content`/`policy`, `ConsecaVerdictEvent` plus `verdict_*`, and a Clearcut equivalent) were emitting user-sensitive content to OTEL log records *and* Clearcut even when `logPrompts: false`, contradicting the documented privacy contract — fix gates each affected field behind `getTelemetryLogPromptsEnabled() && this.xxx` matching the existing pattern from `UserPromptEvent`/`HookCallEvent`/`toSemanticLogRecord`, with a load-bearing test surface at `conseca-logger.test.ts:148-318` pinning the four-corner matrix (event × {OTEL, Clearcut} × {disabled, enabled}) — the disabled-leg tests assert `attrs['user_prompt']` etc. are *all* `undefined` (testing for absence not empty string), the inverse `enabled` tests pin positive cases so future *over*-redaction also trips, and the Clearcut variant explicitly asserts only `{gemini_cli_key: EventMetadataKey.CONSECA_ERROR, value: 'some error'}` survives (the `error` field is intentionally *not* gated as operator-debug signal vs user content) — but the inline `as unknown as Config` mock construction is duplicated per test (a shared `makeConfigMock({logPrompts: false})` factory would cut the +437 closer to +200), and the PR doesn't sweep other `*Event.toLogRecord`/`*Event.toSemanticLogRecord` sites in the same telemetry dir for the same bug class; qwen-code #3721 (+1119/-50) migrates `AgentExecutionDisplay` (the SubAgent runtime panel) onto the same visual-height / visual-width slicing primitives that `ToolMessage` and `ConversationMessages` already use after #3591/#3713, replacing hard-coded `MAX_TASK_PROMPT_LINES = 5` / `MAX_TOOL_CALLS = 5` constants plus character-length truncation (`firstLine.length > 80`) with `sliceTextByVisualHeight` so the panel honors actual rendered cell-height regardless of soft-wrap and updates in-place instead of forcing Ink's clear-screen redraw on every tool addition — but the most impressive part is the new `integration-tests/terminal-capture/subagent-flicker-regression.ts` (+617 LOC), a deterministic TUI ratchet driving a 60×18 PTY against a mock OpenAI server (agent → 5×`read_file` → final → final) and counting `\x1b[2J\x1b[3J\x1b[H` clear-terminal triples, erase-line, cursor-up sequences, with reference numbers given for *both* with-fix (`clearTerminalPair=5, eraseLine=440`) and without-fix (`clearTerminalPair=2, eraseLine=469`) and an honest upfront caveat that the with-fix clear-pair count is *higher* in this scenario (because the new "Showing N visual lines" footer + bounded slicing trigger extra commits) and that the fix's *primary* value (preventing flicker storms when terminal width changes mid-run) isn't exercised here because `TerminalCapture` lacks a `resize()` public method (cross-referenced to the AppContainer `does not clear the terminal just because width changed` test) — but the reference numbers come from "M2 Pro Mac" so a cold CI runner could trivially produce more `eraseLine` events than the 2× threshold allows (worth a `CI=1` thresholds-relaxation knob), the `npm run build && npm run bundle && npx tsx ...` precondition needs a single `package.json` script wrapper to prevent CI from accidentally testing stale binaries, and the always-present "Showing N visual lines" footer should be suppressed when nothing was clipped.

Verdict mix: 0 merge-as-is, 8 merge-after-nits (codex #20120 preserve `--full-auto`→`--permissions-profile` migration hint via clap `after_help` after option removal + release-note the `pub fn create_sandbox_mode` deletion; codex #20110 docstring on `_without_wait_` family + propagate-or-comment the long-standing swallowed `TurnComplete` timeout; opencode #24887 length-check `expected` hex against algorithm digest size + drop `as any` on createReadStream signal + add unit test surface for digest/match/mismatch/abort branches; opencode #24883 wrap `setImmediate` callback in `try/catch` to avoid unhandled-rejection process exits + add trace dir rotation/retention so default-on doesn't accumulate indefinitely + create `Path.trace` with `0o700` + microbenchmark pinning processor.ts no-trace vs trace-enabled stream-event throughput delta; litellm #26735 add a `total_pages > 1` regression test to lock in the actual bug class beyond "renders 1 page" + sanity-grep `app/page.tsx` for residual stale `teams.` reads; litellm #26726 clarify chunk-frame contract in hook docstring + verify `verbose_json`-skip is guarded conditional not delete + confirm `TranscriptionStreamingResponse` exported through public types; gemini-cli #26153 factor repeated `configNoPrompts` mock into shared `makeConfigMock({logPrompts: false})` helper + sweep other `*Event.toLogRecord`/`*Event.toSemanticLogRecord` sites for same bug class; qwen-code #3721 add `CI=1` threshold-relaxation knob to TUI ratchet for cold-runner flake + wrap build+bundle+test in single npm script so CI can't test stale binaries + suppress "Showing N visual lines" footer when nothing clipped), 0 needs-discussion, 0 request-changes. Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (openai/codex, sst/opencode, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, QwenLM/qwen-code).


### W18 drip-162 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). Theme is "the right primitive in the wrong scope leaks state through a side channel" — opencode #24891 adds a built-in `open` tool (OS default-handler launch for files/folders/file-URLs/http-URLs) with allowed-scheme allowlist `{http,https,file}` at `tool/open.ts:212-218` and `ctx.ask` permission gate, but the gate uses `always: ["*"]` (`tool/open.ts:243-256`) so a single user "always" approval grants unlimited open across the filesystem, and the relative-path branch at `:236-241` does `path.resolve(ins.directory, raw)` without canonicalize-vs-project-root check so `target: "../../../../../etc/shadow"` resolves outside the workspace and is stat'd+opened on first approval; opencode #24889 adds a 5-mode bounded `wait` tool (fixed delay / until_file with stable-window / until_url HEAD-then-GET / until_pid_exit / until_text regex tail) with sound bounds (1-3600s, 100ms-60s poll, 1MiB max tail) and a clean `abortable` race shape at `:142-153`, but is **not** gated by `ctx.ask` despite `until_url` issuing real network requests to arbitrary URLs (SSRF vector to LAN/AWS metadata `169.254.169.254` on 5s polls for up to 1 hour), `probeUrl` only falls back from HEAD to GET on 405/501 (not 4xx generally) and never drains response body so socket pool builds buffer pressure, and there's no regex-DoS guard on the user-supplied `until_text_pattern` against a 1MiB tail (catastrophic backtracking can pin the wait until `seconds` expires); codex #20133 walks back the `--full-auto` hard-removal from #20120 by deprecating-not-deleting on `codex exec` (runtime warning), removing entirely from `tui` (covered by trust-flow) and `sandbox <os>` (covered by `-c sandbox_mode="..."`), and adding a `cli_overrides_use_legacy_sandbox_mode` helper at `cli/src/debug_sandbox.rs:639-642` that scans for `-c sandbox_mode=...` overrides so the bare `codex sandbox <os>` correctly preserves the historical "default to read-only when invoked this way" contract — but the new `uses_legacy_sandbox_mode_override = true` branch is the load-bearing logic and ships untested, the `pub fn create_sandbox_mode` deletion lacks a release-note despite the title saying "deprecate", and `run_in_container.sh` shifts from `--full-auto` to `--sandbox workspace-write --ask-for-approval on-request` which may be a slight ask-policy change vs the old default; codex #20123 loosens the rollout-trace reducer's reasoning-item identity check by deleting `ensure_reasoning_consistency` and rewriting `reasoning_body_matches` to compare only on `encrypted_content` (`reducer/conversation.rs:608-624`), with a new `incoming_readable_reasoning_supersedes_existing` (`:664-678`) that upgrades stored body when incoming has strictly-more readable parts via in-order subsequence check (`existing.iter().all(|e| incoming_iter.any(|i| i == e))` — note `.any` advances the iter, so semantics are "ordered subsequence" not "subset"), correctly preventing the bail on legitimate Responses-API replays where snapshots replay only the encrypted blob — but the conflict-not-error policy shift means two reasoning items with the same encrypted blob but conflicting readable text now silently keep existing (no `tracing::debug!` at the supersede-decision site to diagnose), and the order-sensitivity of the subsequence check isn't tested; litellm #26740 restores admin-user access to their own personal-usage view on the dashboard usage page by adding an admin-only `"my-usage"` option to `UsageOption` union at `UsageViewSelect.tsx:14`, with the load-bearing rewrite at `UsagePageView.tsx:172` from `isAdmin ? selectedUserId : userID || null` to `usageView === "my-usage" || !isAdmin ? userID || null : selectedUserId` (forcing admin's own userID when in my-usage mode), and panel-render gate widening at `:480` to include `my-usage` while hiding the user-filter dropdown (`isAdmin && usageView === "global"` at `:483`) — but PR ships zero tests despite "at least 1 test is a hard requirement" in the project template, and it's worth verifying no downstream "showing data for X" header reads `selectedUserId` directly (would label admin's data as "all users" in my-usage mode); litellm #26732 adds two missing Bedrock-Converse pricing entries (`jp.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7` at `model_prices_and_context_window.json:1290-1322` with `input_cost_per_token: 5.5e-06`, and `jp.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6` at `:1462-1490` with `input_cost_per_token: 3.3e-06`) for AWS Japan-region cross-region inference profiles, with internally-consistent ratios across cache-creation (1.25× input), cache-read (0.1×), and output (5×) for both blocks, but the PR description doesn't link the AWS Bedrock JP pricing source-of-truth, and the sonnet block omits `supports_xhigh_reasoning_effort` / `supports_max_reasoning_effort` flags that the opus block has (likely correctly mirroring base sonnet, but worth explicit verification); gemini-cli #26160 closes a signal-orphaning bug in `relaunchAppInChildProcess` where the parent installed no signal handlers so `kill <parent>` left the child orphaned to PID 1 holding ports/locks indefinitely — fix registers handlers for `SIGTERM`/`SIGINT`/`SIGHUP`/`SIGUSR2` (notably includes USR2 for nodemon, excludes SIGQUIT) at `relaunch.ts:73-86` with `child.killed`-gated forwarding via `child.kill(sig)`, and symmetrically removes via `removeForwarders()` at both `child.on('error')` and `child.on('close')` paths to prevent listener-leak across relaunch cycles — but registration errors are silently swallowed (no `console.warn` for diagnostic), the `child.send` block at `:99-101` can throw between handler-registration and Promise-construction creating a leak window, and the test surface only exercises SIGTERM not all four signals; goose #8893 migrates `GooseAcpAgent` from per-instance `Config::new(config_dir.join(CONFIG_YAML_NAME), "goose")` calls to the process-wide `Config::global()` singleton (matching REST API parity), eliminating the `config_dir` field on the agent struct (`acp/server.rs:186`), the `load_config()`/`config()` helpers (`:868-877`), and the per-call YAML disk-read perf footgun — net -110/+104 because the change deletes one error-handling arm per call site since `Config::global()` is infallible, and collapses `let config = Config::new(...)?; ...&config` patterns to direct `Config::global()` deref — but mid-process config-reload semantics silently disappear (pre-PR every method re-read disk; post-PR all reads come from singleton loaded at startup), there's no `Config::is_global_initialized()` check at agent construction so an out-of-order test/binary that constructs the agent before global init will panic, and `config_dir` remains as a one-shot constructor arg used only to seed `PermissionManager` creating a minor signature asymmetry.

| [#8893](https://github.com/block/goose/pull/8893) | refactor: use Config::global() instead of per-instance config in SACP server | [2026-W18/drip-162/block-goose-pr-8893.md](2026-W18/drip-162/block-goose-pr-8893.md) |

Verdict mix: 0 merge-as-is, 8 merge-after-nits (opencode #24891 `always: ["*"]` permission grant is too broad for an OS-default-opener that can read anything + missing canonicalize-vs-`ins.directory` check on relative-path branch lets `target: "../../etc/shadow"` resolve outside workspace + `xdg-open`-only Linux fallback breaks in containers; opencode #24889 `until_url` mode issues real network requests to arbitrary URLs without `ctx.ask` gating creating SSRF vector to LAN/metadata endpoints + `probeUrl` HEAD→GET fallback only on 405/501 not generic 4xx + no regex-DoS guard on user pattern against 1MiB tail + body never drained creating socket-pool buffer pressure on poll loops; codex #20133 `uses_legacy_sandbox_mode_override = true` branch is load-bearing logic shipped untested + `pub fn create_sandbox_mode` deletion lacks release-note despite title saying "deprecate" + verify+document `--full-auto`→`on-request` ask-policy semantic shift in `run_in_container.sh`; codex #20123 conflict-not-error policy shift means same-blob-different-readable now silently keeps existing without `tracing::debug!` diagnostic at supersede site + order-sensitivity of subsequence check (`incoming_iter.any` advances iter) isn't tested + asymmetry with eager-bail `ensure_call_id_consistency` lacks doc comment; litellm #26740 ships zero tests despite project template's "at least 1 test is a hard requirement" + verify no downstream "showing data for X" header reads `selectedUserId` directly which would label admin's data as "all users" in my-usage mode; litellm #26732 PR description doesn't link AWS Bedrock JP pricing source-of-truth + sonnet block omits `supports_xhigh_reasoning_effort`/`supports_max_reasoning_effort` flags opus block has (likely correctly mirrors base sonnet but unverified) + cross-check key-naming convention against sibling regional entries; gemini-cli #26160 silently-swallowed registration errors in `try/catch` make this hard to debug if it ever doesn't work + `child.send` block between handler-registration and Promise-construction can throw and leak handler refs + test only exercises SIGTERM not all four signals; goose #8893 mid-process config-reload semantics silently disappear without release-note + no `Config::is_global_initialized()` check at agent construction can panic on out-of-order init + `config_dir` remains as one-shot constructor arg for `PermissionManager` only creating signature asymmetry), 0 needs-discussion, 0 request-changes. Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).


### W18 drip-163 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 openai/codex, 2 sst/opencode, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "the load-bearing line was either the first line constructed twice or the line that flipped a default" — codex #20137 routes core process/filesystem tools through selected turn environments by threading `Option<String> environmentId` through the v2 app-server protocol surface (`ListMcpServerStatusParams`, `McpResourceReadParams`, `SkillsListParams`/`SkillsListEntry`) plus runtime carriers (`fs_api.rs`, `core/src/session/{turn,mcp,handlers}.rs`, all the `core-skills/src/*.rs` modules, `core/src/apply_patch.rs`, `core/src/context/environment_context.rs`), with skills emitting URIs under a new `oai_env://<environment_id>/<absolute_path>` scheme so the model can disambiguate same-named files across environments — but `oai_env_uri`/`parse_oai_env_uri` are duplicated near-verbatim across `core-skills/src/injection.rs:828-840` and `core-skills/src/render.rs:1199-1202` so a future scheme change drifts in one site silently, the new `environmentId` field is `Option<String>` everywhere with no `maxLength`/`pattern` constraint at the schema layer (so the only gate is server-side `invalid_request(format!("unknown environmentId..."))` after a 1MiB attacker string flows through deserialization), `parse_oai_env_uri` strips `oai_env://` and splits on `/` without URL-decoding so any path containing `?`/`#`/`%` round-trips ambiguously through a URI-aware consumer, and `forceReload`-with-`environmentId=remote` semantics are undocumented (does it invalidate the remote cache only, or local+remote?) — at 78 files this needs splitting into protocol-only / skills / MCP / FS layers for honest review; codex #20134 replaces the legacy startup-marker remote-plugin sync with full reconciliation against `/ps/plugins/installed?includeDownloadUrls=true` (downloads missing plugins to `plugins/cache/<remoteMarketplace>/<pluginName>/<version>`, upgrades on version drift, preserves enable/disable state, prunes stale entries) deduped via a `remote_plugin_sync_state {requested, in_flight}` flag and triggered both at startup and from `plugin/list`, with `run_remote_plugin_sync_loop` clearing `requested` at the *top* of each iteration so that mid-sync re-requests correctly trigger one more loop iteration — but the `Err` arm at diff lines 1015-1059 immediately retries with no backoff (a misconfigured backend URL becomes a tight retry loop hammering the server and spamming `warn!` logs), the `Mutex` poisoning fallback `err.into_inner()` silently uses stale state without a `tracing::warn!` diagnostic, the test rename `sync_plugins_from_remote_ignores_unknown_remote_plugins` → `..._keeps_existing_plugins_when_fetch_fails` may reflect a behavioral shift worth a release-note, and there's no test that exercises N concurrent `maybe_start_remote_plugin_sync` callers and asserts exactly one `tokio::spawn` runs (the dedup is the actual contract); opencode #24895 generalizes DeepSeek's hardcoded reasoning-content injection across AI SDK providers via a new `providerOptionsKey(model)` helper that returns `"openaiCompatible"` for both `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` and `@openrouter/ai-sdk-provider` (because `getOpenAIMetadata()` upstream hardcodes that key), capability-driven detection via `isInterleaved || model.capabilities.reasoning || msgs.some(...has reasoning part)`, and consolidates four `id.includes("deepseek-xxx")` temperature-override checks into a single `id.includes("deepseek")` — but the helper falls back to `"openaiCompatible"` for *every* unknown npm via `sdkKey(npm) ?? "openaiCompatible"` which silently injects an unrecognized `providerOptions` key into custom non-OpenAI-shaped wrappers, the third leg of `hasReasoningContent` retroactively coats every prior assistant message with `reasoning_content: ""` when a user mid-session switches from a non-reasoning to a reasoning model (probably correct but undocumented), the new string-content branch sets `providerOptions` without converting `content` to array-form (whereas the old code did the array conversion — needs DeepSeek round-trip integration verification), the `id.includes("deepseek")` widen now matches future `deepseek-coder`/`my-deepseek-finetune` etc., and `[field]: reasoningText || ""` is dead-code defensive (`[].join("")` is already `""`); opencode #24894 adds a 260-line static security review report (101 findings, risk score 100/100) under `.github/security-reports/` with no code fixes — but F-001/F-002 claims a real Anthropic-format secret at `github/README.md:120` with 94% confidence and the PR cannot land while that finding is unresolved (either rotate+history-clean before any further commits to dev, or annotate it as VERIFIED FALSE POSITIVE), the report is filed against `dev` HEAD without a Source SHA so line numbers go stale immediately, the `.github/security-reports/` location is auto-readable from `pull_request_target` workflow contexts (which the report itself flags as risky in F-007) creating a meta-vulnerability where a malicious fork PR could harvest the curated weak-spots list, six `Server Action missing auth guard` findings at 84% confidence are likely false positives without manual triage (Next.js Server Action analyzers over-fire because guards are commonly hoisted), and `100/100` risk score has no methodology link; litellm #26748 adds MCP sampling/elicitation/logging callback plumbing through `MCPClient.__init__` so upstream MCP servers can request LLM inference, user input, or send logs — but `_execute_session_operation` constructs `session_ctx = ClientSession(read_stream, write_stream)` and *immediately* overwrites it with `ClientSession(read_stream, write_stream, **session_kwargs)` (the first instance is GC'd without `__aexit__` and may have already subscribed to streams or allocated request-id state, double-subscribing on the second construct), and the stdio env handling regression at `mcp_server_manager.py:1184-1208` switches `dict(server.env or {})` → `dict(server.env) if server.env else None` so the load-bearing `NPM_CONFIG_CACHE` injection now skips when `server.env` is None (the common case for "I don't need custom env vars") — exactly the failure mode the original code defended against in containers — plus 100+ lines of pure whitespace deletion mixed into the feature change, deferred imports inside the closure that hide circular-import risk, and `_create_*_callback` factories that capture nothing and could be plain module-level functions; litellm #26744 is a +1/-1 fix for the FLUX.2-pro Azure AI image-edit endpoint silently ignoring the wrong key — `request_body["image"]` → `request_body["input_image"]` at `litellm/llms/azure_ai/image_edit/flux2_transformation.py:115` for a high-impact silent-failure bug (paid call returns wrong result, no error to monitor) — but ships without the test that the project template requires as "a hard requirement," doesn't link the BFL/Azure AI Foundry source-of-truth for the schema, and doesn't confirm sibling Azure AI image transformations weren't subject to the same schema-mismatch bug class; gemini-cli #26161 fixes `tools.shell.executable`/`tools.shell.args` settings being silently ignored by adding plumbing through `loadCliConfig` → `Config` → `ShellExecutionConfig` → `getShellConfiguration(overrideExecutable, overrideArgs)` at `shell-utils.ts:646-665` with PowerShell-detected default args `["-NoProfile", "-Command"]` and bash-shaped `["-c"]` — but PowerShell suffix-detection misses common installation paths like `C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7\pwsh` (no `.exe`), `pwsh-preview`/`pwsh-lts` aliases, and POSIX `/usr/local/bin/pwsh`, the `cmd.exe` case isn't detected at all so `tools.shell.executable: "cmd.exe"` silently gets bash's `["-c"]` instead of `["/c"]`, the schema default `args: [] as string[]` makes `overrideArgs || (default)` never fire so PowerShell users who don't set `args` get empty args instead of the documented defaults (which is exactly the bug class the PR title claims to fix), `requiresRestart: true` on both fields needs a test pinning "no hot-reload" or doc clarification, and `shell: 'bash'` for everything non-PowerShell may produce subtly wrong escaping for zsh/fish if downstream uses `shell` for syntax-aware quoting; qwen-code #3722 surgically fixes `/dream` memory consolidation pointing at the defunct `${QWEN_DIR}/tmp/${projectHash}/chats` path by rerouting both the CLI command at `dreamCommand.ts:30-37` and the auto-memory dream planner at `dreamAgentPlanner.ts:163-168` to `path.join(new Storage(projectRoot).getProjectDir(), 'chats')` (the canonical session-storage location), promotes `getTranscriptDir` from private to `export` to enable the new unit test, and adds two test files with both positive assertions (correct path computed) AND negative assertions (`expect(...).not.toContain('${path.sep}.qwen${path.sep}tmp${path.sep}')`) that lock in the absence of the old buggy path against future refactors — both call sites updated symmetrically, both old imports (`getProjectHash`, `QWEN_DIR`) cleanly dropped, and the `Storage.setRuntimeBaseDir(null)` `afterEach` cleanup correctly prevents per-test runtime-dir leakage.
