Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (qwen-code #3722 surgical two-site `/dream` transcript path fix from defunct `${QWEN_DIR}/tmp/${projectHash}/chats` to canonical `Storage(projectRoot).getProjectDir() + 'chats'` with both positive-and-negative test assertions locking in the absence of the old buggy path), 4 merge-after-nits (codex #20134 add exponential backoff on the `Err` arm of `run_remote_plugin_sync_loop` to prevent tight retry storms on misconfigured backend + `tracing::warn!` on `Mutex` poison recovery + concurrent-callers dedup test that asserts exactly one `tokio::spawn` runs + release-note for the test-rename behavioral shift; opencode #24895 short-circuit `providerOptionsKey` to undefined when sdkKey is unknown rather than silently injecting `openaiCompatible` into non-OpenAI-shaped wrappers + inline-comment the retroactive coating semantics of the `hasReasoningContent` third leg + DeepSeek round-trip integration test for the new string-content branch + remove dead `|| ""` defensive code; litellm #26744 add the project-template-mandated regression test asserting `body["input_image"] == b64` and `"image" not in body` + link BFL/Azure AI source-of-truth in PR body + audit sibling Azure AI image transformations for the same schema-mismatch bug class; gemini-cli #26161 PowerShell detection via `path.basename(executable, path.extname(executable)).toLowerCase()` against `'powershell'`/`'pwsh'`/`pwsh-*` instead of suffix-match + add `cmd.exe` arm with `argsPrefix: ['/c']` + fix the `overrideArgs || default` truthy-check that schema default `[] as string[]` makes never fire), 1 request-changes (litellm #26748 has a real concurrency bug where `session_ctx = ClientSession(...)` is constructed twice with the first GC'd without `__aexit__` potentially double-subscribing to streams + a stdio-server-startup regression where switching `dict(server.env or {})` to `dict(server.env) if server.env else None` skips the load-bearing `NPM_CONFIG_CACHE` injection for the common no-custom-env case breaking npx-based MCP servers in containers — the exact failure mode the original code defended against), 2 needs-discussion (codex #20137 has duplicated `oai_env_uri`/`parse_oai_env_uri` across `core-skills/src/{injection,render}.rs` + 78-file PR shape needs splitting into protocol/skills/MCP/FS layers for honest review + schema-level validation gaps on `environmentId` length/pattern + URL-encoding ambiguity in the `oai_env://` scheme; opencode #24894 cannot land while F-001/F-002's claimed real Anthropic credential at `github/README.md:120` is unresolved — needs either rotation+history-clean or a verified-false-positive annotation before any further commits to dev + report location under `.github/security-reports/` is auto-readable from `pull_request_target` contexts creating a meta-vulnerability + 101 findings as one markdown doc with zero triaged is unactionable). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (openai/codex, sst/opencode, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, QwenLM/qwen-code).


### W18 drip-164 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (1 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "the bug was the field that did not get remapped, the override that did not get scoped, and the static asset that did not get deleted at its source" — opencode #24898 surgically fixes `Session.fork` not remapping `CompactionPart.tail_start_id` through `idMap` (regression introduced in commit `6f5a3d30f`, "keep recent turns during session compaction", 2026-04-10), with `filterCompacted` walking past the intended retention boundary in forked sessions because the stored id no longer matched any message in the fork's id space — observable as `context_length_exceeded` on the next request — fix at `packages/opencode/src/session/session.ts:619-628` builds a typed `p: MessageV2.Part`, then `if (p.type === "compaction" && p.tail_start_id) p.tail_start_id = idMap.get(p.tail_start_id)` with `idMap.get` returning `undefined` on missing-tail being correct fallback (clears the stale ref instead of pointing at a nonexistent id), test at `test/session/messages-pagination.test.ts:843-905` exercises the load-bearing assertion `childFiltered.some((m) => m.info.id === tailPart.tail_start_id)` which is exactly what would have caught the bug; codex #20141 lands a `codex-thread-manager-sample` one-shot binary as a public-surface canary but the load-bearing change is the ownership hoist of `ThreadStore` from per-call `configured_thread_store(&config)` into `ThreadManagerState.thread_store`, threaded through `ThreadManager::new` and replacing six call sites at `core/src/thread_manager.rs:573, 622, 648, 677, 815, 929, 962, 996` with `Arc::clone(&self.state.thread_store)` — a behavioral change because previously each thread re-derived its store from its own (potentially differing) `config.experimental_thread_store`, now all threads in the same `ThreadManager` share whatever store was selected at construction time (eliminates fork/resume diverging from parent), plus a public `Default for ManagedFeatures` with `source: None` that should be scoped to `#[cfg(test)]` since `None` source loses telemetry provenance; codex #20136 is a +192/-150 visual refresh of the localhost OAuth `success.html` adding proper `prefers-color-scheme` dark mode via `--color-*` CSS variables, a fixed-position wordmark, and a flexbox-based centered `<main>` shell that replaces the brittle `position: relative; height: 100%` layout dependent on `<html>`/`<body>` height, with title shifted to past tense ("Signed in to Codex") to reflect success state — pure HTML/CSS, no auth-flow logic, but `--interactive-label-secondary-default` is hardcoded `#0d0d0d` in `:root` and re-declared per-mode instead of derived from existing gray tokens, and the fixed wordmark could overlap content at <360px viewports (theoretical for an OAuth-localhost page); litellm #26746 re-files the closed #26740 with a 2-source-file +13/-6 fix restoring admin's personal-usage view via a new `"my-usage"` option in `UsageOption` union (`UsageViewSelect.tsx:14`) and load-bearing rewrite at `UsagePageView.tsx:172` from `isAdmin ? selectedUserId : userID || null` to `usageView === "my-usage" || !isAdmin ? userID || null : selectedUserId`, with panel-render gate widening at `:480-483` to include `my-usage` while hiding the user-filter dropdown via `isAdmin && usageView === "global"` — but ships zero tests despite project template's "at least 1 test is a hard requirement" (the same critique applied in #26740 went unaddressed in the re-file), and screenshot-binary storage location is inconsistent with PR-body link convention (both `.github/screenshots/` files and user-attachments URLs); litellm #26739 is a pure-deletion -76 PR removing 8 stale Next.js static-export artifacts under `litellm/proxy/_experimental/out/chat*` (`chat.html`, `chat.txt`, and 6 `chat/__next.*.txt` files) so direct-URL access to `/ui/chat` returns 404 — mechanically correct since FastAPI's `StaticFiles` mount needs filesystem absence to 404 — but the unaddressed root cause is the source-side Next.js `app/chat/page.tsx` (or wherever the source page lives) which will silently regenerate these on the next `npm run build && npm run export` from any UI rebuild PR, giving this fix a half-life of one rebuild; gemini-cli #26163 fixes two real bugs in the model-availability subsystem — (1) explicit `--model gemini-3-pro-preview` was being silently routed through auto-routing chains (3-attempt cap + silent Flash downgrade instead of the documented 10-attempt + prompt-before-fallback) by introducing `isAutoSelection` flag through `policyCatalog.ts` and a shared `AUTO_ROUTING_OVERRIDES = { maxAttempts: 3, actions: { ...DEFAULT_ACTIONS, transient: 'silent' }, stateTransitions: { ...DEFAULT_STATE, transient: 'sticky_retry' } }` constant at `:59-66`, and (2) `geminiChat.ts` resolved `maxAttempts` once at turn-start so Flash inherited Pro's 3-attempt limit on mid-turn fallback — fixed by re-resolving `currentMaxAttempts` per `retryWithBackoff` iteration via the existing `getAvailabilityContext` callback, with `errorClassification.ts:15-40` widening each `instanceof` check with structural fallback `(error && typeof error === 'object' && 'name' in error && error.name === 'TerminalQuotaError')` to handle the dual-instance problem from npm-link / vitest module-graph splits — but the **preview branch at `policyCatalog.ts:97-110` inlines the override fragment instead of using `AUTO_ROUTING_OVERRIDES`** creating drift risk for the next policy tweak, the `fallbackIntegration.test.ts:80-113` test pins the per-turn-resolution bug but does NOT exercise the per-iteration resolution bug in `retryWithBackoff` that the PR description claims to fix (need `geminiChat.test.ts` confirmation), and the explicit-preview behavioral shift (transient errors now terminal instead of silent retry) deserves a release-note for users on `--model gemini-3-pro-preview`; goose #8896 is a +1192/-698 mid-sized goose2 UI polish across three tracks — a Tauri-side `project_icons.rs` Rust command for repository-icon discovery with size cap `MAX_PROJECT_ICON_BYTES = 512 * 1024`, frontend `ProjectIcon`/`ProjectIconPicker` components, and a sidebar refactor that deletes `useSidebarHighlight` (-114) and the entire `StatusBar.tsx` (-75) plus the `status` i18n namespace (en+es) in favor of row-local hover state — but the MIME allowlist permits SVG, and SVG can carry `<script>` tags that execute when inlined via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` or `<object>`, so `ProjectIcon.tsx` (+175) needs strict verification that it only renders `<img src={dataUrl}>` (which sandboxes SVG) — and the StatusBar removal is buried under a "polish" PR title without a release-note callout for users who relied on it for model display / token count / connection state; qwen-code #3723 is the **foundation** for Issue #3247's tool-execution unification (not unification itself), introducing a `permissionFlow.ts` module with four pure functions (`evaluatePermissionFlow`, `needsConfirmation`, `isPlanModeBlocked`, `isAutoEditApproved`) plus 17 test cases pinning the decision matrix — `evaluatePermissionFlow` correctly handles four PM states (deny/has-rules-denies/no-rules/ask-rule), `needsConfirmation` correctly special-cases `ToolNames.ASK_USER_QUESTION` to always confirm even in YOLO mode, `isPlanModeBlocked` correctly exempts info-type / exit_plan_mode / ask_user_question tools, and `isAutoEditApproved` correctly auto-approves only `'edit'` and `'info'` confirmation types in AUTO_EDIT mode (NOT `'exec'` — exactly the bug class that ungated AUTO_EDIT modes have shipped in other agents) — but the tracking marker file `TOOL_EXECUTION_UNIFICATION.md` at repo root contains literal `\n\n` escape sequences instead of real newlines (single-line shell-heredoc smell), and the PR title oversells what landed (no callers wired up yet — Interactive/Non-Interactive/ACP migration is follow-up work).

### W18 drip-165 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (1 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "the bug class is the implicit dispatch — silent param drop on GET, silent fallback to the wrong config layer, silent ambiguity-pick on hallucinated tool names, silent env-leak through unstubbed test setup, silent serve-the-cached-response when the SDK asked for a stream resume" — sst/opencode #24853 lifts the Hono-vs-Effect-HttpApi backend selection out of a one-line `Flag.OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_HTTPAPI ? createHttpApi() : createHono({})` ternary into a typed `Selection { backend, reason }` value at `packages/opencode/src/server/backend.ts:609-647`, with an attribute bag (`opencode.server.backend`, `opencode.server.backend.reason`, `opencode.installation.channel`, `opencode.installation.version`) threaded through `LoggerMiddleware(backendAttributes)` at `middleware.ts:667-688` so every request log carries the backend that served it (also folds the dual `log.info`+`log.time` path into a single timed span, which incidentally fixes a zombie-timer leak in the old `if (!skip) timer.stop()` pattern), splits HttpApi composition into `groups/{global,metadata,provider,pty,session,sync,tui,workspace}.ts` route contracts plus `handlers/*` implementations mirroring how Hono's `routes/instance/*` already factors, ships `scripts/diff-sdk-types.sh` (executable, lines 1-58 of the diff) that normalizes the SDK type generation by sorting `export type|function|const` blocks alphabetically and prints `--stat` summary of Hono-only vs HttpApi-only types — load-bearing parity tool that should be wired into CI as advisory before becoming blocking on the deletion phase, also tightens schemas (`Schema.Number → Schema.Finite` for `topP`/`temperature`/`steps` at `agent/agent.ts:88-99` so `NaN`/`±Infinity` can no longer round-trip as null on the JSON wire, `Schema.Number → NonNegativeInt` for `Oauth.expires` at `auth/index.ts:119` so a malformed negative-expires OAuth blob fails at load instead of silently being a "valid" past timestamp); codex #20144 fixes three call sites in `codex-rs/cli/src/external_agent_config.rs` (lines 252 migration write path, 569 per-tool hook lookup, 636 post-migration verification read) that were calling raw `read_external_settings(home, project)` instead of the layered `effective_external_settings(home, project)` — the migration write path is the highest-risk because reading from the wrong layer persists the wrong content into the migrated location which then becomes the new authoritative source, so the fix is not just "your local override didn't apply" but also "your local override won't get clobbered by the migration"; codex #20139 deletes the `interrupt: bool` parameter from `multi_agent_v2`'s `followup_task` tool schema (-420 lines, +3 lines: schema delete, schema-test assertion `!properties.contains_key("interrupt")` at line 524, contract comment) on the grounds that the dispatch logic was inherently racy across {running, completed, awaiting-tool-result, errored} states — load-bearing nit is the simultaneous deletion of `send_message_rejects_interrupt_parameter` test, leaving only static schema-absence enforcement and no runtime test that a model-emitted stale `interrupt` field gets predictably handled (depends on whether the deserializer is `serde(deny_unknown_fields)` or not, unverified); litellm #26750 fixes a silent payload drop on `GET /v1/responses/{id}?stream=true&starting_after=N` — the OpenAI Python SDK encodes cursor-based stream resume as query params on a GET, but the proxy ingress at `litellm/proxy/proxy_server.py:400-422` was doing `data = await _read_request_body(request)` (no-op for GET) and never reading `request.query_params`, so resume was silently demoted to a cached non-stream response; fix reads `stream` (boolean from `("true","1")`) and `starting_after` (int with explicit 400 on non-int) off the query params and merges into `data`, threads through provider transforms with the right wire convention (`data["stream"] = "true"` as string for Azure compatibility on api-version 2025-04-01-preview, base-class signature change forces every transform subclass to either propagate or accept-and-ignore — Volcengine and Manus accept-and-ignore by contract per test at lines 733-749), with sync `get()` adapting `httpx.TimeoutException → litellm.Timeout` but async `get()` not doing the same — sync/async exception-handling asymmetry is the one real concern; gemini-cli #26158 adds two-stage tool-name repair to `Scheduler` at `packages/core/src/scheduler/scheduler.ts:166-207` — stage 1 normalizes kebab-to-snake (`read-file → read_file` via `replaceAll('-','_')`) and only triggers a registry lookup when the string changed, stage 2 fuzzy-matches via `getClosestMatch` (Levenshtein distance ≤ 2, fast-levenshtein lib) with explicit ambiguity rejection (`matches.length > 1 → isAmbiguous: true, repairedName: undefined`) so two equidistant candidates fail rather than coin-flip, plus a 64-char security cap on input length to prevent CPU exhaustion on adversarial names, repair also applied on the followup-tool-call rerun path at line 817-825 to avoid fix-once-fail-forever, `originalRequestName` preserved on the request via `enrichedRequest.originalRequestName = enrichedRequest.originalRequestName || request.name` so audit logs show the model's first mistake not intermediate states; gemini-cli #26162 is +5/-0 test-isolation hardening adding `vi.stubEnv('ANTIGRAVITY_CLI_ALIAS', '')` and `vi.stubEnv('GEMINI_CLI_HOME', tempDir)` to three test suites' `beforeEach` so a developer or CI runner with these env vars set doesn't leak ambient config through the `mockHomedir.mockReturnValue(tempDir)` mock; qwen-code #3720 unifies the BackgroundTasksDialog overlay across subagent and managed-shell registries via a `DialogEntry = (BackgroundTaskEntry & { kind: 'agent' }) | (BackgroundShellEntry & { kind: 'shell' })` discriminated union at `useBackgroundTaskView.ts:711-713` (inline shape preservation keeps existing renderer code untouched on the agent branch, just adds a `kind === 'agent'` guard), merges both registries in `refresh()` sorted by `startTime` with cleanup unsetting both `setStatusChangeCallback`s, dispatches cancel through the right registry per kind (`cancel(agentId)` for agents, `requestCancel(shellId)` for shells — the asymmetry is intentional and matches the `task_stop` tool path from #3687, lets the spawn's settle path record the real terminal moment instead of letting the dialog mark a still-running OS process as cancelled), pins no-op semantics on `requestCancel` against already-terminal entries via the test at `backgroundShellRegistry.test.ts:832` (`reg.requestCancel('a'); // already terminal — also no fire`), retypes `STATUS_VERBS` and `terminalStatusPresentation` to `EntryStatus = DialogEntry['status']` on the comment-only assumption that the shell status union equals the agent status union (both are `'running'|'completed'|'failed'|'cancelled'`) — recommended `satisfies Record<DialogEntry['status'], string>` to compile-check the assumption; goose #8897 extracts skill-path helpers (`normalizePath`, `basename`, `getSkillFileLocation`, `deriveProjectRoot`, `getRenamedSkillFileLocation`) into `lib/skillsPath.ts`, hoists `isValidSkillName` / `formatSkillName` (`MAX_SKILL_NAME_LENGTH = 64`, kebab-only, mirrors Rust `validate_skill_name`) into `lib/skillsHelpers.ts` with a comment-only "keep in sync with the Rust rule" guarantee that's exactly the kind of cross-language drift hazard that only fixture-based parity tests fix mechanically, splits `SkillsToolbar` and `SkillCategoryFilter` out of `SkillsView.tsx`, replaces the bespoke bottom-right notification `<div>` with sonner toasts (good UX, but loses the `console.error(err)` calls on caught exceptions — silent debuggability regression worth re-adding), folds `importInputRef` into the shared `useFileImportZone` hook now exposing `openFilePicker`. Verdict mix: 7 `merge-after-nits`, 1 `merge-as-is`. The `merge-as-is` is gemini-cli #26162 (pure test-env hardening, no production code change). All seven `merge-after-nits` have the same pattern: code is correct, the nits are observability/documentation/cross-language-parity gaps that don't block merge but compound if left unfixed.

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (opencode #24898 surgical `tail_start_id` remap through `idMap` after fork with discriminating test that asserts the remapped id actually points to a message in the child's id space), 7 merge-after-nits (codex #20141 release-note the per-thread `experimental_thread_store` override no longer being respected after the ownership hoist + scope `Default for ManagedFeatures` to `#[cfg(test)]`/`#[cfg(feature = "sample")]` since `source: None` loses telemetry provenance + extract `RolloutConfig::for_tests(codex_home)` helper instead of inline-building + README callout for the sample crate's role as a public-surface canary; codex #20136 derive `--interactive-label-secondary-default` from existing gray tokens instead of redeclaring per-mode + add narrow-viewport media query for the fixed wordmark + `rg success.html` to confirm no test fixtures pin asset bytes; litellm #26746 add the missing unit test covering the four `(isAdmin, usageView)` combinations of `effectiveUserId` resolver + verify no downstream "showing data for X" header reads `selectedUserId` directly which would mislabel admin's data as "all users" in my-usage mode + pick one screenshot storage path; litellm #26739 verify and delete the source-side Next.js `app/chat/` route otherwise this fix has a half-life of one rebuild + add a one-line `assert client.get("/ui/chat").status_code == 404` regression test + gitignore `litellm/proxy/_experimental/out/chat*`; gemini-cli #26163 use `AUTO_ROUTING_OVERRIDES` in the preview branch at `policyCatalog.ts:97-110` instead of inlining the same fragment to eliminate drift risk + confirm `geminiChat.test.ts` actually has a Pro→Flash mid-turn fallback test asserting Flash gets its full 10 attempts + replace duplicated string literals in `errorClassification.ts` with `static readonly NAME` constants for rename-safety + release-note the explicit-preview behavioral shift; goose #8896 audit `ProjectIcon.tsx` to confirm SVG icons render via `<img src={dataUrl}>` only and never via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`/`<object>` (with DOMPurify if inlining is needed for currentColor theming) + surface the StatusBar removal in PR body and CHANGELOG instead of bundling the feature deletion under "polish" + add unit tests for `ProjectIconPicker` and `ProjectIcon` shared components; qwen-code #3723 fix `TOOL_EXECUTION_UNIFICATION.md` literal `\n\n` escape sequences or move under `docs/refactor/` or delete in favor of Issue #3247 + refine PR title to reflect that this lands the primitive not the unified callers + migrate `isPlanModeBlocked`'s positional-boolean signature to a flags-object before more tools need plan-mode exemptions), 0 needs-discussion, 0 request-changes. Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).

### W18 drip-166 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (1 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "the bug class is silent state drift across async boundaries — a Session.list() that scans archived rows because the WHERE clause never carried the archived predicate, a reconnect state machine that flipped `_engine_confirmed_dead = False` *before* awaiting the heavy reconnect (so a concurrent caller could short-circuit straight back into the dead engine), a remote-plugin cache that kept stale entries past account-switch, a parallel provider-resolution `tokio::spawn` fan-out whose `.filter_map(|r| r.ok().and_then(...).flatten())` silently swallows both `JoinError` and per-provider `Err`, and a 'remove unsafe exec()' PR whose diff actually keeps exec() in place and only adds a deny-list regex `/[;&|\`$<>()\n\r\\\\]/` that misses whitespace/quotes/globs/backticks-in-args" — sst/opencode #24907 adds `archived = false` to `Session.list()`'s SQL predicate at `packages/opencode/src/session/index.ts` with a test that asserts an archived row created via `Session.archive()` does not appear in the next `list()` call (one production line + ~70 lines of test scaffolding, the kind of one-liner that should ship with exactly this kind of regression test); litellm #26756 is the load-bearing one — it moves `self._engine_confirmed_dead = False` from *before* the `await self._do_heavy_reconnect()` call to *after* it at `proxy/utils.py:4204-4242`, closing a window where a second concurrent read path would observe `_engine_confirmed_dead = False` while the heavy reconnect was still in flight and re-enter the dead-engine code path, plus adds `db/exception_handler.py` (+135 lines) wrapping all Prisma read paths with retry-on-disconnect and 380 lines of expanded tests covering the concurrent-reentry scenario; codex #20148 lands a fresh `core-plugins/src/remote_cache.rs` (181 lines + 291 lines of tests) gating cache entries on `installed`-not-`enabled` so disabled-but-installed remote plugins survive an account switch (correct semantics — disabling is not uninstalling), wired through `message_processor` and exercised by integration tests in `tests/suite/v2/account.rs`; codex #20149 hoists request input mode gating into the tool layer (net −150 across app-server/core/tools/models-manager/prompt_debug), removing the duplicated mode-check call sites; litellm #26754 plumbs client-supplied `timeout` through `/v1/messages` to the underlying httpx call (~40 lines of production plumbing in 2 files + ~200 lines of focused tests), closing a silent-default-fallback where the client's timeout was being dropped before httpx ever saw it; qwen-code #3727 is a chore deleting tool-token-usage tracking across 22 files (+1/−80, telemetry/types/loggers + tests + UI + docs) — clean removal but worth confirming no downstream dashboard or eval pipeline still reads the dropped fields; gemini-cli #26169 (request-changes) is the title-vs-diff mismatch noted above plus an incomplete deny-list at `packages/a2a-server/src/http/app.ts` — title claims "remove unsafe exec()" but exec() is untouched and the added 12-line validation block uses `/[;&|\`$<>()\n\r\\\\]/` which lets through whitespace splitting, quote escapes, glob chars, and backtick-in-quoted-args; goose #8899 (request-changes) parallelizes provider resolution via `tokio::spawn` + `JoinAll` at `crates/goose/src/providers/inventory/mod.rs` and eagerly inits the SQLite pool in `acp/server.rs`, but the result-collection uses `.filter_map(|r| r.ok().and_then(|inner| inner.ok()).flatten())` which silently drops both task-level `JoinError` (panic-in-task surfaces as missing provider, no log) and per-provider `Err` (config error surfaces as missing provider, no log) — behavioral regression from the prior sequential `.await?` that propagated both, fix is to log-and-skip explicitly rather than `filter_map` away.

| [#8899](https://github.com/block/goose/pull/8899) | perf: parallelize provider resolution and eagerly init SQLite pool | [2026-W18/drip-166/block-goose-pr-8899.md](2026-W18/drip-166/block-goose-pr-8899.md) |
| [#3727](https://github.com/QwenLM/qwen-code/pull/3727) | chore(core): drop tool token usage tracking | [2026-W18/drip-166/QwenLM-qwen-code-pr-3727.md](2026-W18/drip-166/QwenLM-qwen-code-pr-3727.md) |

Verdict mix: 0 merge-as-is, 6 merge-after-nits (opencode #24907 add a second test that creates two sessions, archives one, asserts list() returns exactly the unarchived one + audit other `Session.*` query helpers like `Session.children()` and `Session.recent()` for the same `archived` predicate gap + release-note that archived sessions were leaking into list views; codex #20148 add a test asserting that re-installing a previously-disabled-then-uninstalled plugin does not resurrect stale cache entries from before the uninstall + document the `installed`-vs-`enabled` distinction in `core-plugins/README.md` since the next contributor will reach for `enabled` first; codex #20149 add a regression test pinning that `mode_of(tool_name)` returns the same gating decision the call-site previously made for each of the deleted call-sites + benchmark hot-path latency to confirm the centralization didn't add per-call HashMap lookups; litellm #26756 add an explicit comment at `proxy/utils.py:4204-4242` explaining why `_engine_confirmed_dead = False` must be set *after* the await (otherwise the next contributor will "simplify" it back) + add a stress test with N concurrent readers hitting the dead-engine path simultaneously to confirm the state-machine fix holds under load + verify all existing `_engine_confirmed_dead` write sites follow the same after-await ordering; litellm #26754 add a test asserting that an explicit `timeout=0.001` from the client surfaces as `httpx.TimeoutException` rather than the default 600s + document the timeout-precedence chain (client param > config default > httpx default) in the `/v1/messages` handler docstring; qwen-code #3727 grep external dashboards/eval pipelines for the dropped telemetry field names before merge + add a CHANGELOG entry under "Removed" so downstream consumers know the field is gone, not renamed), 2 request-changes (gemini-cli #26169 title says "remove unsafe exec()" but the diff at `packages/a2a-server/src/http/app.ts` keeps exec() in place and only adds a 12-line validation block — either remove exec() and replace with `execFile`/`spawn` with arg arrays as the title promises, or rename the PR to "harden exec() input validation" and broaden the deny-list to also reject whitespace, single/double quotes, glob chars `*?[]{}`, escaped backticks in quoted args, and null bytes; the current regex `/[;&|\`$<>()\n\r\\\\]/` is bypassable via `--flag="$(evil)"` quoting and via glob expansion on a permissive shell; goose #8899 the result-collection at `crates/goose/src/providers/inventory/mod.rs` uses `.filter_map(|r| r.ok().and_then(|inner| inner.ok()).flatten())` which silently drops `JoinError` (task panic → missing provider, no log) and per-provider `Err` (config error → missing provider, no log) — replace with explicit `match` that `tracing::warn!`s the JoinError and per-provider Err with provider name before skipping, otherwise this is a behavioral regression from the prior sequential `.await?` path that propagated both error classes to the caller), 0 needs-discussion. Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).

### W18 drip-167 (2026-04-29)

9 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "the bug class is silent contract overload across surface boundaries — an Edit tool whose `oldString: ""` means both 'create new file' and 'clobber existing file' depending on filesystem state, two ripgrep-backed search tools that hang forever when the model emits `path: '/'` because the only escape hatch is a session-level abort that never fires in autopilot loops, an OpenAI Responses translator that silently passes through `max_tokens=1` to a provider that 400s on anything below 16, a Bedrock 400 'extra inputs not permitted' caused by `client_metadata` leaking through `**kwargs` whose centralized fix risks over-stripping the field from OpenAI where it's legitimately accepted, a system-prompt bullet that hardcodes POSIX `&&`-chaining and breaks every Windows PowerShell user, a desktop model-picker whose 'open re-fetches inventory' was a silent recovery path that the polish PR removes without replacement, and a DeepSeek thinking-mode endpoint that emits assistant tool-call turns without `reasoning_content` then 400s when those same turns are replayed back" — opencode #24913 narrows Edit's `oldString: ""` semantics to non-existent-files-only at `packages/opencode/src/tool/edit.ts:88-95` with a flipped behavioral test asserting the throw + content-preserved path; opencode #24910 wires `AbortSignal.any([ctx.abort, AbortSignal.timeout(30_000)])` into glob and grep with the discriminating `() => timeoutSignal.aborted && !ctx.abort.aborted` predicate so user aborts still propagate as failures while internal-deadline expiries return a model-actionable "path too broad" message; codex #20150 lands a new `plugin/skill/read` app-server method (+466 lines schema + handler + integration test) returning `{ contents: string | null }` for remote-marketplace skills before local install — the `null` ambiguity needs disambiguation between not-found / disabled / fetch-failed; codex #20147 carves managed-network-proxy startup off the sandbox-network bit by adding a configurable `[features.network_proxy]` table with `mode: limited|full`, three `dangerously_*` fields, and per-domain/unix-socket permission tables, plus reshapes CLI `--enable network_proxy` to emit `features.network_proxy.enabled=true` instead of replacing the whole table; litellm #26758 clamps `max_tokens` to `max(max_tokens, 16)` at the Anthropic→Responses adapter to dodge the OpenAI Responses 400 — silent-clamp without a debug log or response-header surface so callers expecting 1-token output get billed for 16; litellm #26753 adds `client_metadata` to `filter_internal_params._internal_params` to fix a Bedrock `additionalModelRequestFields` 400, but `client_metadata` is a *forwarding* field on OpenAI Responses (used for cost-allocation tagging) so the global strip will silently break OpenAI users; gemini-cli #26174 deletes the "Combine shell commands whenever possible, e.g. `git status && git diff HEAD && git log -n 3`" bullet from both `snippets.ts:499` and `snippets.legacy.ts:368` to fix Windows PowerShell `&&`-not-found errors, with the right empirical-grounded rationale that no amount of "but on Windows use `;`" prompting overrides the original instruction in context; goose #8900 fixes four model-picker startup papercuts (loading-flicker, button disabled while label known, redundant per-open inventory refetch, bare-spinner dropdown) at `ui/goose2/src/features/chat/ui/AgentModelPicker.tsx` net −16 — the per-open refetch removal closes a silent recovery path for users whose startup-time inventory fetch failed transiently; qwen-code #3729 fixes a DeepSeek round-trip asymmetry where the endpoint omits `reasoning_content` on tool-call rounds it didn't reason for, then rejects the next request when that omission is replayed — fix is provider-scoped at `packages/core/src/core/openaiContentGenerator/provider/deepseek.ts` with a three-condition gate (assistant role + non-empty tool_calls + missing/empty reasoning_content) injecting `reasoning_content: ''`, corroborated against openclaw/openclaw@678ed5d as the same workaround in production.

| [#3729](https://github.com/QwenLM/qwen-code/pull/3729) | fix(core): inject reasoning_content on DeepSeek tool-call replays | [2026-W18/drip-167/QwenLM-qwen-code-pr-3729.md](2026-W18/drip-167/QwenLM-qwen-code-pr-3729.md) |

Verdict mix: 0 merge-as-is, 7 merge-after-nits (opencode #24913 add the symmetric `creates file when oldString is empty and file does not exist` test in the same describe block, throw via the project's structured tool-error class so the model treats this as fixable input rather than a defect, update `edit.txt` so capable models stop emitting the bad shape, and CHANGELOG the regressed-to-error case for any prompt template relying on the clobber surface; opencode #24910 replace the abort-immediately tests with timeout-fires tests using a slow fake `rg` since the new tests assert the user-abort path not the timeout path the PR adds, make `SEARCH_TIMEOUT_MS` env-overridable, extract `withSearchTimeout` + `formatTimeoutMessage` helpers to dedupe glob and grep, and add a one-line comment at the `"output" in result` structural-typing discriminator; codex #20150 disambiguate `contents: string | null` into a discriminated union covering not-found / disabled / fetch-failed or document the single null meaning explicitly, add a `_when_remote_plugin_disabled` negative integration test, add a short-TTL in-process cache keyed by `(marketplace, plugin, skill, version)` for the modal-reopen pattern, and add an `ErrorCode::PluginSkillReadFailed` variant; codex #20147 add a `dangerously_*` checklist comment near `NetworkProxyConfigToml`, TODO refactoring `features_schema` into a registry once a third configurable feature appears, round-trip test that applies the `.enabled=` override and asserts the rest of the table survives, Levenshtein "did you mean" suggestion for unknown-feature CLI errors, and JSON-Schema `description` on `NetworkProxyModeToml` enum variants; litellm #26758 add `verbose_logger.debug` when the clamp fires so it's traceable, promote `16` to a named `OPENAI_RESPONSES_MIN_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS` constant, decide explicitly whether `max_tokens=0` should clamp or pass through, and grep for other `max_output_tokens` write sites in the Anthropic pass-through; gemini-cli #26174 either replace the deletion with a positive POSIX-shell guidance bullet ("On POSIX shells prefer `&&` to short-circuit on first failure") or branch on `process.platform` so Windows gets `;` guidance and POSIX gets `&&`, plus add a snapshot test against `renderGitRepo({...})` asserting the rendered output does not contain `"&&"`; goose #8900 either reintroduce a guarded `if (inventory.length === 0)` open-time fetch or add a manual retry affordance in the empty state since the per-open refetch removal closes a silent recovery path, provide a `t("toolbar.selectModel")` fallback for the empty + not-loading button label, and verify close-popover behavior with the disabled-current-model row; qwen-code #3729 use `.trim().length > 0` in the preserve-guard and add tests for the `null` / whitespace-only cases, add a multi-tool-call (3 sequential calls) regression test, audit compression / session-resume paths for other places that rebuild `tool_calls` messages, and optionally export `ensureReasoningContentOnToolCalls` for direct unit testing), 1 needs-discussion (litellm #26753 — `client_metadata` is documented OpenAI Responses forwarding field used for cost-allocation tagging, so the global strip in `filter_internal_params` will silently break OpenAI users who depend on the field reaching their dashboard; right shape is per-provider allow/deny list, not a global `_internal_params` set addition), 0 request-changes. Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).

### W18 drip-168 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 sst/opencode, 1 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "the bug class is the dual source of truth that drifted across an async/lifecycle boundary" — opencode #24877 fixes session routes resolving directory from request `?directory=` instead of the session's own recorded `directory`, with the load-bearing knock-on that the TUI was filtering sync events by `event.directory === project.instance.directory()` so events emitted under the requesting client's CWD were silently dropped by the workspace-attached TUI; opencode #24871 fixes opencode silently dying when an MCP server's `<server>_<tool>` exceeds OpenAI's 64-char `function.name` cap by introducing `buildToolName` with truncation + 8-char `Hash.fast` suffix at `packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts:117-135`, with discriminating tests at `test/mcp/lifecycle.test.ts:786-862` asserting both length-cap and collision-avoidance via `new Set(keys).size === 2` on two tool names sharing the first 55 chars; codex #20155 (slice 2 of 6) retires the test-only `EventMsg::McpStartupUpdate`/`McpStartupComplete` event variants in favor of consuming MCP startup status through the production `handle_server_notification(ServerNotification::McpServerStatusUpdated, ...)` path, with the test migration introducing `notify_mcp_status` / `notify_mcp_status_error` helpers that build real `McpServerStatusUpdatedNotification`s — clean dead-code removal that pulls the test path onto the same notification surface production already uses; litellm #26735 fixes the dashboard teams-table page resetting on every external prefetch by deleting both `teams` and `setTeams` props from `OldTeams`'s call site at `page.tsx:535-537` and from the component's public API, leaving `useTeams()` (paginated `teamListCall(page=1, page_size=10)`) as the single source of truth, with the test migration replacing direct prop injection with `mockTeamsResponse(teams)` helper that stubs `teamListCall` (right shape, but `mockResolvedValueOnce` is fragile to react-query refetch); litellm #26730 adds opt-in TCP `SO_KEEPALIVE` support to aiohttp's `TCPConnector` via the `socket_factory` kwarg (added in aiohttp 3.10) to fix long provider responses (≥350s idle) being NAT-reaped by AWS NAT Gateway and similar before the response arrives — with runtime aiohttp feature detection via `inspect.signature(TCPConnector.__init__).parameters` (correct way to feature-detect without version-pinning), four env-tunable knobs (`AIOHTTP_SO_KEEPALIVE`, `AIOHTTP_TCP_KEEPIDLE/INTVL/CNT`), Linux-vs-Darwin `TCP_KEEPIDLE`/`TCP_KEEPALIVE` constant handling at `_build_aiohttp_keepalive_socket_factory:71-84`, and wiring into both per-request transport and shared-session paths — but tests assert on mocked `TCPConnector` kwargs not on real-socket `getsockopt(SOL_SOCKET, SO_KEEPALIVE) == 1`, and Windows silently doesn't honor the per-knob timing; gemini-cli #26159 adds `MAX_TOKENS` auto-continuation to `LegacyAgentProtocol.processCommand` capped at 3 retries, threading `finishReason` capture through the stream consumer at `legacy-agent-session.ts:227-235` and gating the post-loop `_finishStream` on `finishReason !== FinishReason.MAX_TOKENS` so the loop falls through to the new continuation branch that injects a synthetic user message `[System: Your previous response was truncated due to length. Please continue exactly where you left off.]` with `_meta: { isContinuation: true }`, with the discriminating test asserting 4 messages (start / part 1 / continuation prompt / part 2) and the cap test asserting `sendMock.toHaveBeenCalledTimes(4)` on 5 consecutive `MAX_TOKENS` returns — but the prompt is a hardcoded English literal not a `Config` knob, `maxContinuations = 3` is hardcoded too, the give-up path emits no UI hint, and the unused `Usage.isContinuation?: boolean` field is added but never populated; goose #8893 migrates `GooseAcpAgent` from per-instance `Config::new(config_dir.join(CONFIG_YAML_NAME), "goose")` re-reads to `Config::global()` (process-wide `OnceCell` snapshot), deleting the `config_dir` field, the `load_config()`/`config()` helpers, and one full level of indent off the inventory-refresh ladder at `acp/server.rs:900-1003` — net -110 lines with the load-bearing semantic shift that `config.yaml` edits now require a server restart (previously each `on_new_session` re-read disk), trading a per-session live-reload UX for fail-at-boot production semantics, but no tests added and the `config_dir` parameter still survives as a misleading name (used only by `PermissionManager::new` at `:847`); qwen-code #3717 introduces a session-scoped `FileReadCache` keyed on `(dev, ino)` with stats validation (`mtimeMs` + `size`) and the discriminating semantics that "same path, different inode" (rm + recreate) returns `unknown`-not-`stale` — pinned by an explicit test asserting the safer "must Read first" path at `fileReadCache.test.ts:226-238` — wired into `Config` with cache-clear on `startNewSession()` (the load-bearing correctness invariant — the `file_unchanged` placeholder relies on the model having seen the prior read in the *current* conversation, so /clear and resume MUST drop entries) and a `fileReadCacheDisabled` escape hatch documented as for sessions undergoing context compaction or transcript transformation, with 420 lines of test coverage hitting every transition in the matrix.

### W18 drip-169 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "extend an existing config/protocol surface and watch the seams" — opencode #24914 lays the `apiMode` plumbing (treat `"api": "chat"|"responses"` as an SDK method-call selector rather than a base URL, then rewrite the outbound body for GPT-5.x via gateway: `max_tokens → max_completion_tokens` and inline `$ref` in tool params) at `provider.ts:1165-1574` with the load-bearing `iife` URL fallthrough that prevents `model.api.url` from being silently set to the literal string `"chat"`, plus the silent-SDK-downgrade hazard when `typeof sdk.chat !== "function"`; opencode #24919 layers a Gemini-specific `reasoning_effort → thinking_config` translation onto that same body-rewriter, with the semantic-loss bug `medium → "LOW"` in the level map and the loose `body.model.includes("gemini")` substring detection that fires on `"my-gemini-tuned"` and misses gateway-renamed slugs; codex #20179 (slice 7/7 of the TUI-from-core severance series) tightens `verify_tui_core_boundary.py` to also reject `codex_protocol::protocol`, but the second regex `[^}\n]` class won't match across rustfmt's multi-line `use codex_protocol::{\n    protocol,\n};` braced-import expansion, so `cargo fmt` becomes an attack vector against the new boundary rule; codex #20095 introduces `ActivePermissionProfile` + `ActivePermissionProfileModification` v2 wire types alongside `permission_profile: None, active_permission_profile: None` defaults in the analytics test fixtures (silently dropping the previously-exercised `Some(sample_permission_profile())` test coverage of the populated path), ships a forward-leaning `extends: null` field documented as "currently always null", weakens v1 client guidance from "should use `permissionProfile`" to "experimental clients should prefer", and quietly deletes `FileSystemAccessMode` / `FileSystemPath` from `ThreadForkParams.json` — a breaking change buried under a metadata-exposure title; litellm #26761 is the one-line npm-config fix `min-release-age=3d → min-release-age=3` because npm 11 rejects the `3d` suffix as invalid and was silently treating the field as unset (collapsing the supply-chain delay window to zero on local installs); gemini-cli #26179 adds `/directory remove <path[,path...]>` mirroring `/directory add`, with cwd-protection via `path.resolve()` string compare (misses `/private/var ↔ /var` symlinks on macOS and case-folded paths on case-insensitive FS), restrictive-sandbox blocked, and ~180 lines of test coverage that doesn't exercise "removed the last writable workspace root" or symlink edge cases; goose #8901 adds `LLAMA_STATIC_CRT: '1'` to the env block of the Windows CUDA build path in both `build-cli.yml` and `bundle-desktop-windows.yml` so the bundled `llama.cpp` statically links MSVCRT and the resulting CUDA `.exe` runs on machines without VC++ redistributables, with the empty-string-for-non-CUDA-variant hinging on `llama.cpp`'s `if(DEFINED ENV{LLAMA_STATIC_CRT})` treating `""` as unset (correct for upstream, fragile if the pinned fork checks string-equality to `1`/`ON`); qwen-code #3733 adds multi-select to `SessionPicker` for batch `/delete`, with three new optional props (`enableMultiSelect`, `onConfirmMulti`, `disabledIds`) where `isChecked === undefined` cleanly preserves single-select callers' visual layout, the `disabledIds` set protects the current session from batch deletion (but not against another `qwen` instance holding the same session open), and a JSDoc-vs-rendered-hint mismatch ("Tab toggles" in the prop docs vs "Space to toggle" in the footer) needs to be reconciled before merge.

### W18 drip-170 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 sst/opencode, 1 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "extend a known-good helper to a sibling surface that was either silently broken or hand-rolled the wrong shape" — opencode #24923 ports Claude Code's `apiKeyHelper` pattern as `apiKeyCommand` on the provider, `Bun.spawnSync(["sh","-c", apiKeyCommand], { timeout: 10_000 })` with a 5-min in-memory TTL cache and a `try {} catch {}` swallow that silently falls back to a possibly-401'd cached value, plus unconditional dual-header injection (`Authorization: Bearer` + `x-api-key`) regardless of provider shape; opencode #24921 trades the 2-line `bun install --os="*" --cpu="*"` wildcard for a `for (const { os, arch } of allTargets)` loop with per-iteration `console.log` — correct shape but the PR body's "How did you verify" is left blank and the version of the package being installed isn't in the log line; codex #20180 surgically excludes multi-agent v2 from the legacy `agents.max_depth` guard by adding `&& !config.features.enabled(Feature::MultiAgentV2)` to the existing `depth >= config.agent_max_depth` check at both `agent/control.rs:521` and `session/mod.rs:489`, deleting the explicit `exceeds_thread_spawn_depth_limit` rejection from `multi_agents_v2/spawn.rs:45`, and pinning the change with a discriminating `multi_agent_v2_spawn_agent_ignores_configured_max_depth` test that sets `agent_max_depth = 1` AND `depth = 1` AND enables `Feature::MultiAgentV2` then asserts success — exactly the test shape that proves the v1 guard would have fired; litellm #26766 fixes a real Azure auth bug where `image_edit/transformation.py` was hardcoded to `Authorization: Bearer ${api_key}` regardless of whether the caller passed an API key (which Azure rejects with 401 — Azure expects the `api-key` header for API-key auth and `Authorization: Bearer` only for AD tokens), now correctly delegates to `BaseAzureLLM._base_validate_azure_environment` with three precedence-pinning tests (api-key alone uses `api-key` header, ad-token-provider alone uses `Authorization: Bearer`, both-provided lets api-key win); litellm #26763 is a 3-character typo fix in three identical log message sites in `caching/dual_cache.py` (`Excepton → Exception`); gemini-cli #26131 bundles three orthogonal metrics-script fixes — replacing `gh issue list --limit 1000 --json number --jq length` with a GraphQL `repository.issues(states: OPEN) { totalCount }` (the `--limit 1000` was silently capping a ~2400-issue backlog), refactoring 7 scripts from shell-interpolated GraphQL queries (`-f query='${query}'`) to stdin-fed (`-f query=@-` + `input: query`) to harden against a (currently zero-risk) injection vector if `GITHUB_OWNER`/`GITHUB_REPO` ever held a quote, adding `'COLLABORATOR'` alongside `['MEMBER', 'OWNER']` in the maintainer-association allowlist at `domain_expertise.ts:99` and `review_distribution.ts:43`, and crucially flipping `catch { console.log('open_issues,0'); }` to `catch (err) { process.stderr.write(err); process.exit(1); }` so failures surface loudly instead of silently reporting healthy-zero indistinguishably from broken-pipeline-zero; goose #8884 adds a single 133-line blog post under `documentation/blog/2026-04-29-computer-controller-peekaboo/` announcing the v1.26 Computer Controller rebuild on top of Peekaboo (macOS Accessibility-driven `see → click → type` loop using element IDs `B1`/`T3` instead of pixel coords) plus a hero PNG, with the structural gap that the post never explains how Peekaboo gets installed (bundled? `brew install steipete/peekaboo`? auto-fetched on first use?) and never mentions the macOS Accessibility-permission grant first-time users will hit; qwen-code #3736 is a 2428/-333 Phase-3 slash-command UX overhaul whose centerpiece is a 768-line bilingual (zh-CN-leaning) design doc that audits the current `SlashCommand` shape against each loader's actual fill-in behavior, then proposes extending `Suggestion` with 7 new optional fields (`source`/`sourceLabel`/`sourceBadge`/`argumentHint`/`matchedAlias`/`supportedModes`/`modelInvocable`), factoring shared display logic into a new `services/commandMetadata.ts` (explicitly not in loaders), reworking `Help.tsx` into a tabbed General/Commands/Custom Commands dialog, adding session-scoped (not disk-persisted) recently-used ranking, and enriching ACP `available_commands_update` with claimed backward compatibility — the design discipline is right but the PR is too big to review confidently as a single unit and the highest-blast-radius surface (`Help.tsx` rewrite) lacks per-tab snapshot tests in the diff.

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (litellm #26763 — pure 3-character log-message typo fix in three identical sites in `caching/dual_cache.py`, no behavioral change, no test impact, lowest-risk possible change), 7 merge-after-nits (opencode #24923 add a `log.warn` on `spawnSync` failure instead of silent swallow + treat non-zero exit code as failure rather than success-with-empty-stdout + extract `apiKeyCommandTtlMs` option since 5 min is wrong for both 15-min IAM tokens and 1-hour STS tokens + don't blindly inject both `Authorization: Bearer` and `x-api-key` since strict gateways 400 on unknown auth headers + add tests covering TTL respect / spawn-failure-cache-fallback / TTL expiry; opencode #24921 include the `@opentui/core` and `@parcel/watcher` versions in the per-iteration `console.log` line + add a one-line PR-body justification of why the wildcard form was wasteful or broken + confirm at least one CI job has the new log lines on a fresh run before squash-merge; codex #20180 add the symmetric "depth=100, max=1, v2-enabled → still success" test to prove no upper bound was silently introduced + add `// v2 uses task-path routing instead` comments at the two `&& !v2_enabled` mirror sites so the next contributor doesn't put the guard back + flag `agents.max_depth` as v1-only in the config docs + check whether `exceeds_thread_spawn_depth_limit` has any remaining callers post-merge; litellm #26766 add a fourth test for the eager `azure_ad_token` string (vs callable provider) path + add an ad-token-provider-raises propagation test + CHANGELOG note that Azure image edit now correctly uses `api-key` header (was previously sending API key in `Authorization: Bearer` which Azure 401'd) + verify managed identity / DefaultAzureCredential path works through the helper; gemini-cli #26131 document the metric-semantic change for COLLABORATOR inclusion in dashboards (one-time bump in maintainer-review counts) + extract `interface GraphQLError { message: string }` to replace the 7 `(e: any)` casts + add a unit test pinning the COLLABORATOR/CONTRIBUTOR boundary + audit any downstream consumer that relied on the old "exit 0 emit zero" failure path now exiting 1; goose #8884 verify the v1.26 release tag actually exists at the 2026-04-29 publication date or soften to "upcoming" + explain how Peekaboo gets installed (bundled vs `brew install steipete/peekaboo` vs auto-fetch) + add the macOS Accessibility-permission-grant first-run caveat + drop or qualify the "Windows/Linux fall back to shell-based automation" claim if the rebuild dropped that path + verify the OG hero PNG is reasonably sized and has no leaked path/hostname EXIF; qwen-code #3736 split the 2428/-333 PR into (a) `Suggestion` interface + `commandMetadata.ts` + argumentHint backfills, (b) `Help.tsx` tabbed rewrite, (c) ACP enrichment — each is reviewable on its own + add per-tab snapshot tests for the rewritten Help (General/Commands/Custom Commands + empty-Custom-Commands edge case) + pin a tie-breaking rule for recently-used ranking (alphabetical? insertion order?) + add an ACP integration test that decodes the new `availableCommands` payload using the OLD client schema to actually prove backward compat instead of asserting it + run new badge strings through the existing `t(...)` helper for i18n-readiness), 0 needs-discussion, 0 request-changes. Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).

### W18 drip-171 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "fail loud where the previous code failed silent, or stop dropping data the downstream contract requires" — opencode #24933 swaps a hardcoded `mime: "text/plain"` for `await Filesystem.mimeType(resolvedPath)` so `--file image.png` is finally tagged correctly for multimodal routing; opencode #24935 is the opposite-direction proposal — a 1448-line ASCII-pet + CAVA-audio widget bundled with two repo-root design docs and an external-plugin example, recommended for pure example-plugin form rather than core-TUI inclusion; codex #20176 mops up the last `codex_protocol::protocol::SessionSource` import in the TUI onboarding test module, but does it via `serde_json::from_value(json!("cli"))` — clever but brittle because a future `serde(rename_all)` change would only blow up at runtime; codex #20178 is the closing 1714-line deletion of the temporary `app_server_adapter.rs` bridge whose own header doc-comment said "should shrink and eventually disappear"; litellm #26764 generalizes the proxy multipart auto-decode beyond `metadata` to also cover `user_config` and JSON-array `tags`, with a back-compat carve-out that leaves bare-string `tags=production` untouched via a `value.lstrip().startswith(("{","["))` heuristic and two unit tests; litellm #26769 adds FuturMix as a named OpenAI-compatible provider but bundles two unrelated deletions in `provider_endpoints_support.json` (entire `charity_engine` entry + two `a2a`/`interactions` schema keys on a different provider) that need to be reverted before merge; gemini-cli #26184 makes `ChatRecordingService.deleteSession()` `throw new Error("No session file found for ...")` when the chats directory exists but no session matches the ID, replacing the old fail-open behavior that let the `/resume` browser silently desync from disk; qwen-code #3737 finishes the #3579 reasoning-content series by removing both `stripThoughtsFromHistory` and `stripThoughtsFromHistoryKeepRecent` helpers from `GeminiChat`, removing the rewind-path call in `AppContainer.tsx`, and conditionally appending a sentinel `{ text: ' ', thought: true }` part to the compression-ack model turn so DeepSeek-class converters see continuous reasoning_content across the compression boundary; goose #8781 converts most ACP server request handlers from inline-await to `cx.spawn(...)?` for true concurrent dispatch, but deliberately keeps `InitializeRequest` inline because it sets connection-scoped fs/terminal capabilities — the carve-out is correct but the per-session ordering question (`NewSessionRequest` followed by session-scoped traffic) needs an explicit answer.

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (codex #20178 — pure deletion of an obsolete 1714-line adapter the file's own doc-comment said "should disappear", subject to `cargo check --workspace` confirmation), 5 merge-after-nits (opencode #24933 add `run.test.ts` case for `--file foo.png` asserting `mime === "image/png"` plus pin unknown-extension fallback; codex #20176 replace `serde_json::from_value(json!("cli"))` with the direct app-server `SessionSource::Cli` constructor so a future rename fails at compile time not runtime; litellm #26764 add a malformed-JSON test pinning fail-loud + comment the prefix heuristic + grep callers for any remaining manual `json.loads(parsed_body["user_config"])`; gemini-cli #26184 add a `rg "deleteSession\("` confirmation that all callers handle the new rejection + add a regression test for the pre-existing "no chats directory at all" path so the two error cases stay distinct; qwen-code #3737 add a chatCompressionService test pinning both branches of `hasThoughtContentInKeep` + add a tombstone comment in `geminiChat.ts` saying "intentionally removed in #3737 — reasoning_content must remain contiguous"), 1 needs-discussion (goose #8781 — the per-connection inline→spawn switch is the right shape and the `InitializeRequest` carve-out shows the author understood the ordering constraint, but `NewSessionRequest` → subsequent session-scoped notifications becomes order-undefined under the new model and needs an explicit ACP-spec answer before landing), 2 request-changes (opencode #24935 ship the pet as the external `examples/terminal-pet/` plugin only, drop the in-tree `feature-plugins/sidebar/pet.tsx` and `spectrum.tsx`, move the two `PR-*.md` files out of repo root, and hard-gate the CAVA path on a runtime `which cava` check with snapshot test for no-cava fallback; litellm #26769 revert the unrelated `charity_engine` entry deletion and the `a2a`/`interactions` key removal — submit those separately if intentional — and confirm in PR body that FuturMix's API actually expects `max_tokens` not `max_completion_tokens`). Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).

### W18 drip-172 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "small surgical fixes + one big refactor slice" — opencode #24932 wires `client.onclose` to a new `dropConnection(s, name, client, reason)` helper at `mcp/index.ts:475-482,497-499` so dead MCP transports stop sitting in `s.clients[name]` indefinitely, with the load-bearing `s.clients[name] !== client` identity guard preventing a reconnected slot from being clobbered by a stale onclose; opencode #24930 is a one-line `nix/node_modules.nix:58` deletion of a stale `--filter './packages/shared'` pnpm filter that no longer matches any workspace and was breaking the Nix build on a clean checkout; codex #20172 opens the 7-PR TUI-from-core-protocol severance series with a pure module extraction — ~250 lines of MCP-startup state machine (`update_mcp_startup_status`, `finish_mcp_startup_after_lag`, `on_mcp_server_status_updated`, plus the lossy-delivery "ignore-mode buffers next round" rationale comment) lifted intact from `chatwidget.rs` into a new `chatwidget/mcp_startup.rs`, declared via `mod mcp_startup;` at `chatwidget.rs:389` and clearing four no-longer-needed imports at `chatwidget.rs:33,98,191,194`; codex #20175 is the load-bearing 4-of-7 slice (81 files, +5936/-8043) that swaps the TUI command/approval/realtime/skill/multi-agent/status/token-usage/diff/history/session-display paths over to `codex_app_server_protocol` types or private TUI display models, splits the dispatcher surface into `app/app_server_event_targets.rs` (+218), `app/app_server_events.rs` (+186), and `app/thread_events.rs`, and adds the `ResolvedAppServerRequest` enum at `app_server_requests.rs:44-72` so the TUI gets its own request-correlation type — the only verification claim is `cargo test -p codex-tui --no-run` which is too weak for the blast radius; litellm #26759 (despite the generic "Litellm oss staging" PR title) fixes a real Anthropic 400 by routing `data:application/pdf` and `data:text/plain` URIs in tool-result `image_url` blocks to the document-block code path via a new `_is_anthropic_document_data_uri` predicate at `prompt_templates/factory.py:1664-1675`, with the whitelist `{"application/pdf","text/plain"}` matching Anthropic's documented accepted set and the Union expansion to include `AnthropicMessagesDocumentParam` at lines ~1715-1734 keeping the type-checker honest; gemini-cli #25944 attacks the "Ctrl+L stops working after a model response" class with two fixes — an idle-state `useEffect` at `AppContainer.tsx:2178-2192` that re-arms Kitty/modifyOtherKeys/focus-reporting with a 50ms post-frame delay, plus a `KeypressContext.tsx:359-385` `createDataListener` rewrite that converts `Buffer` inputs to UTF-8 string up front (the `for (const char of buffer)` was silently iterating bytes-as-numbers) and tightens the parser-yield timeout so incomplete escape sequences flush instead of swallowing the next keypress; goose #8785 adds a "Location" column to `goose skills list` with three-way classification at `session/mod.rs:931-937` (`built-in` if `SourceType == BuiltinSkill`, else `global` if `skill.global`, else `project`), surfacing same-name shadowing that was previously invisible; qwen-code #3735 unifies subagent + main-agent compaction by moving the trigger into the chat layer, surfacing a new `compressed` stream event to subagent runtime at `agents/runtime/agent-core.ts:543-552`, and quietly changing the `findCompressSplitPoint` "trailing in-flight functionCall" expected split from `2` to `3` (a real heuristic shift the PR body underplays), with a default-NOOP `tryCompressChat` spy at `core/client.test.ts:407-415` migrating every existing chat-mock test in one go — pragmatic but it silences any future "compression should have fired" regression unless tests explicitly assert call count.

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (opencode #24930 — one-line deletion of a stale Nix filter for a workspace package that no longer exists; codex #20172 — pure module extraction with the lossy-delivery rationale comment preserved intact), 6 merge-after-nits (opencode #24932 gate the warn on shutdown + try/catch the redundant `client.close()` + add an `onclose → status=failed` unit test; codex #20175 strengthen verification beyond `--no-run`, ensure the boundary-enforcing CI script lands before merge, and add a CHANGELOG line for downstream forks; litellm #26759 rename the generic PR title, tighten the regex to require `;base64,`, confirm Bedrock document blocks accept `text/plain`, and add a CHANGELOG entry; gemini-cli #25944 comment the 50ms Ink-frame magic number + add a Buffer-input regression test for `createDataListener` + audit symmetric `?1004l` teardown for focus reporting; goose #8785 convert the `if/else if/else` location ladder to a `match` so future `SourceType` variants force a UX decision + add a 3-row golden test + stylistic tidy of `built-in`/`global`/`project`; qwen-code #3735 pin the new `findCompressSplitPoint` "in-flight fallback" branch with a second test shape + replace the default-NOOP `tryCompressChat` spy with explicit per-test opt-in once feasible + cite the removed eager pre-call site in the PR body + use a `"main"` sentinel when `subagentId` is empty so debug grep stays clean), 0 needs-discussion, 0 request-changes. Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).


### W18 drip-173 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 sst/opencode, 1 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme this drip is "small surgical bug-fixes + one security-flavored sandbox tightening" — opencode #24942 deletes the `!pending()` gates that were hiding tool subtitle/args/action and the collapsible arrow during the permission-prompt window at `basic-tool.tsx:148-187`, restoring full visibility of the very payload the user is being asked to approve while the title `TextShimmer` (line ~144) still carries the "in progress" affordance; opencode #24941 is a pure docs-only addition of a new "Skills" section above Plugins on `ecosystem.mdx:14-25` listing four community Skills with install path `~/.claude/skills/`, with the cosmetic risk that all four entries share the same author and the install-path note doesn't hand-hold OpenCode-only users; codex #20113 fixes a regression from #10941 where heredoc commands containing file redirects could be auto-amended into a prefix rule — the one-liner gate at `exec_policy.rs:271-281` now wraps `derive_requested_execpolicy_amendment_from_prefix_rule` behind `if auto_amendment_allowed` and four new scenario tests at `exec_policy_tests.rs:794-901` lock in (a) heredoc-fallback + matching prefix-rule = no amendment, (b) `PATH=/tmp/evil:$PATH cat <<'EOF'` cannot collapse to bare `cat`, (c) heredoc redirect without escalation runs sandboxed, (d) heredoc redirect with `RequireEscalated` still demands approval, plus two new `ActionKind` variants (`RunCommandWithPolicy`, `RunCommandWithPrefixRule`) at `tests/suite/approvals.rs:98-104` to wire the same scenarios into the integration matrix; litellm #26764 generalizes the existing `metadata`-only multipart JSON decode to also handle `user_config` and `tags` via a new `_parse_form_json_field` helper at `http_parsing_utils.py:16-22` with a `value.lstrip().startswith(("{", "["))` heuristic that lets plain strings like `tags="production"` survive untouched (verified by the new `test_form_data_with_plain_string_tags_is_left_unchanged`), but raw `json.loads` without a `JSONDecodeError` catch means a user who passes `tags="{oops"` now hits a 500; litellm #26730 adds opt-in `SO_KEEPALIVE` (`AIOHTTP_SO_KEEPALIVE` default `False`) plus platform-appropriate `TCP_KEEPIDLE/INTVL/CNT` tunables in `constants.py:227-235`, builds a `socket_factory` callable via `_build_aiohttp_keepalive_socket_factory()` at `http_handler.py:67-99` (using `hasattr(socket, "TCP_KEEPIDLE") elif hasattr(socket, "TCP_KEEPALIVE")` for cross-platform Linux/macOS handling and a module-load-time `_AIOHTTP_SUPPORTS_SOCKET_FACTORY = "socket_factory" in inspect.signature(TCPConnector.__init__).parameters` aiohttp version probe), wires it into both `_create_aiohttp_transport` and the proxy's shared session — the tests reach into `transport._client_factory()` (private) which is brittle; gemini-cli #26184 is a 9-line correctness fix where `ChatRecordingService.deleteSession()` now throws `No session file found for ${id}` at `chatRecordingService.ts:698-700` instead of silently resolving on empty-match, with the test at `chatRecordingService.test.ts:731-737` flipped from `resolves.not.toThrow` to `rejects.toThrow` — the `/resume` browser was treating the no-op as success and removing the row from UI state while the `.jsonl` stayed on disk; gemini-cli #26161 plumbs already-declared-but-never-honored `tools.shell.executable` and `tools.shell.args` settings through the standard five-layer chain (settings schema → `loadCliConfig` → `Config` → `ShellExecutionConfig` → `getShellConfiguration`) at `shell-utils.ts:649-668`, with PowerShell auto-detection across `powershell.exe`/`pwsh.exe`/`powershell`/`pwsh` and an `argsPrefix: overrideArgs || (isPowerShell ? ['-NoProfile','-Command'] : ['-c'])` default — but `[]` is truthy in JS so a user who explicitly sets `tools.shell.args: []` (the schema default!) will get an empty argsPrefix even for pwsh, and the path-suffix detection misses full paths like `/opt/ps/powershell/7/pwsh`; goose #8878 fixes two `Path::join`-of-untrusted-segment bugs in the same file (`github_recipe.rs`) — a new `temp_child_name()` sanitizer at lines 132-150 replaces `/`,`\` with `__` and any non-`[A-Za-z0-9.-_]` char with `_` (so `"../outside"` becomes `"..__outside"` and stays under temp_dir), and `get_local_repo_path` now joins on `format!("{owner}/{repo_name}")` instead of just `repo_name` so `alice/recipes` and `bob/recipes` no longer clobber each other on disk, with `.arg(&local_repo_path)` passed positionally to `gh repo clone`; qwen-code #3737 closes the last three holes from #3579 — removes `geminiClient.stripThoughtsFromHistory()` from the rewind path at `AppContainer.tsx:1740-1745` (with a `// Do NOT strip thought parts — reasoning models (e.g. DeepSeek) require reasoning_content continuity` rationale comment), fixes the missing `thought` part on compressed-summary turns in `chatCompressionService.ts`, fixes `mergeConsecutiveAssistantMessages` in `converter.ts` to not drop `reasoning_content` on merge, then deletes `stripThoughtsFromHistory()` and `stripThoughtsFromHistoryKeepRecent()` outright (most of the −363 line count is mock pruning in `config.test.ts`/`client.test.ts`).

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (opencode #24942 — pure deletion of `!pending()` gates that were hiding the very tool args being approved, with the title shimmer preserving the in-progress affordance; gemini-cli #26184 — 9-line correctness fix at the right service-boundary layer with the test flipped from `not.toThrow` to `rejects.toThrow`; qwen-code #3737 — net deletion of incorrect strip-thoughts behavior with the dead function removed so the bug can't be reintroduced trivially), 5 merge-after-nits (opencode #24941 add a "submit your own Skill" CTA and clarify the `~/.claude/skills/` install path for OpenCode-only users; codex #20113 add a positive `auto_amendment_allowed=true` regression test, defensive comment on the exhaustive `policy_src` match in `tests/suite/approvals.rs:124-136` so future `ActionKind` variants don't silently default to `None`, and expand the PR body with the threat model so future blame-digs surface the *why* without chasing #10941; litellm #26764 wrap `json.loads` in `try/except JSONDecodeError` for the non-strict `tags`/`user_config` path so `tags="{oops"` doesn't trade a silent-failure bug for a noisy-failure bug, broaden PR title to mention `tags`, and close near-duplicate #26723; litellm #26730 expose a non-private test seam so unit tests aren't coupled to `transport._client_factory`, drop the redundant `setblocking(False)` (or comment it as intentional defensive code), and document the manual `tc netem`/`iptables` reproduction recipe for the "long idle TCP flow stays alive" path that CI can't easily exercise; gemini-cli #26161 fix the empty-array truthiness bug — `overrideArgs ||` should be `overrideArgs?.length ?` so explicit `tools.shell.args: []` doesn't strip the pwsh `-NoProfile -Command` defaults — and switch PowerShell detection to `path.basename(...)` so full paths like `/opt/ps/powershell/7/pwsh` are recognized; goose #8878 add edge-case tests for `temp_child_name(".")`, `temp_child_name("..")`, `temp_child_name("./.")`, and a release-notes line about the cache-invalidation behavior change since `repo` directories are now `owner__repo` and existing on-disk caches will silently re-clone), 0 needs-discussion, 0 request-changes. Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).


### W18 drip-174 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 sst/opencode, 1 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 2 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "small surgical fixes + two larger refactors that need scope discipline" — opencode #24951 sets `process.env.OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_FILEWATCHER = "true"` at the top of both `web.ts:34` and `serve.ts:11` handlers to mirror the desktop entry point's prior art so the SSE `file.watcher.updated` events finally fire in web/serve mode (closes #19182), but the experimental-flag-set-from-three-call-sites pattern is itself a smell pointing at "flip the default already"; opencode #24947 swaps two `afs.up()` spreads at `paths.ts:26-44` so the home-walk lands *before* the project-walk in `ConfigPaths.directories()`, restoring the "later wins" merge-order invariant so project `.opencode/` correctly overrides global `~/.opencode` (closes #19296 and #21307 from independent reproductions of the same root cause); codex #20199 is the load-bearing 44-file +3329/-1010 introduction of a new top-level `codex-rs/journal/` crate (lib.rs, journal.rs +345, history submodule with classify/estimate/normalize/transform splits, protocol/journal.rs +383, thread-store/journal_writer.rs +355) plus the deletion of `core/context_manager/history.rs` (−726) and migration of every consumer to free-function `session_history::raw_items(&h)` style — the migration is mechanical and consistent, but the empty PR body, no test claim, free-function ergonomic regression (the `JournalTestExt` adapter trait at `agent/control_tests.rs:36-46` exists only to forward `.raw_items()` back to the free function), silent wire-format introduction, and no `[1/N]` slicing make this not-stamp-able as one PR; litellm #26776 re-files the systemic portion of #26215 (closed for unsigned CLA) — three fixes: snapshot-before-iterate in `safe_deep_copy` at `core_helpers.py:288-372` (atomic `dict(d)` snapshots of top-level + the two known-mutable nested dicts `metadata`/`litellm_metadata` before iterating, with a threading-based test at `test_noma_v2.py:277-330` reliably reproducing the pre-fix `RuntimeError: dictionary changed size during iteration` race plus a separate test pinning that the caller's nested dicts are no longer mutated by the otel-pop dance), single shared `_ProxyDBLogger` at `proxy_server.py:1762-1776` (cosmetic at the I/O level but eliminates the two-distinct-object-addresses-on-/active/callbacks triage problem), and `verbose_proxy_logger.exception` instead of `.error(str(e))` in `noma_v2.py:338` so tracebacks are captured; gemini-cli #26189 tightens one line at `KeypressContext.tsx:660` from `alt = escaped` to `alt = escaped && ch === '\b'` so Git Bash / MSYS2's `ESC + \x7f` plain-Backspace sequence no longer triggers DELETE_WORD_BACKWARD, while genuine Alt+Backspace (which sends `ESC + \b` everywhere) still resolves correctly; goose #8903 adds a single 247-line `.agents/skills/ui-refactor-review/SKILL.md` with opinionated workflow/strict-mode/smell-checklist/rules sections specifically calibrated for ui/goose2 maintainability review (Layer Discipline, DRY/Hooks, Type Hygiene, Tauri/Backend Boundaries with the explicit "Frontend-to-core communication goes through SDK -> ACP -> goose; do not add ad hoc fetch() calls" rule), strictly additive but at the upper end of "agent reads every line each invocation" token cost; qwen-code #3741 adds a footer pill via three new files (`useMCPHealth.ts:+70` subscribing to mcp-client's listener API and exposing raw counts not a label, `MCPHealthPill.tsx:+46` formatting via exported-for-testability `getPillLabel`, `MCPHealthPill.test.tsx:+64` covering 6 cases including the "connecting is suppressed to avoid boot/reconnect flicker" rule) plus a one-line `<MCPHealthPill />` insertion at `Footer.tsx:181` next to `BackgroundTasksPill` — the PR body's "why a parallel pill, not a `kind` extension of Background tasks `DialogEntry`" rationale (terminal-status contract vs connection-state cycle is a category error) is the right architectural call; qwen-code #3739 adds first-class persisted resume/continuation for background subagents via a new `BackgroundAgentResumeService` (+974) that discovers paused sidecars under `subagents/<sessionId>/`, surfaces them as a new `paused` status in the registry/dialog (`r` to resume / `x` to abandon at `BackgroundTasksDialog.tsx:495-498, 524-527`), and switches fork-resume to a transcript-first model (new `system/agent_bootstrap` and `system/agent_launch_prompt` records at `agent-transcript.ts:+131`) so resumed forks reconstruct worker context from the transcript instead of the parent's current state — author audited downstream consumers (export, ACP replay, insight analytics) and confirmed all ignore unknown system subtypes safely.
