Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (qwen-code #3741 — exemplary feature PR: minimal blast radius with one Footer line + 3 small new files, correct hook lifecycle including the resync-after-listener-attach pattern at `useMCPHealth.ts:50-58`, exhaustive tests on the pure formatting layer via the exported `getPillLabel`, well-justified architectural decision to use a parallel pill rather than extending the Background tasks registry that has a terminal-status contract incompatible with MCPs' connection-state cycle, and explicit/honest scope with focus-chain navigation deferred to a follow-up), 5 merge-after-nits (opencode #24951 either flip the `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_FILEWATCHER` default or extract a `enableExperimentalFileWatcher()` helper since three of three first-party entry points now unconditionally set it, plus add a `web.test.ts` assertion that the env var is set after handler runs and confirm `serve` mode was end-to-end smoke-tested not just `web`; opencode #24947 add a `paths.test.ts` case asserting `directories()` returns `[global, home, ...project]` in that exact order and a second case with `OPENCODE_DISABLE_PROJECT_CONFIG=1` asserting the project walk is empty so the next refactor doesn't re-introduce #19296, plus a `// IMPORTANT: order is precedence — last wins on merge` comment at `paths.ts:27` and a behavioral test of the `cwd === ~` edge case to confirm `unique()` keeps the home-walk hit not the project-walk one; litellm #26776 split the independent single-`_ProxyDBLogger` fix into its own PR or add a CHANGELOG line for the cosmetic-but-observable change, mark `test_build_scan_payload_concurrent_mutation_does_not_raise` as `@pytest.mark.timing_sensitive` since its 500ms deadline can produce false negatives on slow CI, file a follow-up issue for `safe_deep_copy_recursive_snapshot()` covering nested dicts beyond `metadata`/`litellm_metadata`, and confirm no existing caller passes a `pydantic.BaseModel`/`dataclass` and depends on the old per-field deepcopy fallback that the new non-dict short-circuit at `core_helpers.py:309-313` removes; gemini-cli #26189 add a `KeypressContext.test.ts` regression case driving `\x1b\x7f` through `emitKeys` asserting `{name: 'backspace', alt: false}` plus a sibling `\x1b\x08` case asserting `alt: true` to lock the asymmetric contract, link the underlying Git Bash backspace bug report, and consider the explicit-branch rewrite that splits the combined `\b || \x7f` predicate into two separate clauses for readability since they're no longer interchangeable; goose #8903 trim the 247-line skill aggressively or split Rules into a separate `RULES.md` referenced from `SKILL.md` since recurring per-invocation token cost is the silent failure mode for skills like this, add "Does NOT handle: ui/goose1 changes (different architecture); backend Rust review; feature-correctness review" negative-trigger frontmatter so the skill router doesn't grab adjacent tasks, add a 4-line "Example output shape" appendix showing what `Applied Well`/`Issues`/`Checklist` actually look like, and harden the "do not stop after first few findings" rule with an explicit termination condition like "emit at least one Issue per Smell Checklist bucket that triggered"; qwen-code #3739 add 1-2 more `send-message`/`task-stop` integration tests to unambiguously establish the paused-agent semantics (block with recoverable error vs buffer until resume — the PR body says they're "extended" but doesn't specify which), add an inline comment at the concurrent-resume coalescing site explaining lock/sentinel semantics since user-double-pressing-`r` is a real race, review the `paused` icon's `theme.status.warning` color since paused isn't a warning but a recoverable interruption, file a follow-up to add explicit schema versioning to the new `system/agent_bootstrap`/`system/agent_launch_prompt` records rather than relying on "all current consumers ignore unknown subtypes," and add a CHANGELOG entry since the recovery-notice startup output is user-facing and may be screen-scraped by downstream automation), 1 needs-discussion (codex #20199 — directionally-reasonable refactor that's not stamp-able in current shape: empty PR body + no test results + 1448-line crate skeleton + 726-line core deletion + free-function ergonomic regression + silent wire-format introduction is too many simultaneous decisions for one PR; ask author to either split into obvious slices like crate skeleton / protocol types / core migration / thread-store integration, or post a detailed PR body with `cargo test --workspace` output and explain why free functions instead of inherent methods on `Journal`), 0 request-changes. Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).


### W18 drip-175 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 sst/opencode, 1 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "small-to-medium correctness fixes plus one feature with scope discipline issues" — opencode #24957 rebases the stalled #16447 onto the Effect.ts-migrated dev branch to add a `mode: "conversation" | "conversation_and_code"` parameter to the revert API with the TUI cleanly factoring the duplicated `onSelect` body into a `revert(mode)` higher-order callback at `dialog-message.tsx:20-67`, but the rebase smuggled in three unrelated `MessageV2.stream`/`parts`/`get` additions in `message-v2.ts:984+` and the persisted-mode behavior at `index.tsx:599-605` and `use-session-commands.tsx:328-329` is undocumented (no test asserts the keyboard-shortcut path picks up the right persisted mode after a "conversation" select); opencode #24955 is a tight 13-line MCP fix wiring `client.onclose` to a new `dropConnection(s, name, client, reason)` helper at `mcp/index.ts:475-482` with the load-bearing stale-closure guard `s.clients[name] !== client || s.status[name]?.status !== "connected"` at line 476 protecting against fast reconnect/disconnect/reconnect races; codex #20207 fixes the `/copy` silently-fails-in-tmux-without-passthrough bug (#19926) by introducing a tmux-native clipboard path with OSC 52 fallback, replacing the prior two-bool parameter list with a `CopyEnvironment { ssh_session, wsl_session, tmux_session }` struct at `clipboard_copy.rs:88-92` and verbose-but-explicit error-message branching at lines 105-179 that names both the tmux failure mode and the OSC 52 fallback failure mode for triage; litellm #26779 closes #25509 by adding optional `budget_duration` to team-member budgets with the correct `data.model_fields_set` distinction at `team_endpoints.py:2729-2739` to separate "explicitly null = lifetime" from "field omitted = inherit team default," threading through both create and update paths in `_upsert_budget_and_membership` at `common_utils.py:357-475` with consistent `get_budget_reset_time()` calls so `budget_reset_at` doesn't drift from `budget_duration`; litellm #26756 layers two related fixes — restoring the `get_generic_data` reconnect-and-retry-once branch lost in 1.83.x (#25143) by factoring the pattern into a canonical `call_with_db_reconnect_retry` helper at `exception_handler.py:142-261` with at-most-one-retry baked into the control flow and exception-chaining at lines 247-255 that preserves the original transport error when reconnect itself raises, plus moving `self._engine_confirmed_dead = False` from before to after `_do_heavy_reconnect()` succeeds at `utils.py:4226-4232` so heavy-reconnect failures don't silently demote subsequent attempts to the lightweight path; gemini-cli #26191 fixes #18030 with a 60s default timeout (down from 300s) plus mapping `UND_ERR_HEADERS_TIMEOUT`/`UND_ERR_BODY_TIMEOUT`/`UND_ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT` onto canonical `ETIMEDOUT` at the `getNetworkErrorCode` boundary in `retry.ts:87-94` so existing backoff resilience auto-handles them; goose #8900 is a UX fix that's net deletion (+118/-99) — the right shape for a "remove duplicated startup logic" change — eliminating the picker's "Loading" flash by reordering label priority to `triggerModelLabel ?? selectedAgentLabel ?? "Loading"`, conditionally disabling only when `loading && !selectedAgentLabel`, extracting `backgroundRefreshInventory(store, initialEntries?)` into `inventory.ts` so startup and picker-open share one implementation, and using a `refreshingRef` ref at `useAgentModelPickerState.ts:166-182` to coalesce concurrent picker-open events; qwen-code #3743 fixes #1804 (file paths like `/Users/zhoushuo/Desktop/...` triggering "Unknown command" errors) by adding a `looksLikeCommandName()` helper validating first-token via `[a-zA-Z0-9:_-]` plus dot regex, with defense-in-depth at `slashCommandProcessor.ts:452-460` independently re-checking inside `handleSlashCommand` so the guard survives future refactors that bypass `isSlashCommand`, and 30+ test cases including explicit Chinese-character regression at `commandUtils.test.ts:198-201`.

Verdict mix: 0 merge-as-is, 7 merge-after-nits (opencode #24955 add a `mcp/index.test.ts` regression test for the stale-closure guard at line 476 — construct two clients sharing a name, fire `onclose` on the first after the second is installed, assert the second is untouched — plus harden `void client.close()` at line 481 with an explicit `.catch()` and debug log so transport-already-torn-down rejections don't surface as `UnhandledPromiseRejectionWarning`; codex #20207 extract the duplicated tmux-vs-OSC52 error-message branching into a single helper to eliminate ~20 lines, document the nested-tmux limitation since `is_tmux_session()` at line 388 will misfire for SSH-into-remote-tmux-from-local-tmux and silently push to the wrong clipboard, and confirm `cargo test -p codex-tui` actually exercises the SSH×tmux×OSC52-fallback cross-product the PR body claims; litellm #26779 add an integration test for the tri-state (omitted vs explicit-null vs explicit-string) at the endpoint layer not just the helper layer since `model_fields_set` semantics are easy to break in pydantic config tweaks, document inheritance behavior in OpenAPI/docs, and confirm the cherry-picked `81518b7` cleanly handles the case where `team_default_budget_id` exists but `_team_budget_row` is None — the `if _team_budget_row else None` ternary at line 2735 silently swallows a "team has stale default budget pointer" misconfiguration that should warn; litellm #26756 split the two fixes into two PRs since the `_engine_confirmed_dead` flag-reset bug at `utils.py:4226-4232` deserves its own change with a CHANGELOG note (it changes recovery semantics in the failure case), follow up with the auth + health inline-copy migrations the PR body promises so `call_with_db_reconnect_retry` has three call sites instead of one, and add a property test asserting that across N random `(transport_error, reconnect_outcome)` sequences the helper never makes more than 2 calls to `coro_factory`; gemini-cli #26191 add a `GEMINI_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` env var with the 60s as default — or per-operation timeout map so `generate` keeps a longer ceiling than `embeddings`/`countTokens` since the 60s default is aggressive for legitimately-slow long-running operations like large embedding batches and `gemini-2.0-pro` thinking-budget responses — and add explicit test cases for `UND_ERR_BODY_TIMEOUT` and `UND_ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT` parallel to the `UND_ERR_HEADERS_TIMEOUT` case at `retry.test.ts:457-471` since the diff adds three codes but only tests one; goose #8900 confirm the dynamic `import("@/features/providers/api/inventory")` at `useAgentModelPickerState.ts:171` is actually buying code-splitting (otherwise just import statically since the indirection costs more than it saves), add a test for `handlePickerOpen` asserting two synchronous opens result in one refresh call and `loading=true` short-circuits and `refreshingRef` is reset even when `backgroundRefreshInventory` rejects, and consider a small visual indicator that a background refresh is in flight so users understand why the model list might update under them; qwen-code #3743 add an explicit test case for `/123 args` documenting the intentional behavior — currently the regex accepts `123` as a valid command name per `commandUtils.test.ts:107` so `/123 something` triggers slash-command flow and produces the same "Unknown command" error this PR is trying to eliminate — consider whether bare `/?` should short-circuit to autocomplete the way bare `/` does, and add one fuzz test that runs 100 random POSIX-shaped paths through `isSlashCommand` asserting every one returns `false` since that's the property the PR is really claiming), 1 request-changes (opencode #24957 — split out the unrelated `MessageV2.stream`/`parts`/`get` additions at `message-v2.ts:984-1057` into their own PR since the author's checklist explicitly claims "I have not included unrelated changes in this PR" while the diff contradicts it, fix the indentation bug at `dialog-message.tsx:101` where the `if (part.type === "file")` line uses 12 spaces inside an 18-space-indented block, document where `revert.mode` is persisted across reverts since both call sites at `index.tsx:599-605` and `use-session-commands.tsx:328-329` *read* `session()?.revert?.mode`/`info()?.revert?.mode` but the diff doesn't show where it's *written* — if the keyboard shortcut silently uses last-mode while the menu shows both options as equally weighted, users who clicked "Revert conversation" once and then hit the keyboard shortcut expecting "code too" will lose work — and add a test asserting the keyboard-shortcut path picks up the correct persisted mode after a "conversation" select followed by an unrelated message), 0 needs-discussion. Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).


### W18 drip-176 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 6 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "small-to-medium correctness fixes plus two TUI-observability features and one big-but-thoughtful feature PR" — opencode #24961 adds a compact provider-quota strip to the prompt metrics row with a clean `codexQuotaLayout(width)` ladder at `metrics.ts:53-57` (200/120/90 col breakpoints) and proper sync re-entrancy guards via `codexQuotaWorkspace`/`providerQuotaWorkspace` at `sync.tsx:114-115`, but `formatQuotaBar` uses `Math.round` at `metrics.ts:38` which makes 99.4% display as a fully-filled 10/10 bar (likely want `Math.floor` so "100% bar = exhausted" stays a one-shot signal), and `syncProviderQuota` silently `catch {}`s at `sync.tsx:139` while `syncCodexQuota` doesn't — pick one error policy and at minimum log via `Log.create("sync.providerQuota").error(err)` so it's debuggable; opencode #24943 refactors the inline small-model priority cascade at `provider.ts:1614-1629` into a typed `Record<string, string[]>` map at `provider/small-model.ts` and gates `getSmallModel()` to only fire for built-in `explore` agent without explicit model at `task.ts:104` (`!next.model && next.name === "explore"` — correct conjunction), but providers omitted from the new map (lmstudio, ollama, together, groq, deepseek, etc.) silently get `[]` and skip the small-model path entirely so add a comment at `small-model.ts:3` explicitly stating the intentional fall-back behavior; codex #20064 includes the Guardian auto-review trunk rollout in `/feedback` uploads via the new `FeedbackAttachmentPath { path, attachment_filename_override }` struct (good — keeps transport path separate from display name) with `auto_review_rollout_filename(thread_id)` at `codex_message_processor.rs:7908` and clean fail-open semantics at `review_session.rs:262-272` (warn-and-return-`None` on resolution error), but the five-clause `if let && && && && let Some(...)` chain at `codex_message_processor.rs:7745-7755` silently swallows four distinct failure modes (missing conversation_id, get_thread error, no rollout materialized yet, dedupe collision) — split into named bindings so each can be logged distinctly, and `auto_review_rollout_filename` lacks path-traversal sanitization on the thread-ID stringification; codex #20060 adds `codex.turn.reasoning_effort` and `codex.request.reasoning_effort` to the OTel turn/response spans via the new `TurnContext::effective_reasoning_effort_for_tracing()` at `turn_context.rs:120-130` with a `"default"` sentinel for unsupported models, but the gate at line 121 (`self.model_info.supports_reasoning_summaries`) conflates "model supports configurable effort" with "model emits reasoning summaries" — verify that's the right `ModelInfo` field or the trace will show `"default"` for models that *did* receive an explicit effort, and the `"default"` sentinel will collide with a future `ReasoningEffort::Default` variant if OpenAI ever ships one (use `"unspecified"` instead); litellm #26778 fixes #25493 (`org_admin` users seeing `$0.00` / 0 requests / empty charts on the Usage page) by replacing the hardcoded `organizations={[]}` at `usage/page.tsx:11` with `useOrganizations()` and adds two well-formed vitest cases via `vi.hoisted()`-pattern mocks at `page.test.tsx:31-43`, but the PR base is `litellm_internal_staging` (cherry-picking community PR #25617) so verify squash-to-main preserves the original author attribution, and confirm `useOrganizations()` doesn't require `proxy_admin` scope (which would silently 403 for `org_admin` and reproduce the bug with extra noise); gemini-cli #26194 adds a 39-line regression test at `confirmation.test.ts:360-396` for issue #22120 (CoreToolScheduler dropping `payload` from confirmation responses) with precise `expect(details.onConfirm).toHaveBeenCalledWith(ToolConfirmationOutcome.ProceedOnce, payload)` assertion and correct `await listenerPromise` ordering before emit — covers exactly one confirmation type (`ask_user`) so a future regression touching only `edit`/`exec` payload paths wouldn't be caught, but the test is solid for what it claims and the code under test is unmodified (zero risk); goose #8784 is a 2-line prompt change at `developer/mod.rs:60-61` instructing the agent to use `python3` instead of `python` for macOS 12+ and modern Debian-based Linux, with a paired snapshot update at `__all_platform_extensions.snap:93-94` providing automatic regression protection — the wording "not available on modern macOS" is overly absolute (Homebrew/pyenv users do have `python`; "not guaranteed to be present" is more accurate) and "many Linux distros" varies wildly by distro family (Arch symlinks `python` → `python3`, Alpine ships neither by default, RHEL needs explicit install) so consider tightening to "prefer `python3` over `python` — `python` is not available on modern macOS or Debian-based Linux 20.04+"; qwen-code #3491 implements #2997 (structured git-diff stats + `/diff` command) at +3374 lines spanning core utility (`gitDiff.ts` with thoughtful linked-worktree handling via `resolveGitDir`, rebase detection covering `rebase-merge/`/`rebase-apply/`, and positional-state parsing instead of prefix-matching that would drop `---` content lines), CLI command registration at `BuiltinCommandLoader.ts:104`, three i18n locales, and a 529-line vitest file with clean `vi.hoisted()` mock patterns — large for single-pass review so split into "core parser PR + UI/i18n PR" would help, the caps (50 files, 1MB/file, 400 lines/file, 500-file `--shortstat` short-circuit) are hardcoded with no override knob for monorepos, and the `?` (untracked) / `~` (binary) sigils aren't documented in any i18n string so downstream automation can't interpret them.

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (gemini-cli #26194 — single-purpose 39-line regression test for a real previously-fixed bug, precise assertion that catches the omission-mode failure, zero risk since no code under test is modified, only nit is single-confirmation-type coverage which is acceptable scope for a "regression test for issue X" PR), 7 merge-after-nits (opencode #24961 fix the `Math.round` → `Math.floor` in `formatQuotaBar` at `metrics.ts:38` so 99.4%/10col doesn't show as a full bar, log instead of silent-`catch {}` at `sync.tsx:139` to match `syncCodexQuota`'s policy, confirm `setInterval`/`onCleanup` wiring for `CODEX_QUOTA_REFRESH_INTERVAL` actually clears timers on unmount, and rename `codexQuotaLayout` to `quotaLayout` since it's reused for provider layouts at `metrics.ts:69`; opencode #24943 add a comment at `small-model.ts:3` documenting that providers omitted from the map intentionally fall through to parent-model behavior, add a negative-path test asserting `(name !== "explore", model unset)` uses parent model and `(name === "explore", model set)` honors the explicit override, and verify the new `if (provider.models[item]) return ...` short-circuit at `provider.ts:1618` doesn't break the Bedrock cross-region prefix logic for users who happen to have both an exact-named model and a region-prefixed variant; codex #20064 unwind the five-clause `if let && && && && let Some(...)` at `codex_message_processor.rs:7745-7755` into named bindings so missing conversation_id / get_thread error / dedupe collision can each log distinctly, sanitize the thread-ID stringification in `auto_review_rollout_filename` against path-traversal shapes, document the snapshot semantics at `review_session.rs:263` (state lock dropped before `ensure_rollout_materialized()`, so trunk-swap-during-resolve is theoretically stale but acceptable), and confirm the `feedback_upload_consent_popup_snapshot` snapshot was actually updated for the new Guardian filename rather than just `cargo insta accept`'d; codex #20060 verify `supports_reasoning_summaries` at `turn_context.rs:121` is the right gate (vs a hypothetical `supports_reasoning_effort`) since the wrong gate makes `"default"` appear in traces for turns that received explicit effort — silently misleading observability, replace the `"default"` sentinel with `"unspecified"` to avoid future collision with `ReasoningEffort::Default`, return `&'static str` instead of `String` to skip 2 allocations per turn, and parameterize the test at `otel.rs:611-625` over the `Low`/`Medium`/`High`/`default-fallback`/`unsupported-model` branches since today only `High` is exercised; litellm #26778 verify cherry-pick attribution from community PR #25617 survives the squash-to-main, add a third test where `useOrganizations()` returns role-filtered data and asserts `UsagePageView` receives it (current tests don't actually exercise the `org_admin` role despite mocking it), confirm `useOrganizations()` doesn't require `proxy_admin` scope (which would 403 for `org_admin` and silently reproduce the bug), and either move tests to `tests/test_litellm/` per CONTRIBUTING or update the PR checklist to acknowledge UI-test paths are acceptable for UI-only fixes; goose #8784 tighten "not available on modern macOS" to "not guaranteed to be present" since Homebrew/pyenv users do have `python`, narrow the Linux claim to "Debian-based Linux 20.04+" since Arch symlinks both ways and Alpine ships neither, update the PR body to acknowledge the snapshot test at `__all_platform_extensions.snap:93-94` *is* the regression guard (PR body wrongly claims "no existing tests cover this"), and file a follow-up to scrub the prompt for related bare-binary references like `pip` vs `pip3` and `virtualenv` vs `python3 -m venv`; qwen-code #3491 split into two PRs — core parser + tests as PR1 and CLI command + i18n + UI tests as PR2 — to make the 3374-line diff reviewable in one sitting, paste actual `vitest` output in the PR body since "adversarial review" is claimed but no test results are shown, confirm license attribution to claude-code-cli's `utils/gitDiff.ts` (likely Apache 2.0 to Apache 2.0, but a NOTICE entry is the conservative move), bound the per-file column width in `computeDiffColumnWidths` so a single +9999/-9999 file doesn't blow up the alignment, document the `?`/`~` sigils in i18n strings so downstream automation can interpret them, and consider a config knob `diffStats.maxFiles` for power users on large monorepos), 0 request-changes, 0 needs-discussion. Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).


### W18 drip-177 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (3 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 block/goose, 2 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "small surgical fixes plus one hairy multi-concern feature PR plus one well-staged systems-level addition" — opencode #24964 is a textbook one-line MCP-spec-compliance fix (`onprogress: () => {}` at `mcp/index.ts:140` to actually engage the SDK's `resetTimeoutOnProgress: true` machinery — without an `onprogress` handler the SDK never installs a `_meta.progressToken` so progress notifications never reach the client and long-running MCP tools die at `timeout` ms despite making progress); opencode #24962 fixes a silent variant-application bug at `session/prompt.ts:902` where the `same` boolean short-circuited to false whenever `ag.model` was undefined, causing agents that declared a `variant` but no explicit `model` to silently drop their variant — new logic `!ag.model || (model.providerID === ag.model.providerID && model.modelID === ag.model.modelID)` reads as "no opinion = treat as match" which is the correct shape; opencode #24952 packages three independently-reviewable changes plus one undocumented behavior change as one PR — adds two new `review` and `test` subagents at `agent.ts:242-289` (read-only and write-capable respectively, with `external_directory` allow-lists), gates a `self_reflect` experimental flag onto the `build` agent's prompt at `agent.ts:109-114`, rewrites the `explore` prompt as a numbered playbook, AND silently deletes `typeAware: true` from `.oxlintrc.json` (the original config had a duplicate `options` block which was a JSON-syntax error; the fix should dedupe-not-delete) and the PR body claims "20+ security/reliability fixes" with zero in the diff; codex #20174 is a clean step 3/7 in a well-staged refactor stripping `codex_protocol::protocol` from `codex-tui` — `AppEvent::CodexOp` and `AppEvent::SubmitThreadOp` now carry `AppCommand` directly instead of bouncing through `Op` (`event_dispatch.rs:316,323` drop the `.into()` translations, `config_persistence.rs:351-368` drops `.into_core()` on the Windows-sandbox path), and `ApproveGuardianDeniedAction` lifts from `Other(Op::...)` into a first-class `AppCommand` variant at `app_command.rs:107-109,191-193,457-459` with the right migration discipline (dual-pattern match at `thread_routing.rs:707-712` accepts both old and new shapes so steps 4-7 can update call sites incrementally without a flag day); codex #20101 installs a first wave of WFP filters for the Windows sandbox at `windows-sandbox-rs/src/wfp.rs:1-409` (~409 lines of WFP C-API wrapping with proper transaction discipline at `:71-87`, persistent-key strategy via stable Codex-owned `PROVIDER_KEY = 2e31d31c-...` and `SUBLAYER_KEY = e65054fd-...` GUIDs documented "do not regenerate" at `:62-65`, idempotent `delete_filter_if_present` before every `add_filter` at `:74` for clean re-run upgrades, scoped to the offline account's user SID via `FWPM_CONDITION_ALE_USER_ID` for correct blast-radius), wires it into the elevated full-setup path at `setup_main_win.rs:609-617` with non-fatal logging, threads an `analytics_enabled` payload field with `#[serde(default = "default_analytics_enabled")]` from orchestrator to setup helper, and bumps `SETUP_VERSION 5→6` at `setup_orchestrator.rs:38` so existing users re-run full setup; goose #8898 is an automated bot release PR for v1.33.2 with mechanically-correct version bumps across 8 workspace crates but bundles a substantial 700-line `canonical_models.json` deletion (`aihubmix/Kimi-K2` and others) into the same release PR which makes per-change author attribution impossible — release PRs should be pure metadata so the audit trail is just version + cherry-picks; qwen-code #3749 fixes a real and embarrassing UX bug where a routine 4xx in non-interactive mode printed three lines (the second being `[API Error: [API Error: 402 ...]]`) followed by a programmer-bug stack trace — introduces an `AlreadyReportedError` sentinel that says "I'm already on stderr, just exit non-zero" and wires three sites (`nonInteractiveCli.ts:395`, `:570`, top-level `cli/index.ts:96-104`) to honor it, with a precise per-line regression test at `nonInteractiveCli.test.ts:867-927` asserting `[API Error: ` appears at most once per line; qwen-code #3747 extends the prior #3729 DeepSeek `reasoning_content` fix to apply to *all* prior assistant turns not just tool-calling ones (live testing rejected the original assumption) — three deleted lines at `deepseek.ts:120-122` plus a comment update plus a renamed/inverted test at `deepseek.test.ts:248,262` (`expect(...).toBeUndefined()` → `expect(...).toBe('')`).

Verdict mix: 0 merge-as-is, 7 merge-after-nits (opencode #24964 add a `// TODO: surface progress events to UI` comment at `mcp/index.ts:140` so the next refactor doesn't delete the empty handler as dead code and silently re-break timeout-extension behavior, plus a regression test using a fake MCP server that asserts a progress notification arriving at `timeout/2` extends the deadline; opencode #24962 add a one-line test in `agent.test.ts` covering the `(ag.model = undefined, ag.variant = "X", input.variant = undefined)` combo asserting `provider.getModel` is invoked with the resolved model since this regression will silently recur on the next refactor of the variant chain, and update the `agent.variant` docs to clarify that variant applies whenever the agent doesn't pin a contradicting model; codex #20174 confirm Windows CI is green before merge since the `cfg(target_os = "windows")` block at `config_persistence.rs:351-368` isn't covered by macOS/Linux test runs and a silent type-mismatch on the Windows path wouldn't be caught locally, add a `CHANGELOG.md` line tracking the 7-PR stack so consumers can audit the migration's progress, and inline a TODO at `thread_routing.rs:707` noting which step in the stack will remove the legacy `Other(Op::ApproveGuardianDeniedAction)` arm so it doesn't become permanent dead code; codex #20101 confirm `delete_filter_if_present` swallows `FWP_E_FILTER_NOT_FOUND` and `add_filter` correctly handles `FWP_E_ALREADY_EXISTS` (the imports are there but the visible diff doesn't show the matchers — a swap would silently leak duplicate filters on re-setup), add an always-on success/failure counter for WFP installation distinct from the OTel-gated analytics gauge so ops can detect silent WFP regressions on Windows updates without enabling full telemetry, add a CHANGELOG/release-notes entry calling out the `SETUP_VERSION 5→6` bump so the unexpected UAC prompt on next launch isn't a support surprise, and file a follow-up tracking issue for an elevated-Windows-VM CI smoke test that exercises the full WFP install path end-to-end; goose #8898 before tagging run `git log v1.33.1..release/1.33.2 -- crates/goose/src/providers/canonical/data/canonical_models.json` and confirm every removed model line maps to an explicit cherry-picked commit so ops can bisect "model suddenly missing" reports back to a single PR, add a CHANGELOG line for the `aihubmix/Kimi-K2` and other canonical-model removals so users with pinned configs aren't silently broken by 1.33.2, and confirm any non-publish workspace crates that opt out of `version.workspace = true` are picked up by the release tooling — a missed crate would ship as a stale 1.33.1 alongside 1.33.2 siblings; qwen-code #3749 confirm the `AlreadyReportedError` constructor sets `this.isAlreadyReported = true` so the duck-typed check at `nonInteractiveCli.ts:786` actually fires (the visible diff imports the class but doesn't show its definition; if the field isn't set, the JSON/STREAM_JSON adapter-skip path silently never engages and the bug recurs), add a positive assertion to the regression test at `nonInteractiveCli.test.ts:867-927` that stderr *does* contain a single `[API Error: 402 ...]` line so a future "suppress everything" bug doesn't silently pass, reconcile the `instanceof` vs duck-type asymmetry between `cli/index.ts:103` and `nonInteractiveCli.ts:786` with a one-line comment at each site explaining why the local check is the right shape, and verify a *genuine* programmer-bug crash (non-API TypeError) still produces the "An unexpected critical error occurred:" frame so this fix doesn't accidentally swallow real bugs; qwen-code #3747 rename `ensureReasoningContentOnToolCalls` to `ensureReasoningContentOnAssistantTurns` in the same PR since the helper no longer only touches tool-calling turns and the misleading name will cost more in future debug time than the rename costs now, file a follow-up tracking issue for a nightly integration test against `api.deepseek.com` gated on the API-key secret since this is the second time DeepSeek's `reasoning_content` contract has silently tightened (#3729 → #3747) and the next tightening shouldn't require a user to file an issue, and add a CHANGELOG note that non-tool-calling assistant turns now carry an empty-string `reasoning_content` field in the outgoing payload — downstream consumers reading back the request payload may need to update their expectations), 1 request-changes (opencode #24952 — split into three PRs: (1) the two new subagents `review`+`test` with their prompts, (2) the `self_reflect` experimental flag with a documented composition story since it's unclear whether `.prompt` on `build` replaces or appends to the built-in system prompt — a silent replace would catastrophically clobber the build-agent's system prompt, (3) the `explore` prompt rewrite as standalone polish; restore `typeAware: true` in `.oxlintrc.json` since the duplicate was a JSON-syntax error not a behavior intent — drop one copy keep the option; in `prompt/test.txt` add an explicit "scope edits to test files only — do not modify implementation under test" line because the agent has write/edit permissions and subagent prompts are the only check between a write-capable agent and arbitrary repo edits; update the PR body to match the actual diff or include the claimed "20+ fixes"), 0 needs-discussion. Repo coverage: 4 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code).


### W18 drip-178 (2026-04-29)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (3 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "small-surgical-fixes plus one well-staged refactor stack and one zero-risk fork-hygiene deletion" — opencode #24973 fixes a real `files()` stream truncation bug in trees with broken symlinks by adding `--no-messages` at `ripgrep.ts:198` and accepting rg exit code 2 alongside 0/1 at `ripgrep.ts:363` with a 19-line regression test that creates `fs.symlink(nonexistent, broken-link)` and asserts the real file still streams; opencode #24974 reorders the system-prompt instruction array from `[env, skills, ...instructions]` to `[env, ...instructions, skills]` at `prompt.ts:1448` AND moves the global-AGENTS.md block above the project-level block at `instruction.ts:125-130` so the final order becomes global → project → skills (more-specific later, which LLMs weight more heavily) and updates the test from order-agnostic `toContain` to ordered `rules[0]/rules[1]` assertions; opencode #24976 is a 97-line docs-only addition for the Perplexity provider showing the `@ai-sdk/openai`-with-`baseURL: api.perplexity.ai/v1` Agent API config plus a Search API section with both direct `curl` and an MCP server pointing at `mcp.perplexity.ai/sse`; codex #20173 is step 2 of a clean 7-PR stack replacing the opaque `pub(crate) struct AppCommand(Op)` newtype at `app_command.rs:28` with an explicit `enum AppCommand { Interrupt, UserTurn { ... }, OverrideTurnContext { ... }, ... }` of ~20 owned variants and preserves an `into_core()` shim at `app_command.rs:297-485` so the app/thread submission boundary doesn't move yet — `#[allow(clippy::large_enum_variant)]` at `app_command.rs:9` is the right tradeoff during the stack but needs a retirement-plan TODO; codex #20082 is a tiny 6-site verb rename from `/goal unpause` to `/goal resume` covering the dispatch arm at `slash_dispatch.rs:620`, two user-visible strings, the snapshot, and two test cases — but hard-removes the `unpause` dispatch arm without an alias which silently breaks user muscle memory; gemini-cli #26199 expands session-retention cleanup from "current project temp dir" to "every sibling project under `Storage.getGlobalTempDir()`" via a new `getCleanupRoots()` at `sessionCleanup.ts:79-101` with a `path.relative` + `!startsWith('..')` + `!isAbsolute` triple-check guard for testability and degrades gracefully to single-root when the project temp dir isn't under global, threads `tempDir`/`chatsDir` through every `SessionFileEntry` so per-file deletion targets the right root, and changes the dedupe key from `shortId` to `${chatsDir}:${shortId}` to prevent cross-project short-ID collisions — but the parallel `Promise.all` at `sessionCleanup.ts:158` has no concurrency cap which fans out unboundedly for users with hundreds of stale project hashes; gemini-cli #26198 is a two-line guard rewrite at `a2aUtils.ts:129-133` from `messageLog[length-1] !== text` to `length === 0 || messageLog[last] !== text` claimed to fix issue #24894 but the original expression *does* return `true` for empty arrays (`undefined !== text` is `true`) so the diff as written is a clarity refactor not a behavior change — the new test passes against both old and new guards; qwen-code #3725 is a pure 736-line deletion across 6 `.github/workflows/*.yml` files inherited from the gemini-cli upstream fork that all guarded on `if: github.repository == 'google-gemini/gemini-cli'` so they were dead-on-arrival in the qwen-code fork — provably zero blast radius.

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (qwen-code #3725 — provably zero behavior change since every deleted workflow was guarded on `if: github.repository == 'google-gemini/gemini-cli'` which is statically false in the qwen-code fork, only nit is filing a follow-up to grep for any *other* lingering upstream-repo-name guards), 7 merge-after-nits (opencode #24973 strengthen the regression test at `ripgrep.test.ts:172-188` to also assert `broken-link` is *not* in results so a future `--follow` regression that yields the broken link as a zero-byte entry doesn't silently pass, and add a `follow: false` variant locking in that broken symlinks remain skipped on the non-follow path with exit code 0; opencode #24974 add a CHANGELOG note that system-prompt instruction order changed from "global → skills → project" to "global → project → skills" since users who tuned project-level overrides expecting them to win against skills will see silent behavior drift, expand the test at `instruction.test.ts:254-259` to cover the (global + project + URL-loaded) trio so URL-loaded instructions can't silently re-shuffle above project files, add a one-line code comment at `instruction.ts:128` documenting the "first global match wins" symmetry with the project-level `break` so the parallel `break` semantics aren't accidentally lost, and verify `prompt.ts:1448` is the only call site building the `system` array (a parallel compact/summarize path with the old order would silently desync); opencode #24976 hedge the routed-third-party model names at `providers.mdx:1683` with an "as of <date>" note since Perplexity rotates routed models frequently, source the `context: 200000`/`output: 8192` token limits at `providers.mdx:1666` against the upstream models doc with an inline link, and add one line to the MCP example at `providers.mdx:1730-1740` warning users to set per-tool budgets since enabling search-as-a-tool consumes Perplexity tokens on every turn; codex #20173 add a `// TODO(stack-step-N): remove once UserTurn moves to thread-routing layer` next to `#[allow(clippy::large_enum_variant)]` at `app_command.rs:9` so the suppression has a known retirement plan, note the `UserTurn`/`OverrideTurnContext` 11-of-12-fields overlap as a future `TurnContextOverlay` consolidation candidate, add a code comment at `app_command.rs:297` saying "this entire `into_core()` impl is removed in step N" so reviewers of step 3+ can connect the dots, confirm the existing `chatwidget/tests/slash_commands.rs` and `tests/status_and_layout.rs` suites stay green since `cargo check` won't catch a flipped-field typo in `Op::OverrideTurnContext` arm, and confirm Windows CI is green since the `windows_sandbox_level` field at `app_command.rs:64` only compiles on Windows; codex #20082 add a backward-compat `"resume" | "unpause" =>` arm at `slash_dispatch.rs:620` plus a one-release-cycle deprecation hint to stderr/footer so user muscle memory isn't silently broken, add a test case to the table at `tests/slash_commands.rs:811-814` asserting `("/goal unpause", None)` produces the unknown-command path (or maps to `Active` if you adopt the alias), and consider rewording `bottom_pane/footer.rs:551` from `"Goal paused (/goal resume)"` to `"Goal paused — type /goal resume"` so the parens read as a hint not a command name; gemini-cli #26199 add a small concurrency limiter (4-8) to the `Promise.all` at `sessionCleanup.ts:158` since long-tail users with 200+ stale project hashes will fan out 200 parallel `readdir` calls on every CLI startup, add a Windows-specific test for `getCleanupRoots` proving the case-mismatched-but-canonically-under-global-temp path falls back correctly since `path.relative` on Windows can return `..`-prefixed values for case disagreements, add a soft cap with a debug log when scanning more than ~50 project temp dirs so users get a "consider pruning" hint, and add an explicit cross-project-collision test where the *current* session in project A shares a short-ID with a stale session in project B to lock dedupe-key ordering semantics; gemini-cli #26198 paste the failing-before / passing-after invocation in the PR body since the diff as written is a no-op semantically — the original `messageLog[length-1] !== text` evaluates to `undefined !== text` which is `true` for empty arrays so both guards push the first message — and replace the test at `a2aUtils.test.ts:587-607` with one that *distinguishes* old from new behavior (e.g., a case where `extractPartsText` returns `""` and the `text &&` short-circuit interacts with the guard differently), retitle the PR to "refactor(core): make A2AResultReassembler empty-log guard explicit" if no behavior bug can be demonstrated, and replace the `as unknown as SendMessageResult` cast at `a2aUtils.test.ts:603` with a properly-typed builder so the test isn't asserting a shape the type system says can't happen), 0 request-changes, 0 needs-discussion. Repo coverage: 4 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, google-gemini/gemini-cli, QwenLM/qwen-code).


### W18 drip-179 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (2 sst/opencode, 1 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 2 block/goose). Theme is "small-surgical fixes plus one well-designed multi-layer correctness fix and one bundled-too-much refactor" — opencode #24979 is a 4-line local rename of the Question service Bus binding from `bus` to `vehicle` at `question/index.ts:135,172,194,211` with no motivation in the PR body, no shadowing pressure in scope, and the new name `vehicle` is *less* specific than `bus` (loses the type-name correspondence with `Bus.Service`); opencode #24971 is a 1-line ecosystem-docs row addition for the `BRHP` plugin at `ecosystem.mdx:47` between `opencode-conductor` and `micode` (a sensible neighborhood for planning/session-state plugins) — zero build risk, the only review work is a 30-second smell test on the linked target repo since an entry in the upstream ecosystem docs lends third-party trust; codex #20207 fixes #19926 (`/copy` silently failing inside tmux when `allow-passthrough` is off) by adding tmux's native `load-buffer -w -` clipboard path as the preferred mechanism inside tmux with a `tmux_clipboard_copy_ready` precheck at `clipboard_copy.rs:381-399` that gates on `set-clipboard != off` and absence of `Ms: [missing]` in `tmux info`, restructured around a `CopyEnvironment { ssh_session, wsl_session, tmux_session }` struct at `:87-92` that replaces the old pair-of-bools call shape, with a clean fallback ladder (SSH→tmux→OSC52, local→arboard→WSL→tmux→OSC52) and careful three-clause error message threading at `:130-153` — but `tmux load-buffer` invocation at `:354` has no `wait_timeout` so a wedged tmux server hangs `/copy`, the `Ms: [missing]` substring match at `:393` is a `tmux info` text-format dependency that should pin a tmux version range in a comment, and the OSC52 size cap at `OSC52_MAX_RAW_BYTES` doesn't apply on the tmux path so the two paths diverge on max-payload size in ways the module doc should call out; litellm #26788 fixes Anthropic's `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,128}$` tool-name pattern rejection (commonly hit by OpenAPI-derived MCP tools like `actions/download-job-logs-for-workflow-run`) with the load-bearing correctness insight at `transformation.py:96-104` that **a bare `re.sub` is unsafe** because `foo/bar` and `foo_bar` collapse to the same sanitized name — the fix builds a *per-request* forward map at `_build_anthropic_tool_name_maps` (`:117-167`) where the reverse map (`:166`) only contains entries for names that were *actually rewritten*, so a tool legitimately named `foo_bar` is never reverse-mapped; threads the forward map through `_map_tool_helper`, `_map_tools`, `_map_tool_choice`, and `_rewrite_tool_names_in_messages` (multi-turn correctness) and the reverse map through both non-streaming `transform_parsed_response` and streaming `chunk_parser` at `handler.py:817-826`, with 685 lines of tests across 3 files — but the multi-turn churn case (turn 1 has `[foo/bar, foo_bar]` sanitized to `foo_bar`/`foo_bar_2`, turn 2 has only `[foo_bar]`) needs an explicit test since the historical assistant `tool_call` referencing `foo_bar_2` won't be rewritten and Anthropic will see a mismatch, `tool_choice` should be sanitized unconditionally not just on map-hit at `:506-512`, and 1028 lines is large for one PR — the `openapi_to_mcp_generator.py` generator-side fix is independently reviewable; gemini-cli #26204 bundles four motivations in one 295-line PR — perf (single-pass serialization via `estimateTextTokens` directly at `toolOutputMaskingService.ts:131-138`, killing 2-3 `JSON.stringify` per part), security #1 (anti-spoof `isAlreadyMasked` at `:309-321` now requires the response to be a single-key `{output: ...}` so attacker-controlled tool output can't fake a "skip me" signal — load-bearing test at `test.ts:351-396`), security #2 (preview HTML-escaping at `:236` so `</INJECTION>` becomes `&lt;/INJECTION&gt;` while the legitimate `<masking_indicator>` wrapper tags are preserved — test at `:587-632`), and a correctness fix dropping `exitCode` metadata leakage — but the snapshot diff at `__snapshots__/toolOutputMaskingService.test.ts.snap:6-26` shows the masked-preview formatter now collapses newlines to spaces (`Line\nLine\n... [6 lines omitted]` becomes `Line Line Line ... [9981 lines omitted]`) which is a real UX regression for shell-tool previews where line-shape *is* the semantic structure, and the four bundled motivations should be three separate PRs so the security changes get focused review and CHANGELOG attention; gemini-cli #26201 fixes #26188 (`remainingTokenCount` going negative when current prompt already exceeds the limit, e.g., 1200 tokens vs 1000-token limit producing user-facing "−200 tokens remaining") with a textbook one-line `Math.max(0, ...)` clamp at `client.ts:646-649` plus a 35-line regression test at `client.test.ts:1555-1589` that pins the exact event payload `{remainingTokenCount: 0, estimatedRequestTokenCount: 3}`; goose #8910 fixes #8871 (`--json`/`--stream-json` reporting per-turn `total_tokens` instead of cumulative session totals) by swapping `session.total_tokens` → `session.accumulated_total_tokens` at three CLI sites (`session/mod.rs:1268,1289,1448`) covering JsonMetadata, StreamEvent::Complete, and the in-process `get_total_token_usage` API, with a 196-line integration test at `agent.rs:1094-1289` that runs three real `Agent.reply` turns through a mock `FixedUsageProvider` and asserts both the cumulative semantics (15→30→45) AND the contract that `total_tokens` stays at 15 (last turn only) — but the test uses `SessionType::Hidden` while production `--json` usage is overwhelmingly `Normal`, and the JSON-output behavior change is a real backward-compat shift that needs a CHANGELOG line; goose #8906 fixes #8752 (custom-provider model names with case mismatches like `GLM-5.1` vs `glm-5.1` silently falling back to default 128k context_limit) with a one-character swap from `==` to `eq_ignore_ascii_case` at `provider_registry.rs:63` plus 64 lines of unit tests covering case-mismatch, exact-match, and unknown-model paths via a clean `make_test_entry` fixture at `:286-308` — `eq_ignore_ascii_case` is the right choice over `to_lowercase().eq(...)` for ASCII-by-convention identifier comparisons (zero allocation, no locale dependence on Turkish dotted-i etc.).

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (gemini-cli #26201 — one-line `Math.max(0, ...)` clamp at `client.ts:646-649` is mechanically correct, exact-payload regression test at `client.test.ts:1555-1589` pins `{remainingTokenCount: 0, estimatedRequestTokenCount: 3}`, and the only meaningful concern about downstream consumers of negative values is almost certainly empty since the field is named `remaining` not `delta`, only suggestions are a JSDoc note about the clamp and a parametric test covering the boundary case 1000-vs-1000; goose #8906 — one-character swap to `eq_ignore_ascii_case` at `provider_registry.rs:63` is the right comparator choice for ASCII-by-convention model-name identifiers, three orthogonal unit tests cover case-mismatch, regression-fence exact-match, and no-match-fallback via a clean `make_test_entry` fixture, and the choice of `eq_ignore_ascii_case` over `to_lowercase().eq(...)` is the correct allocation-free locale-independent answer for identifier comparisons — only nit is a one-line `// ASCII fold` comment to extract maximum educational value), 5 merge-after-nits (opencode #24971 confirm the linked `ZanzyTHEbar/brhp` repo exists, has a license, and is more than a stub before merging since the ecosystem-docs entry lends third-party trust, run `prettier --write` on `ecosystem.mdx` for column-alignment cleanup, file a follow-up to categorize the table once it crosses ~50 entries, and document inclusion criteria so future low-quality submissions have gentle rejection language; codex #20207 add a `wait_timeout(2s)` around `wait_with_output()` at `clipboard_copy.rs:354` so a wedged tmux server can't hang `/copy`, pin a tmux version range in a comment near the `Ms: [missing]` parser at `:393` since `tmux info` text-format may change, document the OSC52-vs-tmux size-cap divergence in the module doc at `:5-15` (OSC 52 has `OSC52_MAX_RAW_BYTES` cap, tmux path has none), expose the chosen path via `tracing::debug!` on success so users can self-diagnose which mechanism worked, and add an explicit test for `set-clipboard external` to lock in that "external" passes the precheck; litellm #26788 add a multi-turn churn test where turn 1 has `[foo/bar, foo_bar]` (sanitized to `foo_bar`/`foo_bar_2`) and turn 2 has only `[foo_bar]` (no map entry, but historical `tool_calls` reference `foo_bar_2`) to lock in correctness when collision partners disappear between turns, sanitize `tool_choice` unconditionally at `transformation.py:506-512` not just on map-hit so a stray slash in `tool_choice` doesn't 400, add CHANGELOG line about user-visible name churn in observability since dashboards keying on tool names will see `foo_bar_2` where they expected `foo/bar`, consider extracting `_build_anthropic_tool_name_maps` to a provider-agnostic helper parameterized on regex+max-len for future strict-name providers, and split into a generator-side PR (`openapi_to_mcp_generator.py` fix is 143-line independently-reviewable) plus the Anthropic transformation PR; goose #8910 flip the test from `SessionType::Hidden` to `Normal` since production `--json` usage is overwhelmingly non-hidden sessions, add CHANGELOG entry calling out the backward-compat shift in JSON output semantics (downstream tools that summed per-chunk values will now double-count), promote `FixedUsageProvider` to `crates/goose/tests/common/` if no shared mock exists, add a monotonicity assertion (`accumulated_total_tokens` should never decrease between turns), and add doc-comments on the `Session` struct distinguishing `total_tokens` (per-turn last-known) from `accumulated_total_tokens` (cumulative since session start) so the next field-consumer doesn't trip over the same naming hazard — followup: rename to `last_turn_tokens`/`cumulative_tokens` for clarity), 1 request-changes (gemini-cli #26204 — the perf and security wins are real but the snapshot diff at `__snapshots__/toolOutputMaskingService.test.ts.snap:6-26` shows masked shell-output previews lose newline structure (`Line\nLine\n...` becomes `Line Line ...`) which is a UX regression the PR doesn't acknowledge; for shell tools specifically, line-breaks are the semantic structure of the output — either restore `\n` preservation in the preview formatter or justify the density tradeoff in writing, and strongly recommend splitting into three PRs (perf / security-with-anti-spoof-and-preview-sanitization / cosmetic+test-refactor) so the two security hardenings get focused review with explicit security-tag and CHANGELOG callouts rather than being buried in line 312 of a "perf" PR), 1 needs-discussion (opencode #24979 — diff is mechanically safe but the rename `bus` → `vehicle` is unmotivated, the new name is *less* specific than `bus` (loses correspondence with the `Bus.Service` type), there's no shadowing in scope that would force the rename, and PR body provides no justification beyond "I renamed it" — maintainer should either close with "we prefer `bus` here" or accept after the author justifies why `vehicle` is clearer than `bus` for a binding to `Bus.Service`). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).


### W18 drip-180 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across 5 repos (1 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 2 block/goose). Theme is "small-surface correctness fixes plus one large-surface generated-SDK reintroduction and one architectural PoC" — opencode #24968 reintroduces a Python SDK at `packages/sdk/python/` (593 files, +67066/-0, mostly generated from `packages/sdk/openapi.json` via `openapi-python-client`) shipped *together* with a regen-CI workflow at `.github/workflows/python-sdk.yml` so the spec-vs-tree drift that killed the prior attempt (`13b2cf50`) can't happen again, hand-written surface limited to `__init__.py`, `server.py` (228 LOC Python mirror of `createOpencodeServer`), `pyproject.toml`, README, the regen script (128 LOC), and the `_overlay/` package — primary review concern is that 593-file PRs are uncacheable on the GH side (`gh pr diff` 406s with "exceeded the maximum number of files (300)") and the workflow `paths:` filter must include `packages/sdk/openapi.json` not just `packages/sdk/python/**` or spec-only PRs will silently pass without regen; codex #20231 adds `apps_mcp_path: Option<String>` config key threaded cleanly through `ConfigToml` → `Config` → `McpConfig` → `codex_apps_mcp_url_for_base_url(base, override)` at `codex-mcp/src/mcp/mod.rs:380` with the override applied after the existing `(base_url, default_path)` per-branch resolution so the no-base-path case still gets the implicit `/api/codex` prefix, four-shape test matrix at `mod_tests.rs:113-148` covers all base-URL forms with and without override, but the override is taken verbatim with only `trim_start_matches('/')` (no validation against `..` or scheme prefix) and the key is placed at config root rather than nested under the existing `apps.` block where it would sit next to `apps_enabled`; codex #20229 is a deliberately-marked PoC (+1104/-549, 19 files) carving a new `codex-kernel` workspace crate out of `codex-core` — moves `Prompt` struct (257 LOC verbatim from `client_common.rs` with re-export `pub use codex_kernel::Prompt;`), retryable outer sampling loop (170 LOC pulled from `session/turn.rs`), and a kernel tool contract (`KernelToolExecutor`/`KernelToolCall`/`CompletedResponseItem`, 226 LOC) — the smoking-gun signal that the seam is right is `stream_events_utils.rs` shrinking from ~94 LOC to 12 LOC, but real concerns are silent `pub(crate)` → `pub` widening on `Prompt.tools`/`parallel_tool_calls`/`get_formatted_input` after the cross-crate move, possible regression-coverage drop for `reserialize_shell_outputs` if the test didn't actually move with the function, and an unrelated `app-server/tests/suite/v2/turn_start.rs` skills-warning structural-assert change bundled in that should be its own PR; litellm #26790 fixes a real parity bug in `litellm/caching/redis_cache.py:1262-1265` where `delete_cache`/`async_delete_cache` were the only public cache methods *not* calling `self.check_and_fix_namespace(key=key)` before the Redis `DEL`, so namespaced deployments saw `SET`/`GET` correctly write/read at e.g. `litellm.caching.caching:cronjob_lock:db_spend_update_job` but `DEL` always targeted the raw key (didn't exist, returned `0`), causing `PodLockManager` to never explicitly release locks and producing `failed to release Redis lock` warnings every batch-write cycle for every pod — two-line fix plus a parametrized regression test at `tests/test_litellm/caching/test_redis_cache.py:476-499` covering both `namespace=None` (anti-regression on the no-prefix contract) and `namespace="myns"` paths in both async and sync; gemini-cli #26203 demotes `debugLogger.warn` → `debugLogger.debug` for the "Ripgrep is not available. Falling back to GrepTool." line at `config.ts:3683` (closes #26193 — startup noise on every CLI launch for users without ripgrep installed), preserves `logRipgrepFallback` telemetry call for adoption tracking, test additions at `config.test.ts:2262-2329` assert *both* `warnSpy` was *not* called and `debugSpy` *was* called with the exact string for both the `canUseRipgrep === false` and the `canUseRipgrep` throws paths — only real concern is whether the throw-path (corrupted ripgrep install, user-actionable via `brew reinstall ripgrep`) should keep a `warn`-level signal while the clean-fall path goes to `debug`; gemini-cli #26202 (+326/-32, 8 files) does three coupled things — platform-specific keybinding reshuffle in `keyBindings.ts` so Linux/WSL prefers `Alt+Z`/`Alt+Shift+Z` over `Win+Z` (intercepted by Windows 11 Snap Layouts), smart-bubble for `Ctrl+Z` in `text-buffer.ts:3454-3471` that returns `false` when `undoStack.length === 0` so the keystroke bubbles to the platform suspend handler (preserves WSL `SIGTSTP` behavior on empty buffer), and a docs+generator update — critical not-easy-to-spot detail is the `useMemo` deps array on `handleInput` at `:3496-3498` correctly gains `undoStack.length, redoStack.length` so the bubble check doesn't read a stale closure; goose #8912 fixes a quiet UX bug in goose2 where Ollama appeared "already connected" because the backend default host counted as configuration — splits the fix across new `fn ollama_host_configured(config)` at `ollama.rs:125-127` (env-or-saved-config check, not just default), `OllamaProvider::inventory_configured()` override, and a UI catalog flip from `setupMethod: "local"` to `"config_fields"` with a new `defaultValue: "http://localhost:11434"` channel on `ProviderField` so the form pre-populates with the default but the `inventory_configured()` check only flips to true after the user explicitly saves — main concern is the silent UX regression for existing users running against the default host (they'll see Ollama as "not configured" after upgrade until they open Settings → Save), worth a one-shot migration that auto-writes `OLLAMA_HOST` if Ollama responds at default and no env value is set; goose #8905 fixes a silent Gemini Code Assist OAuth onboarding failure by parsing `allowed_tiers` (which is what the API actually returns for new users, vs `onboard_tiers` which the code was checking) at `gemini_oauth.rs:303-316`, preferring tiers marked `is_default: true` via `find(|t| t.is_default.unwrap_or(false))` at `:432-436`, flipping the literal final fallback from `"FREE"` (rejected by `onboardUser` as invalid argument) to `"free-tier"` (the actual accepted id), AND closing the amplifier in `ProviderConfigurationModal.tsx:216-225` where `configureProviderOauth`'s error result was being silently swallowed so users saw "OAuth completed" but no Gemini in the dropdown — two-layer fix is correct (cause + amplifier) but no unit test on the tier resolution path and the modal's user-facing error string can be a `JSON.stringify`'d raw error which is hostile to non-developer users.

| [#8905](https://github.com/block/goose/pull/8905) | Fix Gemini OAuth onboarding failure | [2026-W18/drip-180/block-goose-pr-8905.md](2026-W18/drip-180/block-goose-pr-8905.md) |

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (litellm #26790 — minimal, correct, parametrized regression test, tight blast radius, the kind of fix that should land same-day; only nit-of-nits is renaming the test for symmetry with `check_and_fix_namespace` but really not worth a re-push), 6 merge-after-nits (opencode #24968 — confirm `python-sdk.yml` `paths:` filter fires on `packages/sdk/openapi.json` not just the python tree, split into "skeleton+generator+workflow" and "generated artifacts" commits for future archaeology, add a top-of-file generated-warning header in regenerated modules so IDE drift attempts are immediately obvious; codex #20231 — tighten the doc comment / schema description with constraints on the override value (no scheme, no `..`), consider moving the key under the `apps.` config section for namespace hygiene next to `apps_enabled`; codex #20229 — split the unrelated `app-server/tests/suite/v2/turn_start.rs` skills-warning change into its own PR, add a `kernel/README.md` describing the boundary contract while doctrine is most malleable, audit `pub(crate)` → `pub` widenings introduced by the cross-crate move; gemini-cli #26203 — consider keeping `warn` for the `canUseRipgrep` throw path (corrupted install is user-actionable), extract the message string to a const referenced from both `config.ts` and `config.test.ts`, rename test cases to mention the log-level expectation; gemini-cli #26202 — document the `Ctrl+Z` bubble-on-empty contract explicitly in `commands.md` so users aren't surprised by mid-thought suspend, narrow the PR title to "...undo/redo" since smart bubbling only applies to those two commands, confirm the macOS keypress fixtures match real-world terminal emulator output; goose #8912 — add a `// is_secret = false` comment on `config.get("OLLAMA_HOST", false)`, consider a one-shot migration to auto-write `OLLAMA_HOST` for users upgrading from the prior zero-config behavior, confirm `OllamaProvider::from_env` uses the same env-then-config precedence as `inventory_configured` to avoid UI/runtime split-brain; goose #8905 — add a Rust unit test for `LoadCodeAssistResponse` parsing + `setup_code_assist` tier resolution covering the new `allowed_tiers` path, comment the `"free-tier"` literal with verification date so the next API rename is grep-able, render a friendlier error string in the modal and reserve `JSON.stringify` for the log channel), 1 needs-discussion (codex #20229 — explicitly marked PoC, the seam choice (Prompt + sampling-loop + tool-executor-contract) is the right one and the LOC math checks out, but as a PoC needs a design conversation about (a) whether `Prompt` field visibility silently widened on the cross-crate move, (b) whether the unrelated turn_start test change should be split out, (c) whether `codex-kernel` should also own the request/response types or stay purely loop-policy — verdict matches the author's stated intent of "less 'final API polish' and more 'can this boundary carry the existing app-server behavior'"), 0 request-changes. Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos.


### W18 drip-181 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across 4 repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). Theme is "small-surface UX/correctness fixes plus one real security fix" — opencode #24988 is a 4-line guard added to four `onMouseUp` handlers in `question.tsx:280-373` that calls `useRenderer().getSelection()?.getSelectedText()` and bails early if the user is mid-drag selecting text on a question prompt, fixing the silent-commit-during-copy UX bug; opencode #24955 wires `client.onclose` in the existing MCP `watch()` setup at `mcp/index.ts:496-498` to a new `dropConnection` helper at `:475-481` that flips status to `failed`, drops `defs[name]`/`clients[name]`, and `void client.close()`s the zombie — the load-bearing correctness check is the identity guard `s.clients[name] !== client` to prevent late `onclose` from a previous incarnation clobbering a healthy reconnect; codex #20098 is a real security fix closing a supply-chain hole where a checked-in `.codex/config.toml` could redirect `openai_base_url`/`chatgpt_base_url`/`model_provider`/`model_providers`/`profile`/`profiles`/`experimental_realtime_ws_base_url` to attacker endpoints — adds `PROJECT_LOCAL_CONFIG_DENYLIST` at `loader/mod.rs:50-60`, a `sanitize_project_config` helper at `:721-735` that runs after relative-path resolution, plumbs an `ignored_project_config_keys` field through `ConfigLayerEntry` for observability, and ships an end-to-end test at `config_loader_tests.rs:1646-1727` exercising all 7 denylisted keys; codex #20113 fixes two coupled exec-policy bugs around heredoc parsing — gates the `derive_requested_execpolicy_amendment_from_prefix_rule` call at `exec_policy.rs:271-285` on `auto_amendment_allowed` so the derive (and any side-effects in matched-rule expansion) doesn't fire when amendment is disallowed, and adds load-bearing scenario coverage at `exec_policy_tests.rs:817-901` and `tests/suite/approvals.rs:964+` proving that `PATH=/tmp/evil:$PATH cat <<'EOF'` is NOT reduced to the allowed `cat` prefix rule (variable-assignment forge attack), heredoc-with-redirect respects sandbox-vs-escalation distinction, and matching prefix rules don't auto-amend when the heredoc shape is involved; litellm #26763 is a 3-site typo fix in `dual_cache.py:129,359,386` (`"Excepton"` → `"Exception"`) — zero-risk pure string change in error-path log messages; litellm #26753 fixes a Bedrock 400 (`client_metadata: Extra inputs are not permitted`) by adding `"client_metadata"` to the `internal_params` set in `core_helpers.py:434` so Responses-API client metadata is stripped before reaching provider transformations, with a 7-line regression test at `test_core_helpers.py:204-211` — centralized fix per Greptile feedback on prior per-provider PR #26172; gemini-cli #26189 is a 1-line condition tightening at `KeypressContext.tsx:660` from `alt = escaped` to `alt = escaped && ch === '\b'`, fixing the Git Bash / MSYS2 bug where bare Backspace (sent as `ESC + \x7f`) was being interpreted as `Alt+Backspace` and routed to `DELETE_WORD_BACKWARD` instead of `DELETE_CHAR_LEFT` — eats whole words on every Backspace; goose #8869 collapses 31 lines of PWD-based WSL detection in `download_cli.sh:59-99` to a single invariant — "WSL is a Linux kernel; install the Linux binary regardless of `$PWD`" — fixing the bug where running the installer from `/mnt/c/...` silently dropped a Windows `.exe` at `$USERPROFILE/goose`.

Verdict mix: 1 merge-as-is (litellm #26763 — 3-site typo fix in error-path log strings, atomic, zero-risk, obviously not intended to ship as the misspelled form), 7 merge-after-nits (opencode #24988 extract the `isSelecting()` predicate to one local helper since four call sites repeat the identical guard and a future fifth interactive box will silently forget it, audit other TUI routes (slash-command palette, session list) for the same pattern, file a meta-issue tracking "TUI mouse handlers must respect active text selection"; opencode #24955 wrap `client.close()` at `mcp/index.ts:488` in `try/catch` since the post-onclose state of the SDK client isn't contractually re-close-safe across stdio/SSE/WS transports, plumb the SDK's onclose error/code through to `dropConnection`'s `reason` so the failure log distinguishes "stdio EOF" from "WebSocket 1006", add a SDK in-memory transport-pair regression test asserting `state.status[name].status === "failed"` within one tick, consider a follow-up bounded-retry reconnect loop instead of "fail and stay failed"; codex #20098 surface a `tracing::warn!` (or one-line CLI hint) when `ignored_project_config_keys` is non-empty so a silently-redirected user notices on first launch — without this the correct fix produces an indistinguishable UX from "the malicious config worked", replace the order-sensitive `assert_eq!` at `:1696-1706` with a sorted/HashSet comparison, add a test for `[profiles.foo]`-nested denylisted keys, inline a doc comment at `:1003` documenting the sanitize-after-resolve-relative-paths ordering invariant, cross-reference in security advisory / release notes; codex #20113 either drop the `#[cfg(not(windows))]` gate at `exec_policy_tests.rs:817` and run the variable-assignment security test on Windows or document why bash-heredoc parsing diverges, add a test for command-substitution `$(...)` injection inside heredoc bodies flowing to redirect targets, pair with a CLI/TUI follow-up that visually highlights variable-assignment prefixes in the approval prompt, add a brief comment at `:274-285` explaining *why* the derive is gated on `auto_amendment_allowed` (side-effects in matched-rule expansion) so a future refactor doesn't inline it back; litellm #26753 convert `internal_params` literal to a module-level `frozenset` with comment-grouped sub-blocks distinguishing litellm-internal plumbing keys from client-passthrough drop keys, add a parametric test asserting every member is stripped, add a regression-fence test at the Bedrock transformation layer that pipes a Responses-API body and asserts outgoing `additionalModelRequestFields` lacks `client_metadata`, audit grep-callsites of `**kwargs` in provider transformations to verify `filter_internal_params` is upstream of every body construction, add a CHANGELOG line about outbound-wire churn for downstream observability tools; gemini-cli #26189 add a regression test feeding the four interesting sequences (`\x7f` alone, `\b` alone, `ESC + \x7f`, `ESC + \b`) and asserting `{name, alt}` for each — without this a future refactor of `emitKeys` will silently re-break, extract `'\b'` and `'\x7f'` to named constants `BACKSPACE_CTRL_H`/`BACKSPACE_DEL` alongside the existing `ESC` constant, document whether `Ctrl+Backspace` on Git Bash is in scope, list the test matrix (Windows Terminal+WSL bash, Git Bash, MSYS2, PowerShell) in PR body; goose #8869 add a release-note line about the WSL2 install-target shift since users who previously got `goose.exe` from `/mnt/c/...` will silently get the Linux binary on next install, add a 30-line bats test covering the OS-detection matrix `(WINDIR | OSTYPE=msys | OSTYPE=cygwin | /proc/version Microsoft | macOS | Linux | Git Bash)`, audit `download.ps1` and other install entry points for the same WSL-as-Windows heuristic, add `--target-os`/`--target-arch` CLI flag as principled escape hatch for cross-environment CI builds), 0 request-changes, 0 needs-discussion. Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).
