Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (codex #20243 the public-API surface gate is the right shape — change-detection filter at `rust-ci.yml` skips the nightly install for unrelated PRs and the aggregator's per-job exit assertion can't hide a yellow result, follow-ups are doc-only; codex #20240 closes a real System V IPC host-channel gap with the smallest possible diff — one `--unshare-ipc` flag in both bubblewrap argv builders and a regression test that allocates a real `IPC_PRIVATE` segment then re-execs a probe helper inside the sandbox to confirm `shmat` fails, the `bwrap_preserves_writable_dev_shm_bind_mount` test confirms POSIX shm via `/dev/shm` bind-mount survives, README update is correct; goose #8916 corrects the prompt-cache placement strategy from head-anchored to trailing breakpoint, matches Anthropic's documented lookback-from-breakpoint contract, replaces a misleading "caching recent messages would shift positions" comment with an accurate description of the lookup model, the 20-block lookback-window edge case for very wide turns is a documented follow-up not a regression; goose #8904 trivial `else if` → two independent `if` fix that closes the `MAX_TOKEN_AGE_SECONDS` bypass of `exp` validation, plus an explicit test inversion in the same commit — the previous test was literally documenting the bypass with `expect(response.status).toBe(200)` and is now `expect(response.status).toBe(401); expect(...error).toBe("Token expired")`, the operator-style "I changed this test on purpose because it was documenting the bug" note in the PR body is the right pattern for security-adjacent changes), 2 merge-after-nits (opencode #24984 lift the editor-context state out of `onMount` closure into provider scope is the right shape since session-change is the actual trigger and re-mount was incidental, the `WebSocketImpl` injection makes the new test suite stub-able without monkey-patching the global, but the `socket.readyState !== 1` literal should be `WebSocketImpl.OPEN` for self-documentation and to survive polyfill enums, the `lastSubmittedEditorSelectionKey` closure-let resets on `<Prompt>` remount and needs a comment or `createSignal`, dropping `editor.clearSelection()` in favor of a dedupe-key assignment is a UX shift that should be confirmed with the design owner; gemini-cli #26208 the `skipExtensions: true` opt-out for the bootstrap `loadCliConfig` call is correctly minimal and the `SimpleExtensionLoader([])` fallback preserves the type contract so downstream `extensionLoader` consumers don't have to handle `undefined`, but the diff includes a stray top-level `pr_body.md` that's clearly an artifact of a `gh pr create --body-file` workflow and should be removed, the referenced unit test isn't visible in the diff, and a quick audit is needed to confirm nothing in the auth/sandbox bootstrap consumes `extensionPlanSettings`), 1 request-changes (gemini-cli #26207 the `@gemini-cli-robot` mention trigger, association-based maintainer gate, `<untrusted_context>` prompt-injection wrapper, and `bot/`-prefix push guard are all correctly designed, but the critique-result semantics flip from "neutral = approve" to "explicit `[APPROVED]` required" is hidden in a comment when it should be a release note, the `Post PR/Issue Comment` step lost its `if:` guard so it now runs unconditionally even on scheduled runs with no comment artifact, the `gh issue view ... 2>/dev/null || gh pr view ...` fallback proceeds with an empty `trigger_context.md` if both fail rather than aborting, and the long-lived `GEMINI_CLI_ROBOT_GITHUB_PAT` should be re-scoped to a GitHub App installation token narrowed to `refs/heads/bot/*` since any maintainer comment can now trigger a workflow with write access), 1 needs-discussion (litellm #26793 the schema and endpoint shape for `/v1/workflows/runs` are good and the `session_id` bridge to `LiteLLM_SpendLogs` is a clean reuse, but the `_get_next_sequence_number` MAX+1 read-then-insert pattern is racy under concurrent appenders for the same `run_id` and needs either a `SERIALIZABLE` transaction or a Postgres sequence, none of the endpoints filter `where` by the calling key's tenant/team so any authenticated key can read another team's full conversation messages — the conversation-message table doesn't truncate content like spend logs do so the blast radius is high, and `raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))` leaks Prisma error details to callers). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).

### W18 drip-183 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across all 6 tracked upstream repos (1 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 1 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "small-surface correctness fixes plus one pricing-data top-up" — opencode #24996 generalizes the Mistral reasoning gate at `provider/transform.ts:763` from two hard-coded `String.includes` checks to a `MISTRAL_REASONING_IDS` array driven by `Array.some(includes)`, picking up Medium 3.5 and any future reasoning-capable Mistral model with a one-line edit, with a parametrized regression test at `transform.test.ts:2277-2293`; codex #20242 codifies the existing Rust-trait migration convention into `AGENTS.md:22-27`, spelling out the preferred `fn foo(&self, ...) -> impl Future<Output = T> + Send` shape literally and naming `#[async_trait]` and `#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]` as the two anti-patterns to avoid, with reference commit `3c7f013f9735` / PR #16630 for archaeology; codex #20245 adds a sibling `BrowserUseExternal` feature flag at `features/src/lib.rs:170-174` and `:903-908` (Stage::Stable, default_enabled: true) plus matching schema entries at `config.schema.json:361-363,3306-3308` and the standard `(stage, default_enabled, key→Feature lookup)` triple-assertion test at `tests.rs:160-166`; litellm #26800 fills in `cache_creation_input_token_cost_above_1hr` for 14 Bedrock Claude variants (Haiku 4.5, Sonnet 4.5/4.6, Opus 4.5/4.6/4.7 across `anthropic.*`, `global.anthropic.*`, `us.anthropic.*` prefixes) at the consistent 1.6× multiplier, plus the `_above_1hr_above_200k_tokens` quadrant for the long-context Sonnet 4.5 entries — closes a silent under-counting bug where `cache_control: {ttl: "1h"}` callers were billed at the 5-minute rate in spend logs while AWS billed at the 1-hour rate, both `model_prices_and_context_window.json` and its `_backup.json` mirror updated atomically; gemini-cli #25937 is a one-line `throw error;` at `mcp-client.ts:1347` that propagates `discoverTools` failures out instead of returning fake-empty `[]`, fixing the "transient `tools/list_changed` discovery failure → permanent server tool wipe via `removeMcpToolsByServer`" bug, with a behaviour-flipping test at `mcp-client.test.ts:1462-1465` whose prior assertion was literally documenting the bug; gemini-cli #25936 swaps the strict-equality `blockedServer.name === serverName` predicate at `McpStatus.tsx:51-54` to symmetric `.trim().toLowerCase()` on both sides, fixing the UI-vs-runtime split-brain where a server configured as `SERVER-1` and blocked as `server-1` rendered as healthy in the status view while the runtime correctly refused tool calls, with a deliberately-ugly test fixture at `McpStatus.test.tsx:205-228` exercising trailing/leading whitespace + case mismatch on both sides; goose #8722 introduces `ui/goose2/scripts/start-dev-vite.sh` with a load-bearing reuse predicate that requires both `PROC_CWD == PROJECT_DIR` AND `PROC_ARGS == *"vite --port ${VITE_PORT}"*` (`:90`) before reusing an existing port-holder, plus operator-friendly failure messages naming the recovery commands, plus a `VITE_PORT` env-var override path threading through the renamed `default_vite_port` (was `vite_port`) at `justfile:1-3,86,136,190` — fixes the "second `just goose2 dev` invocation tears down the relaunch on Vite `--strictPort` failure" UX bug; qwen-code #2968 fixes a textbook JS-truthiness-vs-Map-membership LRU bug at `LruCache.ts:16-23` by replacing `if (value)` (which treated `0`/`''`/`false`/`null` as cache misses for recency-promotion purposes) with `if (!this.cache.has(key)) return undefined;` followed by unconditional reorder — caches holding falsy values now get correct LRU promotion and stop being prematurely evicted under capacity pressure.

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (codex #20242 — pure doc clarification of an already-established RPITIT convention with concrete commit reference, zero code/CI risk; litellm #26800 — pure pricing-data PR with verifiable 1.6× multiplier across 14 entries and the `_above_1hr_above_200k_tokens` quadrant correctly handled, the kind of fix that should land same-day to stop spend-log under-reporting; gemini-cli #25937 — two-line correctness fix backed by a behaviour-flipping test that addresses a real "transient failure → permanent tool loss" bug; qwen-code #2968 — textbook JS-truthiness-vs-Map-membership LRU fix, six-line diff, single function, no API change, obviously correct from inspection), 4 merge-after-nits (opencode #24996 lift `MISTRAL_REASONING_IDS` to module scope so the next reasoning gate (Codestral, Devstral) can compose with it instead of copy-pasting, tighten the `String.includes` matching to anchored or exact-equality so a hypothetical `mistral-medium-3.5-preview-mini-tool` doesn't accidentally pick up reasoning, parametrize the renamed pluralized test across all entries to keep the name honest; codex #20245 the doc comment never defines what "external" means in contrast to the parent `BrowserUse` flag and a one-sentence delta would make requirements-side gate decisions easier, the new test cases are inlined into the parent `browser_controls_*` fn which now mixes assertions about three features in one body and would benefit from an `assert_stable_default_on(Feature, &str)` helper, `default_enabled: true` for an external-integration flag deserves a justification line since most external-integration gates default to off, no CHANGELOG entry for a Stage::Stable flag shipping enabled by default; gemini-cli #25936 extract `.trim().toLowerCase()` into a `normalizeName` helper colocated with the filter since the same comparison will be needed elsewhere, audit the runtime layer (`mcp-client.ts` / registry) for the same case/whitespace split-brain since this PR fixes the UI lie but may not fix the underlying policy match, consider trimming at config-parse time so this defensive matching doesn't paper over an upstream validation gap, no test for the `null`/`undefined` `blockedServer.name` case where `.trim()` throws; goose #8722 tighten the substring match `*"vite --port ${VITE_PORT}"*` at `:90` to include `--strictPort` since that's uniquely ours and would avoid eating a hypothetical `vite ... --mode preview` sibling, the `lsof -Fn` parser is macOS-tested but Linux may emit additional metadata fields and deserves a comment + smoke test, the `set -euo pipefail` + trailing `|| true` at `:88` masks the "PID held but lookup failed" case, add a one-line goose2 README note pointing at `start-dev-vite.sh` as the source of truth), 0 request-changes, 0 needs-discussion. Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code) — full coverage of the tracked upstream set this drip.

### W18 drip-184 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across all 6 tracked upstream repos (1 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "real correctness fixes plus one substantive feature plus one design-discussion packaging RFC" — opencode #25003 fixes the "switch Claude models mid-session → next message 400s with `messages.N.content.0.thinking.signature: Field required`" bug with a two-layer defence: a narrowed predicate `keepReasoningMetadata = !differentModel || (isAnthropicFamily(new) && isAnthropicFamily(old))` at `message-v2.ts:840-855` codifies that the Anthropic signature is portable across Sonnet/Opus/Haiku/Bedrock-Claude but must be dropped when crossing to a non-Anthropic provider, plus a defensive provider-edge sanitiser at `transform.ts:96-122` that strips reasoning parts whose `providerMetadata.anthropic.signature` is missing/empty — heals replayed sessions and partial migrations without manual intervention; codex #20256 migrates the built-in `tool_suggest` from a top-level Responses-API function into its rightful namespace via `ToolSpec::Namespace(ResponsesApiNamespace { name: TOOL_SUGGEST_TOOL_NAMESPACE = "tool_suggest", tools: vec![ResponsesApiNamespaceTool::Function(... name: TOOL_SUGGEST_TOOL_NAME = "tool_suggest_tool" ...)] })` at `tool_discovery.rs:312-336`, with the test surface honestly updated to use `namespace_child_tool(body, NAMESPACE, NAME)` instead of pretending the tool is still a top-level function — load-bearing detail is the constant rename trick where `TOOL_SUGGEST_TOOL_NAME` keeps its identifier but flips value from `"tool_suggest"` to `"tool_suggest_tool"`, forcing every call site to update via compiler errors; codex #20252 adds responsive Markdown table rendering with two layout strategies (`render_box_table` for wide terminals, `render_vertical_table` field-stacked fallback when columns would be unreadably narrow) plus the architectural change to streaming that preserves raw markdown source for stable blocks while keeping the in-progress block as a width-sensitive live tail at `chatwidget.rs:2039-2045,4781-4787,4858`, with a lifecycle test at `app/tests.rs:3917-3978` that asserts sentinels-around-table appear exactly once at both width=44 and width=96, the box character `'┌'` survives in scrollback at both widths, and `narrow_text.len() > wide_text.len()` (the reflow actually changed the wrap, not just re-rendered the same layout) — that last assertion is the regression-fence for "scrollback re-render is silently a no-op"; litellm #26801 adds implicit-SSL SMTP support via `use_ssl = smtp_use_ssl or smtp_port == 465` at `utils.py:4720-4721` then conditional `smtplib.SMTP_SSL(host, port)` vs `smtplib.SMTP(host, port)` at `:4724-4727`, with the critical `not use_ssl and ...` gate on the `starttls()` call at `:4730` (calling starttls on an already-SSL channel is a 503 protocol error), backed by a 4-test matrix covering `(port=465 vs other) × (SMTP_USE_SSL set vs unset)` plus case-sensitivity plus a port-587 backwards-compat regression guard with `mock_ssl.assert_not_called()`; litellm #26797 fixes the "Daily Spend chart shows N+1 days for non-UTC users on single-day selection" UI bug by collapsing `_adjust_dates_for_timezone` at `common_daily_activity.py:385-407` to `return start_date, end_date` with a docstring explaining why (the frontend already sends local-formatted YYYY-MM-DD that matches the UTC YYYY-MM-DD slice in storage; the prior ±1-day expansion was dragging in adjacent days) — locked by three tests covering PST/IST/None offset variants; gemini-cli #26218 closes a "silent success on model failure" UX gap in non-interactive mode by adding an `InvalidStream` event arm at `nonInteractiveCli.ts:399-413` that routes through all three output formats (stderr `[ERROR] ...`, JSON-stream `severity: "error"`, JSON envelope `error: { type: "INVALID_STREAM" }`), with the `result.status` finaliser at `:524-538` flipping from hard-coded `"success"` to `invalidStreamError ? "error" : "success"`, plus per-format tests asserting both the right error surface and `sendMessageStream).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)` (no-retry-past-InvalidStream); goose #8911 introduces a "distro bundle" packaging concept for the goose2 Tauri shell with `GOOSE_DISTRO_DIR` discovery, `distro.json` manifest (feature toggles, providerAllowlist, extensionAllowlist, appVersion), optional `config.yaml` layered into goose serve via new `GOOSE_ADDITIONAL_CONFIG_FILES` env at `crates/goose/src/config/base.rs:164-179`, and frontend wiring that filters provider/model picker UI and hides cost UI based on the manifest — README explicitly disclaims that the allowlists are UI-suggestions-only and don't enforce backend access control; qwen-code #3752 fixes the "/directory add resolves and adds to in-memory workspace but never persists to .qwen/settings.json" silent-state-loss bug by threading `settings.setValue(SettingScope.Workspace, 'context.includeDirectories', Array.from(new Set([...existing, ...added])))` through the command at `directoryCommand.tsx:154-173`, with the additional shape fix of unifying path representation (single `directory = expandHomeDir(pathToAdd.trim())` binding used for both in-memory `addDirectory(directory)` and persisted `added.push(directory)`, fixing a latent inconsistency where the success message showed `~/foo` while the workspace stored `/home/user/foo`).

Verdict mix: 2 merge-as-is (litellm #26801 — exhaustive 4-test matrix locks (port=465, SMTP_USE_SSL set/unset, case-insensitivity, port-587 backwards-compat) and the auto-detect + explicit env override is the right ergonomic shape; gemini-cli #26218 — uniform error-surfacing across all three output formats with per-format tests asserting both the format-specific error shape and no-retry-past-InvalidStream, single source of truth for the message string), 5 merge-after-nits (opencode #25003 extract a shared `hasValidAnthropicSignature(part)` helper colocated with `isAnthropicFamily` so the next signed-reasoning provider can compose, replace the `(part as any).providerMetadata` cast with typed accessor, add Sonnet→Opus replay regression test + transform-layer scrub test, comment that `modelID.includes("claude")` is an over-permissive heuristic chosen to avoid signature-preservation false negatives, one-line CHANGELOG for Bedrock operators currently working around with /compact; codex #20256 the constant-rename-trick (same identifier, different value) hostile to grep on `tool_suggest` from prior logs/transcripts so prefer retiring the old identifier entirely as `TOOL_SUGGEST_FUNCTION_NAME`, the boilerplate `default_namespace_description("Tools in the {namespace} namespace.")` is pure noise for a single-tool namespace whose function has a 60-line embedded prompt and should be special-cased or replaced with a one-line discriminator, no test asserting backwards-compat on the external wire name (transcripts that recorded `"tool_suggest"` will fail to dispatch since the model must now call `tool_suggest.tool_suggest_tool`), audit `tool_registry_plan` ordering for cache-stable namespace-vs-function emission; codex #20252 document `width: Option<usize>` semantics, exercise the vertical-stacked fallback at width=20 in the app test, harmonise the active-cell-revision guard pattern (inline-`if` vs hoisted-bool) across the agent-stream and plan-stream sites, add one-line comment at `:4781-4787` explaining why `emitted_cells` gates the tail-sync (not "always"), split next time into "extract markdown source preservation + reflow plumbing" then "add table renderer module" for bisectability, follow-up issue for table copy-as-tsv UX; litellm #26797 the workflow_management_endpoints rebase artifact (~1000 LOC of #26793's content) needs to be rebased off this PR so the trivial date-filter fix isn't blocked by the security/correctness items in drip-182's needs-discussion verdict on #26793, mark `timezone_offset_minutes` parameter with `# noqa: ARG001` plus deprecation comment naming follow-up that drops it, add a frontend snapshot/e2e test for "single-day selection shows exactly one bar" to fence the symmetric rendering-side bug class, audit other callers of `_adjust_dates_for_timezone` for accidental reliance on the old expansion behaviour; qwen-code #3752 normalise via `path.normalize().toLowerCase()` on Windows before the dedupe Set membership check so `C:\Foo` and `c:\foo` collapse, one-line comment at `:157-158` naming why writes-to-workspace-only is the correct scope, sanitise the raw `(error as Error).message` interpolation at `:165` to avoid leaking unrelated paths in permission-error cases, add a partial-success test case (one path adds, another fails workspace validation, persist runs only with the successful one), consolidate the `expandHomeDir` test imports), 0 request-changes, 1 needs-discussion (goose #8911 the README is unusually good about disclaiming that `providerAllowlist` is UI-only and doesn't enforce backend access control, but that disclaimer needs to be either accepted-as-is with even louder caveats OR paired with a real enforcement layer since saved sessions pinned to a now-hidden provider keep working, the user can type into a free-form provider field, and direct ACP clients bypass entirely; the new `GOOSE_ADDITIONAL_CONFIG_FILES` env on `Config::default()` is a global affecting every `goose` consumer not just goose2-launched serves so consider scoping to `GOOSE2_ADDITIONAL_CONFIG_FILES` or a `Config::default_with_extras` constructor; the empty `config.yaml` ships without merge-semantics specification (full config vs deltas? user-wins vs distro-wins?); `featureToggles` open-ended `Record<string, boolean>` needs a decision now on unknown-toggle behaviour (silently ignored vs warned vs rejected) before in-the-wild distros depend on the implicit silent semantics; no test for either the Rust env-var loader or the frontend manifest reader and the bug class is "config silently doesn't apply" which makes test absence load-bearing; backwards-compat doc for older `goose` binaries launched by goose2 shells setting the env var). Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code) — full coverage of the tracked upstream set this drip.

### W18 drip-185 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across 5 of 6 tracked upstream repos (1 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). Theme is "small-surface correctness fixes plus one billing-correctness plumbing change plus one analytics-protocol additive field" — opencode #24995 is a one-line fix to the `gemini.txt` system prompt at `:30` adding "using the `question` tool" to the existing clarification-questions instruction so the Gemini agent stops emitting plain-text questions and starts invoking the structured multi-choice picker (matches `trinity.txt`'s established pattern, screenshot-verified behaviour flip); codex #20257 threads `Option<TurnExecutionEnvironment>` through `TurnStartParams`, `TurnSteerParams`, the analytics reducer, and `CodexTurnEventParams` so turn-event JSON now carries `event_params.execution_environment: "remote" | null` for downstream dashboard splits, with end-to-end pipeline tests at `analytics_client_tests.rs:2172-2243` (new) and `:2261-2270` (steer-path extension) plus the serialization-shape lock at `:1837`; codex #20246 fixes a real flag-coupling bug at `tool_config.rs:145-147` where `MultiAgentV2`-only configurations got an empty toolset because `include_collab_tools` keyed solely on `Feature::Collab` — the fix is `let include_collab_tools = include_multi_agent_v2 || features.enabled(Feature::Collab)` plus matching sub-agent disable additions at `guardian/review_session.rs:882` and `tasks/review.rs:113` plus a regression test at `tool_registry_plan_tests.rs:386-422` that explicitly disables Collab, enables MultiAgentV2, asserts the six v2 tool names appear and the collab-only `send_input`/`resume_agent` don't; litellm #26811 closes a silent under-billing gap in `_cost_per_token_custom_pricing_helper` at `cost_calculator.py:191-203` — when callers supplied `custom_cost_per_token` with `cache_read_input_token_cost*` / `cache_creation_input_token_cost*` keys (including the `_above_1hr` and `_above_200k_tokens` time-tier suffixes), the helper was ignoring them and falling through to flat input/output math, fix detects via `_custom_cost_per_token_has_cache_pricing` predicate and routes through the existing `generic_cost_per_token_from_model_info` engine via a synthetic `ModelInfo` constructed by `_get_model_info_from_custom_cost_per_token`; litellm #26809 is a single-line three-condition fix at `auth_checks.py:3296` adding `team_membership.litellm_budget_table.max_budget is not None` to the per-member-budget extraction guard, plus two tests at `:2342-2486` covering both "null per-member clone, valid team-default fallback" (asserts `BudgetExceededError(current=500, max=65)` and `find_unique.assert_awaited_once()`) and "both null → enforcement correctly skips"; gemini-cli #26225 flips `MessageBus.publish()` from "swallow + emit" to "emit + throw" by adding `throw error;` after `this.emit('error', error)` at `message-bus.ts:147` and updates the load-bearing `request()` method at `:243-247` to chain `.catch()` that calls `cleanup()` (preventing response-subscription leak when publish fails inside request) plus six callsite updates across `local-invocation.ts`, `state-manager.ts`, `tools.ts`, `scheduler.ts` to either `.catch(() => {})` (fire-and-forget paths) or `try/await/catch` with `debugLogger.error` (the scheduler confirmation path); gemini-cli #26224 fixes a UTF-16-vs-UTF-8 length-counting gap in `readStdin.ts:36-46` by replacing `chunk.length` with `Buffer.byteLength(chunk, 'utf8')` everywhere and adding a continuation-byte-safe `truncateUtf8Bytes` helper at `:11-18` that walks backward over `(buf[end] & 0xc0) === 0x80` continuation bytes to land on a clean character boundary, with two tests asserting the truncated output (a) hits exactly `Math.floor(MAX_STDIN_SIZE / 3) * 3` bytes for 3-byte Korean chars and (b) contains no `\uFFFD` replacement chars; goose #8919 adds a Codex provider intelligence-tier selector to the switch-model modal mirroring the existing Claude-thinking selector pattern — `isChatGptCodexProvider` predicate at `:224`, `supportsExtraHighChatGptCodexIntelligence` model allowlist at `:228-230`, four-tier i18n labels (`low/medium/high/xhigh`) at `:48-65`, and the load-bearing `selectedChatGptCodexReasoningEffort` clamp at `:363-368` that coerces persisted `xhigh` back to `medium` when the user switches to a non-supporting model.

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (opencode #24995 — one-line prompt addition closing upstream issue #24994 with screenshot-verified behaviour flip and conformance to the existing `trinity.txt` pattern, zero risk to non-Gemini paths; codex #20246 — minimal correct fix to a real flag-coupling bug with a regression test that locks the new behaviour at exactly the right granularity and both sub-agent feature-set construction sites updated symmetrically; litellm #26809 — single-line three-condition null-clone fallthrough fix with two tests that lock both fallback-applies and fallback-also-null branches plus `find_unique.assert_awaited_once()` verifying the fallback path actually fired; gemini-cli #26224 — textbook UTF-8 byte-vs-character bug fix with continuation-byte-safe truncator and two regression tests covering both "multi-byte truncation produces valid UTF-8" and "byte length actually enforces the byte limit" with the `expect(result).not.toContain('\uFFFD')` assertion as the regression fence), 4 merge-after-nits (codex #20257 the `Option<TurnExecutionEnvironment>` serializing to `null` vs an explicit `"local"` enum variant should be a deliberate decision documented either way since downstream BigQuery/Snowflake schema-evolution policies vary, no test asserting the `None` case's exact JSON shape, the steer-path test extension now mixes two assertions in one body and would benefit from a focused `steer_preserves_execution_environment` test, no CHANGELOG/event-schema-doc entry, the enum itself needs a doc comment naming what `Remote` means; litellm #26811 the `usage_object is None` short-circuit silently falls back to flat math with no debug warning, the synthetic `ModelInfo` hardcodes `mode="chat"` and `max_*=None` without naming the cost-only intent, no test in the diff for the new code path, the `_cost_per_token_custom_pricing_helper` signature gained three parameters and existing callers need an audit, billing-retroactive change needs a CHANGELOG; gemini-cli #26225 the empty `.catch(() => {})` pattern at four call sites loses diagnostic information that was in the swallowed error and at least the `subagent_activity`/`tool_calls_update`/`update_policy` paths should debug-log, the `tools.ts:233-237` semantics shifted from sync-only-throw to sync-or-async-failure-with-fail-open which has security implications and deserves a comment naming the decision, inconsistent error-handling shape between the upgraded scheduler `try/await/catch` and the four `.catch(() => {})` sites, missing test for the cleanup-then-reject ordering in `request()` which is the actual subscription-leak bug class; goose #8919 the provider/model name comparisons use strict equality which is brittle to future config-key casing changes, the `xhigh`-supporting model allowlist is hardcoded inline rather than lifted to a module-level set with a documented criterion, the visible-options filter mutates display but underlying state can still hold `xhigh` for unsupported models which is probably intentional UX but undocumented, no unit test on the visibility/clamp predicates, and the persistence call site (`upsert('CHATGPT_CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT', ...)`) isn't visible in this diff and needs verification), 0 request-changes, 0 needs-discussion. Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose).

### W18 drip-186 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across all 6 tracked upstream repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code). Theme is "small-surface correctness fixes plus one operator-facing networking knob plus one CI auth migration plus one cross-tool permission-leak fix" — opencode #25012 lowercases two `model.api.id.includes("deepseek")` / `includes("deepseek-v4")` gates at `provider/transform.ts:200,576` so mixed-case ids from Bedrock/OpenRouter/Together catalogue snapshots (`DeepSeek-V4`, `Deepseek-r1`) now correctly trip the assistant-reasoning-required normalization and the `efforts.push("max")` reasoning-effort extension instead of silently bypassing both; opencode #25007 aligns Azure provider defaults to OpenAI provider defaults to fix the user-visible "Item ... of type 'reasoning' was provided without its required following item" 400 caused by Azure-specific defaults reordering reasoning/follow-up adjacency on the constructed Responses-API request; codex #20186 is a pure 0+/306- dead-code removal of the unreached `memory_trace` module + its tests + three `pub use` re-exports at `lib.rs:198-200`, the `build_memories_from_trace_files` early-stage offline scaffold superseded by the live memory pipeline; codex #20112 softens the skill-budget warning surface across `core-skills/src/render.rs` by raising the truncation-warning threshold 10→100 chars at `:19`, replacing the numeric-tail "Warning: ... truncated by an average of N characters per skill" with two static calmer copies (`SKILL_DESCRIPTION_TRUNCATED_WARNING` + `_WITH_PERCENT` chosen by `SkillMetadataBudget::Tokens` vs `Characters` at `:234-241`), stripping the `"Warning: "` prefix from `SKILL_DESCRIPTIONS_REMOVED_WARNING_PREFIX` at `:23-24` and four downstream test sites, and quietly fixing a real averaging-denominator bug at `:436-444` where the prior `truncated_description_count` divisor made "200 chars across 100 skills" report as "200 chars per skill" instead of the correct "2 chars per skill"; litellm #26730 adds opt-in `AIOHTTP_SO_KEEPALIVE` env-gated TCP keepalive to aiohttp's `TCPConnector` via `socket_factory` (aiohttp 3.10+ detected once at import via `inspect.signature(TCPConnector.__init__).parameters` at `http_handler.py:65-67`) plus three companion knobs `TCP_KEEPIDLE=60` / `TCP_KEEPINTVL=30` / `TCP_KEEPCNT=5` at `constants.py:227-237`, with the load-bearing docstring at `:227-232` naming the AWS NAT Gateway 350s vs OpenAI/Azure 600s timeout mismatch as the motivating bug class, plus cross-platform `TCP_KEEPIDLE`-vs-Darwin-`TCP_KEEPALIVE` `hasattr` chain at `:104-117`, plus a 161-line dedicated test file covering the env-on/off × aiohttp-old/new × Linux/Darwin matrix, plus matching wiring at both `_create_aiohttp_transport` and `proxy_server._initialize_shared_aiohttp_session`; litellm #26741 narrows a flaky `caplog` assertion in `test_model_alias_map.py:33-35` from "no record has levelname == ERROR" (which tripped on unrelated `asyncio` `Unclosed ClientSession` records from sibling tests) to "no record from a `LiteLLM*` logger has levelname == ERROR" plus a `pytest.fail(f"Unexpected litellm ERROR log: {rec.getMessage()}")` that names the offending message instead of just the level; gemini-cli #26223 migrates the bot-brain workflow off a long-lived PAT (`secrets.GEMINI_CLI_ROBOT_GITHUB_PAT`) to on-demand `actions/create-github-app-token@v2` (SHA-pinned `a8d61614...` with ratchet annotation at `:196`) with explicit narrow `{"contents":"write","pull_requests":"write","issues":"write","workflows":"write"}` permissions at `:202`, gated by the same `enable_prs || issue_comment || run_interactive` triple that needs the token, plus a `github.event.comment.user.login != 'gemini-cli[bot]'` self-trigger guard at `:44`, plus identity flip `gemini-cli-robot` → `gemini-cli[bot]@users.noreply.github.com` at `:223-224`, plus REST-API `gh api repos/.../comments` instead of GraphQL `gh issue/pr comment` at `:269,278` to dodge App-token GraphQL friction; goose #8913 enables ACP inventory refresh for Google Gemini at `providers/google.rs:140-142` with `InventoryIdentityInput` keyed by `GOOGLE_HOST` (public, default `GOOGLE_API_HOST`) and `GOOGLE_API_KEY` (secret via `config_secret_value`) at `:144-159`, plus a status-first error-mapping fix at `:178-183` that calls `map_http_error_to_provider_error(status, payload)` before parsing the `models` array — fixes the "invalid Google API key shows misleading 'no models found' instead of '401 Unauthorized'" bug caused by the prior code's `json.get("models").as_array().unwrap_or_default()` path silently swallowing non-success bodies; qwen-code #3726 introduces a dedicated `Monitor(...)` permission namespace with `SHELL_LIKE_TOOLS = new Set(['run_shell_command', 'monitor'])` at `permission-manager.ts:36`, alias parsing at `rule-parser.ts`, and `permissionRules` emission flip from `Bash(...)` → `Monitor(...)` at `tools/monitor.ts` — fixes a real cross-tool permission leak where "Always Allow" on a `monitor` invocation simultaneously (a) failed to suppress future `monitor` re-prompts because the saved `Bash(tail -f *)` rule didn't match `toolName: 'monitor'` and (b) silently granted `run_shell_command` access to anything matching the same pattern, with five-axis test coverage at `:973-1042` including the load-bearing cross-tool isolation regressions ("Monitor approval does NOT allow run_shell_command", "Bash approval does NOT allow monitor").

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (opencode #25012 — minimal correct case-insensitive lowercasing on both DeepSeek gates with no false negatives versus prior check, addresses real catalogue-source variance; codex #20186 — clean 0+/306- dead-code removal with co-located test cleanup, no behavioural risk; litellm #26741 — single-file 7-line targeted deflake narrowing the `caplog` ERROR assertion to `rec.name.startswith("LiteLLM")` with strictly better failure message naming the offending log; qwen-code #3726 — security-correctness fix to a real cross-tool permission leak with comprehensive multi-layer test coverage including the load-bearing cross-tool isolation regressions that lock the bug class against future re-collapse), 5 merge-after-nits (opencode #25007 lacks a cross-provider request-shape regression test that would fence future Azure-vs-OpenAI defaults drift, no CHANGELOG note for Azure operators currently working around with model-pinning, consider extracting a shared `defaultReasoningInterleave` policy so the next divergence is deliberate; codex #20112 the static `_WITH_PERCENT` warning string hardcodes `"2%"` while `SKILL_METADATA_CONTEXT_WINDOW_PERCENT == 2` lives in a separate const so the next budget bump silently makes the warning lie, the magic `49` in `Characters(minimum_cost + 49)` test fixture deserves a one-line comment naming the relationship to the 202-char truncation assertion, the new `total_count == 0 || truncated_description_chars == 0` guard's second clause is belt-and-suspenders deserving a comment, no CHANGELOG entry for what is effectively a user-visible UX wording change to the noisiest diagnostic surface; litellm #26730 the duplicate factory-invocation block at `http_handler.py:996-1000` and `proxy_server.py:687-690` invites drift and should lift to an `apply_keepalive_socket_factory(kwargs)` helper, no Windows code path comment despite Windows aiohttp also supporting `socket_factory` and the `hasattr(TCP_KEEPIDLE)` chain returning False there leaving Windows users with `SO_KEEPALIVE=on` but registry-default 2-hour timing knobs, the `addr_info[0..2]` positional unpack at `:91` is fragile against aiohttp tightening to a typed tuple later, no integration test that opens a socket and `getsockopt`-confirms the options stuck; gemini-cli #26223 the deletion of the PR-author safety check at `:268-273` is necessary for the migration but loses real protection — the replacement should re-instate `if [ "$PR_AUTHOR" != "gemini-cli[bot]" ]; then exit 1; fi` keeping the abort behaviour with the new identity, App private-key rotation cadence undocumented (PATs presumably had expiration policy), the mention-text breaking change `@gemini-cli-robot` → `@gemini-cli` deserves a transition window where both substrings are accepted, the job-level `permissions:` block at `:188-191` is now mostly cosmetic since the App-minted token does the writes; goose #8913 the `serde_json::from_str(&body).ok()` at `:183` silently drops parse errors so non-JSON HTML error pages from corporate proxies produce status-only diagnostics with no body context, no test coverage in diff (both `supports_inventory_refresh()` and `inventory_identity()` are testable, and the HTTP error path via `mockito` would catch the actual user-visible bug regression), the `unwrap_or_else(|_| GOOGLE_API_HOST.to_string())` at `:152` silently treats `TypeMismatch` config errors the same as `MissingKey` which masks misconfigurations), 0 request-changes, 0 needs-discussion. Repo coverage: 6 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose, QwenLM/qwen-code) — full coverage of the tracked upstream set this drip.

### W18 drip-187 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across 5 tracked upstream repos (1 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 2 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose). Theme is "schema/contract sanitization plus auth and bytes-bound discipline" — opencode #25011 expands the Moonshot/Kimi MFJS sanitizer at `provider/transform.ts:1089` from immutable rebuild to in-place mutation that also strips `prefixItems`/`unevaluatedItems`/`exclusiveMinimum`/`exclusiveMaximum`/`minContains`/`maxContains` and the annotation keys `title`/`$comment`/`format`, collapsing tuple-style `prefixItems` to a single `items` schema as MFJS requires, with three new tests covering the new branches at `transform.test.ts:1000-1073`; codex #20260 adds `truncate_mcp_tool_result_for_event` at `mcp_tool_call.rs:654-696` that bounds the rollout/event copy of `CallToolResult` to `DEFAULT_OUTPUT_BYTES_CAP` by serializing then text-truncating into a single content item while clearing `structured_content`/`meta` and preserving `is_error`, applied at both event-emit sites (line 365 approved-call, 1909 skip-path) with the model-visible payload still flowing through the existing `sanitize_mcp_tool_result_for_model` unchanged; codex #20263 adds the missing `originator` header at `backend-client/src/client.rs:206` so usage-snapshot requests align with every other backend call site, locked in by an integration-test wiremock matcher at `rate_limits.rs:152`; litellm #26821 fixes a real auth bypass at `auth_checks.py:377` where `any(coerced.get(key) for key in _GUARDRAIL_MODIFICATION_KEYS)` truthiness-gating let `metadata={"guardrails": {}}` skip the permission check while downstream guardrail evaluation still treated key-presence as "disable all guardrails", switched to `any(key in coerced for ...)` so auth and evaluation finally agree on intent, with a 4×5 parametrized regression at `test_auth_checks.py:2081-2106`; litellm #26810 rips out `_adjust_dates_for_timezone` to remove the extra phantom day-bar in the Daily Spend chart but does so by returning dates unchanged and silently ignoring `timezone_offset_minutes`, which trades the cosmetic phantom for a silent data-loss bug at the day boundary for any non-UTC user since rows landing in UTC Feb 5 from a 23:59 PST Feb 4 request now fall outside the unexpanded range; gemini-cli #26229 wires `ToolActionsContext.isExpanded(callId)` into both `ShellToolMessage.tsx:62` and `ToolMessage.tsx:68` so Ctrl+O actually expands long shell-command headers instead of leaving them truncated, with the `availableTerminalHeight === undefined` fallback preserving non-pty render paths and three new render-tests at `ShellToolMessage.test.tsx:359-415`; gemini-cli #26220 patches the git-repo prompt snippet in both `snippets.ts` and `snippets.legacy.ts` (and the matching snapshot) to recommend `git add <file>...` and explicitly discourage unprompted `git add .` / `git add -A`, addressing a documented agent-stages-unrelated-files footgun; goose #8920 adds a first-class `PerplexityProvider` at `crates/goose/src/providers/perplexity.rs` that delegates to `OpenAiCompatibleProvider`, exposes `sonar`/`sonar-pro`/`sonar-reasoning`/`sonar-reasoning-pro` as known models with `sonar-pro` default, and resolves keys via `PERPLEXITY_API_KEY` with `PPLX_API_KEY` fallback (returning the primary error on miss for log greppability).

### W18 drip-188 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across 5 tracked upstream repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code; block/goose skipped this drip — every open PR there was already covered in prior drips). Theme is "make the failure path honest" — opencode #25014 replaces a 10ms `Effect.sleep` race in the Runner test with a `Deferred<void>` handshake at `runner.test.ts:118-124` so the busy-state assertion only fires after the inner effect provably begins; opencode #25010 ships RTL layout support across `prompt-input.tsx:1347/1370`, `markdown.css` (logical-property migration `padding-left` → `padding-inline-start` etc. plus `text-align: start` and an explicit `direction: ltr` clamp on code blocks at `:208-209`), and a `markBlockDirection` helper at `markdown.tsx:176-181` that runs `dir="auto"` on each block-level child of the rendered markdown root; codex #20277 closes a parser-level evasion where `e\xec` (backslash-escaped) hid as a different word token from `is_known_safe_command` by introducing `decode_unquoted_shell_word` at `bash.rs:194-208` for three call sites, and adds a `sandbox_writable_executable_path_requires_review` gate at `exec_policy.rs:707-727` that forces `Decision::Prompt` (with no `proposed_execpolicy_amendment`) when the command's program is an absolute path inside a workspace-writable region under `RequireEscalated` — the path-identity check beats the name-identity shortcut at `:606-614`; codex #20270 fixes elevated Windows sandbox named-pipe ACL access for Ninja by reading the base token's `TokenUser` SID via two-call `GetTokenInformation`/`GetLengthSid`/`CopySid` in new `get_user_sid_bytes` at `token.rs:255-291` and threading it as an extra restricting SID through new `_with_caps_and_user_from` factory variants wired into `command_runner_win.rs:244-249`; litellm #26823 stops leaking `client_messages` / `dynamic_callback_params` (often carries integration credentials) into the `ValueError` message at `prompt_management_base.py:90,117` and drops `hidden_params`/`_response_headers` (Authorization echoes, set-cookie) from a deserializer-error kwargs forward at `convert_dict_to_response.py:824-829`; litellm #26819 closes an IDOR cluster on team-callback + org-member mutation endpoints by routing every handler through `_verify_team_access`/`_verify_org_access` (four sites in `team_callback_endpoints.py`, four in `organization_endpoints.py`) plus per-handler `except HTTPException: raise` branches at `:251-259, 338-345` so legitimate 4xx responses no longer get rewrapped as 500-Internal-Server-Error noise, with an `unauthorized_caller`+`patched_org_prisma` fixture pair locking the 403 invariant for unauthenticated org access; gemini-cli #26225 fixes a hang where `MessageBus.request()` set up a response listener that never resolved when `publish` failed schema or policy validation — the catch block at `message-bus.ts:144-148` now `throw`s after `emit('error', ...)`, `request` at `:240-247` does `publish(...).catch((error) => { cleanup(); reject(error); })`, and the six fire-and-forget call sites get explicit `.catch(() => {})` to preserve their intentional-ignore semantics now that publish actually rejects; qwen-code #3749 fixes the `[API Error: [API Error: 402 ...]]` double-wrap and duplicate stderr emission in non-interactive mode by introducing an `AlreadyReportedError` marker at `errors.ts:22-46` that the runner throws after formatting-and-printing once at `nonInteractiveCli.ts:392-396, 564-569`, with `handleError` short-circuiting at `:155-172`, the JSON adapter `emitResult` skipped in TEXT mode at `:768-789`, and a belt-and-suspenders `isAlreadyFormatted` idempotency guard at `errorParsing.ts:29-33, 65-71, 79-82` that returns already-bracketed input unchanged.

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (opencode #25014 — minimal correct deflake replacing a 10ms sleep with a Deferred handshake, exactly the canonical Effect-TS pattern; codex #20277 — two-pronged hardening (parser-level escape decode + sandbox-writable-program prompt) with regression tests at both layers locking the contract against future bypass; litellm #26819 — surgical IDOR cluster closure routing every mutating handler through existing `_verify_*_access` helpers with a regression suite that asserts the *correct* status code 403 not 500, plus the secondary fix to stop rewrapping legitimate HTTPException as 500; qwen-code #3749 — canonical double-format fix shape combining a marker-error short-circuit with an idempotency net at the formatter, regression tests lock both layers independently with the failure-mode `dump`-on-mismatch pattern), 4 merge-after-nits (opencode #25010 the per-block `markBlockDirection` is the right shape but doesn't cover inline `<code>` inside RTL paragraphs which would still flip via `dir="auto"` parent inheritance — a `code:not(pre code) { unicode-bidi: plaintext }` carve-out closes that, and the streaming render path needs a sanity-check that `decorate` reapplies on every chunk not just on completion; codex #20270 the `read_unaligned::<TOKEN_USER>` aliases into `user_buf` and a future refactor that drops `user_buf` early would yield UAF — a one-line lifetime comment at `:271` would prevent that, plus the new `_with_caps_and_user_from` factories don't deprecate the existing `_with_caps_from` for elevated paths leaving future contributors no compiler nudge, and the SID-array ordering at `:411-420` isn't validated by Linux CI since the whole module is `#[cfg(target_os = "windows")]`; litellm #26823 drops `e` chaining context that's load-bearing for operator debugging — moving the rich detail behind `verbose_proxy_logger.debug` would preserve operator visibility while keeping it out of error responses, plus `prompt_id={prompt_id!r}` would close an injection-into-log-line corner if the id is user-controlled; gemini-cli #26225 the fail-open `resolve('allow')` at `tools.ts:368` is now triggered by *policy-denied* publishes too since publish actually rejects — flipping that fallback to `'deny'` (failing closed on a publish error) is the safer default, plus the `MessageBus.publish`-now-throws contract change is publicly observable and deserves a CHANGELOG/migration note for downstream consumers), 0 request-changes, 0 needs-discussion. Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, QwenLM/qwen-code) — block/goose skipped because all 30 currently-open PRs were already reviewed in prior drips. Threat-of-the-drip: three of the eight (codex #20277, litellm #26823, litellm #26819) are direct security/safety fixes to real bypass classes (parser evasion, PII/credential leakage, IDOR) — denser than the recent baseline.

Verdict mix: 4 merge-as-is (codex #20260 — surgical fix to a real rollout-bloat bug at both event-emit sites with honest accounting for the JSON-escape inflation in the test budget, model-facing semantics preserved through the unchanged `sanitize_mcp_tool_result_for_model` path; codex #20263 — trivial parity fix where the missing `originator` header was clearly oversight not design, with a wiremock matcher that locks the contract; litellm #26821 — minimal correct security fix replacing truthiness-based with presence-based gating so auth finally aligns with downstream evaluation, parametrized 4×5 regression covering every falsy variant of every key; gemini-cli #26220 — prompt-engineering fix applied symmetrically to both `snippets.ts` and `snippets.legacy.ts` render paths with the matching snapshot, conservative "unprompted" phrasing preserves the explicit-user-request path), 3 merge-after-nits (opencode #25011 the switch from immutable rebuild to in-place mutation is a subtle contract change deserving a "mutates input" comment above the helper plus a sanity check that callers don't share the schema object across providers since a subsequent non-Moonshot call could see the already-stripped schema; gemini-cli #26229 the byte-identical `isExpanded` derivation duplicated across `ShellToolMessage.tsx:62` and `ToolMessage.tsx:68` should lift to a `useToolHeaderExpansion(callId, availableTerminalHeight)` hook before a third tool component copies it, the `lines.length > 5` test assertion is brittle to terminal-width × description-length math and a more targeted assertion on the wrap-mode prop or full-description-string presence in `lastFrame()` would survive layout tweaks better; goose #8920 the hand-maintained `PERPLEXITY_KNOWN_MODELS` will go stale as Perplexity rotates Sonar variants and the setup-steps array should explicitly link the model-list page so wizard users find new models without a goose release, the doc URL `/docs/getting-started` may need updating to the current `/getting-started/overview` path, and the empty system-prompt passed to `OpenAiCompatibleProvider::new` deserves a follow-up to test default behavior on Sonar reasoning-pro), 1 needs-discussion (litellm #26810 — the chart-bar bug is real but "return dates unchanged" trades the cosmetic phantom-bar for silent data loss at the day boundary for any non-UTC user since a 23:59 PST request lands in UTC Feb 5 and the unexpanded Feb 4 query now misses it; the right fix is likely either bucketing spend by *local* date in the aggregator or de-duplicating the phantom bar in post-processing, plus dropping the now-unused `timezone_offset_minutes` parameter and adding a cross-midnight regression test).

### W18 drip-189 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across 5 tracked upstream repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose; QwenLM/qwen-code skipped — every open PR was already in prior drips). Theme is "test-coverage gaps + two big security-surface hardenings + one privacy-sensitive new feature on a wide diff" — opencode #25017 fills a real coverage gap on the HttpApi WebSocket proxy with a new `it.live` case at `workspace-proxy.test.ts:133-152` that wires client → proxy listener → `HttpApiProxy.websocket` → `echoWebSocket` upstream and asserts both subprotocol forwarding (`protocol:chat`) and bytes-bidirectional relay (`echo:hello`), with the load-bearing comment at `:27-28` ("Build into the current test scope so the listener stays alive until the test finishes. Using `Effect.provide` here would release it immediately") explaining the per-test scope discipline; opencode #25016 fixes a TUI UX bug where drag-to-select-to-copy on a question-prompt option silently submitted the option, by importing `useRenderer` at `:3` and adding `if (renderer.getSelection()?.getSelectedText()) return` guards at four `onMouseUp` sites (`:283-286, 311-314, 338-341, 370-373`) following the established pattern from other clickable surfaces; codex #20276 adds a stable v2 `remoteControl/enrollment/read` request at `app-server-protocol/src/protocol/common.rs:754-758` returning `{enrollment: null}` or `{enrollment: {serverId, environmentId}}` with explicit redaction policy named in the README at `:208` ("the enrollment contains only `serverId` and `environmentId`"), a wire-shape lock test at `common.rs:2396-2412`, and a new `state_db: Option<Arc<StateRuntime>>` threaded through `MessageProcessorArgs` — but conflates "no enrollment" with "state DB failed to init" via the `.ok()` swallow at `in_process.rs:402-406`; codex #20271 bumps `.github/workflows/rust-release.yml:52-55` from `timeout-minutes: 60` to `90` with an inline three-line rationale comment ("Codex binaries can take a long time to build, particularly on Windows"); litellm #26825 closes GHSA-5c3m-qffq-4r9m by adding `ALLOWED_OAUTH2_PROXY_FIELDS = frozenset({user_id, user_email, team_id, team_alias, org_id, models})` at `oauth2_proxy_hook.py:9-37` plus a runtime check at `:67-81` that raises `ValueError` with operator-friendly multi-line messaging when `oauth2_config_mappings` keys aren't in the allowlist, preventing the `X-User-Role: proxy_admin` Pydantic-coerce-into-LitellmUserRoles privesc class with 192 lines of regression tests; litellm #26815 closes GHSA-3pcp-536p-ghjc (LFI) and GHSA-pjc9-2hw6-78rr (SSRF) on `/get_image`, `/get_favicon`, and `/get_logo_url` by introducing a 137-line `static_asset_utils.py` with `resolve_local_asset_path` (realpath-based containment in `[assets_dir, current_dir]` rejecting symlink-out-of-allowed-roots) and `fetch_validated_image_bytes` (delegates to `async_safe_get` for per-redirect-hop SSRF revalidation, enforces `ALLOWED_IMAGE_CONTENT_TYPES` allowlist that intentionally excludes `image/svg+xml` per the load-bearing comment at `:38-46` since SVG can embed JS on an unauthenticated endpoint), with `/get_logo_url` redacted to HTTP(S)-only at `proxy_server.py:12224-12239` so local paths no longer leak internal hostnames/filesystem details, plus a Vault `lookup-self` `async_safe_get` retrofit at `config_override_endpoints.py:393-410`; gemini-cli #26234 reverts the HTTPS-required `validateBaseUrl` check (third trip on the same wagon — removed in #23976, re-added in #25357, removed again now) by deleting `LOCAL_HOSTNAMES` and the protocol gate at `contentGenerator.ts:113-126` plus the corresponding rejection test at `contentGenerator.test.ts:854-863`, unblocking enterprise container auth-proxy configurations but leaving the silent-plaintext-API-key-leak class wide open with no warn/opt-in compromise; goose #8922 adds 1459 LOC of new Nostr-based encrypted session sharing — `crates/goose/src/session/nostr_share.rs` (370 LOC) defines `NostrPublisher`/`NostrFetcher` traits separation at `:39-47`, a `LiveNostrClient::publish` at `:55-72` that fans out to `DEFAULT_RELAYS` (four hardcoded public relays at `:14-19`) with `Duration::from_secs(8)` connect timeout and partial-success semantics (one relay accepting suffices), publishes session JSON encrypted with NIP-44 v2 (ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD via the new `chacha20poly1305 0.10.1` Cargo.lock dep) as kind-30278 events, with the decryption key embedded in the resulting deeplink — privacy-sensitive surface (sessions contain prompts, tool outputs, possibly API keys) that needs explicit crypto + privacy review before landing.

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (opencode #25017 — fills a real WS-coverage gap with the right `Queue`-ordering shape, the per-test-scope discipline named in the comment is the hard-won lesson; opencode #25016 — minimal correct UX guard duplicating the established `getSelection()?.getSelectedText()` pattern from sibling clickable surfaces at four `onMouseUp` sites; codex #20271 — pure release-tooling pain-fix with rationale comment, the per-target timeout discipline can be a follow-up), 3 merge-after-nits (codex #20276 the `.ok()` swallow on `StateRuntime::init` at `in_process.rs:402-406` conflates "no enrollment" with "DB failed to init" leaving operators no way to distinguish, the README should name the call-before-`initialize` contract, and the diff slice doesn't show whether all `MessageProcessorArgs` construction sites got the new `state_db` field; litellm #26825 the `premium_user` gate referenced in the PR body and test docstring isn't visible in the diff slice and needs verification, `models` stays in the allowlist but is enforcement-shaped not pure identity and deserves a one-line rationale comment, and the headline `X-User-Role: proxy_admin` privesc scenario should have an explicit regression test asserting config-load-time rejection; litellm #26815 confirm `default_logo` is constant-derived not env-var-derived so the bypass path stays closed, add an e2e smoke test for `GET /get_image` with `UI_LOGO_PATH=/etc/passwd` returning the default not the file, and consider lifting the lazy `from litellm.proxy.common_utils.static_asset_utils import ...` to top-level if circular imports allow), 0 request-changes, 2 needs-discussion (gemini-cli #26234 third trip on the validation-flip wagon (#23976 → #25357 → this) without (a) a positive test asserting the new acceptance contract, (b) a console-warning fallback for the silent-plaintext-API-key-leak class, or (c) a follow-up for a configurable strictness default — pure unconditional revert puts this PR on the same flip-flop trajectory and the legitimate user-protection concern that motivated #25357 deserves a "warn but allow" or "configurable" right-shape conversation before landing the third flip; goose #8922 1459-LOC privacy-sensitive feature touching 13 files with NIP-44 v2 crypto from a previously-unused crate that needs a crypto-aware reviewer pass on nonce derivation/MAC-before-plaintext/key-zeroization, default-relay metadata leakage to four third-party operators that needs a UX warning, decryption key in the deeplink that survives clipboard/Slack/browser-history with no rotate/revoke story, session contents potentially carrying API keys + filesystem paths + secrets that flowed through the conversation needing a pre-share scrubber or explicit confirmation dialog, and a Cargo.toml feature-flag audit so `nostr-sdk`'s WASM transitive deps don't bloat non-WASM builds). Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose) — QwenLM/qwen-code skipped because all currently-open PRs were already reviewed in prior drips. Threat-of-the-drip: two of the eight (litellm #26825, litellm #26815) directly close named CVE classes (GHSA-5c3m-qffq-4r9m header-forgery privesc, GHSA-3pcp-536p-ghjc LFI + GHSA-pjc9-2hw6-78rr SSRF on unauthenticated endpoints), plus one (goose #8922) introduces a brand-new privacy-sensitive crypto surface that needs explicit security sign-off before main.

### W18 drip-190 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across 5 tracked upstream repos (3 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose; QwenLM/qwen-code skipped — every recent open PR was already covered in prior drips). Theme is "perf + correctness on hot caches and resolution paths" — opencode #25021 is a minimal canonical-shape Effect-TS deflake at `runner.test.ts:118-124` replacing a 10ms `Effect.sleep` race with a `Deferred<void>` handshake whose `Deferred.succeed(started, void 0)` fires before `Effect.never`, so the busy-state assertion provably runs after the inner effect has begun executing; opencode #25020 eliminates two unconditional git subprocesses (`rev-parse --git-common-dir`, `--show-toplevel`) on cold-startup by adding a `readLinkedWorktree` helper at `project.ts:175-196` that parses `gitdir:`-pointer files + `commondir`/`gitdir` admin metadata in parallel (`concurrency: 2`) with a `pathSvc.normalize` round-trip identity check at `:191`, plus a `directRepo = dotgitInfo?.type === "Directory"` short-circuit at `:230-261` that skips topology subprocesses entirely for ordinary repos and only falls through to `git rev-list --max-parents=0 HEAD` when the cached `opencode` ID file is missing — locked by a new `worktree resolves from gitdir metadata without git topology subprocesses` test at `:204-221` that mocks all three git args (`--git-common-dir`, `--show-toplevel`, `core.bare`) to fail simultaneously; opencode #25015 fixes Home/End caret navigation across mixed contenteditable content (text + `<br>` + non-editable pills) at `prompt-input.tsx:1167-1180` by routing through `Selection.modify(alter, direction, granularity)` with a correct mapping table — `direction = key === "Home" ? "backward" : "forward"`, `granularity = ctrlKey ? "documentboundary" : "lineboundary"`, `alter = shiftKey ? "extend" : "move"` — and an early-return on `metaKey || altKey` to defer to OS-level macOS shortcuts; codex #20282 is a one-line side-chat-return shortcut expansion at `platform_actions.rs:65-75` adding Ctrl-D alongside the existing Ctrl-C with a flipped negative→positive test assertion at `:100-103` (and an explicit `Char('D')` uppercase case) that captures the contract change as an audit trail; codex #20275 plumbs the *runtime-resolved* Bedrock endpoint into `/status` instead of the placeholder configured `base_url` by adding a default `runtime_base_url` method at `provider.rs:107-112` returning `self.info().base_url.clone()` (free for non-Bedrock providers), a Bedrock-specific override at `amazon_bedrock/mod.rs:88-91` that delegates to a new `mantle::runtime_base_url` (private `resolve_region` moved out of `auth.rs:58-64` into `mantle.rs:51-65` with `BedrockAuthMethod` and `resolve_auth_method` hoisted to `pub(super)`), one resolution at `App::run` startup at `app.rs:797-799` with a `tracing::warn!` fallback-to-`None` on AWS SDK error, threaded through `ChatWidgetInit` (`chatwidget.rs:609,826,10379`) into `format_model_provider` at `status/card.rs:706-714` whose previous `provider.base_url.as_deref()` is replaced with `runtime_base_url.and_then(sanitize_base_url)` while preserving the `is_default_openai` "no chip" UX, with a regression test at `status/tests.rs:243-292` that asserts the runtime URL `eu-west-1` is rendered AND the configured `us-east-1` URL is NOT (the second assertion is what locks the contract); litellm #26830 surfaces YAML-defined guardrails in the dashboard Guardrail Monitor by (a) fixing the root-cause field-drop at `guardrail_registry.py:487-491` where `initialize_guardrail` was discarding `guardrail_info`, (b) introducing `_get_guardrail_field` and `_to_dict` helpers at `usage_endpoints.py:140-162` that handle Prisma row + dict + `LitellmParams` Pydantic uniformly (also fixing a falsy-value bug class in the previous `getattr-or-get` ladder), and (c) union-deduping DB + `IN_MEMORY_GUARDRAIL_HANDLER.list_in_memory_guardrails()` at `:281-296` (DB precedence, parity with `get_guardrail_info`) plus in-memory fallback on DB-miss for `/usage/detail` at `:359-365` and `/usage/logs` at `:597-606`, locked by a 360-line regression suite covering YAML-only, DB-only, both-with-DB-precedence (`assert_not_called` on the in-memory handler), metric-by-logical-name with explicit anti-orphan-row assertion (`orphan_rows == []`), and an integration test exercising the *real* `IN_MEMORY_GUARDRAIL_HANDLER` to lock the persistence fix end-to-end; litellm #26829 fixes two coupled cache-hierarchy bugs — Bug 1: the spend-counter Redis TTL was set on key creation but never refreshed, evicting hot counters mid-billing-window; fixed by an opt-in `refresh_ttl: bool = False` on `redis_cache.py:824-845`'s `async_increment` (default preserves rate-limit-window semantics) wired through `dual_cache.py:392-426` and enabled at the three counter-write sites (`proxy_server.py:1975, 1979-1981`, `spend_counter_reseed.py:155`); Bug 2: per-pod in-memory cache was masking cross-pod increments on a Redis clean-miss, fixed via a `redis_clean_miss = False` flag at `proxy_server.py:1790-1817` and `spend_counter_reseed.py:131-148` that distinguishes "Redis returned None → go straight to DB-reseed (authoritative)" from "Redis errored → graceful fall-back to in-memory" — plus a `_invalidate_spend_counter` static helper at `reset_budget_job.py:55-77` that centralizes "zero both layers + warn naming over-enforcement consequence" and is now applied uniformly across team-member, key, user, team, and budget-linked-key reset paths (previously only team-member had counter invalidation, the other four leaked stale counters past DB-row resets); locked by `expire.assert_awaited_once_with(key, 60)` for `refresh_ttl=True` and `expire.assert_not_awaited()` for the default-`False` window case in `test_redis_cache.py:54-93`, plus four budget-reset-path invalidation regressions in `test_reset_budget_job.py:1057-1148+`; gemini-cli #26238 fixes active-topic-marker leakage into chat output by migrating from `[Active Topic: ...]` square-bracket markers to `<active_topic>\n${sanitized}\n</active_topic>` XML-tag shape at `promptProvider.ts:280-285` (XML tags are far less likely to be re-emitted by the model as natural-language formatting), broadens the topic-string sanitization regex from `/\]/g` to `/[\[\]<>]/g` to defend against tag-injection in user-supplied topic text, and adds a "No topic markers in chat" mandate to both `snippets.ts:659` and `snippets.legacy.ts:530` so coverage holds across both prompt builders; goose #8916 fixes a Bedrock prompt-cache-hit-rate-of-zero regression where the cache breakpoint was anchored to the first three messages (`MESSAGE_CACHE_BUDGET = 3` with `idx < cache_count`) — but Bedrock's cache is keyed by SHA-of-prefix-ending-at-the-breakpoint and lookback walks backward from each *new* breakpoint, so a static [0..2] prefix never grew and turn N always re-paid for messages [3..N]; the fix at `bedrock.rs:235-256` anchors the breakpoint to the trailing visible message every turn (`Some(idx) == last_idx`) so each new turn's breakpoint subsumes the previous turn's as a prefix and fresh tokenization is bounded to "content added since the last request", with the comment block updated to name the prefix-hash + lookback semantics and link the Claude-platform prompt-caching reference.

| [#8916](https://github.com/block/goose/pull/8916) | fix(bedrock): cache trailing message for stable prefix across agent turns | [2026-W18/drip-190/block-goose-pr-8916.md](2026-W18/drip-190/block-goose-pr-8916.md) |

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (opencode #25021 — canonical Effect-TS deflake using a `Deferred<void>` handshake instead of a 10ms sleep, the signal-then-park ordering provably runs the busy-state assertion only after the inner effect has begun; codex #20282 — one-line side-chat-return shortcut expansion adding Ctrl-D alongside Ctrl-C with the previous negative-assertion flipped positive as the audit trail and an explicit uppercase `Char('D')` case locking the case-insensitive contract; litellm #26830 — root-cause persistence fix at `guardrail_registry.py:491` plus three coordinated endpoint fixes (`/usage/detail` + `/usage/overview` + `/usage/logs`) using a clean `_get_guardrail_field` / `_to_dict` consolidation, with a 360-line regression suite locking DB-precedence-on-collision via `assert_not_called`, anti-orphan-row metric-by-logical-name lookup via `orphan_rows == []`, and an integration test exercising the real in-memory handler end-to-end), 5 merge-after-nits (opencode #25020 the directRepo and linked-worktree paths each write a project-ID cache file at slightly different anchors (`dotgit/opencode` vs `linkedWorktree.common/opencode`) and the precedence between them on cache-disagreement isn't named in a comment, plus the `Effect.all` `concurrency: 2` at `:181` is the right call but the comment should disambiguate "perf hint" from "ordering doesn't matter", and the gratuitous blank-line removal at `:18-20` is unrelated diff noise; opencode #25015 the `selection.modify` call should be wrapped in `try/catch` for older WebKit/mobile contexts that silently no-op on `documentboundary` granularity, the `event.preventDefault()` should be unconditional at the top of the block since the null-selection path currently lets the browser's default Home/End fire creating inconsistent behavior, and a jsdom regression test on the argument-mapping table (`alter`/`direction`/`granularity`) would prevent a future "simplification" from inverting the args silently; codex #20275 the `create_model_provider(provider.clone(), /*auth_manager*/ None)` at `app.rs:604` captures the runtime URL once at startup and can't refresh on AWS SDK region drift mid-session, the 17-arg `new_status_output_with_rate_limits_handle` signature with `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]` at `card.rs:185` is begging for a struct-args refactor on the next touch, and the test thread-through across 5 fixture files would be absorbed by a `Default` impl on `ChatWidgetInit`; litellm #26829 the `refresh_ttl: bool = False` default is a footgun — any new spend-counter caller forgetting `refresh_ttl=True` recreates Bug 1 silently, splitting into `async_increment_counter` / `async_increment_window` would make misuse a type error, plus the `redis_clean_miss` flag pattern is duplicated at `proxy_server.py:1797` and `spend_counter_reseed.py:140` and would benefit from a shared `redis_get_with_clean_miss(cache, key) -> tuple[Optional[float], bool]` helper, and the new `find_many` before `update_many` in `reset_budget_for_keys_linked_to_budgets` is one extra DB round-trip per reset cycle that may need chunking for large tenants; gemini-cli #26238 the topic sanitization at `:283` is character-stripping not escaping (a topic literally named `Refactor <auth>` becomes `Refactor auth`, silent information loss), no negative regression test that asserts the *output* doesn't contain `[Active Topic:` so the pre-image marker shape isn't locked out, and the prompt-instruction approach is a soft contract — a render-layer regex scrub on the model's actual output would be a stronger guarantee against occasional leakage; goose #8916 the trailing-message cache anchor depends on `is_agent_visible()` filter ordering so a future hidden tool-result placeholder added to the conversation shape would silently shift the breakpoint, no focused regression test asserting only `idx == last_idx` gets `cache_breakpoint=true`, the doc URL is canonical-Anthropic-platform but Bedrock's *implementation* of prompt caching may drift from it and a second link to AWS Bedrock prompt-caching docs would shore up the rationale, and the deleted `MESSAGE_CACHE_BUDGET` constant should live on as a `CACHE_BREAKPOINTS_PER_REQUEST: usize = 1` named-thing for the eventual multi-breakpoint expansion (Bedrock allows up to 4)), 0 request-changes, 0 needs-discussion. Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos (sst/opencode, openai/codex, BerriAI/litellm, google-gemini/gemini-cli, block/goose) — QwenLM/qwen-code skipped because all currently-open PRs were already reviewed in prior drips. Theme-of-the-drip: cache-correctness density — three of the eight (codex #20275 runtime-vs-config endpoint mismatch, litellm #26829 TTL-never-refreshed + cross-pod in-memory masking, goose #8916 prompt-cache-anchored-to-static-prefix) are all "the thing displayed/cached/keyed wasn't actually the thing being used" bugs in three unrelated codebases on the same drip.

### W18 drip-191 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across 5 tracked upstream repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code; block/goose skipped — only fresh open candidate (#8924) was a multi-thousand-line goose2 feature outside drip scope). Theme is "perimeter + plumbing" — opencode #25027 lands a 437-line workspace-routing test suite plus a small `service-use.ts` Effect helper to lock the HttpApi middleware contract; opencode #25009 adds the missing `DELETE /project/:id` half of the project CRUD across handler/route/service layers with both unit and httpapi tests; codex #20294 introduces `/ide` context support in the TUI via a new ~543-line IPC channel (`ide_context/ipc.rs`) that needs trust-boundary review before merge; codex #20280 swaps the regex-based PowerShell command-safety matcher for an AST parser (`exec_policy.rs` + `powershell.rs`, +228 lines of parser, +181 lines of policy tests); litellm #26832 forwards `extra_headers` through the OpenAPI-backed MCP server path; litellm #26836 encrypts user-scoped MCP credentials at rest with a 294-line test suite; gemini-cli #26241 swaps a stale terminal-size source for ink's `useStdout` to fix tmux scroll behavior; qwen-code #3753 adds an explicit `proxy` settings entry plumbed through CLI config + JSON schema.

### W18 drip-192 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across 5 tracked upstream repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 QwenLM/qwen-code; block/goose skipped — fresh open candidates either out of drip scope or already covered in prior drips). Theme is "boundary tightening" — three of the eight close real trust/security boundaries on shipping endpoints (litellm #26841 gates X-Forwarded-* on OAuth discovery behind a trusted-proxy CIDR list, codex #20298 adds server-side reject-before-download for admin-disabled remote plugins, opencode #25035 swaps mutable global env reads for typed `Effect.Config`-derived layers in HttpApi auth middleware), and three more harden test/operational surfaces (opencode #25036 dual-suite migration of HttpApi tests, gemini-cli #26217 fixes a real `mkdtempSync(relative-prefix)` repo-pollution bug + adds explicit tmpdir cleanup, qwen-code #3754 replaces SKILL.md inline `gh api` shell-outs with typed `qwen review` subcommands using `execFileSync` everywhere). The remaining two are codex #20300 (consolidates two analytics state maps into one + centralizes drop-warn sites; carries one suspect destructure pattern at the guardian path that may silently drop events for threads with metadata present) and litellm #26833 (propagates `cache_control` through Responses-API → Chat-Completion transform; real money-on-the-table fix for Responses-on-cache-aware-providers callers).

Verdict mix: 3 merge-as-is (sst/opencode #25035, openai/codex #20298, BerriAI/litellm #26841), 5 merge-after-nits (sst/opencode #25036, openai/codex #20300, BerriAI/litellm #26833, google-gemini/gemini-cli #26217, QwenLM/qwen-code #3754), 0 request-changes, 0 needs-discussion. Repo coverage: 5 distinct repos. Note for codex #20300: the guardian-event destructure pattern `Some(ResolvedAnalyticsContext { connection_state, thread_metadata: None, })` at the new resolver call site only matches when metadata is absent, which contradicts the `ThreadMetadataRequirement::NotRequired` semantic — flagged as nit #1 and worth a recheck before merge as it may silently drop guardian events on threads with metadata.

### W18 drip-193 (2026-04-30)

8 fresh PRs across 6 tracked upstream repos (2 sst/opencode, 2 openai/codex, 2 BerriAI/litellm, 1 google-gemini/gemini-cli, 1 block/goose; QwenLM/qwen-code skipped — every recent merged PR was already covered in prior drips). Theme is "lock the contract, then refactor under green" — opencode #25033 lands the HttpApi authorization middleware test suite that #25035's layer-injected refactor depends on, and opencode #25042 covers the new `ConfigService` factory with a focused 65-line suite that locks all three entry points (`defaultLayer` parse-from-provider, `defaultLayer` use-defaults, `layer(input)` direct-value) including a `satisfies Context.Service.Shape` clause that catches type-shape drift at compile time. codex #20284 reshapes session-import to run in a `tokio::spawn` background job behind a 1-permit `Semaphore`, with the trust-boundary check (`fs::canonicalize` + `starts_with` on the projects-root) gating the API thread before any work is queued and a regression test that uses `tokio::sync::Notify` to assert the JSON-RPC response returns *before* the background materialization completes; codex #20291 is a -750-line dead-code purge of legacy API-version branching across `bespoke_event_handling.rs` (`handle_error` deleted entirely at `:2415-2519`, `-105/+6`) and `codex_message_processor.rs` (~14 single-line pass-through removals). litellm #26710 fixes a real wire-rejection bug on the tool-result path: `data:application/pdf;base64,...` URIs were being wrapped as Anthropic `image` blocks (and rejected) — fix sniffs the data-URI mime via `_is_anthropic_document_data_uri` against the constant set `{application/pdf, text/plain}` at `factory.py:1664`, synthesizes a `ChatCompletionFileObject`, and routes through the existing document-translation path; mirror change on the Bedrock Converse path, +407 lines of regression covering both providers and both branches. litellm #26831 fixes a real identity-leak: `_store_batch_managed_object` was reading `kwargs.get("user_id", "default-user")` instead of `kwargs["litellm_params"]["metadata"]["user_api_key_user_id"]` (the canonical place identity lives elsewhere in the proxy), so every Anthropic and Vertex batch managed-object was being stored under `"default-user"` with `team_id=None` regardless of who authenticated; 8-line fix per provider, parameterized regression locking both metadata-present and metadata-absent branches with `assert_called_once()` + field-value assertions on the captured `UserAPIKeyAuth`. gemini-cli #26230 patches a plan-mode escape where the model called `run_shell_command({command: "exit_plan_mode ..."})` instead of the actual tool, bypassing the approval gate — fix strengthens the prompt at all four mention sites (`snippets.ts:607,631`, `snippets.legacy.ts:480`, plus 6 mirror snapshot updates) with an explicit `**CRITICAL: NEVER attempt to call this tool via run_shell_command.**` directive, locked by a behavioral eval at `plan_mode.eval.ts:423-473` with both negative (`exitPlanViaShell` undefined) and positive (`exitPlanToolCall` defined) assertions on the same prompt that historically triggered the wrong shape. goose #8914 deletes the `preserveSetupLayout` local-state machine in `ModelProviderRow` that was holding the setup form open after a successful save (overriding the canonical `isConnected` truth), simplifies the render guard at `:401` from three conditions to two (`hasFields && isConnected`), and locks the new behavior with a 55-line regression that asserts Disconnect/Edit are present and Saved is absent after a save inside a stateful `SetupSaveRow` wrapper.
